EDCS-00380828.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Valens beneficiarius consularis missus honesta missione Barbaro et Regulo consulibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380828.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Ulpius Valens, a consular beneficiarius, honorably discharged, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Barbarus and Regulus were consuls.
EDCS-00380828.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a discharged consular beneficiarius, Marcus Ulpius Valens, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dated to 215 CE.

EDCS-00380829.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni et Minervae ceteris dis deabusque Acestius Victor beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis X Kalendas Septembres Materno et Bradua consulibus
EDCS-00380829.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, and Minerva, and to the other gods and goddesses, Acestius Victor, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, [set this up] on the 10th Kalends of September, when Maternus and Bradua were consuls.
EDCS-00380829.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Acestius Victor, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, Minerva, and other deities, dated to August 23, 201 CE.

EDCS-00380830.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Aemilius Titianus beneficiarius consularis ex legione II adiutrice votum solvit libens merito Crispino et Aeliano consulibus
EDCS-00380830.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Aemilius Titianus, a consular beneficiarius from the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Crispinus and Aelianus were consuls.
EDCS-00380830.summary: This inscription commemorates the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Aemilius Titianus, a consular beneficiarius, dated to 223 CE.

EDCS-00380831.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque dis inmortalibus Tiberius Claudius Apollinaris beneficiarius consularis ex legione II adiutrice votum solvit libens merito duobus Silanis consulibus
EDCS-00380831.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the other immortal gods, Tiberius Claudius Apollinaris, a consular beneficiarius from the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when the two Silani were consuls.
EDCS-00380831.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Tiberius Claudius Apollinaris, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other immortal gods, dated to 196 CE.

EDCS-00380832.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus beneficiarius consularis ex voto posuit Pedone et Bradua consulibus
EDCS-00380832.translation: Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius, set this up in accordance with a vow when Pedo and Bradua were consuls.
EDCS-00380832.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius, as a vow fulfillment, dated to 196 CE.

EDCS-00380833.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis iterata statione ex voto posuit Tertullo et Clemente consulibus
EDCS-00380833.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the Emperor, Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, at his renewed station, set this up in accordance with a vow when Tertullus and Clemens were consuls.
EDCS-00380833.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the Emperor, made at his new post as a vow fulfillment, dated to 195 CE.

EDCS-00380834.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Deo Silvano pro salute et reditu domminorum nnostrorum Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini Auggustorum et Luci Septimi Getae Caesaris Titus Aelius Secundus beneficiarius consularis iterata statione sub Claudio Claudiano consulare votum solvit libens merito Anullino II consule
EDCS-00380834.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the god Silvanus, for the safety and return of our lords, the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, and Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar, Titus Aelius Secundus, a consular beneficiarius, at his renewed station, under the consular Claudius Claudianus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Anullinus was consul for the second time.
EDCS-00380834.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Titus Aelius Secundus, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Silvanus, for the safety and return of the imperial family, dated to 204 CE.

EDCS-00380835.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Auggustorum agens in statione sub Baebio Caeciliano legato Auggustorum pro praetore Tiberius Claudius Aeternalis beneficiarius consularis in patria sua votum solvit libens merito duobus Augustis consulibus
EDCS-00380835.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of the Augusti, serving at the station under Baebius Caecilianus, legate of the Augusti pro praetore, Tiberius Claudius Aeternalis, a consular beneficiarius, in his homeland, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when the two Augusti were consuls.
EDCS-00380835.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Tiberius Claudius Aeternalis, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of the emperors, made in his homeland, dated to 200 CE.

EDCS-00380836.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio dominorum nnostorum Auggustorum et Getae Caesaris Caius Marcius Lupianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito PlIautiano#Plautiano#PIAU et Geta  consulibus
EDCS-00380836.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of our lords the Augusti and Geta Caesar, Gaius Marcius Lupianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Plautianus and Geta were consuls.
EDCS-00380836.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Marcius Lupianus, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genii of the emperors, dated to 203 CE.

EDCS-00380837.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Geniis Augustorum Publius Aelius Augurinianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis Cilone et Libone consulibus
EDCS-00380837.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genii of the Augusti, Publius Aelius Augurinianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, [set this up] when Cilo and Libo were consuls.
EDCS-00380837.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Aelius Augurinianus, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genii of the emperors, dated to 204 CE.

EDCS-00380838.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci patriae suae Aulus Cassius Clemens beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit Imperatore Antonino II et Geta Caesare consulibus
EDCS-00380838.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of this place, his homeland, Aulus Cassius Clemens, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow when Emperor Antoninus for the second time and Geta Caesar were consuls.
EDCS-00380838.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local Genius by Aulus Cassius Clemens, a beneficiarius consularis, in 205 CE.

EDCS-00380839.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Geniis ddominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Marcus Valerius Reburrus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis Albino et Aemiliano  consulibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380839.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genii of our lords the Augusti, Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Albinus and Aemilianus were consuls.
EDCS-00380839.summary: This inscription commemorates a vow made by Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the imperial Genii, dating to 206 CE.

EDCS-00380840.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imppperatorum Severi et Antonini Auggustorum et Getae Caesaris Marcus Valerius Reburrus beneficiarius consularis iterata statione in patria sua votum solvit libens merito  Apro et Maximo consulibus
EDCS-00380840.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of the Emperors Severus and Antoninus Augusti and Geta Caesar, Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a beneficiarius consularis, on a renewed tour of duty in his homeland, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Aper and Maximus were consuls.
EDCS-00380840.summary: This inscription records a vow by Marcus Valerius Reburrus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the imperial family, likely from 207 CE.

EDCS-00380841.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci patriae suae Titus Aurelius Valentinus beneficiarius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit Imperatore Antonino Augusto III et Geta Caesare II consulibus
EDCS-00380841.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of this place, his homeland, Titus Aurelius Valentinus, a beneficiarius of the Second Adiutrix Legion, fulfilled his vow when Emperor Antoninus Augustus for the third time and Geta Caesar for the second time were consuls.
EDCS-00380841.summary: This inscription details a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local Genius by Titus Aurelius Valentinus, a beneficiarius, during the joint consulship of Caracalla and Geta in 208 CE.

EDCS-00380842.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute dddominorum nnn(ostrorum) Auggg(ustorum) Publius Aelius Frequentianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis Pompeiano et  Avito consulibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380842.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our three lords the Augusti, Publius Aelius Frequentianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Pompeianus and Avitus were consuls.
EDCS-00380842.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Publius Aelius Frequentianus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the three Augusti, dating to 209 CE.

EDCS-00380843.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio [] Ulpius Vitalis et Iulius Secundianus bbenefficiarii consularis Grato et Seleuco consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-00380843.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius, Ulpius Vitalis and Julius Secundianus, beneficiarii consulares, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows when Gratus and Seleucus were consuls.
EDCS-00380843.summary: This inscription commemorates a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius, made by two beneficiarii consulares, Ulpius Vitalis and Julius Secundianus, in 221 CE.

EDCS-00380844.text_edition: I et Genio Imperatoris [domini nostri Severi Ale]xandri Augusti  lv(ius) Vitalis et Iulius Secundianus bbenefficiarii consulares legionis II adiutricis Maximo II et Aeliano consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-00380844.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the Emperor, our lord Severus Alexander Augustus, Ulpius Vitalis and Julius Secundianus, beneficiarii consulares of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows when Maximus for the second time and Aelianus were consuls.
EDCS-00380844.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Emperor Severus Alexander by two beneficiarii consulares, Ulpius Vitalis and Julius Secundianus, in 223 CE.

EDCS-00380845.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Augusti Octavius Avitus et Iulius Secundianus bbenefficiarii consulares legionis I et II adiutricis Crispino et Iuliano consulibus merito votum libentes solverunt
EDCS-00380845.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus, Octavius Avitus and Julius Secundianus, beneficiarii consulares of the First and Second Adiutrix Legions, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows when Crispinus and Julianus were consuls.
EDCS-00380845.summary: This inscription commemorates a vow by Octavius Avitus and Julius Secundianus, beneficiarii consulares, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Emperor Severus Alexander, dating to 224 CE.

EDCS-00380846.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  et Genio huius loci pro salute domini nostri Severi Alexandri Augusti totaeque domus eius Claudius Aurelius et Valerius Caesianus bbenefficiarii consulares votum solverunt libentes merito Modesto et Probo  consulibus
EDCS-00380846.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of this place, for the well-being of our lord Severus Alexander Augustus and his entire household, Claudius Aurelius and Valerius Caesianus, beneficiarii consulares, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows when Modestus and Probus were consuls.
EDCS-00380846.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local Genius by two beneficiarii consulares, Claudius Aurelius and Valerius Caesianus, for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander and his family, in 228 CE.

EDCS-00380847.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti Aurelius Domitianus et Iulius Potentinus bbenefficiarii consulares  Imperatore Severo Alexandro Augusto  et Dione consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-00380847.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of this place, for the well-being of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, Aurelius Domitianus and Julius Potentinus, beneficiarii consulares, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows when Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus and Dio were consuls.
EDCS-00380847.summary: This inscription details a vow made by Aurelius Domitianus and Julius Potentinus, beneficiarii consulares, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local Genius for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander, in 229 CE.

EDCS-00380848.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio domini nostri [Imperatoris Severi] [Alexandri] [Augusti] Domitianus et Florentinus bbenefficiarii consulares ex voto posuerunt Agricola Clementino consulibus
EDCS-00380848.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the Genius of our lord [Emperor Severus] [Alexander] [Augustus], Domitianus and Florentinus, consular beneficiarii, placed this in fulfillment of a vow, in the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus.
EDCS-00380848.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Emperor Severus Alexander, dedicated by two consular beneficiarii during the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus, likely in 230 AD.

EDCS-00380849.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Dignianus et Titius Faustinus bbenefficiarii consulares ex voto posuerunt Agricola et Clementino consulibus
EDCS-00380849.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius Dignianus and Titius Faustinus, consular beneficiarii, placed this in fulfillment of a vow, in the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus.
EDCS-00380849.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by two consular beneficiarii, Aelius Dignianus and Titius Faustinus, during the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus, likely in 230 AD.

EDCS-00380850.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salutem domini nostri Imperatoris Caesa[ris] [Marci Aureli] [Severi] [Alexandri] [Augusti] Titius Faustinus et Lautius Emeritus bbenefficiarii consulares ex voto posuerunt Pompeiano et Paeligniano consulibus
EDCS-00380850.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of our lord Emperor Caesar [Marcus Aurelius] [Severus] [Alexander] [Augustus], Titius Faustinus and Lautius Emeritus, consular beneficiarii, placed this in fulfillment of a vow, in the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus.
EDCS-00380850.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander, dedicated by two consular beneficiarii, Titius Faustinus and Lautius Emeritus, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus, likely in 231 AD.

EDCS-00380851.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aconius Karus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380851.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Aconius Karus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380851.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow by Titus Aconius Karus, a consular beneficiarius.

EDCS-00380852.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Annius Quietus agrimensor miles legionis X geminae piae fidelis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380852.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Annius Quietus, a land surveyor, soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina Pia Fidelis, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380852.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow by Gaius Annius Quietus, a land surveyor and consular beneficiarius of Legio X Gemina Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-00380853.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Porcius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380853.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Porcius, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380853.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow by Publius Aelius Porcius, a consular beneficiarius.

EDCS-00380854.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Sextilianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380854.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Sextilianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380854.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow by Publius Aelius Sextilianus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-00380856.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Vegetus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380856.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Vegetus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380856.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow by Publius Aelius Vegetus, a consular beneficiarius.

EDCS-00380857.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris et Genio collegii Sextus Aemilius Victor beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380857.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the Genius of the Emperor, and to the Genius of the college, Sextus Aemilius Victor, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380857.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Genius of the Emperor, and the Genius of the college, fulfilling a vow by Sextus Aemilius Victor, a consular beneficiarius.

EDCS-00380858.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Silvano et Libero Patri et Genio Sirmii Marcus Aufidius Victor beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380858.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Silvanus, and Liber Pater, and to the Genius of Sirmium, Marcus Aufidius Victor, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380858.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Silvanus, Liber Pater, and the Genius of Sirmium, fulfilling a vow by Marcus Aufidius Victor, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-00380859.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris Caesaris []  Aurelius Septimus et Septimius Quintilianus bbenefficiarii consulares
EDCS-00380859.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of Imperator Caesar [---] Aurelius Septimus and Septimius Quintilianus, consular beneficiarii.
EDCS-00380859.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of an unnamed emperor, made by two consular beneficiarii, Aurelius Septimus and Septimius Quintilianus, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380860.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et ceteris Dis Deabus Marcus Aurelius Titullus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis D C votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380860.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the other Gods and Goddesses, Marcus Aurelius Titullus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380860.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Aurelius Titullus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other deities, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380861.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Barbius Sabinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380861.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aulus Barbius Sabinus, a consular beneficiarius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380861.summary: This inscription commemorates Aulus Barbius Sabinus, a consular beneficiarius, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380862.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Bassius Pietas beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380862.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publius Bassius Pietas, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, dutiful and loyal, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380862.summary: This inscription documents Publius Bassius Pietas, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380863.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Calavius Maximus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380863.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sextus Calavius Maximus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380863.summary: This inscription records Sextus Calavius Maximus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380864.text_edition: Lucius Cassius Praesens beneficiarius Caecili Faustini legati Augusti pro praetore Iovi Optimo Maximo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380864.translation: Lucius Cassius Praesens, beneficiarius of Caecilius Faustinus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-00380864.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Cassius Praesens, a beneficiarius serving under Caecilius Faustinus, a legate of Augustus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380865.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Dius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380865.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Tiberius Claudius Dius, a consular beneficiarius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380865.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Tiberius Claudius Dius, a consular beneficiarius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380866.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Fortis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380866.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiarius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380866.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiarius, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380867.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum Tiberius Claudius Fortis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380867.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiarius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380867.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Mars Augustus by Tiberius Claudius Fortis, a consular beneficiarius, for a vow he fulfilled, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380868.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Maximinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380868.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Tiberius Claudius Maximinus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, dutiful and loyal, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380868.summary: This inscription documents Tiberius Claudius Maximinus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380869.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Postuminus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380869.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Tiberius Claudius Postuminus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380869.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a Roman soldier, Tiberius Claudius Postuminus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380870.text_edition: Marti Tiberius Claudius Postuminus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380870.translation: To Mars. Tiberius Claudius Postuminus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380870.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mars by a Roman soldier, Tiberius Claudius Postuminus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380871.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae ceteris Deis sic Deabusque et Genio Flaviae Sirmii Tiberius Claudius Valentinus beneficiarius consularis ex legione IIII Flavia felice factus ex numero frumentariorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380871.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Queen, Minerva, and the other gods and goddesses, and to the Genius of Flavia Sirmium. Tiberius Claudius Valentinus, a consular beneficiarius from the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, having been made one of the frumentarii, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380871.summary: This is a comprehensive votive dedication to multiple deities and the Genius of Flavia Sirmium by Tiberius Claudius Valentinus, a soldier who was promoted to the frumentarii from Legio IIII Flavia Felix, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380872.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Dindius Latinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380872.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Publius Dindius Latinus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380872.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a Roman soldier, Publius Dindius Latinus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380873.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Egnatius Magnus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380873.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Egnatius Magnus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380873.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a Roman soldier, Marcus Egnatius Magnus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380874.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Fortunae Marti Campestri Dis Deabusque sacrum Titus Flavius Aulus beneficiarius consularis ex numero frumentariorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380874.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Queen, Minerva, Fortuna, Mars Campestris, and the gods and goddesses. Titus Flavius Aulus, a consular beneficiarius from the frumentarii, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380874.summary: This is a comprehensive votive dedication to numerous deities by Titus Flavius Aulus, a soldier from the frumentarii, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380875.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Claudianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380875.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Titus Flavius Claudianus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380875.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a Roman soldier, Titus Flavius Claudianus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380876.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavius Romanus beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-00380876.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Flavius Romanus, a consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-00380876.summary: This is an incomplete votive dedication to Jupiter by Flavius Romanus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD, with the standard 'fulfilled his vow' clause missing.

EDCS-00380877.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Gallonius Iustus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380877.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Gallonius Iustus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380877.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a Roman soldier, Gaius Gallonius Iustus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380878.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Aquila beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380878.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Iulius Aquila, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380878.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a Roman soldier, Gaius Iulius Aquila, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-00380879.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Iulius Iulianus et Aelius Viator bbeneffE(iciarii)#bbeneff(iciarii)#BBFE consularis
EDCS-00380879.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, Iulius Iulianus and Aelius Viator, consular *beneficiarii*, [set this up].
EDCS-00380879.summary: This inscription records a dedication by two consular *beneficiarii*, Iulius Iulianus and Aelius Viator, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local genius.

EDCS-00380880.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Iulius Paulinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380880.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tiberius Iulius Paulinus, a consular *beneficiarius*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380880.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Tiberius Iulius Paulinus, a consular *beneficiarius*, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380881.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Caius Iulius Valens beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380881.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Caius Iulius Valens, a consular *beneficiarius*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380881.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Caius Iulius Valens, a consular *beneficiarius*, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380882.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum Caius Iulius Valens beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380882.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, Caius Iulius Valens, a consular *beneficiarius*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380882.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Caius Iulius Valens, a consular *beneficiarius*, to Mars Augustus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380883.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Licinius Sparsus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380883.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Licinius Sparsus, a consular *beneficiarius*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380883.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Gaius Licinius Sparsus, a consular *beneficiarius*, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380884.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio coloniae Sirmii Lucius Licinius Valens beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Flaviae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380884.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the colony of Sirmium, Lucius Licinius Valens, a consular *beneficiarius* of the Fourth Flavian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380884.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Lucius Licinius Valens, a consular *beneficiarius* of Legio IIII Flavia, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the colony of Sirmium.

EDCS-00380885.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque Lucius Magius Proculus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis missus honesta missione Idibus Decembribus votum solvit hoc die libens merito
EDCS-00380885.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all gods and goddesses, Lucius Magius Proculus, a veteran formerly a consular *beneficiarius*, discharged with honorable mission on the Ides of December, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly on this day.
EDCS-00380885.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Lucius Magius Proculus, a veteran who was formerly a consular *beneficiarius*, made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses on the day he received an honorable discharge.

EDCS-00380886.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Popilius Bassus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380886.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Popilius Bassus, a consular *beneficiarius*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380886.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Marcus Popilius Bassus, a consular *beneficiarius*, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380887.text_edition: Titus Publicius Flavia Martinus Sirmio beneficiarius consularis ex legione IIII Flavia votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380887.translation: Titus Publicius Flavia Martinus of Sirmio, a consular *beneficiarius* from the Fourth Flavian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380887.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Titus Publicius Flavia Martinus, a consular *beneficiarius* of Legio IIII Flavia from Sirmio, fulfilling a vow to an unstated deity.

EDCS-00380888.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Quintius Augustalis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380888.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Quintius Augustalis, a consular *beneficiarius*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380888.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Titus Quintius Augustalis, a consular *beneficiarius*, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380889.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Rubrius Macedo beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380889.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Rubrius Macedo, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380889.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Rubrius Macedo, a consular beneficiarius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380890.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Claudianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380890.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Claudianus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380890.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Claudianus, a consular beneficiarius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380891.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Iulianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380891.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380891.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a consular beneficiarius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380892.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Ulpia Lucius Ratiaris beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380892.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Ulpia Lucius Ratiaris, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380892.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Ulpia Lucius Ratiaris, a consular beneficiarius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380893.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Vaberius Urbanus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380893.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Vaberius Urbanus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380893.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Vaberius Urbanus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380894.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Valerius Iulianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380894.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Valerius Iulianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380894.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Valerius Iulianus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380895.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Veturius Maximus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380895.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Veturius Maximus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380895.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Veturius Maximus, a consular beneficiarius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380896.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Vibidius Priscus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380896.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Vibidius Priscus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380896.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Vibidius Priscus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380897.text_edition: Aelius Martinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380897.translation: Aelius Martinus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380897.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aelius Martinus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-00380898.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Iustus beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-00380898.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-00380898.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a consular beneficiarius.

EDCS-00380899.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Aelius Atticilianus M CE s bbenefficiarii
EDCS-00380899.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of the place, Aelius Atticilianus, and M. C. E., beneficiarii, [dedicated this].
EDCS-00380899.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and the local Genius by two beneficiarii, Aelius Atticilianus and M. C. E.

EDCS-00380900.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Calvinius Frontonianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380900.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Calvinius Frontonianus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380900.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Titus Calvinius Frontonianus, a consular beneficiarius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380901.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci  Valerianus et Iulius Valentinus bbenefficiarii consulares
EDCS-00380901.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of the place, Valerianus and Iulius Valentinus, consular beneficiarii, [dedicated this].
EDCS-00380901.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and the local Genius by Valerianus and Iulius Valentinus, both consular beneficiarii.

EDCS-03000772.text_edition: Expacto nutrimento valetudinarii legionis II adiutricis
EDCS-03000772.translation: After the provision of food for the infirmary of the Second Adiutrix Legion.
EDCS-03000772.summary: This inscription briefly records the provision of sustenance for the infirmary of the Legio II Adiutrix, possibly marking a completion or milestone.

EDCS-03300763.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caraco miles cohortis I Alpinorum equitatae centuria Publi <qui=O>#<qui=O>#QO vixit nnos L  posuit
EDCS-03300763.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Caraco, a soldier of the First Alpine Cohort, cavalry-equipped, in the century of Publius, who lived 50 years, erected this.
EDCS-03300763.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caraco, a cavalry soldier of the Cohors I Alpinorum equitata, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-03700684.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Imperatoris item Genio collegii Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-03700684.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of the Emperor, also to the Genius of the college, Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Loyal, [dedicated this].
EDCS-03700684.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Ulpius Frequentinus, a consular beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix, to Jupiter, the Genius of the Emperor, and the Genius of his college.

EDCS-03700685.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Tranquillus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito  Iovi Optimo Maximo
EDCS-03700685.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Tranquillus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. To Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-03700685.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Titus Flavius Tranquillus, a consular beneficiarius, to Jupiter for the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-03700686.text_edition: Marinus  v et Marcus Aurelius Mamaeanus miles praetorianus
EDCS-03700686.translation: Marinus, V. E., and Marcus Aurelius Mamaeanus, praetorian soldier, [dedicated this].
EDCS-03700686.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marinus and Marcus Aurelius Mamaeanus, a praetorian soldier.

EDCS-03700687.text_edition: Dis Manibus Curtius Felix veteranus cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum vixit nnos LX Aelia Annamata coniux Aelia Carina filia annorum X Aelius Primitivus filius coniux carissimis posuerunt
EDCS-03700687.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Curtius Felix, a veteran of the milliary Cohort of Mauretanians, lived 60 years. Aelia Annamata, his dearest wife, and Aelia Carina, his daughter of 10 years, and Aelius Primitivus, his son, placed this.
EDCS-03700687.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Curtius Felix, a veteran of the Cohors Maurorum milliaria, erected by his wife and children.

EDCS-03700688.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Comatio Publi filio Claudia Adiutori Viruno veterano ex decurione cohortis I Alpinorum equitatae annorum LXX hic situs est Publius Comatius Lamyrus libertus patrono pientissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-03700688.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Publius Comatius, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, Adiutor Virunus, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the First Alpine Cohort, cavalry-equipped, aged 70 years, he lies here. Publius Comatius Lamyrus, his freedman, took care to have this made for his most devoted patron.
EDCS-03700688.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Comatius Adiutor Virunus, a veteran and former decurion of the Cohors I Alpinorum equitata, erected by his freedman Publius Comatius Lamyrus.

EDCS-03700690.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Candidinus miles cohortis Maurorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-03700690.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Claudius Candidinus, a soldier of the cohort of Mauretanians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700690.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Claudius Candidinus, a soldier, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-03700691.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae  voverat hanc aram Ulpius Baccho Marinus veteranus ex tesserario legionis I adiutricis pro salute sua suorumque omnium et nunc solvit laetus cum honore sacrorum Albino II et Aemili ano consulibus
EDCS-03700691.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera. Ulpius Baccho Marinus, a veteran from a tesserarius of the First Adiutrix Legion, had vowed this altar for his own safety and that of all his kin, and now joyfully fulfilled it with the honor of sacred rites, when Albinus for the second time and Aemilianus were consuls.
EDCS-03700691.summary: This votive altar was dedicated to Liber Pater and Libera by Ulpius Baccho Marinus, a veteran, to fulfill a vow made for his family's safety, dated to the consulate of Albinus II and Aemilianus (AD 250).

EDCS-04900895.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Agrippae ex provincia Arabia civitate Cilis Sahareno qui vixit nnos XXIIII Marcus Aurelius Bassus eques Romanus nepoti dulcissimo
EDCS-04900895.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aurelius Agrippa from the province of Arabia, from the city of Cilis, a Saharenus, who lived for 24 years. Marcus Aurelius Bassus, a Roman knight, to his sweetest grandson.
EDCS-04900895.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Agrippa by his grandfather, Marcus Aurelius Bassus, a Roman knight, recording the death of his 24-year-old grandson from Arabia.

EDCS-04900896.text_edition: Caius Attius Quadratus miles legionis II adiutricis annorum XXXXV stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Capitonius Gesatus signifer legionis eiiusdem sic heres Conduni Saturnini ex testamento faciendum curavit
EDCS-04900896.translation: Gaius Attius Quadratus, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, 45 years old, 25 years in service, is buried here. Capitonius Gesatus, a standard-bearer of the same legion, as heir of Condunus Saturninus, arranged for this to be made according to the will.
EDCS-04900896.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Attius Quadratus, a veteran soldier, erected by Capitonius Gesatus, a standard-bearer, acting as heir according to a will.

EDCS-04900898.text_edition: Silvanabes sic Lucilla  TA SVAATA IVEIA GRISP PRO IVI SOLVI
EDCS-04900898.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Silvana and Lucilla, likely related to a vow or dedication.

EDCS-04900899.translation: To holy Fortuna. Agapa placed this vow.
EDCS-04900899.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna Sancta by Agapa.

EDCS-05000669.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Viator (Silvanus the Wayfarer).

EDCS-05000677.text_edition: Vivianus Atrasi fi=IFlius#filius#IFL nnorum III hic situs est titulum posuit sibi vivo Enamaico Aponis filius nnorum XXV vivus et Secunda Atrasi filia annorum XXII defuncta
EDCS-05000677.translation: Vivianus, son of Atrasius, 3 years old, is buried here. Enamaicus, son of Aponis, 25 years old, while living, placed this title for himself, and Secunda, daughter of Atrasius, 22 years old, deceased.
EDCS-05000677.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, possibly a family monument, recording the burial of Vivianus (3 years old) and Secunda (22 years old), and a self-dedication by Enamaicus (25 years old) while still alive.

EDCS-05000678.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Mathi nnorum XXII hic sita est
EDCS-05000678.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Mathi, 22 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-05000678.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavia Mathi, who died at 22 years old.

EDCS-05000680.text_edition: Priepo sic Vettius Marcianus et Iuli
EDCS-05000680.translation: To Priapus, likewise Vettius Marcianus and Juli[---].
EDCS-05000680.summary: This is a dedication to Priapus by Vettius Marcianus and another individual whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-05000681.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius VS L--- F V P--- R posuerunt
EDCS-05000681.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius V.S. L... F.V.P.R. placed this.
EDCS-05000681.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius, likely for a votive offering.

EDCS-05000683.translation: From the workshop of Arn(ensian tribe) Maxentius, Arn(ensian tribe).
EDCS-05000683.summary: This inscription identifies a workshop belonging to Maxentius, who is associated with the Arnenisan tribe, likely marking a product or place of manufacture.

EDCS-05000684.translation: From the workshop of Arn(ensian tribe) Maxentius, Avin(iensis tribe).
EDCS-05000684.summary: This inscription identifies a workshop belonging to Maxentius, associated with both the Arnensian and Avinensian tribes, likely marking a product or place of manufacture.

EDCS-05000685.translation: Of the Legion X Vindobonensis, by Maxentius, master.
EDCS-05000685.summary: This inscription attributes an object or action to Maxentius, a master of the Tenth Legion, which was stationed in Vindobona.

EDCS-05000686.translation: Of the Legion X, by Maxentius, master.
EDCS-05000686.summary: This inscription attributes an object or action to Maxentius, a master of the Tenth Legion.

EDCS-05000687.translation: Of the Tenth Legion, by Ursicinus, centurion.
EDCS-05000687.summary: This inscription identifies Ursicinus as a centurion of the Tenth Legion, likely marking his property or a dedication.

EDCS-05000688.translation: Of the Tenth Legion, by Saturninus.
EDCS-05000688.summary: This inscription identifies Saturninus as a member of the Tenth Legion, likely marking his property or a dedication.

EDCS-05000689.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Teutano pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Augusti et incolumitate civitatis Eraviscorum Publius Aelius Praesens et Marcus Aurelius Proculus IIviri coloniae Aquincensium dedicaverunt III Iunias Imperatore Caesare Severo Alexandro II et Marcello consulibus
EDCS-05000689.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Teutano, for the health of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Augustus and the well-being of the city of the Eravisci, Publius Aelius Praesens and Marcus Aurelius Proculus, duumvirs of the colony of Aquincum, dedicated this on the third of June, with Emperor Caesar Severus Alexander for the second time and Marcellus as consuls.
EDCS-05000689.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Teutano by two duumvirs of Aquincum, Publius Aelius Praesens and Marcus Aurelius Proculus, for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander and the welfare of the Eravisci, dated to June 3rd, 223 AD.

EDCS-05000692.summary: This inscription simply names Publius Iturius Sabinus, likely indicating ownership or commemoration.

EDCS-05200563.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Uccinus Curmillus solvit votum libens  merito
EDCS-05200563.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Uccinus Curmillus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200563.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Uccinus Curmillus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05200564.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aelius Rusticus et Aelia Vera eius pro salute Titi Aeli Veri fili sui ex voto libens merito posuerunt Silanis II consulibus
EDCS-05200564.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Aelius Rusticus and Aelia Vera, his wife, willingly and deservedly placed this offering for the welfare of their son, Titus Aelius Verus, according to a vow, in the consulship of the two Silani.
EDCS-05200564.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Aelius Rusticus and his wife, Aelia Vera, for their son's health, dated to the consulship of the two Silani (likely AD 152).

EDCS-05200565.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mariae Euphrosinae quondam uxori Publius Aelius Crispus maritus et Maria Prima coniugi et patronae carissimae faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-05200565.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Aelius Crispus, her husband, and Maria Prima took care to have this made for Maria Euphrosyna, once his wife, and for her dearest spouse and patroness.
EDCS-05200565.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Publius Aelius Crispus for his late wife Maria Euphrosyna, with Maria Prima also mentioned as having contributed to the monument for her dearest spouse and patroness.

EDCS-05200566.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Septimiae Crescentinae coniugi carissimae et obsequentissimae quae vixit nnos XL Sittius Felix maritus cum Sittia Ursa filia communi et Septimi-- Quintiius faciendum curaverunt optamus sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-05200566.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Septimia Crescentina, dearest and most devoted wife, who lived 40 years. Sittius Felix, her husband, with Sittia Ursa, their common daughter, and Septimius Quintius, took care to have this made. We hope the earth lies light upon you.
EDCS-05200566.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Septimia Crescentina, who lived 40 years, by her husband Sittius Felix, their daughter Sittia Ursa, and Septimius Quintius, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-05200569.text_edition: Publius Aelius Publi filius Aelia Licinius Mursa legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-05200569.translation: Publius Aelius Licinius Mursa, son of Publius, of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful.
EDCS-05200569.summary: This inscription is a military dedication for Publius Aelius Licinius Mursa, identifying him as a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-05200570.text_edition: Caius Valerius Cai filius Publilia Macer Verona miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Caecili Quinti annorum XL stipendiorum XX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Marcus Calabonius Secundus veteranus legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-05200570.translation: Gaius Valerius Macer, son of Gaius, of the Publilia tribe, from Verona, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, of the century of Caecilius Quintus, 40 years old, having served 20 years, lies here. He ordered this to be made by will. Marcus Calabonius Secundus, a veteran of the same legion and his heir, took care to have it made.
EDCS-05200570.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Valerius Macer, a soldier of Legio II Adiutrix from Verona, who died at age 40 after 20 years of service, erected by his heir Marcus Calabonius Secundus according to Macer's will.

EDCS-05200571.text_edition: Deo Soli I(nvicto) M(ithrae) Cassius Pius? Marcellinus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Acmazon locum Deo constituerunt votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-05200571.translation: To the God Sol Invictus Mithras. Cassius Pius Marcellinus, laticlavius tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, and Acmazon, established this place for the god and willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-05200571.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a sacred place to Sol Invictus Mithras by Cassius Pius Marcellinus, a laticlavius tribune of Legio II Adiutrix, and Acmazon, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05200572.text_edition: Deo Soli  Invicto Mithrae  locum constituit Pius Marcellinus
EDCS-05200572.translation: To the God Sol Invictus Mithras. Pius Marcellinus established this place.
EDCS-05200572.summary: This is a brief dedication recording that Pius Marcellinus established a sacred place for the god Sol Invictus Mithras.

EDCS-05200573.text_edition: Soli Invicto sacrum Caius Iulius Cai filius Arnensi Lepidus Tertullus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum exsolvit
EDCS-05200573.translation: Sacred to Sol Invictus. Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus, son of Gaius, of the Arnensis tribe, laticlavius tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200573.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Sol Invictus by Gaius Julius Lepidus Tertullus, a laticlavius tribune of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-05200574.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mitrhae sic Caius Minucius Ticidianus Annius Faustus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit
EDCS-05200574.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras. Gaius Minucius Ticidianus Annius Faustus, laticlavius tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200574.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Sol Invictus Mithras by Gaius Minucius Ticidianus Annius Faustus, a laticlavius tribune of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-05200575.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Sextus Decimius Verus Barbarus tribunus legionis II adiL(utricis)#adi(utricis)#ADL clarissimus iuvenis pro salute sua et suorum ex voto
EDCS-05200575.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras. Sextus Decimius Verus Barbarus, tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, most distinguished youth, made this offering according to a vow for his own welfare and that of his family.
EDCS-05200575.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Sol Invictus Mithras made by Sextus Decimius Verus Barbarus, a tribune of Legio II Adiutrix and a distinguished young man, for his own and his family's well-being.

EDCS-05200576.text_edition: Invicto  Mithrae pro salute Luci Aureli Galli tribuni laticlavii  Aurelius Aliphus posuit
EDCS-05200576.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, for the well-being of Lucius Aurelius Gallus, tribune laticlavius, Aurelius Aliphus placed this.
EDCS-05200576.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Mithras, set up by Aurelius Aliphus for the health of Lucius Aurelius Gallus, a high-ranking military officer.

EDCS-05200577.text_edition: Dis Manibus  hic iacet ecce vides pupa sine fine decoris quae mihi  tam subito rapta est neque adhuc credere possim non egCo#ego#ECO tam modica potui maculare parentem Cassia sum vocita iaceoT#iaceo#IACET sub saxo reclusa IIII annos vixi et menses V tu quidem quem recitas ablatu pietate parentis quisquis es vale
EDCS-05200577.translation: To the Divine Manes. Here lies, behold, a girl of endless beauty, who was snatched from me so suddenly, nor can I yet believe it. I could not, so young, stain my parent. I am called Cassia. I lie enclosed beneath this stone. I lived 4 years and 5 months. You, indeed, whom you recite, robbed by a parent's piety, whoever you are, farewell.
EDCS-05200577.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Cassia, placed by her grieving parents, lamenting her premature death at four years and five months old.

EDCS-05200607.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaio Antonio Victori quondam optioni spei legionis III Gallicae qui vixit nnos XLVII Trachenti origine DEISTA civitate Lasseorum curam  Antonio Prot col amico pos(uit)
EDCS-05200607.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Antonius Victor, once an optio of the spei of the Third Gallic Legion, who lived 47 years, from Trachentum by origin, of the city of the Lassians. Antonius Protcolus, his friend, placed this care.
EDCS-05200607.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Antonius Victor, a veteran of the Third Gallic Legion, set up by his friend Antonius Protcolus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000826.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Augustales municipii libentes fecerunt
EDCS-07000826.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Augustales of the municipality willingly made this.
EDCS-07000826.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, erected by the Augustales (a college of priests) of a municipality as a voluntary act of devotion.

EDCS-07600380.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et  Iunoni Reginae Aurelius Monimus decurio cohortis |(miliariae) novae Severianae pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600380.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, Aurelius Monimus, decurion of the new Severian one-thousand-man cohort, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being and that of his family.
EDCS-07600380.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, set up by Aurelius Monimus, a decurion, for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-07600381.text_edition: Solia Medullionis filia Bruti  Risimarus Scutalonis filius titulum posuit sibi vivo et coniugi Ducae Dimiuci filiae annorum LX et Ritimauro filio annorum XXX
EDCS-07600381.translation: Solia, daughter of Medullio, and Brutus Risimarus, son of Scutalo, placed this monument for himself while living, and for his wife Duca, daughter of Dimius, aged 60, and for his son Ritimaurus, aged 30.
EDCS-07600381.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a family tombstone, set up by Brutus Risimarus for himself while still alive, and for his wife Duca and son Ritimaurus.

EDCS-07600382.text_edition: io e licus coniugi carissimae et sibi vivo posuit
EDCS-07600382.translation: Io Elicus placed this for his dearest wife and for himself while living.
EDCS-07600382.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, set up by Io Elicus for his wife and for himself while still alive.

EDCS-07600383.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Iodvia, presumably aged 20, set up by her heir, Tiro.

EDCS-07600384.text_edition: Matiata R filia vixit annos   filius posuit titulum sibi vivo et coniugi
EDCS-07600384.translation: Matiata R., daughter, lived X years. [---] son placed this monument for himself while living and for his wife.
EDCS-07600384.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Matiata R., a daughter who lived a certain number of years, with an unnamed son placing a monument for himself and his wife.

EDCS-07600385.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio iano vixit annos  stipendiorum X et Tito Aurelio  vixit nnos XXVIII et Tito Aurelio Valenti vixit annos  stipendiorum VI duplarius sic  Aurelius Iustus veteranus  et Aelia Quinta parentes filiis dulcissimis ponendum curaverunt
EDCS-07600385.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Aurelius Ianuarius, who lived X years, with 10 years of service; and to Titus Aurelius [---], who lived 28 years; and to Titus Aurelius Valens, who lived X years, with 6 years of service, a duplarius. Thus, Aurelius Iustus, a veteran, and Aelia Quinta, the parents, arranged for this to be placed for their sweetest sons.
EDCS-07600385.summary: This is a funerary inscription for three sons, Titus Aurelius Ianuarius, Titus Aurelius [---], and Titus Aurelius Valens, who were soldiers, placed by their parents, Aurelius Iustus (a veteran) and Aelia Quinta.

EDCS-07600387.text_edition: Aelius Valerius speculator legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600387.translation: Aelius Valerius, a speculator of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600387.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Aelius Valerius, a scout from the Second Adiutrix Legion, to an unnamed deity, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600391.text_edition: Terrae Matri  Marcus Antonius Victorinus IIvir coloniae Aquincensium cum Ulpia Spectata coniuge votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600391.translation: To Mother Earth, Marcus Antonius Victorinus, duumvir of the colony of Aquincum, with his wife Ulpia Spectata, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07600391.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth by Marcus Antonius Victorinus, a duumvir of Aquincum, and his wife Ulpia Spectata, indicating a religious offering, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600392.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Augustae Marcus Aurelius Epigonus decurio coloniae Aquincensium pro salute sua et suorum omnium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600392.translation: To Juno Regina Augusta, Marcus Aurelius Epigonus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own welfare and that of all his kin.
EDCS-07600392.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Juno Regina Augusta by Marcus Aurelius Epigonus, a decurion of Aquincum, made for the well-being of himself and his family, characteristic of a religious votive, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600393.text_edition: Augusti et Antonini Augustorum e[t Getae] sanctissimi Cae[saris] Quintus Cornelius Quinti filius Arnensis Secundus praefectus cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum posuit
EDCS-07600393.translation: For the Augusti and Antoninus Augustus and the most holy Caesar Geta, Quintus Cornelius Secundus, son of Quintus, from the Arnensis tribe, prefect of the milliary cohort of Mauretanian troops, set this up.
EDCS-07600393.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the emperors by Quintus Cornelius Secundus, a prefect of a Mauritanian cohort, likely made during the co-reign of Caracalla and Geta (198-209 CE).

EDCS-07600394.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Ulpius Florentinus magister canabarum ad legionem II adiutricem votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600394.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, Ulpius Florentinus, master of the canabae (settlement outside the legionary fortress) near the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600394.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus by Ulpius Florentinus, an official from the civilian settlement adjacent to the Second Adiutrix Legion, likely a religious votive from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600395.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Oceanus miles stipendiorum II vixit annos XX menses VI et dies VII vivito
EDCS-07600395.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Aurelius Oceanus, a soldier of two years' service, lived for 20 years, 6 months, and 7 days. May he live on.
EDCS-07600395.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Aurelius Oceanus, a young soldier, detailing his short life and military service, characteristic of a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600397.text_edition: Turma Maximi Macedonis  Turma Maximi Constantis
EDCS-07600397.translation: The troop of Maximus Macedo. The troop of Maximus Constans.
EDCS-07600397.summary: This inscription likely records the names of two cavalry units (turmae), possibly indicating their presence or a dedication related to their service, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600398.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument given the mention of age, dedicated to or by an individual named Bass[...], with too little information to infer specific details.

EDCS-08500524.text_edition: Dullibogius Flori filius nnorum XL hic situs est Flavia Giboruna coniugi pientissimo et sibi vivae faciendum curavit
EDCS-08500524.translation: Dullibogius, son of Florus, aged 40 years, is buried here. Flavia Giboruna had this made for her most devoted husband and for herself while living.
EDCS-08500524.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Flavia Giboruna to her husband, Dullibogius, son of Florus, who died at 40, and also prepared for herself, characteristic of a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500525.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Magni Pii filio divi Severi nepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli curante Pontio Pontiano legato Augusti pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum XXIII
EDCS-08500525.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, son of Divus Antoninus Magnus Pius, grandson of Divus Severus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul. Pontius Pontianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, oversaw this from Aquincum, 23 miles away.
EDCS-08500525.summary: This inscription is a monumental dedication to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus), likely a milestone or a boundary stone, set up under the authority of Pontius Pontianus, legate of Augustus, and dating to Caracalla's reign (211-217 CE).

EDCS-08500526.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae ab Aquinco milia passuum XXII
EDCS-08500526.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, father of his country, 22 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-08500526.summary: This inscription is a milestone dedicated to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, indicating a distance of 22 miles from Aquincum, likely dating to his reign (235-238 AD).

EDCS-08500527.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Invicto
EDCS-08500527.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus.
EDCS-08500527.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Gordian III, likely part of a monument or statue, and dates to his reign (238-244 AD).

EDCS-08600606.text_edition: Deae Syriae  et Balti Deae pro salute Augusti  Caius Iulius Sextinus conductor ex decreto ordinis kanabarum secundum conductionem arcum cum ianuis tegula tectum inpendiis suis fecit magistris Iulio Viatore et Bellicio Firmino  locus publice datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-08600606.translation: To the Syrian Goddess and the Goddess Baltis, for the well-being of the Augustus. Gaius Julius Sextinus, a contractor, by decree of the order of the boatmen (kanabarum), in accordance with the contract, built an arch with doors, roofed with tiles, at his own expense, under the magistracies of Julius Viator and Bellicius Firminus. The location was publicly granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08600606.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an arch with doors to the Syrian Goddess and Baltis by Gaius Julius Sextinus, a contractor, for the emperor's well-being, funded by himself under the authority of the boatmen's guild and decurions.

EDCS-08600607.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Marcianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08600607.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Marcianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600607.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Marcianus, expressing the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08600608.text_edition: Asclepio et Hygiae Caius Gavillius Eutychus
EDCS-08600608.translation: To Asclepius and Hygia, Gaius Gavillius Eutychus.
EDCS-08600608.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the healing deities Asclepius and Hygia by Gaius Gavillius Eutychus, likely for health or gratitude.

EDCS-08600609.text_edition: Apollini et Seranae sic Titus Iulius Mercator decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08600609.translation: To Apollo and Serana, thus Titus Julius Mercator, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600609.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Apollo and Serana by Titus Julius Mercator, a decurion of a Roman colony, signifying the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08600610.text_edition: Silvano  Anticessori Titus Flavius Lucius decurio coloniae Aquincensium quaestorius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08600610.translation: To Silvanus Anticessor, Titus Flavius Lucius, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, quaestor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600610.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Anticessor by Titus Flavius Lucius, a decurion and quaestor of the colony of Aquincum, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08600612.text_edition: Soli Invicto Aurelius Vindicanus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08600612.translation: To Sol Invictus, Aurelius Vindicanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600612.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Sol Invictus by Aurelius Vindicanus, marking the willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08600613.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Afranius Victor |(centurio) cohortis I Alpinorum annorum L hic situs est Claudia IngCenua#Ingenua#INCENUA coiiugCi sic#coiiugi sic#COIIUCI pientissimo et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-08600613.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Afranius Victor, centurion of the First Cohort of Alpinorum, aged 50, lies here. Claudia Ingenua, his most devoted wife, placed this monument for him and for herself while living.
EDCS-08600613.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Publius Afranius Victor, a centurion, erected by his wife Claudia Ingenua for both him and herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-08600617.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudius Ingenuus bucinator cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum et Flavia Paulina coniunx vixit annos XXVI et Claudius Paulinus et Claudia Ingenua patri et coniugi eius
EDCS-08600617.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudius Ingenuus, trumpeter of the Maurorum milliaria cohort, and Flavia Paulina, his wife, who lived 26 years, and Claudius Paulinus and Claudia Ingenua, to their father and her husband.
EDCS-08600617.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Claudius Ingenuus, a trumpeter, and his wife Flavia Paulina, erected by their children Claudius Paulinus and Claudia Ingenua.

EDCS-08600618.translation: Adjusted to 1 (unit) of the military camp.
EDCS-08600618.summary: This inscription records a measurement or adjustment related to the military camp, indicating a specific unit of measure.

EDCS-08600619.translation: Adjusted to half of the military camp.
EDCS-08600619.summary: This inscription records a measurement or adjustment related to the military camp, indicating half of a unit of measure.

EDCS-08600620.translation: Adjusted to one-third of the military camp.
EDCS-08600620.summary: This inscription records a measurement or adjustment related to the military camp, indicating one-third of a unit of measure.

EDCS-08600621.summary: This inscription likely identifies Aelius Iulianus as a mortar-maker, possibly marking his work or presence.

EDCS-08600622.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Septimii libentes m(erito)
EDCS-08600622.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus, Septimii gladly (and) deservedly (made this).
EDCS-08600622.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Hercules Augustus, made by individuals named Septimii, expressing their devotion and fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08600624.text_edition: Ulpiae Quaetae annorum LX Ulpio Quaetino annorum XV Ulpiae Crispinae annorum XX Ulpia Quaetina mater
EDCS-08600624.translation: To Ulpia Quaeta, 60 years old; to Ulpius Quaetinus, 15 years old; to Ulpia Crispina, 20 years old; Ulpia Quaetina, their mother (made this).
EDCS-08600624.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of Ulpia Quaeta, Ulpius Quaetinus, and Ulpia Crispina, erected by Ulpia Quaetina, their mother.

EDCS-08900539.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul ab Aquinco milia passuum VI
EDCS-08900539.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, consul; six miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-08900539.summary: This inscription is a milestone or public dedication, identifying Emperor Maximinus Thrax and indicating a distance of six miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-08900540.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-08900540.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-08900540.summary: This inscription is a public dedication or monument honoring Emperor Gordian III, listing his imperial titles.

EDCS-08900541.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurellius sic Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia pRotestate#potestate#ROTEST VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit Aquinco milia passuum III
EDCS-08900541.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the third time, father of the country, restored (this); three miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-08900541.summary: This inscription is a milestone or restoration marker, indicating that Emperor Severus Alexander restored a road or structure, located three miles from Aquincum, and detailing his imperial titles and years in office (circa 230-231 AD).

EDCS-08900542.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus consul proconsul tribunicia potestate Opellius Diadum[enianus] nobilissimus Caesar vias et pontes vetustate corruptas restituerunt cura agente Aelio T[ricciano] legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum XLIII
EDCS-08900542.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus, consul, proconsul, with tribunician power; Opellius Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, restored roads and bridges damaged by age, with Aelius T[riccianus], legate of the Augusti, acting as governor; forty-three miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-08900542.summary: This inscription is a milestone or public works dedication, recording the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, under the supervision of Aelius Triccianus, located forty-three miles from Aquincum (circa 217-218 AD).

EDCS-08900543.translation: To the Divine Manes. Felix, while living.
EDCS-08900543.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felix, made during his lifetime, typical of Roman epitaphs.

EDCS-08900544.text_edition: Marti Auggustorum sacrum Apolaustus Auggustorum libertus
EDCS-08900544.translation: Sacred to Mars of the Emperors. Apolaustus, freedman of the Emperors.
EDCS-08900544.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars, made by Apolaustus, a freedman of the emperors, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-08900549.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Valeno et Bitutiae Marcellinae uxori
EDCS-08900549.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Publius Aelius Valens and Bitutia Marcellina, his wife.
EDCS-08900549.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to Publius Aelius Valens and his wife, Bitutia Marcellina.

EDCS-08900550.text_edition: G Ulpi-- Florcoloniae Cibalensium Marcus ViValentinus dec(urio)? col(oniae) Cibalensium equo publico  Coccei-- V flamen pi
EDCS-08900550.translation: G. Ulpius [---]. Marcus Valentinus, decurion of the colony of Cibalae, with a public horse. Cocceius [---], flamen pius.
EDCS-08900550.summary: This fragmented inscription records the names and titles of Roman officials, including a decurion of the colony of Cibalae and a flamen, suggesting a public or civic context.

EDCS-08900551.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Marcus Aurelius Quintilianus decurio coloniae Cibalensium quaestorius ob incolumitatem suam suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito
