EDCS-08900551.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Quintilianus, decurion of the colony of Cibalae, former quaestor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of all his kin.
EDCS-08900551.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Marcus Aurelius Quintilianus, a decurion and former quaestor of Cibalae, in gratitude for his and his family's well-being.

EDCS-08901098.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iuliae Secundinae Quinti Iuli Secundi filiae domo municipio A C nnorum XL Aurelius Victor veteranus legionis II adiutricis ex stratore consularis coniugi karissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-08901098.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Julia Secundina, daughter of Quintus Iulius Secundus, from the municipality of [---], 40 years old. Aurelius Victor, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, former consular orderly, had this made for his dearest wife.
EDCS-08901098.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Victor, a veteran, to his beloved wife, Julia Secundina, who was 40 years old and from an unnamed municipality.

EDCS-09300476.translation: [...] which a cornicularius had vowed, having been made a centurion of the Fourth Flavian Legion by Caecilius Rufinus, legate of the Augusti with propraetorial power, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09300476.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a centurion of Legio IIII Flavia, who had previously been a cornicularius and was promoted by a proconsular legate.

EDCS-09300480.text_edition: Nomina heredum Rutilii Ven Agathemerus Ven
EDCS-09300480.translation: Names of the heirs of Rutilius [---]. Agathemerus [---].
EDCS-09300480.summary: This inscription lists the names of heirs, Rutilius [---] and Agathemerus [---], likely related to a will or inheritance document.

EDCS-09300481.text_edition: Sexto Lucanio M causidico qui vixit annos XXVIII Lucanius D
EDCS-09300481.translation: For Sextus Lucanius M. the advocate, who lived for 28 years. Lucanius D. [---].
EDCS-09300481.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Lucanius M., an advocate who died at the age of 28, likely erected by a relative named Lucanius D.

EDCS-09300485.text_edition: Titus Flavius Titi filius Quirina IustIinus#Iustinus#IUSIINUS veteranus legionis II adiutricis domo Sirmio nnorum LXX sibi et Iuliae Optatae coniugi vivaes faciendum curavit decreto decurionum Laterano et Rufino consulibus
EDCS-09300485.translation: Titus Flavius Iustinus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, from Sirmio, 70 years old, had this made for himself and for his wife, Julia Optata, while they were living, by decree of the decurions, during the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus.
EDCS-09300485.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titus Flavius Iustinus, a 70-year-old veteran from Sirmio, for himself and his wife Julia Optata, during their lifetime, by decree of the decurions and dated to the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus.

EDCS-09300486.text_edition: Dominis nostris Diocletiano et Maximiano et Constantio nobilissimis Caesaribus
EDCS-09300486.translation: To our lords Diocletian and Maximian and Constantius, most noble Caesars.
EDCS-09300486.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman emperors Diocletian and Maximian, and to Constantius as Caesar, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-09301194.text_edition: Minervae Augustae sacrum  Lucii Aurelii Valentinus et Silvester decuriones coloniae Aquincensium pro se et suis ex voto posuerunt Sabino et Anullino consulibus
EDCS-09301194.translation: Sacred to Minerva Augusta. Lucius Aurelius Valentinus and Silvester, decurions of the colony of Aquincum, placed this for themselves and their families, in fulfillment of a vow, in the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus.
EDCS-09301194.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva Augusta by two decurions from Aquincum, fulfilling a vow for themselves and their families, dated to 243 AD during the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus.

EDCS-09400595.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aeflanus Onirus nnorum XL hic situs est Marcus Aeflanus Licinianus praefectus castrorum patronus eius fieri iussit
EDCS-09400595.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Marcus Aeflanus Onirus, 40 years old, lies here. Marcus Aeflanus Licinianus, prefect of the camp, his patron, ordered this to be made.
EDCS-09400595.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aeflanus Onirus, aged 40, commissioned by his patron Marcus Aeflanus Licinianus, a camp prefect.

EDCS-09400597.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo S Arrius Super veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens laetus
EDCS-09400597.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, S. Arrius Super, a veteran of the Second Adjutrix Legion, loyal and faithful, willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09400597.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by S. Arrius Super, a veteran of Legio II Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09400598.text_edition: ]   E T R LVIL MADEIREN et Marcus Ulpius Carus optio statorum consularis sumptibus suis votum solverunt libentes merito  Praesente et Albino consulibus XV Kalendas Iulias
EDCS-09400598.translation: [---] E.T.R. Luil Madeiren and Marcus Ulpius Carus, optio statorum consularis, fulfilled their vow deservedly and willingly at their own expense, on the 15th Kalends of July, in the consulship of Praesens and Albinus.
EDCS-09400598.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made at their own expense by Luil Madeiren and Marcus Ulpius Carus, an optio statorum consularis, dated to June 17, 217 AD, during the consulship of Praesens and Albinus.

EDCS-09400599.text_edition: Aug--- Maximo II et Aeliano consulibus
EDCS-09400599.translation: In the consulship of Aug[ustus] Maximus II and Aelianus.
EDCS-09400599.summary: This inscription provides a consular date, likely 227 AD, referencing the second consulship of an Augustus Maximus and Aelianus.

EDCS-09400600.text_edition: Invicto Deo Aelius Culvisius ex volto
EDCS-09400600.translation: To the Unconquered God, Aelius Culvisius, from a vow.
EDCS-09400600.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to an Unconquered God by Aelius Culvisius, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09400602.translation: Syrea, thus all things, to the innocent.
EDCS-09400602.summary: This brief inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph for an innocent individual named Syrea, possibly expressing a sentiment about mortality or fate.

EDCS-09400603.text_edition: MV annorum XXXX hic situs est Utte Maxima ponendum curavit bene merenti
EDCS-09400603.translation: M.V., 40 years old, lies here. Utte Maxima took care to place this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-09400603.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual, M.V., aged 40, set up by Utte Maxima for the well-deserving deceased.

EDCS-09400606.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul procons(ul) restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum
EDCS-09400606.translation: Imperator Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, restored this at a distance of miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09400606.summary: This inscription records a restoration project initiated by the Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely a milestone or boundary marker, specifying a distance from Aquincum.

EDCS-09400607.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul proconsul restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum L
EDCS-09400607.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Father of the Country, Consul, Proconsul, restored this fifty miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09400607.summary: This milestone records a restoration project fifty miles from Aquincum by Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely dating to the early years of his reign (AD 235-238).

EDCS-09400608.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae proconsul et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus Augustus nobilissimus Caesar restituerunt ab Aquinco milia passuum L
EDCS-09400608.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, acclaimed Imperator for the third time, Consul, Father of the Country, Proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus Augustus, most noble Caesar, restored this fifty miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09400608.summary: This milestone records a restoration project fifty miles from Aquincum undertaken jointly by Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son, Maximus Caesar, likely dating to AD 236.

EDCS-09400609.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III imperatori V consuli proconsuli patri patriae Imperatori et Caio Iulio Vero Maximo Augusto nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis Augusti nostri filio Germanicis Dacicis Sarmaticis maximis cohors Hemesenorum civium Romanorum Maximiniana ab Aquinco milia passuum L
EDCS-09400609.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the third time, acclaimed Imperator for the fifth time, Consul, Proconsul, Father of the Country, and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus Augustus, most noble Caesar, Princeps Iuventutis, son of our Augustus, victorious over the Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, the Cohort of Hemeseni, Roman citizens, named Maximiniana, [set this up] fifty miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09400609.summary: This milestone, erected by the Cohort of Hemeseni, celebrates Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, commemorating their victories and noting a distance of fifty miles from Aquincum, likely dating to AD 237.

EDCS-09401166.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Volcano Augustis sacrum Lucius Septimius Flaccus legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-09401166.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, Minerva, and Vulcan Augusti, sacred. Lucius Septimius Flaccus, legate of the Emperor with propraetorian power, [set this up].
EDCS-09401166.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a group of gods, including Jupiter, Juno, Minerva, and Vulcan, made by Lucius Septimius Flaccus, an imperial legate, indicating a public or official religious act.

EDCS-09401169.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Claudio Tacito Pio Felici Invicto Augusto V M P
EDCS-09401169.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus. V(otum) M(erito) P(osuit) – He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09401169.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Tacitus, likely a milestone or an honorific base, which also includes a formulaic expression of a vow fulfilled.

EDCS-09401544.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli prPoconsuli#proconsuli#PPOCOS et Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis filio Augusti nostri et Marciae Otaciliae Severae Augustae coniugi Augusti nostri milia passuum LXXVIIII
EDCS-09401544.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Father of the Country, Consul, Proconsul; and to Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, Princeps Iuventutis, son of our Augustus; and to Marcia Otacilia Severa Augusta, wife of our Augustus. Seventy-nine miles.
EDCS-09401544.summary: This milestone honors Emperor Philip the Arab, his son Philip II, and his wife Otacilia Severa, marking a distance of seventy-nine miles, and dates to their co-reign (AD 244-249).

EDCS-09401549.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Censorinius Maximus beneficiarius consularis accepta missione votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09401549.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Censorinius Maximus, a consular beneficiarius, having received his discharge, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09401549.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Censorinius Maximus, a beneficiarius consularis, who fulfilled a vow upon his honorable discharge from service.

EDCS-09401550.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Iustus beneficiarius consularis legionis IIII Macrino et Celso consulibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09401550.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Justus, a consular beneficiarius of the Fourth Legion, in the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09401550.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Titus Flavius Justus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio IIII Flavia, dating to AD 218 based on the named consuls Macrinus and Celsus.

EDCS-09401551.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Salutaris beneficiarius consularis quod aras in hunc locum recollocaverit
EDCS-09401551.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Julius Salutaris, a consular beneficiarius, because he re-erected the altars in this place.
EDCS-09401551.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Julius Salutaris, a beneficiarius consularis, specifically commemorating his act of restoring altars at a particular location.

EDCS-09700224.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Marcus Foviacius Verus iunior decurio kanabarum decurio municipii Aquinci IIvir quinquennalis flaminicius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09700224.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia. Marcus Foviacius Verus Junior, decurion of the kanabae, decurion of the municipium of Aquincum, duumvir quinquennalis, flamen, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700224.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the healing gods Aesculapius and Hygia by Marcus Foviacius Verus Junior, a notable local official holding multiple municipal and religious offices in Aquincum.

EDCS-09700225.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri  Ulpius Nundinus discens regulatorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09700225.translation: To Silvanus of the Woods, Ulpius Nundinus, a trainee regulator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700225.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Silvanus Silvester by a Roman soldier in training, Ulpius Nundinus.

EDCS-09700226.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  et Iunoni Septimius Antiochianus votum solvitC#solvit#SOLVIC libens merito
EDCS-09700226.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno, Septimius Antiochianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700226.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno by Septimius Antiochianus.

EDCS-09700230.text_edition: Aurelius Victor et Ponlius Messor votum solverunt libentes laeti merito
EDCS-09700230.translation: Aurelius Victor and Ponlius Messor willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09700230.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Victor and Ponlius Messor.

EDCS-09700231.text_edition: MLSIXS SA votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09700231.translation: MLSIXS SA willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09700231.summary: This is a votive inscription, with the dedicant's name partially preserved or abbreviated as MLSIXS SA.

EDCS-09700233.text_edition: V D    SVPQVES   Ulpius Nundinus beneficiarius consularis posuit  Maximo II et  Urbano consulibus  SVP QVES
EDCS-09700233.translation: V D SVPQVES. Ulpius Nundinus, a consular beneficiarius, set this up when Maximus II and Urbanus were consuls. SVP QVES.
EDCS-09700233.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Ulpius Nundinus, a beneficiarius consularis, dated to the consulship of Maximus II and Urbanus (AD 234).

EDCS-09700236.text_edition: legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XX Secundus etius Epaphroditus CCC B
EDCS-09700236.translation: Of the Second Adjutrix Legion, of twenty years' service, Secundus and Epaphroditus, CCC B.
EDCS-09700236.summary: This fragment mentions two individuals, Secundus and Epaphroditus, serving in Legio II Adiutrix with twenty years of military service, possibly on a military diploma or dedication.

EDCS-09700238.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, preserving only partial names or words.

EDCS-09700241.summary: This brief inscription records that Resatus was the maker or artisan of the object.

EDCS-09700242.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulius Candidianus miles legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XXIIII qui vixit nnos XXXX Claudia Bonosa coniugi carissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-09700242.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Julius Candidianus, a soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion, of 24 years' service, who lived 40 years. Claudia Bonosa had this made for her dearest husband.
EDCS-09700242.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Julius Candidianus, a soldier of Legio II Adiutrix, by his wife Claudia Bonosa.

EDCS-09700243.text_edition: Dis M(anibus) Marco Ulpio Maxim militi legionis II adiutricis centuria Herenni nnorum XXX
EDCS-09700243.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Marcus Ulpius Maximus, a soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion, from the centuria of Herennius, 30 years old.
EDCS-09700243.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Marcus Ulpius Maximus, a soldier of Legio II Adiutrix serving in the centuria of Herennius.

EDCS-09700288.text_edition: Dis Magnis pro salute templensium Lucius Virius Luci filius Mercator sacerdos votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09700288.translation: To the Great Gods, for the well-being of the people of Templum, Lucius Virius Mercator, son of Lucius, a priest, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700288.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Great Gods by Lucius Virius Mercator, a priest, for the health and welfare of the residents of Templum, fulfilling a vow he had made.

EDCS-09700290.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelia Sura quae vixit annos XXXV menses X Aurelius Fortunio qui vixit annos XIIII menses XI Aureliae Candidae vivae Aurelia Lucilla libertis carissimis Marcus Aurelius Maximianus nutrici pientissimae faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-09700290.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelia Sura, who lived for 35 years and 10 months. Aurelius Fortunio, who lived for 14 years and 11 months. To Aurelia Candida, while living. Marcus Aurelius Maximianus arranged for this to be made for his most devoted nurse, and for his dearest freedpersons.
EDCS-09700290.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Aurelia Sura and Aurelius Fortunio, and also honors the living Aurelia Candida and Marcus Aurelius Maximianus's nurse, commissioned by Marcus Aurelius Maximianus for his beloved freedpersons and devoted nurse.

EDCS-09700291.text_edition: Flavia Tattunis filia Usaiu Eravisca nnum LXXX hic sita est Quintus Flavius Titucus matri ob pietatem posuit
EDCS-09700291.translation: Flavia Tattunis, daughter of Usaius, an Eraviscan woman, aged 80, lies here. Quintus Flavius Titucus placed this for his mother out of devotion.
EDCS-09700291.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Flavia Tattunis, an 80-year-old Eraviscan woman, placed by her son Quintus Flavius Titucus out of filial piety.

EDCS-09700292.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Nigrinus miles legionis I adiutricis Severianae strator consularis Aurelio Victori fratri qui vicG(sit) sic#vic(sit) sic#VIG nnos XXVIII merenti opto sR(it)#s(it)#R tIibi#tibi#IIBI terra lIevis#levis#IEV Aurelio Modesto veterano legionis I adiutricis Severianae et Aureliae ScGenuae#Scenuae#SGENUE matri vivus vivis parentibus suis  posuit
EDCS-09700292.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Nigrinus, a soldier of the First Adiutrix Severiana Legion, consular strator, to his brother Aurelius Victor, who lived 28 years. I wish that the earth may be light for you. To Aurelius Modestus, a veteran of the First Adiutrix Severiana Legion, and to Aurelia Scenua, his mother. While living, he placed this for his living parents.
EDCS-09700292.summary: This funerary and dedicatory inscription honors Aelius Nigrinus's deceased brother, Aurelius Victor, and also commemorates his living parents, veteran Aurelius Modestus and Aurelia Scenua, placed by Aelius Nigrinus while alive.

EDCS-09700293.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Do veteranus ex beneficiario cons(ularis) an(norum) -- patri sanctissimo Mo--- p(osuit)
EDCS-09700293.translation: Marcus Aurelius Do[---], a veteran, formerly a consular beneficiarius, aged [---] years, placed this for his most holy father Mo[---].
EDCS-09700293.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Aurelius Do[---], a veteran, to his deceased and most holy father Mo[---].

EDCS-09701113.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae  Suruco
EDCS-09701113.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Juno Regina. Surucus [placed this].
EDCS-09701113.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, made by Surucus.

EDCS-09701114.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Mercu[rius placed this].
EDCS-09701114.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Mercurius.

EDCS-09701117.text_edition: Aurelius Mucianus quondam beneficiarius consularis I stipendiorum XVIIII et Aureliae Firmillae coniugis et Aureli-- Domni
EDCS-09701117.translation: Aurelius Mucianus, formerly a consular beneficiarius, who served 19 years, and for Aurelia Firmilla, his wife, and Aureli[us] Domnus.
EDCS-09701117.summary: This inscription commemorates Aurelius Mucianus, a former consular beneficiarius, along with his wife Aurelia Firmilla and Aurelius Domnus, likely a family monument.

EDCS-09701118.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Tutor veteranus legionis II adiu(tricis) p(iae) f(idelis) hic situs Aelius Atius veteranus legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-09701118.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Flavius Tutor, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, lies here. Aelius Atius, a veteran of the same legion, his heir, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09701118.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Flavius Tutor, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, and was commissioned by his heir, Aelius Atius, also a veteran of the same legion.

EDCS-09701121.text_edition: L e annorum XX hic sit-- est Valerius Felicio
EDCS-09701121.translation: L. E. aged 20, lies here. Valerius Felicio [set this up].
EDCS-09701121.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates an individual identified only as L. E., aged 20, and was set up by Valerius Felicio.

EDCS-09701122.text_edition: hic situs est Marcus Birrius Teretina Gla Genn
EDCS-09701122.translation: Here lies Marcus Birrius Genn(adius) of the Teretina tribe.
EDCS-09701122.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Birrius Gennadius, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09701123.text_edition: PAE Flavia Tertia matri viva fecit
EDCS-09701123.translation: Flavia Tertia made this for her mother while she was alive.
EDCS-09701123.summary: This is a funerary dedication, possibly a tombstone, made by Flavia Tertia for her mother during the mother's lifetime, indicating a 'viva fecit' (made while living) inscription common in Roman funerary practice.

EDCS-09701126.translation: Ulpius Antos, soldier of the legion.
EDCS-09701126.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a funerary monument, for Ulpius Antos, a soldier, probably from the Imperial Roman army.

EDCS-09701128.text_edition: Alorix Bassi filius decurio Eraviscus nnorum XXXV hic situs est filii patri posuerunt
EDCS-09701128.translation: Alorix, son of Bassus, a decurion of the Eravisci, aged 35 years, lies here. His sons placed this for their father.
EDCS-09701128.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Alorix, a decurion of the Eravisci, erected by his sons after his death at age 35.

EDCS-09701129.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III imperatori V consuli proconsuli patri patriae et Caio Iulio Vero Maximo nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis Augusti nostri filio Germanicis Dacicis Sarmaticis maximis ab Aquinco milia passuum XXII  Imperatori Caesari Marco Annio Floriano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae proconsuli ab Aquinco milia passuum XXII
EDCS-09701129.translation: To Imperator Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, imperator for the fifth time, consul, proconsul, father of the country, and to Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, princeps iuventutis, son of our Augustus, for his greatest victories over the Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, 22 miles from Aquincum. To Imperator Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul, 22 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09701129.summary: This is a fragmentary milestone or honorific inscription, originally dedicated to Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, then re-inscribed or repurposed for Emperor Florianus in 276 CE, marking a distance of 22 miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-09701131.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul pater patriae proconsul et Marcia Otacilia Severa sanctissima Augusta coniunx Augusti nostri ab Aquinco milia passuum XXIII
EDCS-09701131.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, and Marcia Otacilia Severa, most holy Augusta, wife of our Augustus, 23 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09701131.summary: This is a milestone or similar public inscription dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab and his wife Otacilia Severa, dating to their reign (c. 244-249 CE) and marking a distance of 23 miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-09701132.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul pater patriae proconsul et Marcus Iulius Philippus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis et Marcia Otacilia Severa sanctissima Augusta coniunx Augusti nostri ab Aquinco milia passuum XXII
EDCS-09701132.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, and Marcus Iulius Philippus, most noble Caesar, princeps iuventutis, and Marcia Otacilia Severa, most holy Augusta, wife of our Augustus, 22 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09701132.summary: This is a milestone or public inscription dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab, his son Philip II, and his wife Otacilia Severa, dating to their joint reign (c. 247-249 CE) and marking a distance of 22 miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-09701134.summary: This is a fragmentary milestone or public inscription, likely from the late Imperial period, dedicated to an unnamed emperor, marking 22 miles from an unspecified location.

EDCS-09701135.text_edition: centuria Antonini ES Crescens Propinquus  centuria Cassi Potentis  Propinquus  PR
EDCS-09701135.translation: The century of Antoninus, Crescens Propinquus. The century of Cassius Potens, Propinquus.
EDCS-09701135.summary: This inscription appears to be a military record, possibly marking work done by specific centuries (military units) under their respective centurions or indicating land allotments.

EDCS-09701141.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Vellatus votum solvit libens m(erito)
EDCS-09701141.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Vellatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09701141.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Vellatus, giving thanks for a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-09701142.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aelius Sabinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09701142.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aelius Sabinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09701142.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Aelius Sabinus, likely an altar or shrine, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09701145.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum XXXVIIII
EDCS-09701145.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the third time, father of the country, restored (this) 39 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09701145.summary: This inscription commemorates the restoration of a monument or road by Emperor Severus Alexander, likely in 230 AD given his ninth tribunician power and third consulship, located 39 miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-09701148.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a simple name tag or ownership mark on an object or monument.

EDCS-09701150.text_edition: Bozi Vellases filia annorum XXX hic sita est Proculus et Calvisius Cassites fili titulum merito posuerunt
EDCS-09701150.translation: Bozi, daughter of Vellases, aged 30, lies here. Proculus and Calvisius Cassites, her sons, deservedly placed this monument.
EDCS-09701150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bozi, daughter of Vellases, erected by her sons Proculus and Calvisius Cassites.

EDCS-09701151.text_edition: Exounomarae Annamati filiae annorum XVII hic sita est Annamatus Casulonis filius Suollae Troucissae et sibi vivus fEecitI#fecit#EECII
EDCS-09701151.translation: Exounomara, daughter of Annamatus, aged 17, lies here. Annamatus, son of Casulo, made this for Suolla Troucissa and for himself while living.
EDCS-09701151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Exounomara, daughter of Annamatus, with Annamatus, son of Casulo, also preparing the monument for Suolla Troucissa and himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-09701153.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Attianus votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-09701153.translation: Marcus Ulpius Attianus willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09701153.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Ulpius Attianus, likely an altar or shrine, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09701154.text_edition: Naius Radai filius annorum XL hic situs est Radaius pater et fili et Nata coniunx fecerunt
EDCS-09701154.translation: Naius, son of Radai, aged 40, lies here. Radaius, his father, and his sons, and Nata, his wife, made this.
EDCS-09701154.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Naius, son of Radai, erected by his father Radaius, his sons, and his wife Nata.

EDCS-09701155.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae proconsuli ab Aquinco milia passuum LI
EDCS-09701155.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, 51 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-09701155.summary: This inscription commemorates Emperor Gordian III, likely a milestone or an imperial monument, located 51 miles from Aquincum, dating to his reign (238-244 AD).

EDCS-09701156.text_edition: Valauna Nigrini filia annorum XXX Dervae Comonis filiae vivae Nigrinus Namtiboli filius sibi et coniugi et filiae titulum merito posuit
EDCS-09701156.translation: Valauna, daughter of Nigrinus, aged 30. For Derva, daughter of Comonus, while she was living. Nigrinus, son of Namtibolus, deservedly placed this monument for himself and his wife and daughter.
EDCS-09701156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valauna, daughter of Nigrinus, and Derva, daughter of Comonus (likely Valauna's daughter), with Nigrinus, son of Namtibolus, preparing it for himself, his wife, and his daughter while living.

EDCS-09701157.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Decoratus imaginifer votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09701157.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Decoratus, standard-bearer, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09701157.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Decoratus, a standard-bearer, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09701162.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum XLIIIII#XLIIII#XIIIII
EDCS-09701162.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country, restored this from Aquincum for 44 or 45 miles, 44, 15 miles.
EDCS-09701162.summary: This inscription records a restoration of a road or milestone by Emperor Severus Alexander, indicating distances from Aquincum, likely dating to 230 or 231 AD.

EDCS-09701169.text_edition: Bauso Boni filius nnorum L hic situs est Atta fratri optimo pietati sic titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-09701169.translation: Bauso, son of Bonus, aged 50, lies here. Atta placed this title of memory to his excellent brother, out of piety.
EDCS-09701169.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Atta for his brother Bauso, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-09701170.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Ulpio Revocato Trurso frater heres ex testamento posuit
EDCS-09701170.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Ulpius Revocatus Trurso. His brother, heir by testament, erected this.
EDCS-09701170.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Ulpius Revocatus Trurso, erected by his brother, who was also his heir.

EDCS-09800272.text_edition: quod voverat A IIII Flaviae factus |(centurio) sub Caecilio Rufino legato Auggustorum pro praetore Pannoniae Inferioris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09800272.translation: What he had vowed, A. IIII Flavia, having been made a centurion under Caecilius Rufinus, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power of Lower Pannonia, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09800272.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a centurion named A. IIII Flavia, made while serving under the legate Caecilius Rufinus in Lower Pannonia.

EDCS-09800273.text_edition: Lucius Cornelius Latinianus legatus Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09800273.translation: Lucius Cornelius Latinianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09800273.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Lucius Cornelius Latinianus, a provincial governor (legate of Augustus).

EDCS-09800274.text_edition: decurio coloniae Aquincensis duumviralis equo publico et Aurelia  Vibianus adiutor officii consularis  Rusticus et Rusticianus  inpendiis suis fecerunt  ad extruendum nominibus nte his lavacris utentur Aelius Verinus Flavius Opimianus Victor Terentius Vitalis erianus Septimius Valerianus us Septimius Aurelius  ter et semel consulibus
EDCS-09800274.translation: A decurion of the colony of Aquincum, duumviral, with a public horse, and Aurelia Vibianus, assistant of the consular office, Rusticus and Rusticianus, made this at their own expense, for building something, using these baths in their names. Aelius Verinus, Flavius Opimianus Victor Terentius Vitalis, erianus Septimius Valerianus, us Septimius Aurelius, when they were consul three times and once.
EDCS-09800274.summary: This complex inscription appears to record the construction or renovation of baths, funded by various individuals including decurions and officials, and potentially dating to a specific consular period indicated by repeated consulships.

EDCS-09800275.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Stratonico militi legionis I adiutricis ARES P officii consularis stipendiorum XVII vixit annos XXXV Flavia Flavina coniugi carissimo arcam ex suo posuit
EDCS-09800275.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Stratonicus, soldier of the First Adiutrix Legion, of the consular office, with 17 years of service, who lived 35 years. Flavia Flavina, his dearest wife, placed this sarcophagus from her own funds.
EDCS-09800275.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Stratonicus, a soldier of Legio I Adiutrix, who died at 35 after 17 years of service, set up by his wife Flavia Flavina.

EDCS-09900343.text_edition: Calmenus Sindionis filius annorum XXV titulum posuerot sic filii heredes
EDCS-09900343.translation: Calmenus, son of Sindio, aged 25, placed this title. His sons are the heirs.
EDCS-09900343.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calmenus, who died at 25, and was set up by his sons as heirs.

EDCS-09900364.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septemio sic Severo Pertinaci Augusto patri patriae Arabico Adiabenico imperatori V consuli II tribunicia potestate III pontifici maximo et Marco Aurelio Antonino Caesari cohors I Mmiliaria Aurelia Antoniniana nova Severiana constituta Pisone et Iuliano consulibus
EDCS-09900364.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, father of his country, Arabicus Adiabenicus, imperator for the 5th time, consul for the 2nd time, holding tribunician power for the 3rd time, pontifex maximus, and to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar. The First Millenary Aurelia Antoniniana Nova Severiana cohort was established in the consulship of Piso and Iulianus.
EDCS-09900364.summary: This inscription records the establishment of the Cohors I Milliaria Aurelia Antoniniana Nova Severiana, dedicated to Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, during the consulship of Piso and Iulianus (likely 198 AD).

EDCS-09900367.text_edition: Centuriae sacrum Exsuperatus |(centurio) cohortis Hemesenorum Gordianae  Genii   CV
EDCS-09900367.translation: Sacred to the Centuria. Exsuperatus, centurion of the Cohors Hemesenorum Gordiana, to the Genius of the centuria. (Value) 105.
EDCS-09900367.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Exsuperatus, a centurion of the Cohors Hemesenorum Gordiana, to the Genius (protective spirit) of his centuria.

EDCS-09900368.text_edition: Septimius Constantinus optio spei et Iulius Iulianus  beneficiarius legati Praesente et Extricato consulibus
EDCS-09900368.translation: Septimius Constantinus, *optio spei*, and Iulius Iulianus, *beneficiarius* of the legate, when Praesens and Extricatus were consuls.
EDCS-09900368.summary: This inscription records the names and ranks of two Roman soldiers, Septimius Constantinus and Iulius Iulianus, and is dated to the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus (likely 260 CE).

EDCS-09900369.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a longer dedication or tombstone, recording the name Lucius Septimius Ganus N.

EDCS-09900370.translation: When Praesens and Extricatus were consuls.
EDCS-09900370.summary: This brief inscription serves as a consular date, indicating the year 260 CE.

EDCS-09900386.text_edition: us Maqui et Pattuo custores armorum cohortis |(milliariae) novae Severianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900386.translation: ---us Maquius and Pattuo, armourers of the Severian new thousand-strong cohort, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09900386.summary: This inscription records a vow made by two armorers, Maquius and Pattuo, from the *Cohors Nova Severiana Milliaria*.

EDCS-09900390.text_edition: Genio turmae et Eponae Reginae Iulius Victor eques vexillarius cohortis |(milliariae) novae Severianae Gordianae Surorum sagittariorum in honorem turmae votum solvit libens merito Imperatore domino nostro Gordiano II et et Pompeiano consulibus  Kalendis Iuniis
EDCS-09900390.translation: To the Genius of the troop and to Queen Epona, Iulius Victor, cavalryman and vexillarius of the Severian Gordian new thousand-strong cohort of Syrian archers, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow in honor of the troop, on the Kalends of June, when our lord Emperor Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls.
EDCS-09900390.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius of his troop and Epona Regina by Iulius Victor, a cavalry vexillarius from a Syrian archer cohort, dated to the Kalends of June during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus (likely 241 CE).

EDCS-09900392.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Publius Aelius Aelianus praefectus legionis II adiutricis protector Gallieni Augusti nostri agens vices legati votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900392.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Publius Aelius Aelianus, prefect of the Second Adiutrix Legion, protector of our Emperor Gallienus, acting legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900392.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules Augustus by Publius Aelius Aelianus, a prefect of Legio II Adiutrix acting as legate, in connection with his role as a protector of Emperor Gallienus (mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-09900393.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Victor votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900393.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Ulpius Victor willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900393.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Ulpius Victor.

EDCS-09900394.text_edition: Invicto Optimo Maximo Patrio  Iunoni Reginae  pro salute sua suorumque omnium Aurelius Marcellus quod miles votavit veteranus solvit IImmpperatoribus ddominis nnostris Maximiano Augusto V et Maximiano nobilissimo Caesare II consulibus  votum libens solvit
EDCS-09900394.translation: To the Unconquered Optimus Maximus Patrio and to Queen Juno, for his own well-being and that of all his family, Aurelius Marcellus, what he vowed as a soldier he fulfilled as a veteran, willingly fulfilled his vow in the consulship of our lords Emperor Maximian Augustus for the fifth time and Maximian the most noble Caesar for the second time.
EDCS-09900394.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Aurelius Marcellus to the Unconquered Optimus Maximus Patrio and Juno Regina, fulfilled as a veteran after vowing it as a soldier, and is dated to the fifth consulship of Maximian Augustus and second of Maximian Caesar (likely 307 CE).

EDCS-09900395.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Lucius Atticius Atticinus et Caius Atticius Verecundus deccuriones
EDCS-09900395.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Lucius Atticius Atticinus and Caius Atticius Verecundus, decurions.
EDCS-09900395.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Mercury Augustus by two decurions, Lucius Atticius Atticinus and Caius Atticius Verecundus.

EDCS-09900396.text_edition: $ legionis piae] fidelis Philippianae   Aurelius Avitianus Aurelius Pyrrh=HRRus#Pyrrhus#PYHRRUS Aurelius Bitus Claudius Proculus Aurelius Iustus Aurelius Florus Domitius Lupianus Vipsanius Marinianus Iulius Severinus Septimius Atta Claudius Felicianus Aurelius Albanus Iulius Silvanus Aurelius Numerius  Titianus   Aurelius Carus Septimius Mucianus Dips Maximus Aurelius Florus Aelius Maximus Iulius Lernatus Aelius Crescens Iulius Nodia Claudius Valens Aurelius Trice Iulius Vitalis Septimius Ius Aurelius lianus Aurelius Antoninus Aurelius ius
EDCS-09900396.translation: Of the pious and loyal Philippian legion. Aurelius Avitianus, Aurelius Pyrrhus, Aurelius Bitus, Claudius Proculus, Aurelius Iustus, Aurelius Florus, Domitius Lupianus, Vipsanius Marinianus, Iulius Severinus, Septimius Atta, Claudius Felicianus, Aurelius Albanus, Iulius Silvanus, Aurelius Numerius, Titianus, Aurelius Carus, Septimius Mucianus, Dips Maximus, Aurelius Florus, Aelius Maximus, Iulius Lernatus, Aelius Crescens, Iulius Nodia, Claudius Valens, Aurelius Trice, Iulius Vitalis, Septimius Iustus, Aurelius Iulianus, Aurelius Antoninus, Aurelius ---ius.
EDCS-09900396.summary: This inscription lists numerous names of soldiers from the 'pious and loyal Philippian legion,' likely commemorating a group dedication or contribution (mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-09900397.text_edition: et Ilus  Aelius A Septimius Ca Septimius Prob Septimius Num Septimius Au Septimius Urs Septimius AtI Septimius  Septimius  Septimius  Vi Au
EDCS-09900397.translation: and Ilus, Aelius A., Septimius Ca., Septimius Prob., Septimius Num., Septimius Au., Septimius Urs., Septimius AtI., Septimius, Septimius, Septimius, Vi. Au.
EDCS-09900397.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, likely part of a larger dedication or record of individuals, potentially members of a family or group, given the recurring 'Septimius' cognomen.

EDCS-09900398.summary: This fragment provides a dating clause, indicating that the event recorded occurred during the consulship of Maximus.

EDCS-09900400.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Crispino quondam corniculario legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-09900400.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Publius Aelius Crispinus, formerly a bugler of the Second Auxiliary Legion, loyal and faithful.
EDCS-09900400.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Crispinus, a former bugler of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-09900401.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelius Lupus vixit nnos VIIII menses XI dies VIII Aelius Ingenuus decurio filio pio faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900401.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Lupus lived 9 years, 11 months, 8 days. Aelius Ingenuus, a decurion, had this made for his dutiful son.
EDCS-09900401.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Ingenuus, a decurion, to his beloved son, Aelius Lupus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-09900402.text_edition: Dis Manibus Proculo signifero legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis  et Iuliae Optatae eius vivae  s veteranus legionis supra scriptae pater et Aelia M parentes filio karissimo et Aelio Procul  vivi sibi et Proculo supra scripto faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-09900402.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Proculus, standard-bearer of the Second Auxiliary Legion, loyal and faithful, and for Iulia Optata, his wife, while alive. A veteran of the above-written legion, his father, and Aelia M., his parents, had this made for their dearest son and for Aelius Procul, while alive for themselves and for the above-written Proculus.
EDCS-09900402.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Proculus, a standard-bearer of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, and to his living wife, Iulia Optata, erected by his parents, who were also anticipating their own burial within the same monument.

EDCS-09900404.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aeliae Severinae Septimius Severinus eques matri vivae fecit
EDCS-09900404.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelia Severina. Septimius Severinus, a knight, made this for his living mother.
EDCS-09900404.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Septimius Severinus, a knight, to his mother, Aelia Severina, while she was still alive, indicating a monument prepared in advance.

EDCS-09900405.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marcus Aurelius Domitianus veteranus legionis II adiutricis domo Ancyra vivos sic sibi fecit adiutantibus nepotes sic suos sic filies sic filios sic Gregorio et Laurentio fratres sic
EDCS-09900405.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius Domitianus, a veteran of the Second Auxiliary Legion, from Ancyra, made this for himself while alive, with the help of his grandsons, his sons, his sons, and his brothers Gregorius and Laurentius.
EDCS-09900405.summary: This is a self-dedication funerary monument by Marcus Aurelius Domitianus, a veteran from Ancyra, who built it for himself while alive, assisted by his family members.

EDCS-09900406.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sabatiae Senecae filiae suae I S ESI   Dis Manibus Aurelia Maxima vixit nnos LVI coniugi entius vivus titulum posuit
EDCS-09900406.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Sabatia Seneca, his daughter. I. S. ESI. To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Maxima lived 56 years. Her husband Entius, while alive, placed this titulus.
EDCS-09900406.summary: This inscription comprises two distinct funerary dedications, one to Sabatia Seneca from a father, and another to Aurelia Maxima, who lived 56 years, from her living husband, Entius.

EDCS-09900407.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Monnio collegium fabrum
EDCS-09900407.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelius Monnius. The college of artisans.
EDCS-09900407.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Monnius by a 'college of artisans,' indicating he was likely a member or patron of this professional guild.

EDCS-09900410.text_edition: Blastaiu Batei filius annorum LXX Comatumarus Saconis filius sibi et coniugi titulum posuit
EDCS-09900410.translation: Blastaius, son of Bateius, 70 years old. Comatumarus, son of Saco, placed this titulus for himself and his wife.
EDCS-09900410.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Comatumarus, son of Saco, for himself and his wife, also recording the age of Blastaius, son of Bateius.

EDCS-09900411.text_edition: Calio Blasi filius nnm XXXXV est defunctus
EDCS-09900411.translation: Calio, son of Blasius, died at 45 years of age.
EDCS-09900411.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Calio, son of Blasius, at the age of 45.

EDCS-09900412.text_edition: Claudio Trophimo nnorum LXXX hic situs est collegium fabrum et centonariorium posuit
EDCS-09900412.translation: Claudius Trophimus, 80 years old, is laid to rest here. The guild of craftsmen and felt-makers erected this.
EDCS-09900412.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius Trophimus, aged 80, erected by the guild of craftsmen and felt-makers.

EDCS-09900414.text_edition: Comatumara Vani filia annorum XXVI hic sita est titulum posuit Mesio coniugi suae et socrui vivae Usiae
EDCS-09900414.translation: Comatumara, daughter of Vanus, 26 years old, is laid to rest here. Mesio placed this monument for his wife and for his mother-in-law, Usia, while she was still alive.
EDCS-09900414.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Comatumara, daughter of Vanus, erected by her husband Mesio, who also included his living mother-in-law Usia on the monument.

EDCS-09900415.translation: Crispus, son of Atto, 60 years old.
EDCS-09900415.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crispus, son of Atto, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-09900417.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Titus Flavius Felicio Augustalis coloniae Aquincensium vivos sic sibi et Flaviae Secundinae quondam conlibertae et uxori quae vixit annos LV et Tito Flavio Felicissimo quondam filio naturali qui vixit nnos XXIII et Tito Flavio Ingenuo filio legitimo et Tito Flavio Felici filio naturali et Flaviae Feliculae filiae naturali et Flaviae Felicissimae nepti vivis Titus Flavius Felicio supra scriptis et sibi faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900417.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Flavius Felicio, Augustalis of the colony of Aquincum, while alive, arranged for this to be made for himself and for Flavia Secundina, formerly his freedwoman and wife, who lived 55 years, and for Titus Flavius Felicissimus, formerly his natural son, who lived 23 years, and for Titus Flavius Ingenuus, his legitimate son, and for Titus Flavius Felix, his natural son, and for Flavia Felicula, his natural daughter, and for Flavia Felicissima, his granddaughter, all of whom are living. Titus Flavius Felicio, as written above, made this for himself and them.
EDCS-09900417.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Divine Manes, prepared by Titus Flavius Felicio for himself and various deceased and living family members, including his wife, children, and granddaughter, dating from approximately the 2nd-3rd century AD given the Augustalis title.

EDCS-09900418.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Flavius Superianus biarcho qui militavit Constantia vixsit sic nnos
EDCS-09900418.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Superianus, a biarch, who served at Constantia, lived [---] years.
EDCS-09900418.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Flavius Superianus, a biarch, who served in Constantia.

EDCS-09900419.text_edition: Iulius Rufus domo Dalmata veteranus legionis II adiutricis annorum LX collegium fabrum et centonariorum posuit
EDCS-09900419.translation: Iulius Rufus, from Dalmatia, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, 60 years old. The guild of craftsmen and felt-makers erected this.
EDCS-09900419.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Rufus, a veteran from Dalmatia of the Legio II Adiutrix, erected by the guild of craftsmen and felt-makers.

EDCS-09900420.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Sperati filius nnrum XX
EDCS-09900420.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius, son of Speratus, 20 years old.
EDCS-09900420.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Lucius, son of Speratus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09900422.text_edition: Bellicus amicus  Masuia Casui filia annorum LXV titulum posuit Namio Atunis filius coniugi et sibi vivo
EDCS-09900422.translation: Bellicus, a friend. Masuia, daughter of Casus, 65 years old. Namio, son of Atto, placed this monument for his wife and for himself while living.
EDCS-09900422.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Masuia, daughter of Casus, erected by her husband Namio, son of Atto, who also included himself while still alive, and possibly involved a friend named Bellicus.

EDCS-09900423.text_edition: Tatunnis filius vixit annos XXV Medulivia Tuionis filia vixit annos XXX
EDCS-09900423.translation: Son of Tatunnis lived 25 years. Medulivia, daughter of Tuio, lived 30 years.
EDCS-09900423.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of two individuals: an unnamed son of Tatunnis, who lived 25 years, and Medulivia, daughter of Tuio, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-09900424.text_edition: Mira Crescentis filia annorum XXX titulum posuit Marcus Attius Rufus veteranus legionis II adiutricis sibi et coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-09900424.translation: Mira, daughter of Crescens, aged 30. Marcus Attius Rufus, veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, placed this tombstone for himself and for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-09900424.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mira, erected by Marcus Attius Rufus, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, for himself and his wife.

EDCS-09900425.text_edition: X C P R D  Mutsia Bonionis filia nnrum L
EDCS-09900425.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Mutsia, daughter of Bonio, aged 50.
EDCS-09900425.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mutsia, daughter of Bonio, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-09900426.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nonio Ianuario veterano legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis et Ae[liae &
EDCS-09900426.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Nonius Ianuarius, veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, and to Aelia [--].
EDCS-09900426.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Nonius Ianuarius, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, and Aelia, likely his wife or relative.

EDCS-09900427.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Masculi filius annorum XX hic situs titulum possuit sic Masculus fEilio#filio#EIL suo
EDCS-09900427.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus, son of Masculus, aged 20, lies here. Masculus placed this tombstone for his son.
EDCS-09900427.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus, son of Masculus, who died at age 20, erected by his father.

EDCS-09900428.text_edition: Retdimara Atalonis filia annorum LX Sacro Nertomari filius vivos fecit
EDCS-09900428.translation: Retdimara, daughter of Atalo, aged 60. Sacro, son of Nertomarus, made this during his lifetime.
EDCS-09900428.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Retdimara, made by Sacro during his lifetime, presumably for her.

EDCS-09900429.text_edition: Ritimarus Rismari filius annorum XXXX titulus positus
EDCS-09900429.translation: Ritimarus, son of Rismarus, aged 40. This tombstone was placed.
EDCS-09900429.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ritimarus, son of Rismarus, who died at age 40.

EDCS-09900430.text_edition: Dis Manibus P Saco Vindelionis filius annorum LX
EDCS-09900430.translation: To the spirits of the departed. P. Saco, son of Vindelio, aged 60.
EDCS-09900430.summary: This is a funerary inscription for P. Saco, son of Vindelio, who died at age 60.

EDCS-09900431.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimia Solia annorum LX Septimius Senecio libertus patronae suae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-09900431.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Septimia Solia, aged 60. Septimius Senecio, her freedman, placed this for his well-deserving patroness.
EDCS-09900431.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimia Solia, erected by her freedman Septimius Senecio.

EDCS-09900432.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sisiu, a young girl who died at age 6.

EDCS-09900433.text_edition: Speratus filius annorum XVIII pater titulum posuit Marcellus sibi et coniugi vivus L CO
EDCS-09900433.translation: Speratus, son, aged 18. His father Marcellus placed this tombstone for himself and his wife during their lifetime.
EDCS-09900433.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Speratus, who died at age 18, placed by his father Marcellus for himself and his wife while they were still alive.

EDCS-09900434.text_edition: Tertio Noibionis filio nnorum XL Satimara Atresi filia marito caro et sibi viva faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900434.translation: Satimara, daughter of Atresis, had this made for her dear husband Tertio, son of Noibio, 40 years old, and for herself, while living.
EDCS-09900434.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Satimara for her husband, Tertio, and for herself, while they were still alive.

EDCS-09900435.text_edition: Urso marito pientissimo et Tertio nepoti eius Tacattu uxor viva et sibi et eis faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900435.translation: Tacattu, his wife, had this made for her most devoted husband Urso and for Tertio, his grandson, and for herself, while living.
EDCS-09900435.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Tacattu for her husband Urso, their grandson Tertio, and for herself, while still alive.

EDCS-09900436.text_edition: Dis Manibus Velideatus Teraniscionis filius annorum XLI dierum C et sibi Vironianius Atalonis filius
EDCS-09900436.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Velideatus, son of Teraniscium, 41 years and 100 days old, and for himself, Vironianius, son of Atalo.
EDCS-09900436.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Velideatus and Vironianius.

EDCS-09900437.text_edition: Verodubena Cesonis filia annorum XXX titulum posuit Maius Cuci
EDCS-09900437.translation: Maius Cucius placed this memorial stone for Verodubena, daughter of Ceso, 30 years old.
EDCS-09900437.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Maius Cucius erected a memorial for Verodubena.

EDCS-09900442.translation: Beneficiary of the Second Adiutrix Legion, in the consulship of Fuscus and Dexter.
EDCS-09900442.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a beneficiary of the Legio II Adiutrix and dates the event to the consulship of Fuscus and Dexter.

EDCS-09900443.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimius Avvo et Septimii Att=IIa#Atta#AIIA etI#et#EI Avva filii eorum posueruntI#posuerunt#POSUERUNI SeptIimiae#Septimiae#SEPIIMIE Paulinae coniugi carissimae cum parbulos sic fEilios sic#filios sic#EILIOS eorum
EDCS-09900443.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Septimius Avvo and Septimii Atta and Avva, their children, placed this for their dearest wife Septimia Paulina, with their little children.
EDCS-09900443.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Septimius Avvo and his children, Atta and Avva, to their beloved wife and mother, Septimia Paulina, and their young children.

EDCS-09900444.text_edition: miles cohortis eiusdem  Septimia Theodora pientissimis vivus sibi faciendum curavit  et Zosimenae coniugi nniano filio et  nepoti et Diomedi nepoti  nepoti et Omainae et
EDCS-09900444.translation: Septimia Theodora, a soldier of the same cohort, while living, had this made for her most devoted self, and for her wife Zosimena, and for her son Nnianus, and for her grandson, and for her grandson Diomedes, and for Omaina and...
EDCS-09900444.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a tomb by Septimia Theodora for herself, her wife Zosimena, her son, and her grandsons, and Omaina.

EDCS-09900446.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo V  Antonino et Vero consulibus
EDCS-09900446.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, in the consulship of Antoninus and Verus.
EDCS-09900446.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dated to the consulship of Antoninus and Verus.

EDCS-09900447.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the deities Liber Pater and Ariadne.

EDCS-09900450.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Iulius Crispinianus veteranus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900450.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Iulius Crispinianus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900450.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription where Iulius Crispinianus, a veteran, fulfilled a vow to the deity Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-09900451.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimio Dasio quondam militi legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XIIII vixit annos XXXVI Aelia Sura mater eius et Aurelius Severus immunis legionis eiusdem heredes titulum bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-09900451.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Septimius Dasius, formerly a soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix, with 14 years of service, who lived 36 years. Aelia Sura, his mother, and Aurelius Severus, an exempted soldier of the same legion, his heirs, took care to have this monument made for the well-deserving.
EDCS-09900451.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimius Dasius, a soldier of Legio II Adiutrix, set up by his mother and an exempted soldier of the same legion, acting as his heirs.

EDCS-09900452.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Verienda Terti filia annorum XL hic sita est titulum posuit Valerinus sibi vivo et coniugi
EDCS-09900452.translation: To the Divine Manes. Verienda, daughter of Tertius, aged 40 years, lies here. Valerinus placed this monument for himself, while living, and for his wife.
EDCS-09900452.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Verienda, daughter of Tertius, set up by her husband Valerinus, who also intended it for himself.

EDCS-09900462.text_edition: pro salute domini nostri Gordiani Augusti Cicerius Homuncio beneficiarius consularis legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito Sabino II et Venusto consulibus
EDCS-09900462.translation: For the safety of our lord Gordian Augustus, Cicerius Homuncio, beneficiarius consularis of the First Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Sabinus II and Venustus.
EDCS-09900462.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Cicerius Homuncio, a beneficiarius consularis, for the well-being of Emperor Gordian, dated to the year 242 AD.

EDCS-09900463.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Publius Aelius Constans candidatus legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900463.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, Publius Aelius Constans, a candidate soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900463.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, made by Publius Aelius Constans, a candidate soldier of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-09900464.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Iulius Martinianus et Aurelius Dines et Memorina votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-09900464.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, Julius Martinianus, Aurelius Dines, and Memorina willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09900464.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, made by Julius Martinianus, Aurelius Dines, and Memorina.

EDCS-09900465.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Crispius Potens votum solvit  Perpetuo et Corneliano consulibus
EDCS-09900465.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, Crispius Potens fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelianus.
EDCS-09900465.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Crispius Potens to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, dated to the year 237 AD.

EDCS-09900466.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Terrae Matri Iulius Rufus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900466.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mother Earth, Julius Rufus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900466.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Terra Mater, made by Julius Rufus.

EDCS-09900467.text_edition: Libero Patri Paulinus votum libies sic solvit
EDCS-09900467.translation: To Father Liber, Paulinus willingly thus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900467.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Liber Pater, made by Paulinus.

EDCS-09900468.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Publius Nigrius Quintilianus beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-09900468.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, Publius Nigrius Quintilianus, beneficiarius consularis.
EDCS-09900468.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Nemesis Augusta, made by Publius Nigrius Quintilianus, a beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-09900469.text_edition: Silvano Iulius Rufus votum solvit libens merito Laeliano et Pastore consulibus
EDCS-09900469.translation: To Silvanus, Julius Rufus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Laelianus and Pastor.
EDCS-09900469.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Julius Rufus to Silvanus, dated to the year 269 AD.

EDCS-09900471.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Crispius Potens votum solvit
EDCS-09900471.translation: To Silvanus Silvester, Crispius Potens fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900471.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory offering made by Crispius Potens to the deity Silvanus Silvester, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900473.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurus vixit nnos XXII Vitalis vixit nnos V bruti et neboti sic posuerunt Atrevius Cata parentes bene merentibus
EDCS-09900473.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurus lived 22 years, Vitalis lived 5 years, bruti and neboti thus placed this. Atrevius Cata, the parents, for their well-deserving children.
EDCS-09900473.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents, Atrevius Cata, for their two deceased children, Aurus and Vitalis, who died at ages 22 and 5 respectively.

EDCS-09900474.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning the name Iulianus, with most of the text missing.

EDCS-09900475.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caesernius Martialis miles legionis primae adiutricis signifer vixit annos XL Aelia ata coniugi carissimo et Caeserniae Provinciae filiae carissimae posuit
EDCS-09900475.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caesernius Martialis, a standard-bearer of the First Adjutrix Legion, lived 40 years. Aelia Ata placed this for her dearest husband and for her dearest daughter Caesernia Provincia.
EDCS-09900475.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Ata to her beloved husband, Caesernius Martialis, a 40-year-old standard-bearer of the First Adjutrix Legion, and to their beloved daughter, Caesernia Provincia.

EDCS-09900476.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Propinco sic  annos XV et Aelius Attianus  vixit annos XVII Aelius itus coniugi
EDCS-09900476.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Propinquus thus [lived] 15 years, and Aelius Attianus lived 17 years. Aelius itus [placed this] for his spouse.
EDCS-09900476.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely erected by Aelius itus for his spouse and possibly another individual named Propinquus, recording their ages at death as 15 and 17 years respectively.

EDCS-09900477.text_edition: vixit annos V Sabinus vixit annos et Serotinus iunior vixit Lucius Septimius Serotinus domo nia Sabinilla parentes
EDCS-09900477.translation: Sabinus lived 5 years, and Serotinus Iunior lived [---]. Lucius Septimius Serotinus from the house of Sabinilla, parents.
EDCS-09900477.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Lucius Septimius Serotinus and Sabinilla for their deceased children, Sabinus and Serotinus Iunior, one of whom died at the age of 5.

EDCS-09900478.text_edition: Dis Manibus N D  annos  menses VII Inus us signifer bene merito posuit
EDCS-09900478.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. N.D. [lived] [---] years, 7 months. Inus us, a standard-bearer, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-09900478.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely placed by Inus us, a standard-bearer, for a well-deserving individual who lived a certain number of years and 7 months.

EDCS-09900481.text_edition: veteranus et I totiani posu--- pro salute August3
EDCS-09900481.translation: veteran and I totiani posu[it] / [---] for the health of Augustus.
EDCS-09900481.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication by a veteran and others, made for the health or well-being of an Augustus, indicating a probable imperial cult context.

EDCS-09900482.translation: Tiberius made this for himself while alive and [---].
EDCS-09900482.summary: This is a funerary inscription, made by Tiberius for himself during his lifetime, indicating a pre-mortem tomb construction.

EDCS-09900484.text_edition: ci filiae Rusticae annorum XXXXV Iulius Honoratus coniugi pientissimae et Moderato filio annorum V posuit
EDCS-09900484.translation: To his daughter Rustica, 45 years old. Iulius Honoratus placed this for his most devoted wife and for his 5-year-old son Moderatus.
EDCS-09900484.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Iulius Honoratus for his most devoted wife Rustica, who was 45 years old, and for their 5-year-old son Moderatus.

EDCS-09900485.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Severa Aelio Saturnino coniugi pientissimo vixit annos LXXX et Aelio Exsuperato et Apuleio Macriniano filiis et Aureliae Nigrinae matri et FIMS et Atois et sibi memoriam posuit
EDCS-09900485.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Severa placed this memorial for Aelius Saturninus, her most devoted husband, who lived 80 years, and for her sons Aelius Exsuperatus and Apuleius Macrinianus, and for her mother Aurelia Nigrina, and for FIMS and Atois, and for herself.
EDCS-09900485.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelia Severa to commemorate her husband, sons, mother, other individuals, and herself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09900486.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio V annos VII dom me
EDCS-09900486.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Aurelius V, who lived 7 years. [The rest is illegible or abbreviated, possibly indicating 'at home' or the dedicator.]
EDCS-09900486.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Aurelius V, who died at the age of seven, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-09900488.text_edition: Surius Essimni filius Cattenas miles cohortis I Vindelicorum nnorum XXXX stipendiorum XX hic situs est Florus et Bassus CE
EDCS-09900488.translation: Surius, son of Essimnus, a Cattenian, soldier of the First Cohort of Vindelici, 40 years old, with 20 years of service, is laid here. Florus and Bassus [placed this].
EDCS-09900488.summary: This is a military funerary inscription for Surius, a soldier of the Cohors I Vindelicorum, erected by Florus and Bassus, likely commemorating his service and death.

EDCS-09900489.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Celer veteranus alae Brittonum pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-09900489.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Claudius Celer, a veteran of the Ala Brittonum, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-09900489.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Claudius Celer, a veteran, for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-09900493.text_edition: Libero Augusto  V  votum solv- liben- merito
EDCS-09900493.translation: To Liber Augustus. V... fulfilled a vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900493.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to Liber Augustus, with the dedicator's name largely missing, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09900494.translation: For Mansuetus of the new milliary cohort, who lived 6 years.
EDCS-09900494.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mansuetus, associated with a 'new milliary cohort,' who died at the age of six, likely erected by his family or comrades.

EDCS-09900495.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Dobnina quae vixsit sic annos viginti et duos
EDCS-09900495.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Dobnina, who lived twenty-two years.
EDCS-09900495.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Dobnina, who died at the age of twenty-two, likely erected by her family or loved ones.

EDCS-09900496.text_edition: Dis Manibus Attio Germanioni liberto vixit nnos XL Apuleius Basileus posuit bene merenti
EDCS-09900496.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Apuleius Basileus placed this for Attius Germanio, his freedman, who lived 40 years, for his well-deserving service.
EDCS-09900496.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Apuleius Basileus to his freedman, Attius Germanio, who lived for forty years, in recognition of his good service.

EDCS-09900497.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Ingenuae coniugi pientissimae vixit nnos XXX Aurelius Crescentio posuit et Aurelio Crescentiano et Aureliae Ursae
EDCS-09900497.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Crescentio placed this for Aurelia Ingenua, his most devoted wife, who lived 30 years, and for Aurelius Crescentianus and Aurelia Ursa.
EDCS-09900497.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Crescentio to his beloved wife, Aurelia Ingenua, and also mentioning Aurelius Crescentianus and Aurelia Ursa, likely family members.

EDCS-09900501.text_edition: Deo Azizo pro salute domini nostri Alexandri Augusti et Iuliae Augustae Iulius manus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900501.translation: To the god Azizus, for the health of our lord Alexander Augustus and Julia Augusta, Julius [---] willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900501.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Azizus by Julius [---] for the health and well-being of Emperor Alexander Severus and Julia Mamaea, his mother, indicating a date between 222 and 235 CE.

EDCS-09900502.summary: This is a brief dedication to the deity Cautes.

EDCS-09900504.text_edition: Dianae Augustae pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Alexandri Augusti vetterani cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-09900504.translation: To Diana Augusta, for the well-being of our lord Imperator Alexander Augustus, the veterans of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09900504.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Diana Augusta by veterans of the Cohors Hemesenorum for the health of Emperor Alexander Severus (222-235 AD).

EDCS-09900505.text_edition: Dianae Titus Flavius Quintianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900505.translation: To Diana, Titus Flavius Quintianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900505.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication by Titus Flavius Quintianus fulfilling a vow to Diana.

EDCS-09900506.text_edition: Deae Dianae  Rat Fuscus  pro se Prisciano et Valeriano et suis votum solvit libens merito Modesto et Probo consulibus
EDCS-09900506.translation: To the goddess Diana, Rat Fuscus, for himself, Priscianus, Valerianus, and his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-09900506.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Diana by Rat Fuscus on behalf of himself and his family, dated to the consulship of Modestus and Probus (228 AD).

EDCS-09900508.text_edition: Dis oimnibus Sabinus veteranus Sabininanus veteranus ex voto pBosuit sic#posuit sic#BOSUIT
EDCS-09900508.translation: To all the gods, Sabinus the veteran and Sabinianus the veteran placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-09900508.summary: This inscription is a dedication to all the gods by two veterans, Sabinus and Sabinianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900509.text_edition: Deo Dobrati Eutices sic servus dedit
EDCS-09900509.translation: To the god Dobratus, Eutices, a slave, gave this.
EDCS-09900509.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Dobratus by a slave named Eutices.

EDCS-09900510.text_edition: Fortunae pro salute domini nostri Severi Alexandri Augusti collegius sic Fortunae ex voto posuerunt
EDCS-09900510.translation: To Fortuna, for the well-being of our lord Severus Alexander Augustus, the collegium of Fortuna placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-09900510.summary: This inscription records a vow made by a collegium dedicated to Fortuna, for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander (222-235 AD).

EDCS-09900511.text_edition: Herculi Conservatori domus Heraclitianae Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus |(centurio) cohortis XI urbanae Severianae cum Aureliis Italla Thraca et Heraclito iuniore filiis  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900511.translation: To Hercules Conservator of the house of Heraclitus, Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, centurion of the Severian 11th Urban Cohort, with Aurelii Italla, Thraca, and Heraclitus junior, his children, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900511.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Hercules Conservator by Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, a centurion of the Cohors XI Urbana Severiana, along with his children.

EDCS-09900514.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo votum solverunt libentes merito Publii Aelii Septimus et Decoratus decuriones municipii et arm civitatis Eraviscorum
EDCS-09900514.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Septimus and Decoratus, decurions of the municipium and citizens of the civitas Eraviscorum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09900514.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two decurions, Septimus and Decoratus, from the civitas Eraviscorum.

EDCS-09900515.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Castus immunis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900515.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Castus, exempt, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900515.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Castus, who is noted as an 'immunis' (exempt from certain duties).

EDCS-09900516.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Damas veteranus Hemesenorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900516.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurelius Damas, veteran of the Hemeseni, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900516.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by a veteran named Aurelius Damas, likely from the Hemesene unit, indicating a personal fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09900517.text_edition: Pro salute domini nostri Severi Alexandri Augusti et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori domus Heraclitianae Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus |(centurio) statorum praetorianorum cum filiis suis votum solvit libens merito
