EDCS-09900517.translation: For the well-being of our lord Severus Alexander Augustus and Julia Mamaea Augusta, to Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Preserver of the house of Heraclitus, Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, centurion of the praetorian sta[tor]s, with his sons, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900517.summary: This votive inscription, likely dating to the reign of Severus Alexander (222-235 AD), was dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, a centurion in the praetorian guard, and his sons to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator for the health of the emperor and empress and the preservation of his own household.

EDCS-09900519.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Aurelius Rufus |(centurio) pro se suisque omnibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900519.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurelius Rufus, centurion, for himself and all his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900519.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Aurelius Rufus, a centurion, made on behalf of himself and his entire family.

EDCS-09900520.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Verissimus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900520.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Aurelius Verissimus, consular beneficiarius of the Second Auxiliary Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900520.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to Jupiter by Marcus Aurelius Verissimus, a beneficiarius of the Legio II Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900522.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cornelius beneficiarius votum libens merito
EDCS-09900522.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Cornelius, beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly [fulfilled his] vow.
EDCS-09900522.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by a beneficiarius named Cornelius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900523.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Flavius Cai filius Domitius Severus
EDCS-09900523.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Flavius Domitius Severus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-09900523.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Flavius Domitius Severus, son of Gaius, likely indicating a votive offering or a monument erected in his name.

EDCS-09900524.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Paterno.
EDCS-09900524.summary: This brief inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual named Paterno.

EDCS-09900525.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Libero Telluri Aurelius Barsamsus strator consularis sacravit
EDCS-09900525.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, Liber, and Tellus. Aurelius Barsamsus, consular strator, consecrated this.
EDCS-09900525.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication by Aurelius Barsamsus, a consular strator, consecrating a monument to a pantheon of deities: Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Liber, and Tellus.

EDCS-09900527.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Culminali Aurelius Mammianus veteranus cum suis omnibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900527.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Culminalis. Aurelius Mammianus, veteran, with all his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900527.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis by Aurelius Mammianus, a veteran, on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-09900528.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus.
EDCS-09900528.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in his syncretic form as Jupiter Dolichenus, indicating a cultic offering.

EDCS-09900529.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Heroni pro salute Imperatoris domini nostri Severi Alexandr[i] Augusti cultores collegii eius templum a solo extruxerunt curantibus sic Aurelio MestErio#Mestrio#MESERIO patre
EDCS-09900529.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heroni, for the well-being of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus, the worshippers of his college built the temple from the ground up, with Aurelius Mestrio, the father, caring for it.
EDCS-09900529.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple from the ground up to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heroni by a college of worshippers, dedicated for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus (dated 222-235 AD).

EDCS-09900530.text_edition: Libero Patri  Aurelius Iustinianus armorum custos votum solvit libentes merito
EDCS-09900530.translation: To Liber Pater, Aurelius Iustinianus, custos armorum (keeper of arms), willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900530.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aurelius Iustinianus, a keeper of arms, to Liber Pater.

EDCS-09900531.text_edition: Libero Patri Aurelius Monimus veteranus pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900531.translation: To Liber Pater, Aurelius Monimus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being and that of his family.
EDCS-09900531.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aurelius Monimus, a veteran, to Liber Pater for the safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-09900532.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Aurelius Maturus summus curator votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900532.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Aurelius Maturus, the chief curator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900532.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aurelius Maturus, a chief curator, to Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-09900533.text_edition: Mercurio  lucrorum potenti Aurelius Hermes negotians pro salute sua
EDCS-09900533.translation: To Mercury, powerful in gains, Aurelius Hermes, a merchant, for his own well-being.
EDCS-09900533.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aurelius Hermes, a merchant, to Mercury, god of profit, for his own well-being.

EDCS-09900534.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae Antonius Veranus pater pientissimus suo in loco feliciter posuit
EDCS-09900534.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Antonius Veranus, the most devoted father, happily placed this in his own place.
EDCS-09900534.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, placed by Antonius Veranus in a private or designated location.

EDCS-09900535.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae  Atta Vervici libert-- votum merito solvit
EDCS-09900535.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Atta, freedman of Vervicus, deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900535.summary: This inscription records a deserved votive offering to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras by Atta, a freedman of Vervicus.

EDCS-09900537.translation: Sacred to the god Silvanus, Aurelius Mari[---].
EDCS-09900537.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to the god Silvanus by Aurelius Mari[...].

EDCS-09900538.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Saturnina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900538.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Saturnina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09900538.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Saturnina to Silvanus Domesticus, a household deity.

EDCS-09900539.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Secundinus pro se et suis votum posuit
EDCS-09900539.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, Secundinus placed this vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-09900539.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus by Secundinus for the protection of himself and his household.

EDCS-09900540.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Antonius Veranus pater votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-09900540.translation: To the Unconquered Sun god, Antonius Veranus, father, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900540.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Sol Invictus by Antonius Veranus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900541.text_edition: Soli Invicto Aurelius Arbas Aelia innina  Soli Aelius Vitalianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900541.translation: To the Unconquered Sun, Aurelius Arbas, Aelia Innina, [and] Aelius Vitalianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09900541.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sol Invictus by Aurelius Arbas, Aelia Innina, and Aelius Vitalianus, fulfilling their vow.

EDCS-09900542.text_edition: Deo Soli Aurelius Barsamsus optio cohortis ex voto paterno cum suis posuit
EDCS-09900542.translation: To the god Sol, Aurelius Barsamsus, optio of the cohort, placed this with his family according to his father's vow.
EDCS-09900542.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Sol by Aurelius Barsamsus, an optio of a cohort, fulfilling a vow made by his father.

EDCS-09900543.text_edition: Deo Soli Iulius Donatus summus curator votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-09900543.translation: To the god Sol, Iulius Donatus, chief curator, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900543.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Sol by Iulius Donatus, a chief curator, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900544.text_edition: Deo  Soli Aelagabalo sic pro salute Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii et Marci Aureli Antonini Pii et Cai Septimi GeAtae#Getae#GATAE Caesaris Auggustorum cohors |(miliaria) Antoniniana Hemesenorum civium Romanorum sagittariorum cui sub Baebio Caeciliano legato Auggustorum praeest Quintus Modius Quinti filius Quirina RufEinus#Rufinus#RUEINUS tribunus  templIum#templum#MPIUM a solo extruxit
EDCS-09900544.translation: To the god Sol Elagabalus, for the welfare of the emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius and Gaius Septimius Geta Caesar Augusti, the Antoninianian cohort, 1000 strong, of Emesene Roman citizens, archers, under Baebius Caecilianus, legate of the Augusti, whose commander is Quintus Modius Rufinus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, built this temple from its foundations.
EDCS-09900544.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple from its foundations by the Cohors I Antoniniana Hemesenorum sagittariorum to Sol Elagabalus for the welfare of Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900547.text_edition: Aelagabali sic VI umtario filio   EupreFpete#Euprepete#EUPRFPETE libeFr[to#liberto#LIBFR
EDCS-09900547.translation: To Elagabalus, son of Umtarius, Euprepete, freedman...
EDCS-09900547.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to Elagabalus, son of Umtarius, by his freedman Euprepete.

EDCS-09900549.text_edition: I Rusticus Aurelia Italia mater votum libens solvit
EDCS-09900549.translation: I. Rusticus [and] Aurelia Italia, mother, willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09900549.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Rusticus and his mother Aurelia Italia.

EDCS-09900550.translation: Castus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900550.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Castus.

EDCS-09900551.text_edition: Chr=RHysanthus#Chrysanthus#CRHYSANTHUS servus arkarius sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900551.translation: Chrysanthus, slave, cashier, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900551.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Chrysanthus, a slave who worked as a cashier.

EDCS-09900552.text_edition: Iulius Sallustianus veteranus ex decO(urione)#dec(urione)#DEO cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900552.translation: Iulius Sallustianus, veteran, formerly decurion of the 1000-strong Emesene cohort, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900552.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Iulius Sallustianus, a veteran and former decurion of the Cohors I Hemesenorum.

EDCS-09900553.text_edition: Primitivus Zoili votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09900553.translation: Primitivus, servant of Zoilus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900553.summary: This inscription records Primitivus, a servant of Zoilus, fulfilling a vow to an unknown deity, likely a private dedication.

EDCS-09900556.text_edition: legC(ionis)#leg(ionis)#LEC II adiutricis piae fidelis ex voto pro se et suis
EDCS-09900556.translation: A soldier of the Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, from a vow, for himself and his family.
EDCS-09900556.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by a soldier of Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis for his own well-being and that of his family, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900557.text_edition: Utere feliEx#felix#FELEX Severe coniux dulcissime somnis
EDCS-09900557.translation: Be happy, Severa, sweetest wife, in your sleep.
EDCS-09900557.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from a tombstone, commemorating a deceased wife named Severa, expressing a wish for her peaceful rest.

EDCS-09900558.text_edition: SI sacrum  m sol  con KRO
EDCS-09900558.translation: Sacred to SI. The sun... with Kro...
EDCS-09900558.summary: This is a fragmentary and largely incomprehensible inscription, possibly a religious dedication, with only a few words discernible.

EDCS-09900560.translation: …sa willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-09900560.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription recording that an individual whose name ends in '-sa' willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to an unknown deity, likely a private dedication.

EDCS-09900562.text_edition: Pro salute  Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti patris patriae Publius Aelius Firmus Rusconis filius ob honorem aedilitatis locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-09900562.translation: For the well-being of the Imperator Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, father of the country, Publius Aelius Firmus, son of Rusco, for the honor of the aedileship, the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09900562.summary: This inscription, dated to the reign of Hadrian (117-138 AD), records the granting of a public space to Publius Aelius Firmus by the town council (decurions) in honor of his aedileship, likely for the construction of a public monument, and dedicated to the emperor's well-being.

EDCS-09900563.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patre patriae pontes facti iussu Gai Gemini Capelliani legati Augusti pro praetore curante Tiberio Claudio Euprepete
EDCS-09900563.translation: During the reign of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrian Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, bridges were built by order of Gaius Geminius Capellianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, under the supervision of Tiberius Claudius Euprepes.
EDCS-09900563.summary: This inscription, dated to the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 AD), records the construction of bridges by imperial order under the supervision of the provincial governor Gaius Geminius Capellianus and an individual named Tiberius Claudius Euprepes.

EDCS-09900567.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore
EDCS-09900567.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixth time, acclaimed imperator four times, consul four times, father of the country, fortified the entire riverbank by building burgi from their foundations, and also by establishing garrisons in suitable places against the secret crossings of bandits, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-09900567.summary: This inscription, dated to 181 AD during Commodus's reign (specifically his 6th tribunician power and 4th consulship), records the emperor's fortification of a riverbank with burgi (fortified towers) and garrisons to combat bandits, carried out under the command of the provincial governor Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus.

EDCS-09900569.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidiis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore
EDCS-09900569.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixth time, acclaimed imperator four times, consul four times, father of the country, fortified the entire riverbank by building burgi from their foundations, and also by establishing garrisons in suitable places against the secret crossings of bandits, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-09900569.summary: This inscription, dated to 181 AD during Commodus's reign (specifically his 6th tribunician power and 4th consulship), records the emperor's fortification of a riverbank with burgi (fortified towers) and garrisons to combat bandits, carried out under the command of the provincial governor Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus.

EDCS-09900573.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus Brittannicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidiis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore
EDCS-09900573.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus Brittannicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixth time, acclaimed imperator four times, consul four times, father of the country, fortified the entire riverbank by building burgi from their foundations, and also by establishing garrisons in suitable places against the secret crossings of bandits, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-09900573.summary: This inscription, dated to 181 AD during Commodus's reign (specifically his 6th tribunician power and 4th consulship), records the emperor's fortification of a riverbank with burgi (fortified towers) and garrisons to combat bandits, carried out under the command of the provincial governor Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus; the addition of 'Brittannicus' suggests it was made after his acclamation with that title.

EDCS-09900574.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus Britannicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI imperator IIII consul IIII pater patriae ripam omnem burgis a solo extructis item praesidiis per loca opportuna ad clandestinos latrunculorum transitus oppositis munivit per Lucium Cornelium Felicem Plotianum legatum pro praetore
EDCS-09900574.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus Britannicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 6th time, acclaimed imperator for the 4th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, fortified the entire river bank with burgi built from the ground up, and likewise with garrisons placed in opportune locations against clandestine crossings by robbers, through Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, his propraetorian legate.
EDCS-09900574.summary: This inscription records Emperor Commodus's fortification of a river bank with burgi and garrisons to prevent robber crossings, carried out by his legate Lucius Cornelius Felix Plotianus, likely dating to c. 180-185 AD based on his imperial titles.

EDCS-09900577.text_edition: Pro salute et victoria Germanica Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi Britannici maximi Germanici maximi pontificis maximi patris patriae tribunicia potestate consulis IIII Deo Patrio Soli Elagabalo milites cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum Antoninianae  dedicatum opus X Kalendas Septembres  Messala et Sabino consulibus
EDCS-09900577.translation: For the health and Germanic victory of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, father of the country, holding tribunician power, consul for the 4th time, the soldiers of the Cohors Miliaria Hemesenorum Antoniniana dedicated this work on the 10th Kalends of September, during the consulship of Messala and Sabinus.
EDCS-09900577.summary: This inscription is a dedication by soldiers of the Cohors Miliaria Hemesenorum Antoniniana for the health and Germanic victory of Emperor Caracalla, dated August 23rd, 214 AD, during the consulship of Messala and Sabinus.

EDCS-09900578.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico Britannico maximo pontifici maximo Germanico maximo cohors I |(miliaria) Hemesenorum Aurelia Antoniniana sagittariorum equitata civium Romanorum devotissima numini eius
EDCS-09900578.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus, Britannicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, Germanicus Maximus, the first Cohort Miliaria Hemesenorum Aurelia Antoniniana of mounted archers, Roman citizens, most devoted to his divinity.
EDCS-09900578.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla by the Cohors Miliaria Hemesenorum Aurelia Antoniniana, a unit of mounted archers composed of Roman citizens, expressing their devotion to his divine majesty.

EDCS-09900581.text_edition: Sanctissimo ac super omnes principes clementissimo Imperatori Caesari Gaio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II patri patriae proconsuli cohors I |(miliaria) Hemesenorum maiestati eorum devotissima
EDCS-09900581.translation: To the most holy and above all princes most clement Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, proconsul, the first Cohort Miliaria Hemesenorum, most devoted to their majesty.
EDCS-09900581.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Cohors I Miliaria Hemesenorum to Emperor Trebonianus Gallus, expressing their profound devotion to his imperial majesty, likely dating to 252 AD based on his second consulship and tribunician power.

EDCS-09900583.text_edition: Absucus Adnamonis filius annorum LX hic situs est Nertomarus et Locco Atedunus filii et heredes titulum memoriae posuerunt
EDCS-09900583.translation: Absucus, son of Adnamo, aged 60, lies here. Nertomarus and Locco Atedunus, his sons and heirs, placed this memorial inscription.
EDCS-09900583.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Absucus, son of Adnamo, aged 60, erected by his sons and heirs, Nertomarus and Locco Atedunus.

EDCS-09900584.text_edition: Adnamata Carveicionis  filia nnorum L hic sita est Claudius Cesoris coniugi piissimae titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-09900584.translation: Adnamata, daughter of Carveicio, aged 50, lies here. Claudius Cesor placed this memorial inscription for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-09900584.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Adnamata, daughter of Carveicio, aged 50, erected by her husband, Claudius Cesor, in her memory.

EDCS-09900585.text_edition: Adinamo Acuti filius annorum XXXX hic est situs eques occisus in civitate Eraviscorum defunctus Tibeurnus et Montanus filii devotissimi pateri sic suo titulum posuerunt memoriae
EDCS-09900585.translation: Adinamo, son of Acutus, aged 40, an equestrian, lies here, killed in the city of the Eravisci. Tibeurnus and Montanus, his most devoted sons, thus placed this memorial inscription for their father.
EDCS-09900585.summary: This is a tombstone for Adinamo, son of Acutus, an equestrian aged 40, who died in the city of the Eravisci, erected by his devoted sons, Tibeurnus and Montanus.

EDCS-09900587.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  Aeliae Mattunae quae et Au hic eC(st)#e(st)#C sita nnorum XXX coniuigi bene merenti  Verecumberae pientissimae Aurelius Vindo et sibi vivo posuit
EDCS-09900587.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Mattuna, also known as Au, who lies here, aged 30. Aurelius Vindo placed this for his most deserving and most devoted wife, Verecumbera, and for himself while living.
EDCS-09900587.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Manes for Aelia Mattuna, also known as Au, aged 30, erected by Aurelius Vindo for his deceased wife, Verecumbera, and also for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-09900588.translation: To Publius Aelius, son of Aelius OlyM.
EDCS-09900588.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely intended as a dedication or funerary monument for Publius Aelius, son of Aelius OlyM, with the full context missing.

EDCS-09900589.text_edition: Aelius Cesumarius Libbonis filius sibi et coniugi et filio vivo posuit
EDCS-09900589.translation: Aelius Cesumarius, son of Libbo, placed this for himself and his wife and his son while living.
EDCS-09900589.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelius Cesumarius, son of Libbo, for himself, his wife, and his son, while they were still alive, indicating a pre-prepared family tombstone.

EDCS-09900590.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelio Munatio capsario cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum stupendiorum sic XXVIII domo Samosata Aurelia Cansauna coniunx Antonio Basso vexillario secundo herede sanctissimo coniugi con sic se sic natibusque suis fecit monumentum memoriae
EDCS-09900590.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aelius Munatius, paymaster of the cohort of Hemeseni, who served 28 years, from Samosata. Aurelia Cansauna, his wife, made this monument in memory of her most sacred husband, with Antonius Bassus, vexillarius, as second heir, and for herself and her children.
EDCS-09900590.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Aurelia Cansauna, wife of Aelius Munatius, a paymaster (capsarius) of the Cohort of Hemeseni, for her deceased husband, herself, and her children, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900593.text_edition: Publius Aelius Vitalis annorum XIII et Publius Aelius Victor annorum I hic siti sunt Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius filiis posuit
EDCS-09900593.translation: Publius Aelius Vitalis, 13 years old, and Publius Aelius Victor, 1 year old, are buried here. Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius placed this for his sons.
EDCS-09900593.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating two young brothers, Publius Aelius Vitalis and Publius Aelius Victor, erected by their father, Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900595.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Antonius Censorinus domo Apamia annorum LXXX et Marcus Antonius Optatus nepos eius annorum VII hic siti sunt et Ulpiae Zosimenae vivae coniugi eius Marcus Antonius Marcianus parentibus et filio eorum faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900595.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Antonius Censorinus, from Apamia, 80 years old, and Marcus Antonius Optatus, his grandson, 7 years old, are buried here. Marcus Antonius Marcianus arranged for this to be made for his parents and their son, and for Ulpia Zosimena, his wife, while still living.
EDCS-09900595.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Marcus Antonius Marcianus to his deceased father, Marcus Antonius Censorinus, and his grandson, Marcus Antonius Optatus, as well as for his living wife, Ulpia Zosimena, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900596.text_edition: militi legionis qui vixit nns   Appius filius et Serena filia pientissima cum? --- ---? Equite et Claudio Cy
EDCS-09900596.translation: To a soldier of the legion who lived for [unknown] years. His most devoted son Appius and daughter Serena [made this] with [---] Equites and Claudius Cy[---].
EDCS-09900596.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone for a legionary soldier, erected by his devoted children, Appius and Serena, with others mentioned, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900597.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aureliae Barachae vixit nnos XXXV et AureHliae#Aureliae#AURHL Germanillae vixit nnos IIII et altera filia Aurelia Germanilla vixit nnos II et Immostae matri suae vixit nnos LX Germanius Valens miles cohortis Mmiliariae Hemesenorum uxori et matri et filiis posuit et sibi vivius fecit
EDCS-09900597.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelia Baracha, who lived 35 years, and to Aurelia Germanilla, who lived 4 years, and to another daughter, Aurelia Germanilla, who lived 2 years, and to her mother Immosta, who lived 60 years. Germanius Valens, soldier of the Cohort of Hemeseni, placed this for his wife, mother, and daughters, and made it for himself while living.
EDCS-09900597.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Germanius Valens, a soldier of the Cohort of Hemeseni, for his deceased wife, two daughters (both named Aurelia Germanilla, perhaps one adopted or a second child named after a deceased one), and his mother, Immosta, and for himself while still living, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900598.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Heraclitae filiae quae vixit annis XVII mensibus VII diebus XXII Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus hastatus legionis VII Claudiae pater et Pia mater et ante eos Eppius Flavianus evokatus sic maritus pietatis causa posuerunt
EDCS-09900598.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelia Heraclita, daughter, who lived 17 years, 7 months, 22 days. Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, hastatus of the Seventh Claudian Legion, her father, and Pia, her mother, and before them Eppius Flavianus, evocatus, her husband, placed this out of piety.
EDCS-09900598.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Aurelia Heraclita by her father, Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus (a hastatus of Legio VII Claudia), her mother, Pia, and her husband, Eppius Flavianus (an evocatus), expressing their piety, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900599.text_edition: inae Corneliae Aurelia Iustina filia et mater titulum posuoerunt Aurelio Sal Augustali coloniae Aquincensium
EDCS-09900599.translation: To Cornelia [---]. Aurelia Iustina, daughter and mother, placed this title for Aurelius Sal[---], Augustalis of the colony of Aquincum.
EDCS-09900599.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone dedicated by Aurelia Iustina, as both daughter and mother, to Cornelia [---] and to Aurelius Sal[---], an Augustalis of Aquincum, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900600.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcia et Flavius Avo Aureliae Agrippinae filiae quae vixit nnos  VI titulum bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-09900600.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Marcia and Flavius Avo placed this title for their well-deserving daughter, Aurelia Agrippina, who lived 6 years.
EDCS-09900600.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Aurelia Marcia and Flavius Avo to their deceased daughter, Aurelia Agrippina, who lived only six years, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900601.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  Aureliae Nardanosae cives sic Armeniacae vixit nnos XLIII Aurelius Manaia veteranus maritus et Aurelius Tata Pusintulus filius memoriae posuerunt
EDCS-09900601.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelia Nardanosa, an Armenian citizen, who lived 43 years. Aurelius Manaia, veteran, her husband, and Aurelius Tata Pusintulus, her son, placed this in her memory.
EDCS-09900601.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Aurelia Nardanosa, an Armenian citizen, by her husband, Aurelius Manaia (a veteran), and her son, Aurelius Tata Pusintulus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900602.text_edition: Dis Manibus memoriae Aureliae Proculinae qua e vixit annos XVII Aurelia Ursula avia posuit
EDCS-09900602.translation: To the Divine Spirits. In memory of Aurelia Proculina, who lived 17 years. Aurelia Ursula, her grandmother, placed this.
EDCS-09900602.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Aurelia Ursula, the grandmother, in memory of her granddaughter, Aurelia Proculina, who died at the age of seventeen, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900603.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  Aurelia Serena suo coniugi Aristio Fortunato fecit memoriam qui fuit defunctus centurio nnorum quinquaginta ligionis sic II adiutricis
EDCS-09900603.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Serena made this memorial for her husband, Aristius Fortunatus, who died as a centurion at the age of fifty, of the Second Adiutrix Legion.
EDCS-09900603.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a memorial made by Aurelia Serena for her husband, Aristius Fortunatus, a deceased centurion of the Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-09900604.text_edition: Dis Manibus V  vixit nnos LIX et Aureliuss summus curator cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum filius patri piissimo posuit
EDCS-09900604.translation: To the Divine Spirits. He lived for 59 years. Aurelius, the chief curator of the Cohors Miliaria Hemesenorum, his son, placed this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-09900604.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius, chief curator of the Cohors Miliaria Hemesenorum, to his deceased father.

EDCS-09900605.text_edition: Aurelius qui vixit nnos  qui et A Marcus Aurelius
EDCS-09900605.translation: Aurelius, who lived for [---] years, also Marcus Aurelius.
EDCS-09900605.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning an Aurelius, also known as Marcus Aurelius, and his age.

EDCS-09900606.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to a Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-09900607.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely mentioning Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-09900608.text_edition: X et Marco  no et Marco Aurelio  Maxentinae fili-
EDCS-09900608.translation: [---] and for Marcus [---] and for Marcus Aurelius [---] of Maxentina, daughter.
EDCS-09900608.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or memorial, mentioning multiple individuals including Marcus and Maxentina's daughter.

EDCS-09900610.text_edition: Aurelius Abibelus ria filiae coniugi  piissimis faciendum curavit instante As
EDCS-09900610.translation: Aurelius Abibelus had this made for his daughter and most devoted wife, at the urging of As[---].
EDCS-09900610.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording Aurelius Abibelus having a monument made for his daughter and wife.

EDCS-09900611.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Alexandro veterano legionis II adiutricis qui vixit nnos LXX et Aureliae Rufinae eius quae vixit nnos LXI et Marco Aurelio Valeriano militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum qui vixit nnos XXV filio et Aureliae Valeri<a>nae quae vixit nnos XVIII filiae eorum Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus veteranus legionis supra scriptae ex stratore consularis cogCnatis#cognatis#COCNATIS dignissimis
EDCS-09900611.translation: For Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a veteran of the Legio II Adiutrix, who lived for 70 years; and for Aurelia Rufina, his wife, who lived for 61 years; and for Marcus Aurelius Valerianus, a soldier of the Cohors Miliaria Hemesenorum, who lived for 25 years, their son; and for Aurelia Valeriana, who lived for 18 years, their daughter. Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus, a veteran of the aforementioned legion, formerly a consular strator, [placed this] for his most worthy relatives.
EDCS-09900611.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus, a veteran, to multiple deceased family members: Marcus Aurelius Alexander (veteran), Aurelia Rufina (his wife), Marcus Aurelius Valerianus (their soldier son), and Aurelia Valeriana (their daughter).

EDCS-09900613.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Antonius veteranus ex beneficiario tribuni cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum posuit sibi vivus et Aureliae  coniugi vivae posuit et Aureliae Iuliae filiae=EIA#filiae#FILEIA carissimae quae vixit annos
EDCS-09900613.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Antonius, a veteran formerly a beneficiarius tribuni of the Cohors Miliaria Hemesenorum, placed this for himself while living, and for Aurelia [---], his wife, while living, and for Aurelia Iulia, his dearest daughter, who lived for [---] years.
EDCS-09900613.summary: This is a funerary inscription (or cenotaph) set up by Aurelius Antonius, a veteran, for himself and his wife while they were living, and for his deceased daughter, Aurelia Iulia.

EDCS-09900614.text_edition: Dis Manibus  memoriae Aureli Barbae veterani legionis II adiutricis et Aureliis Probiano militi liegionis supra scriptae Gaudentio et Firminiano qui vixit nnos VIII Aurelia Heraclia liberta ex testamento faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900614.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For the memory of Aurelius Barba, a veteran of the Legio II Adiutrix; and for Aurelius Probianus, a soldier of the aforementioned legion; Gaudentius and Firminianus, who lived for 8 years. Aurelia Heraclia, a freedwoman, had this made according to the testament.
EDCS-09900614.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Aurelia Heraclia, a freedwoman, according to a testament, for Aurelius Barba (veteran), Aurelius Probianus (soldier), and Gaudentius and Firminianus.

EDCS-09900616.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Cassiano filio vixit annis IIII Aurelius Silvanus beneficiarius tribuni pater bene merenti posuit
EDCS-09900616.publication: RIU-05, 01181 = RHP 00329 = Schober 00222 = CBI 00399 = Mander 00731 = BfIntercisa 00009 = AE 2013, +01269
EDCS-09900616.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Silvanus, a beneficiarius of the tribune, father, placed this for his son Aurelius Cassianus, who lived for 4 years, a well-deserving individual.
EDCS-09900616.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a father's dedication to his young son, Aurelius Cassianus, who died at the age of four; the father, Aurelius Silvanus, was a beneficiarius of a tribune.

EDCS-09900617.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marcus Aurelius Cerdon veteranus ex curatore cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum domo Arethusa sibi et Aureliae Iuliae suae et Marco Aurelio Silvano filio qui vixit annos XVI et Aureliae Heliodorae quae vixit nnos XXVII et Aureliae Immedaru quae vixit nnos XXIIII filiis et Marco Aurelio Vincentio qui vixit nnos VIII et Marco Aurelio Heliodoro nepotibus faciendum curavit monumentum memoriae
EDCS-09900617.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Aurelius Cerdon, a veteran formerly a curator of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, from Arethusa, took care to have this monument made in memory for himself and his Aurelia Iulia, and for his son Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, who lived 16 years, and for his daughters Aurelia Heliodora, who lived 27 years, and Aurelia Immedaru, who lived 24 years, and for his grandsons Marcus Aurelius Vincentius, who lived 8 years, and Marcus Aurelius Heliodorus.
EDCS-09900617.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Cerdon, a veteran from Arethusa, for himself, his wife, two sons, two daughters, and two grandsons, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-09900618.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Constituto militi legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis vixit annos XXIIII stipendiorum IIII filio cecidit in bello Germanico et Septimiae tillae matri vivae  et Aureliae Constitutae filiae vivae et Aureliae Pudentillae filiae vivae et Aurelio Cupito filio vivo et Aurelio Cupitiano filio vivo Aurelius catus veteranus ex ala I Thracum pater faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900618.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelius Constitutus, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, who lived 24 years and served 4 years, my son, who fell in the Germanic war, and for Septimia Tilla, his mother, alive, and for Aurelia Constituta, his sister, alive, and for Aurelia Pudentilla, his daughter, alive, and for Aurelius Cupitus, his son, alive, and for Aurelius Cupitianus, his son, alive. Aurelius Catus, a veteran from the First Thracian Wing, father, took care to have this made.
EDCS-09900618.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aurelius Catus, a veteran, for his son Aurelius Constitutus, a soldier who died in the Germanic war, and also commemorates his living wife and several children, suggesting a family tomb.

EDCS-09900619.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus veteranus  ex |(centurione) cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum annorum LXX vivus fecit sibi et Aureliis Heraclito et Sereno vivis filiis suis et Serenae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-09900619.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, a veteran, formerly a centurion of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, 70 years old, made this while living for himself and his living sons Aurelius Heraclitus and Aurelius Serenus, and for Serena. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-09900619.summary: This funerary monument was prepared by Marcus Aurelius Heraclitus, a 70-year-old veteran and former centurion, for himself and his living sons, explicitly stating it is not for an heir, indicating a specific family burial arrangement.

EDCS-09900621.translation: To the memory of Marcus Aurelius Lucifer.
EDCS-09900621.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the memory of Marcus Aurelius Lucifer.

EDCS-09900622.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Malcihianus veteranus legionis II adiutricis et Valeriae Verencundae coniugi quae vixit nnos XXXV et Marco Aurelio Marcellino militi legionis supra scriptae filio qui vixit nnos XVIIII et Valeriae Pulchr=RHae#Pul]chrae#CRHE coniugi et Valeriae Optatae filiae eorumanta fecit
EDCS-09900622.translation: Marcus Aurelius Malcihianus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, and for Valeria Verencunda, his wife, who lived 35 years, and for Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus, a soldier of the above-written legion, his son, who lived 19 years, and for Valeria Pulchra, his wife, and for Valeria Optata, their daughter. He made these.
EDCS-09900622.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marcus Aurelius Malcihianus, a veteran, for himself, his wife Valeria Verencunda, his son Marcus Aurelius Marcellinus, and also for his wife Valeria Pulchra and their daughter Valeria Optata, likely indicating multiple family burials.

EDCS-09900623.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Aurelio Marco militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum qui vixit annos XXX et Theodorae et Marcus Aurelius Marianus patri et sibi et matri karissimae vivus monumentum posuit
EDCS-09900623.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Marcus Aurelius Marcus, a soldier of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, who lived 30 years, and for Theodora. Marcus Aurelius Marianus, while living, placed this monument for his father, for himself, and for his dearest mother.
EDCS-09900623.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Marcus Aurelius Marianus while still alive, for his father Marcus Aurelius Marcus, his mother Theodora, and for himself, suggesting a family burial plot.

EDCS-09900624.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Maximiano militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum stipendiorum II qui vixit annos XVI menses VII et Aurelio Prisco fratri ipsius qui vixit annos XI Aurelius Bassus miles cohortis supra scriptae heres ex testamento eorum posuit
EDCS-09900624.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelius Maximianus, a soldier of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, with 2 years of service, who lived 16 years and 7 months, and for Aurelius Priscus, his brother, who lived 11 years. Aurelius Bassus, a soldier of the above-written cohort, placed this as their heir according to their will.
EDCS-09900624.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Aurelius Bassus, a soldier and heir, to his deceased comrades-in-arms and brothers, Aurelius Maximianus and Aurelius Priscus, who were also soldiers.

EDCS-09900625.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Monimo beneficiario tribuni cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum stipendiorum XXIIII vixit annos XLV G Bassus librarius bene merenti posuit ex ipsius praecepto
EDCS-09900625.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelius Monimus, a beneficiarius of the tribune of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, with 24 years of service, who lived 45 years. G. Bassus, a scribe, placed this for the well-deserving according to his instruction.
EDCS-09900625.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by G. Bassus, a scribe, for Aurelius Monimus, a beneficiarius of a tribune who served 24 years, at Monimus' own request.

EDCS-09900626.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Aurelio Monimo veterano ex decurione cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum domo Hemesa qui vixit nnos LXIIII coniugi Iulia Ticima et Aurelius Iulianus strator? consularis filius
EDCS-09900626.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Marcus Aurelius Monimus, a veteran formerly a decurion of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, from Hemesa, who lived 64 years. For his wife Iulia Ticima and for Aurelius Iulianus, a strator consularis, his son.
EDCS-09900626.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Marcus Aurelius Monimus, a 64-year-old veteran and former decurion from Hemesa, and also commemorating his wife Iulia Ticima and his son Aurelius Iulianus, a strator consularis, indicating a family burial.

EDCS-09900627.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Monimo veterano legionis II adiutricis Sabini quondam veterani domo Hemesa filio patri qui vixit nnos LV et Aureliae Alexandriae matri quae vixit nnos XXXV et Aureliae Athenu sorori quae vixit nnos XX Marcus Aurelius Alexander miles legionis librarius officialis praesidis
EDCS-09900627.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Monimus, a veteran of the Legion II Adiutrix, from Sabinus, formerly a veteran from Emesa, his son, and to his father who lived 55 years, and to Aurelia Alexandria, his mother who lived 35 years, and to Aurelia Athenu, his sister who lived 20 years. Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a soldier of the legion, clerk and official of the governor, [set this up].
EDCS-09900627.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a military clerk, to his father, Marcus Aurelius Monimus, a veteran, and to his mother and sister.

EDCS-09900628.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Primianus veteranus ex decurione cohortis |(milliariae) Hemesenorum nnorum L et Septimiae Gratae uxori pientissimae annorum XXXVI et Aureliae Ianuariae filiae annorum XX Marcus Aurelius Primianus sibi et suis vivos faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900628.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Primianus, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the cohors milliaria Hemesenorum, 50 years old, and to Septimia Grata, his most devoted wife, 36 years old, and to Aurelia Ianuaria, his daughter, 20 years old. Marcus Aurelius Primianus had this made for himself and his family while still alive.
EDCS-09900628.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Marcus Aurelius Primianus, a veteran decurion, for himself, his devoted wife, and his daughter, while he was still alive.

EDCS-09900631.summary: This appears to be a fragment of an inscription dedicated to Marcus Aurelius Romanus.

EDCS-09900632.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Aurelio Rufiniano qui vixit nnos X filio item Aureliae Rufinae filiae superstiti Marcus Aurelius Rufinus miles legionis I adiutricis et Ulpia Firmina coniux eius parentes sibi quoque posuerunt
EDCS-09900632.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus, who lived 10 years, his son; likewise to Aurelia Rufina, his surviving daughter. Marcus Aurelius Rufinus, a soldier of the Legion I Adiutrix, and Ulpia Firmina, his wife, his parents, also placed this for themselves.
EDCS-09900632.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Aurelius Rufinus, a soldier, and his wife Ulpia Firmina, for their deceased son Marcus Aurelius Rufinianus, and their surviving daughter Aurelia Rufina, also reserving space for themselves.

EDCS-09900633.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Salamati veterano ex dIucenario#ducenario#IUCEN civi Aedessitano sic qui vixit annis LXX in his militavit nnis XL Aurelius Pusintulus gener ex  faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900633.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Salamas, a veteran, formerly a ducenarius, a citizen of Edessa, who lived 70 years, of which he served 40 years. Aurelius Pusintulus, his son-in-law, had this made.
EDCS-09900633.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Pusintulus, a son-in-law, to Aurelius Salamas, a veteran ducenarius from Edessa who lived to a considerable age and served in the military for four decades.

EDCS-09900634.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Silvano veterano ex benficiario tribuni domo Hemesa vexit sic annis LX Aurelia Hilara coniuxs sic et Aurelii Salvianus et Silvanus etI#et#EI et Silvina filii carissimi et conheredes bene merenti et digno posuerunt
EDCS-09900634.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran, formerly a beneficiary of the tribune, from Emesa, who lived 60 years. Aurelia Hilara, his wife, and Aurelii Salvianus and Silvanus and Silvina, his dearest children and co-heirs, placed this for the well-deserving and worthy man.
EDCS-09900634.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelia Hilara, his wife, and their children, Aurelii Salvianus, Silvanus, and Silvina, to Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran and former beneficiary of a tribune from Emesa.

EDCS-09900635.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marcus Aurelius Silvanus benefE(iciarius)#benef(iciarius)#BE consularis legionis I adiutricis et Aurelia Firmina coniux eius vivi sibi posuerunt  VIVI
EDCS-09900635.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a beneficiary of the consular of Legion I Adiutrix, and Aurelia Firmina, his wife, placed this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-09900635.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a consular beneficiary of Legion I Adiutrix, and his wife Aurelia Firmina, for themselves while they were still alive.

EDCS-09900636.text_edition: Aurelius Monimus eques fratri carissimo memoriam posuit  Dis Manibus Aurelius Valens barbaricatus numeri eqquitum Scutariorum sub cura Marini praepositi
EDCS-09900636.translation: Aurelius Monimus, a knight, placed this memorial for his dearest brother. To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Valens, a 'barbaricatus' of the unit of Scutarian cavalry, under the command of Marinus, the praepositus.
EDCS-09900636.summary: This is a double funerary inscription; one part is a memorial by Aurelius Monimus for his brother, and the other honors Aurelius Valens, a cavalryman in the Scutarian unit.

EDCS-09900637.text_edition: Dis Manibus Axeto curator alae I Thracum hic situs est Ulpia Ingenua bene merenti et Ulpio Optato et Ulpiae Sensutae filiis vivis et sibi viva posuit curam agente Arruntio Postumo herede
EDCS-09900637.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Axetus, curator of the First Thracian Wing, is buried here. Ulpia Ingenua placed this for the well-deserving man, and for Ulpius Optatus and Ulpia Sensuta, her living children, and for herself while alive, with Arruntius Postumus, the heir, taking care of the work.
EDCS-09900637.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Ulpia Ingenua for her deceased husband, Axetus, a curator of a cavalry unit, and for her living children, Ulpius Optatus and Ulpia Sensuta, and for herself, with the heir Arruntius Postumus overseeing the construction.

EDCS-09900638.text_edition: Basia Arionis filia annorum L hic sita est Vercomberus  Tattunis filius
EDCS-09900638.translation: Basia, daughter of Arion, 50 years old, is buried here. Vercomberus, son of Tattunis, [placed this].
EDCS-09900638.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Basia, daughter of Arion, who died at 50 years old, set up by Vercomberus, son of Tattunis.

EDCS-09900639.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caeciliae IngenuaeV#Ingenuae#INGENUAV quae vixit annis XVI diebus XVII Iulia Fortunata mater
EDCS-09900639.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Caecilia Ingenua, who lived for 16 years and 17 days. Iulia Fortunata, her mother, [set this up].
EDCS-09900639.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl, Caecilia Ingenua, set up by her mother, Iulia Fortunata.

EDCS-09900640.text_edition: Caeserini filia rtus  GOI T et  Optato filio  fecit
EDCS-09900640.translation: Caeserinus made this for his daughter rtus GOI T and for his son Optatus.
EDCS-09900640.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Caeserinus for his daughter and son, Optatus, although parts of the text are unclear.

EDCS-09900641.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Calpurnius Dionysius annorum XLV hic situs est Calpurnia Procula fratri pientissimo et Caius Calpurnius Philetus posuerunt
EDCS-09900641.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Calpurnius Dionysius, 45 years old, is buried here. Calpurnia Procula and Gaius Calpurnius Philetus set this up for their most devoted brother.
EDCS-09900641.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Calpurnius Dionysius, erected by his sister Calpurnia Procula and Gaius Calpurnius Philetus.

EDCS-09900642.text_edition: CVI I ANIC Castor Publio Faladi amico posuit
EDCS-09900642.translation: C.V.I. I. Anicius Castor set this up for his friend Publius Faladi.
EDCS-09900642.summary: This inscription records a monument set up by Anicius Castor for his friend Publius Faladi.

EDCS-09900645.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius onis filius Pintamus |(sesquiplicarius) natione Zoela sic eques alae Asturum II nnorum LIII stipendiorum
EDCS-09900645.translation: Tiberius Claudius Pintamus, son of Onis, a sesquiplicarius of the Zoela tribe, an equestrian of the Second Asturian Cavalry Wing, 53 years old, with [number] years of service.
EDCS-09900645.summary: This inscription records the military service and identity of Tiberius Claudius Pintamus, a cavalryman from the Zoela tribe.

EDCS-09900646.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudius Antonius vixit annis V Antoninus Russus vexillarius filio merenti posuit
EDCS-09900646.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Antonius lived for 5 years. Antoninus Russus, a vexillarius, set this up for his deserving son.
EDCS-09900646.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius Antonius, a young boy, erected by his father Antoninus Russus, a standard-bearer.

EDCS-09900647.text_edition: Crispina annorum C Enigenus titulum posuit
EDCS-09900647.translation: Crispina, 100 years old. Enigenus set up this titulus.
EDCS-09900647.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument for Crispina, who lived to be 100 years old, set up by Enigenus.

EDCS-09900648.text_edition: Cusa Nertomari filius annorumX hic situs est Bucla Saturnini filia titulum posuit coniugi pientissimo et sibi viva  viv
EDCS-09900648.translation: Cusa, son of Nertomarus, 10 years old, is buried here. Bucla, daughter of Saturninus, set up this monument for her most devoted spouse and for herself while living.
EDCS-09900648.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cusa, a ten-year-old boy, erected by his wife Bucla, who also prepared the monument for herself in advance.

EDCS-09900649.text_edition: Cusides Disalae filius eques alae Frontonianae hic situs est Bitugentus heres titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-09900649.translation: Cusides, son of Disala, a cavalryman of the Frontonian Wing, is buried here. Bitugentus, his heir, set up this monument in his memory.
EDCS-09900649.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cusides, a cavalryman, erected by his heir Bitugentus.

EDCS-09900650.text_edition: Danuius Diassumari filius annorum LXXX hic situs est et Concordiae Flori filiae coniugi eius annorum LXXX vivae titulum memoriae posuit Maturus filius eorum parentibus pientissimis
EDCS-09900650.translation: Danuius, son of Diassumarus, 80 years old, is buried here, and for Concordia, daughter of Florus, his wife, 80 years old, while living, Maturus their son set up this monument in memory for his most devoted parents.
EDCS-09900650.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Danuius and his wife Concordia, both 80 years old, set up by their son Maturus for his devoted parents, with Concordia still living at the time of dedication.

EDCS-09900653.text_edition: Osa Demunici liberta  Dis Manibus  Demiuncus Couci filius annorum C et Angulata Campionis filia annorum C coniunx titulum posuerunt Batalus et Louco liberti faciendum curaverunt et Veneriae sorori vivae
EDCS-09900653.translation: Osa, freedwoman of Demunicus, for the Spirits of the Departed. Batalus and Louco, freedmen, ensured the erection of this monument for Demiuncus, son of Coucus, who lived 100 years, and Angulata, daughter of Campio, his wife, who lived 100 years. And for their sister Veneria, while she was still alive.
EDCS-09900653.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by freedmen for their deceased patrons, Demiuncus and Angulata, and for their sister, Veneria, while she was still alive.

EDCS-09900654.text_edition: Deomarus DurRati filius hic situs est
EDCS-09900654.translation: Deomarus, son of DurRatus, is buried here.
EDCS-09900654.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Deomarus, son of DurRatus.

EDCS-09900655.text_edition: Derva Sisinae filiae annorum XX hic sita est Ceresces coniugi
EDCS-09900655.translation: Derva, daughter of Sisina, who lived 20 years, is buried here. Ceresces [set this up] for his wife.
EDCS-09900655.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Derva, daughter of Sisina, set up by her husband Ceresces.

EDCS-09900656.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Domitio Longino veterano cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum et Domitiae Avitae parentibus posuerunt Domitias sic Revocata et Amata filias sic vivae instantibus PAurelio Firmo veterano et Aurelio Aunio genAeris#generis#GEAERIS
EDCS-09900656.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Domitius Longinus, veteran of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, and Domitia Avita, their parents. Domitias Revocata and Amata, their daughters, set this up while still alive, at the insistence of Publius Aurelius Firmus, a veteran, and Aurelius Aunius, their sons-in-law.
EDCS-09900656.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Domitius Longinus, a veteran, and his wife Domitia Avita, by their daughters, Domitias Revocata and Amata, at the urging of their sons-in-law, Publius Aurelius Firmus and Aurelius Aunius.

EDCS-09900657.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Domitius Super miles legionis II adiutricis qui vixit nnos XXXII menses VI et defunctus bello Germanico et Aurelius Iulius qui vixit nnos XXVI menses V et Revocata mater eorum qui sic vixit annos L Concordius lib(ertus) eorum posuit
EDCS-09900657.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Domitius Super, soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, who lived 32 years and 6 months and died in the Germanic War, and Aurelius Iulius, who lived 26 years and 5 months, and Revocata, their mother, who lived 50 years. Concordius, their freedman, set this up.
EDCS-09900657.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Concordius, a freedman, to his patrons Marcus Domitius Super, a soldier who died in the Germanic War, Aurelius Iulius, and their mother Revocata.

EDCS-09900658.text_edition: qui vixit annos  et  Donatiano N qui vixit nnos XVII et  Marinae quae vixit annos et Aureliae  quae vixit nnos X  Marinia et  Donatus  Dubitatus posuerunt
EDCS-09900658.translation: Who lived [---] years, and for Donatianus N., who lived 17 years, and for Marina, who lived [---] years, and for Aurelia, who lived 10 years. Marinia and Donatus Dubitatus set this up.
EDCS-09900658.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly for multiple family members including Donatianus and Aurelia, erected by Marinia and Donatus Dubitatus.

EDCS-09900660.text_edition: Tito Flavio Ato veterano ex summo curatore cohortis III Batavorum vixit nnos L Titus Flavius Atticinus |(centurio) voluntariorum
EDCS-09900660.translation: For Titus Flavius Atus, veteran and former chief curator of the Third Cohort of Batavians, who lived 50 years. Titus Flavius Atticinus, centurion of the volunteers [set this up].
EDCS-09900660.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Titus Flavius Atus, a veteran and former curator, by Titus Flavius Atticinus, a centurion.

EDCS-09900661.text_edition: Marco Herennio Pudenti veterano legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis nnorum LXXVIII domo Claudia Apamie sic collegium cultorum posuit
EDCS-09900661.translation: For Marcus Herennius Pudens, veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, pious and loyal, 78 years old, from the Claudia tribe, from Apamea. The collegium of cultores set this up.
EDCS-09900661.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Herennius Pudens, a veteran from Apamea, by a collegium of cultores (worshippers or members of an association).

EDCS-09900662.text_edition: Iantumalius Blatonis filius eques alae I Ituraeorum annorum XXX stipendiorumCVZO
EDCS-09900662.translation: Iantumalius, son of Blato, a horseman of the First Ala of Ituraeans, 30 years old, with [---] years of service.
EDCS-09900662.summary: This is a military epitaph for Iantumalius, a cavalryman of the First Ituraean Wing, noting his age and years of service.

EDCS-09900664.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Proculi filia annorum LX et Bietumara Bucconis filia annorum XXV hic sitae sunt Bucco DeivisiEs#Deivisis#DEIVISES sibi vivos sic coniugi et filiae titulum faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900664.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia, daughter of Proculus, 60 years old, and Bietumara, daughter of Bucco, 25 years old, are buried here. Bucco, son of Deivisis, while still alive, ensured the erection of this monument for himself, his wife, and his daughter.
EDCS-09900664.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia and Bietumara, set up by Bucco, son of Deivisis, for himself, his wife (Iulia), and his daughter (Bietumara) while he was still alive.

EDCS-09900665.text_edition: Iuliae Silvinae matri dulcissimae quae vixit nnos LXVI et Aelio Valentiano veterano cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum ex duplicario vivo patri dulcissimo Aelius Vitalis beneficiarius consularis faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900665.translation: To Iulia Silvina, the sweetest mother, who lived 66 years, and to Aelius Valentianus, veteran of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, a former duplicarius, the sweetest father, while still living. Aelius Vitalis, beneficiarius consularis, saw to the erection of this monument.
EDCS-09900665.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Iulia Silvina and Aelius Valentianus, erected by their son Aelius Vitalis, a beneficiarius consularis, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900666.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lentinus Prudentis eques cohortis III Batavorum annorum XLV stipendiorum XX Germanus vexillarius
EDCS-09900666.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lentinus, son of Prudens, a German horseman of the 3rd Cohort of Batavians, aged 45, with 20 years of service, a vexillarius.
EDCS-09900666.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lentinus, a German cavalryman and standard-bearer (vexillarius) of the 3rd Batavian Cohort, probably from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900667.text_edition: Au eques alae annorum X con eius a Lupo  Bas
EDCS-09900667.translation: Au, horseman of the ala, aged 10, his comrade from Lupus Bas...
EDCS-09900667.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a 10-year-old cavalryman, possibly erected by a comrade, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09900668.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Magnus, an Eraviscan, aged 40, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09900670.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marino Silvani militi cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum annorum L<II=TC>#L<II>#LTC stipendiorum XXVIII Marcus Aurelius miles cohortis suprascriptae heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-09900670.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marinus Silvani, soldier of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, aged 52, with 28 years of service. Marcus Aurelius, a soldier of the aforementioned cohort, his heir, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-09900670.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marinus Silvani, a soldier of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, erected by his heir, Marcus Aurelius, a fellow soldier, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900671.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Mario Mariniano qui vixit nnos VI menses VII Marcus Aurelius Marius Marinus decurio municipii Volg et Annia Marina parentes filio dulcissimo  Thalassius  Encolpio
EDCS-09900671.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Marius Marinianus, who lived 6 years and 7 months. Marcus Aurelius Marius Marinus, decurion of the municipality of Volg, and Annia Marina, his parents, placed this for their sweetest son. Thalassius Encolpio [also present].
EDCS-09900671.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Marcus Marius Marinianus, erected by his parents, Marcus Aurelius Marius Marinus (a decurion) and Annia Marina, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09900673.text_edition: Matutina Flaviani filia annorum XX hic sita est Flavianus Sabini filiae pientissimae et Comatimarae Colvedionis coniugi et Flavianae Flaviani filiae et Marcelliano nepoti et sibi vivus vivis posuit
EDCS-09900673.translation: Matutina, daughter of Flavianus, aged 20, is laid here. Flavianus, son of Sabinus, placed this while living for his most dutiful daughter, and for Comatimara, daughter of Colvedio, his wife, and for Flaviana, daughter of Flavianus, and for Marcellianus, his grandson, and for himself, while living, for the living.
EDCS-09900673.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription erected by Flavianus Sabinus for himself and multiple family members, including his daughter Matutina, his wife Comatimara, his other daughter Flaviana, and his grandson Marcellianus, indicating a family plot from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09900674.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marcus C Messius annorum LX hic situs est Ulpia Amma coniux pientissima C Iustus filius parentibus faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900674.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus C. Messius, aged 60, is laid here. Ulpia Amma, his most dutiful wife. C. Iustus, his son, saw to the erection for his parents.
EDCS-09900674.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus C. Messius, erected by his son C. Iustus for his parents, Marcus and his wife Ulpia Amma, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09900675.text_edition: Mulsus Aivisae filius annorum XX hic est situs eqC(ues)#eq(ues)#EC occisus in civitate LitugCenus#Litugenus#LITUCENUS Aivisae filius annorum XVIII hic est situs milites alae primae Frontonianae in civitate Heraviscorum sic defuncti frater Bellicus titulum posuit memoriae
EDCS-09900675.translation: Mulsus, son of Aivisa, aged 20, is laid here, a horseman killed in the civitas Litugenus. Aivisa, son of Aivisa, aged 18, is laid here. Soldiers of the First Frontonian Ala thus died in the civitas Heraviscorum. Their brother Bellicus placed this monument in memory.
EDCS-09900675.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Bellicus for his brothers, Mulsus and Aivisa, both horsemen of the First Frontonian Ala, who died in different Roman settlements (civitas Litugenus and civitas Heraviscorum), likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900676.text_edition: Dis Manibus Olumnius Valens veteranus cohortis I Antoninae |(miliariae) Hemesenorum   cohortis primae Novae |(miliariae) Severianae Surorum sagittariorum et Gaius Maximus veteranus secundus heres bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-09900676.translation: To the Divine Manes. Olumnius Valens, veteran of the First Antonine milliary cohort of the Hemeseni and of the First New Severian milliary cohort of Syrian archers, and Gaius Maximus, second veteran and heir, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-09900676.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Olumnius Valens, a veteran who served in two different milliary cohorts, erected by Gaius Maximus, another veteran and his heir, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09900677.text_edition: c hic situs est Optatus heres filius et Germaniu coniux titulum memoriae posuerunt
EDCS-09900677.translation: Here lies Optatus. His son and heir and his wife, Germanus, placed this memorial for him.
EDCS-09900677.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Optatus, whose memorial was set up by his son and heir, and his wife, Germanus.

EDCS-09900678.text_edition: Otiouna vixit annos XII et Regilia annorum IIII hic sitae sunt Ianuarius Otiouna parvis sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-09900678.translation: Otiouna lived for 12 years and Regilia for 4 years. They lie here. Ianuarius and Otiouna placed this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-09900678.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Otiouna and Regilia, placed by Ianuarius and Otiouna for themselves while still living, suggesting it was a family plot or monument.

EDCS-09900681.text_edition: Respectus Gellini filius annorum XIIX hic situs estI#est#ESI Cania Carsulia mater filio posuit
EDCS-09900681.translation: Respectus, son of Gellini, 18 years old, lies here. Cania Carsulia, his mother, placed this for her son.
EDCS-09900681.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Respectus, aged 18, placed by his mother, Cania Carsulia.

EDCS-09900683.text_edition: Senio Comatonis filius natione Eraviscus aH(nnorum)#a(nnorum)#H XXX in civitate Eraviscorum in Aquinco et Comatuia mater eius sibi et filio viva titulum posuit
EDCS-09900683.translation: Senio, son of Comato, of the Eraviscan nation, 30 years old, in the city of the Eravisci in Aquincum, lies here. And Comatuia, his mother, placed this memorial for herself and her son while alive.
EDCS-09900683.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Senio, an Eraviscan from Aquincum, placed by his mother Comatuia for both her son and herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-09900684.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Septimiae Constantinae coniugi quae vixit nnos XXXII et Aureliae Florentinae filiae quae vixit nnum I Marcus Aurelius Bassus faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900684.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Septimia Constantina, his wife, who lived for 32 years, and for Aurelia Florentina, his daughter, who lived for 1 year, Marcus Aurelius Bassus saw to its making.
EDCS-09900684.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of Septimia Constantina (wife) and Aurelia Florentina (daughter), commissioned by Marcus Aurelius Bassus.

EDCS-09900687.text_edition: ntino  filiae quae vixit annos VIII pientissim faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900687.translation: [For -----]ntina, his daughter, who lived for 8 years. He arranged for it to be made for the most dutiful.
EDCS-09900687.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a daughter named [-]ntina, who lived 8 years, commissioned by an unnamed individual for the most dutiful.

EDCS-09900688.text_edition: Veriuga Danui filia annorum XXX hic sita est Florus Egretari filius coniugi piae titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-09900688.translation: Veriuga, daughter of Danus, 30 years old, lies here. Florus, son of Egretarus, placed this memorial for his devoted wife.
EDCS-09900688.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Veriuga, aged 30, placed by her husband Florus.

EDCS-09900689.text_edition: Ulpio Basso filio qui vixit nnos X et Ulpiae Tert libertae vivae sibi coniux viva Titia Paulina filio et marito bene merentibus faciendum curavit
EDCS-09900689.translation: For Ulpius Bassus, his son, who lived for 10 years, and for Ulpia Tert[ia], his freedwoman, still living. His wife, Titia Paulina, still living, arranged for this to be made for her son and deserving husband.
EDCS-09900689.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Bassus (son) and Ulpia Tertia (freedwoman), commissioned by Titia Paulina for her deceased son and deserving husband, implying Ulpius Bassus was both her son and husband.

EDCS-09900690.text_edition: Ulpius Enubico sesquiplicarius alae I Flaviae Britannicae nnorum XXXV hic situs est Ressatus et Succo fratres et heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-09900690.translation: Ulpius Enubico, a sesquiplicarius of the First Flavian British Wing, 35 years old, lies here. Ressatus and Succo, his brothers and heirs, had this made.
EDCS-09900690.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Enubico, a Roman cavalryman, placed by his brothers and heirs Ressatus and Succo.

EDCS-09900691.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Iuliano veterano ex |(centurione) vixit annos LXIIII
EDCS-09900691.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a veteran from the rank of centurion, who lived for 64 years.
EDCS-09900691.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of Marcus Ulpius Iulianus, a veteran who served as a centurion.

EDCS-09900694.text_edition: s annorum XXV frater posuit memoriae
EDCS-09900694.translation: His brother placed this in memory of S., 25 years old.
EDCS-09900694.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a male named S., set up by his brother, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09900695.text_edition: e annorum XII hic sitae s posuit i fili- coniux  posuerunt  curaverunt
EDCS-09900695.translation: E., 12 years old, lies here. His wife placed this for her son and her husband. They placed it and took care of it.
EDCS-09900695.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 12-year-old named E., dedicated by his wife for their son and her husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000341.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  dis deabusque pro se et suis Caius Antonius Vitalis |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito  Torquato et Iuliano consulibus
EDCS-10000341.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the gods and goddesses, for himself and his family, Gaius Antonius Vitalis, centurion of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Torquatus and Julianus.
EDCS-10000341.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other deities, dedicated by Gaius Antonius Vitalis, a centurion, for himself and his family, dating to 291 CE.

EDCS-10000342.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Ingennuo sic claviculario ex officio praesides decidit in bello Aelia Procella coniugi karissimo memoriam posuit Septimio Decorato claviculario ex officio praesides decidit in beIllo#bello#BILO Aelia Basilissa coniugi karissimo memoriam posuit
EDCS-10000342.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aelius Ingenuus, clavicularius from the office of the governors, who fell in battle. Aelia Procella placed this memorial for her dearest husband. To Septimius Decoratus, clavicularius from the office of the governors, who fell in battle. Aelia Basilissa placed this memorial for her dearest husband.
EDCS-10000342.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating two clavicularii (officials) named Aelius Ingenuus and Septimius Decoratus, both of whom fell in battle, with separate memorials dedicated by their respective wives, Aelia Procella and Aelia Basilissa, likely from the late Imperial period.

EDCS-10000343.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gratius Artilleus et Cludius sic Glamosus ||(centuriones) legionis VIII Augustae bello Serdicensi desideratis schola ||(centurionum) legionis supra scriptae collegis bene merentibus de se p(osuit)
EDCS-10000343.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gratius Artilleus and Claudius Glamosus, centurions of the Eighth Augustan Legion, desired in the Serdican war, the college of centurions of the aforesaid legion placed this for their well-deserving colleagues.
EDCS-10000343.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the college of centurions of the Eighth Augustan Legion to commemorate two of their colleagues, Gratius Artilleus and Claudius Glamosus, who died in the Serdican War, likely from the 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-10000344.text_edition: Dis Manibus Restitutius Silvanus |(centurio) legionis I Menerviae sic Aurelius Martius heres et Aurelia Bona secundus heres coniugi bene merenti faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-10000344.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Restitutius Silvanus, centurion of the First Minerva Legion. Aurelius Martius, his heir, and Aurelia Bona, his second heir, had this monument made for their well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-10000344.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Restitutius Silvanus, a centurion, commissioned by his heirs, Aurelius Martius and Aurelia Bona, for their well-deserving spouse, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000345.text_edition: Sammonius Samminus imaginifer legionis VIII Augustae fecit Verina coniux
EDCS-10000345.translation: Sammonius Samminus, imaginer of the Eighth Augustan Legion, made this. Verina, his wife, [made this].
EDCS-10000345.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription made by Sammonius Samminus, an imaginer, with his wife Verina also involved, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000346.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a woman whose name ends in -ratilla Papia Serata, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000347.text_edition: IVCI parentes et Petronius Septumianus sic
EDCS-10000347.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning parents and Petronius Septumianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000349.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Iulius Celer scriba classis votum solvit libens laetus merito Pisone consule
EDCS-10000349.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, Julius Celer, scribe of the fleet, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Piso.
EDCS-10000349.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, dedicated by Julius Celer, a fleet scribe, in the consulship of Piso, likely from the Imperial period (e.g., 27 CE or later).

EDCS-10000350.text_edition: Dibus maioribus sacrum Aurelius Valentinus pro se et suis monitus somnio votum libens posuit
EDCS-10000350.translation: Sacred to the greater gods. Aurelius Valentinus willingly placed this vow for himself and his family, having been warned in a dream.
EDCS-10000350.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Valentinus to the greater gods, recording a vow made due to a dream.

EDCS-10000351.text_edition: Titus Flavius Proculus princeps praefectus Scordiscorum annorum XXXXI hic situs est Titus Flavius Dulcis pater filio suo pientissimo fecit
EDCS-10000351.translation: Titus Flavius Proculus, princeps praefectus of the Scordisci, aged 41, is buried here. Titus Flavius Dulcis, his father, made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-10000351.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Titus Flavius Proculus, a Roman official, erected by his father Titus Flavius Dulcis.

EDCS-10000359.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Antonius Barbilus negotiator votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000359.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Antonius Barbilus, a merchant, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000359.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mithras by Antonius Barbilus, a merchant, to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-10000362.text_edition: Urbana Aterigis liberta nnorum L et Gaetulus Urbanae filius Aterigis libertus nnorum XVI hic siti sunt Arcto heres posuit fratres et sorores fratri pientissimo
EDCS-10000362.translation: Urbana, freedwoman of Aterigis, aged 50, and Gaetulus, son of Urbana, freedman of Aterigis, aged 16, are buried here. Arcto, as heir, placed this. Brothers and sisters (placed this) for their most dutiful brother.
