EDCS-13700284.translation: To Nemesis Regina Augusta, Aurelius Vindicianus, cornicularius of the prefect, and Marcus Aurelius Vindex Iunior, his son.
EDCS-13700284.summary: This dedication records an offering to Nemesis Regina Augusta by Aurelius Vindicianus, a cornicularius (aide) to a prefect, and his son Marcus Aurelius Vindex Iunior.

EDCS-13700286.text_edition: Telesphoro sacrum Titus Flavius Priscus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae ex optione valetudinarii dono dedit
EDCS-13700286.translation: Sacred to Telesphorus. Titus Flavius Priscus, a veteran of the Fourth Flavian Legion, from the option of the infirmary, gave this gift.
EDCS-13700286.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Telesphorus by Titus Flavius Priscus, a veteran of Legio IIII Flavia, who made this gift by choice from the infirmary.

EDCS-13900031.text_edition: Aelio Licinio Iu Singiduno decurioni canabarum  qui vixit annis LXX sibi et Aeliae Iuliae filiae et Aelio filio Lupo qui vixit annis
EDCS-13900031.translation: To Aelius Licinius Iu[---] of Singidunum, decurion of the canabae, who lived for 70 years. For himself and Aelia Iulia, his daughter, and Aelius Lupus, his son, who lived for [---] years.
EDCS-13900031.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aelius Licinius, a decurion of the canabae of Singidunum, who lived for 70 years, and his children Aelia Iulia and Aelius Lupus.

EDCS-13900032.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus consul proconsul tribunicia potestate Opellius Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar vias et pontes vetustate corruptas restituerunt cura agente Aelio Tricciano legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum XLV
EDCS-13900032.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Augustus, Consul, Proconsul, holding tribunician power, and Opellius Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, restored roads and bridges ruined by age, with the care of Aelius Triccianus, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power. 45 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-13900032.summary: This milestone records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperor Macrinus and his son Diadumenianus, overseen by Aelius Triccianus, located 45 miles from Aquincum, dating to 217-218 AD.

EDCS-13900033.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Gallo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II patri patriae et Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Afinio Gallo Velldumniano Volusiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto tribunicia poestate II legio II adiutrix ter pia ter fidelis constans Galliana Volusiana curante Cosinio Felice legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum
EDCS-13900033.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Gallus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, father of his country, and to the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Veldumnianus Volusianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, the Second Legion Adiutrix, thrice pious, thrice faithful, constant, Galliana, Volusiana, with Cosinius Felix, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, in charge. [---] miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-13900033.summary: This milestone records the dedication by Legio II Adiutrix to Emperors Gallus and Volusianus, under the supervision of legate Cosinius Felix, dating to 251-253 AD, indicating road construction or repair from Aquincum.

EDCS-13900034.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae pro salute et incolumitate civitatis Eraviscorum Titus Aelius Valens et Publius Aelius Dubitatus IIviri municipii Aquinci
EDCS-13900034.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, for the health and safety of the community of the Eravisci, Titus Aelius Valens and Publius Aelius Dubitatus, duoviri of the municipium of Aquincum.
EDCS-13900034.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina for the well-being of the Eraviscan community, made by Titus Aelius Valens and Publius Aelius Dubitatus, both duoviri of the municipium of Aquincum.

EDCS-14800177.text_edition: M  Minervae Publius P Certus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14800177.translation: M. To Minerva. Publius P. Certus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14800177.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Publius P. Certus's fulfillment of a vow to Minerva.

EDCS-14800184.summary: This brief inscription records the name and rank of Lupicinus, a tribune, likely indicating ownership or an official's presence.

EDCS-15000151.inscription_type: carmina, tituli fabricationis, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-15000151.text_edition: Memoriae Quinto Aelio Apolloni militi cohortis |(miliariae) novae Surorum stipendiorum III vixit nnos XX Aelia Marcia mater filio dulcissimo et Aelia Apollonia soror eius faciendum curaverunt lubrica quassa levis fragilis bona vel mala fallax vita data est homini non certo limite cretae per varios casus tenuato stamine pendes vivito mortalis dumdum dant tibi tempora Parcae seu te rura tenent urbes seu castra vel aequor flores ama Veneris Cereris bona munera carpe et Nysyi larga et pinguia dona Minervae candida vita cole iustissima mente serenus iam puer et iuvenis iam vir et fessus ab annis talis eris tumulo superumque oblitus honores
EDCS-15000151.translation: To the memory of Quintus Aelius Apollonius, a soldier of the new milliary cohort of Syrians, who served for three years and lived for 20 years. Aelia Marcia, his mother, and Aelia Apollonia, his sister, took care of erecting this for their dearest son. Life, slippery, broken, light, fragile, good or bad, deceptive, is given to man not by a certain boundary marked; through various misfortunes you hang by a thin thread. Live, mortal, as long as the Fates grant you time, whether fields hold you, or cities, or camps, or the sea. Love the flowers of Venus, pluck the good gifts of Ceres, and the ample, rich gifts of Nysus and Minerva. Cultivate a bright life with a most just and serene mind. Now a boy, and now a youth, now a man, and weary from years, such will you be in the tomb, oblivious to the honors of those above.
EDCS-15000151.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelia Marcia and Aelia Apollonia for Quintus Aelius Apollonius, a Syrian soldier who died at the age of 20 after three years of service, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15200180.text_edition: Herculi Illyrico Caius Oppius Ingenuus tribunus militum legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae
EDCS-15200180.translation: To Hercules Illyricus, Caius Oppius Ingenuus, military tribune of the Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana.
EDCS-15200180.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Illyricus by Caius Oppius Ingenuus, a military tribune, likely from the early 3rd century AD during the Severan dynasty.

EDCS-15300221.text_edition: Soli Invicto  Mithrae Lucius Septimius Victor armorum custos legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis ex viso posuit Praesente et Extricato consulibus
EDCS-15300221.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Lucius Septimius Victor, armorer of the Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, placed this as a result of a vision, in the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.
EDCS-15300221.summary: This is a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Lucius Septimius Victor, an armorer, made in fulfillment of a vision, dated to 217 AD by the named consuls.

EDCS-15500115.text_edition: Gaius Iulius Viatorinus decurio coloniae Aquincensium aedilicius praefectus collegii centonariorum hydram collegio supra scripto de suo dono dedit Modesto et Probo consulibus
EDCS-15500115.translation: Gaius Iulius Viatorinus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, aedile, prefect of the guild of felt-makers, gave this hydra as a gift to the aforementioned guild from his own funds, in the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-15500115.summary: This inscription records a donation of a hydra to the guild of felt-makers by Gaius Iulius Viatorinus, a decurion and former aedile, dated to 228 AD by the named consuls.

EDCS-15500116.summary: This is a simple building inscription recording that Hilarus successfully constructed a gate, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15700084.text_edition: Valentianus signifer legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae domo Mursa donum dedit Imperatore Caesare Antonino Augusto [III] et Comaszonte II consulibus
EDCS-15700084.translation: Valentianus, standard-bearer of the Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Antoniniana, from Mursa, gave this gift in the consulship of Emperor Caesar Antoninus Augustus III and Comazon II.
EDCS-15700084.summary: This is a dedication by Valentianus, a standard-bearer from Mursa, made in the consulship of Antoninus Augustus III and Comazon II, which dates to 220 AD.

EDCS-15800369.text_edition: Luci-- Vepintania Luci  libert-- Sergia Aquinco annorum LXX hic situs est collegiato Salutis Augustae Agrippinenses Transalpini posuer(unt)
EDCS-15800369.translation: Lucius Vepintania, freedman of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, from Aquincum, aged 70, lies here. The members of the College of Salus Augusta Agrippinenses Transalpini erected this.
EDCS-15800369.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Vepintania, a 70-year-old freedman from Aquincum, erected by his funerary college, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15900106.text_edition: Flavus Blandi filius domo Batavus sic eques alae Frontonianae
EDCS-15900106.translation: Flavus, son of Blandus, a Batavian by origin, cavalryman of the Frontonian ala, lies here.
EDCS-15900106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavus, a Batavian cavalryman of the Frontonian ala, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15900107.text_edition: Deae Vagdaevercusti Marcus Simplicius Quietus tribunus cohortis III Batavorum |(miliariae) equitatae Antoninianae
EDCS-15900107.translation: To the goddess Vagdavercustis, Marcus Simplicius Quietus, tribune of the III Batavian milliary mounted cohort Antoniniana.
EDCS-15900107.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Vagdavercustis by Marcus Simplicius Quietus, a military tribune, likely from the early 3rd century AD during the Antonine period.

EDCS-15900420.text_edition: Aesculapio Tiberius Martius Castrensis medicus legionis II adiutricis sub Quinto Fuficio Cornuto consule designato
EDCS-15900420.translation: To Aesculapius, Tiberius Martius Castrensis, doctor of the Legion II Adiutrix, under Quintus Fuficius Cornutus, consul designate.
EDCS-15900420.summary: This is a dedication to Aesculapius by Tiberius Martius Castrensis, a legionary doctor, made during the consulship of Quintus Fuficius Cornutus, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15900421.text_edition: C S   Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aurelius Atta votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900421.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, sacred. Aurelius Atta willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900421.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aurelius Atta to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-15900423.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Silvanus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900423.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Silvanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900423.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Silvanus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-15900424.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Augustis sacrum Titus Venusius Titi filius Menenia Aper Praeneste optio valetudinarii votum solvit laetus libens merito  V Kalendas Octobres posuit
EDCS-15900424.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia Augusti, sacred. Titus Venusius Aper, son of Titus, of the Menenian tribe, from Praeneste, an 'optio' of the infirmary, joyfully, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow. He set this up on the fifth day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-15900424.summary: This inscription, dated to the fifth day before the Kalends of October, is a dedicatory monument by Titus Venusius Aper, an 'optio' of an infirmary, to Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-15900425.text_edition: Asculepio sic et Hygiae Iulius Iulianus votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-15900425.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia. Julius Julianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900425.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Julius Julianus to Aesculapius and Hygia.

EDCS-15900427.text_edition: Genio |(centuriae) Luci Aebuti Certi primi pili Titus Flavius Flavianus optio eeius sic
EDCS-15900427.translation: To the Genius of the century of Lucius Aebutius Certus, the first centurion (primus pilus). Titus Flavius Flavianus, his 'optio', [set this up].
EDCS-15900427.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Titus Flavius Flavianus, an 'optio', to the Genius of the century of Lucius Aebutius Certus, a primus pilus.

EDCS-15900428.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Quintus Aelius Quintianus fratres milites legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-15900428.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aelius Quintus and Aelius Quintianus, brothers, soldiers of the Second Adiutrix legion, Pious and Faithful, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15900428.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by two brothers, Aelius Quintus and Aelius Quintianus, soldiers of the Legio II Adiutrix, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-15900429.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Publius Aelius Tertius cornicen legionis II adiutricis ex voto posuit pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito Imperatore Maximino et Africano consulibus
EDCS-15900429.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius Tertius, a cornicen of the Second Adiutrix legion, set this up in accordance with a vow for his own health and that of his family. He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow during the consulship of Emperor Maximinus and Africanus.
EDCS-15900429.summary: This inscription, dated to the consulship of Emperor Maximinus and Africanus (236 AD), is a dedicatory monument by Publius Aelius Tertius, a cornicen of the Legio II Adiutrix, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-15900431.text_edition: Deae Dianae  Licinius GCaudentius#Gaudentius#CAUDENTIUS Papia legionis II adiutricis ex voto posuit
EDCS-15900431.translation: To the goddess Diana. Licinius Gaudentius, of the Papia tribe, of the Second Adiutrix legion, set this up in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-15900431.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Licinius Gaudentius, a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix, to the goddess Diana in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15900432.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo equites singulares Hateri Saturnini legati Auggustorum pro praetore
EDCS-15900432.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The imperial horse guards of Haterius Saturninus, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, [set this up].
EDCS-15900432.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the imperial horse guards (equites singulares) serving under Haterius Saturninus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-15900434.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Libero Patri  Aelius Anntianus pro salute suae sic et vexillariorum collegii centonariorum Ulpius Victorinus Aurelius Antoninus Dubius Florentinus Vegetius Septiminus  VIBVL ISTATA PHELVM  votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-15900434.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Liber Pater. Aelius Antianus, for his own health, and the vexillarii of the guild of felt-makers: Ulpius Victorinus, Aurelius Antoninus, Dubius Florentinus, Vegetius Septiminus, Vibulus, Istata, Phelvus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15900434.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Liber Pater by Aelius Antianus for his health, along with several vexillarii of a guild of felt-makers.

EDCS-15900435.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Iulius Iulianus veteranus ex evokato sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900435.translation: To Father Liber and Libera, Julius Julianus, a veteran formerly called up for special service, thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900435.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Julius Julianus, a veteran, to the gods Liber Pater and Libera, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15900438.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae  Caelius Anicetus cum filio suo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900438.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Caelius Anicetus with his son willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15900438.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Caelius Anicetus and his son to the god Mithras, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15900441.text_edition: cius decurio municipii Aquincensium IIvir iure dicundo  praefectus collegii fabrum votum solvit
EDCS-15900441.translation: [---]cius, decurion of the municipality of Aquincum, duumvir with judicial power, prefect of the college of craftsmen, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900441.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an unnamed public official from Aquincum, who served as a decurion, duumvir, and prefect of craftsmen, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15900442.text_edition: Neptuno sac(rum) Marcus O P MV AI O
EDCS-15900442.translation: Sacred to Neptune. Marcus O. P. M. V. A. I. O.
EDCS-15900442.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Neptune by Marcus, likely in fulfillment of a vow or as an offering, with the meaning of the abbreviated text remaining unclear.

EDCS-15900444.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Polyidus Aureli Aniceti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900444.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, Polyidus, slave of Aurelius Anicetus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900444.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Polyidus, a slave, to Silvanus Domesticus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15900446.translation: To Silvanus, Flavius Abascantus placed his vow.
EDCS-15900446.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Flavius Abascantus to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-15900447.text_edition: Silvano Magno  Claudius Maximinus pro salute Claudi Probini fili quod voverat votum solvit libens merito III Idus Iunias Perpetuo et Corneliano consulibus
EDCS-15900447.translation: To Silvanus Magnus, Claudius Maximinus, for the health of his son Claudius Probinus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled the vow he had made, on the third day before the Ides of June, during the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelianus.
EDCS-15900447.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Claudius Maximinus to Silvanus Magnus for his son's health, dated to June 11, 237 AD.

EDCS-15900448.text_edition: Silvanis sacrum Appius Rosionis votum libens merito
EDCS-15900448.translation: Sacred to the Silvani, Appius Rosionis willingly and deservedly [fulfilled] his vow.
EDCS-15900448.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Silvani by Appius Rosionis, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15900449.text_edition: Soli Deo sacrum Caius Iulius Primus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900449.translation: Sacred to the Sun God, Gaius Julius Primus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900449.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Julius Primus to the Sun God, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15900450.text_edition: Soli Deo Callistus ex voto votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900450.translation: To the Sun God, Callistus, in accordance with a vow, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900450.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Sun God by Callistus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15900451.text_edition: Sulevis sacrum Ulpia Ilurica votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15900451.translation: Sacred to the Suleviae. Ulpia Ilurica willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-15900451.summary: This is a dedication to the Suleviae, likely a local or Celtic deity, made by Ulpia Ilurica in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15900452.text_edition: et L pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Augusto III et Cassio Dione consulibus
EDCS-15900452.translation: And L. for his own and his family's well-being, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the third consulship of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus and Cassius Dio.
EDCS-15900452.summary: This inscription records an offering made by an individual, L., for the health of himself and his family, dated to the year 229 CE during the consulship of Severus Alexander and Cassius Dio.

EDCS-15900453.text_edition: eques ala Hispanorum primae Lugius nnorum XL stipendiorum XXII hic situs est Tranco Iorae libertus et Marcus Apisius heredes posuerunt
EDCS-15900453.translation: Lugius, a cavalryman of the First Spanish Wing, aged 40, with 22 years of service, is buried here. Tranco, freedman of Ioras, and Marcus Apisius, his heirs, set this up.
EDCS-15900453.summary: This is a tombstone for Lugius, a cavalryman of the First Spanish Wing, erected by his freedman Tranco and Marcus Apisius.

EDCS-15900454.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Publi Favoriano collegium centonariorum
EDCS-15900454.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Publius Aelius Publius Favorianus, the guild of blanket-makers (centonarii).
EDCS-15900454.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Publius Favorianus by the collegium centonariorum, a guild of textile workers.

EDCS-15900455.translation: To the undefeated, fortunate, triumphant Constantine.
EDCS-15900455.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to the Emperor Constantine, celebrating his victories.

EDCS-16000052.text_edition: Titus Plotius Pamphilus Celeia miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Nimphidiani defunctus Alaxandria sic stipendiorum XV natione Noricus posuit heres Magnius Atticus
EDCS-16000052.translation: Titus Plotius Pamphilus Celeia, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Loyal, from the century of Nimphidianus, died in Alexandria. Thus, with 15 years of service, of Norican origin. His heir, Magnius Atticus, set this up.
EDCS-16000052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Plotius Pamphilus Celeia, a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix, who died in Alexandria, set up by his heir Magnius Atticus.

EDCS-16100290.translation: To the auspicious Concord of the Augusti. Quintus M. Verus [---]
EDCS-16100290.summary: This is a dedication to the Concord of the Augusti, likely an imperial cult, made by Quintus M. Verus.

EDCS-16100291.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Seutes domo Bessus sic annorum LXXX Marcus Ulpius Philumenus heres ex testamento faciendum curavit ad hoc sepulcrum collegium fabrum contulit |(denarios)
EDCS-16100291.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Valerius Seutes, from Bessa, aged 80. Marcus Ulpius Philumenus, his heir, arranged for this to be made according to his will. For this tomb, the guild of craftsmen contributed denarii.
EDCS-16100291.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Seutes, set up by his heir Marcus Ulpius Philumenus, with a contribution from the guild of craftsmen for the tomb.

EDCS-16400366.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae Publius Aelius Atta actuarius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16400366.translation: To the undefeated Mithras. Publius Aelius Atta, actuary of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Loyal, Antoniniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16400366.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Mithras, made by Publius Aelius Atta, an actuary of the Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-16400368.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aeliae Secundinae filiae Aelii Secundini veterani ex beneficiario consularis quae vixit annis XXIIII diebus XXX et II Aelius Caesianus beneficiarius consularis coniugi dulcissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-16400368.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aelia Secundina, daughter of Aelius Secundinus, a veteran who had been a beneficiarius consularis, who lived 24 years, 32 days. Aelius Caesianus, a beneficiarius consularis, arranged for this to be made for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-16400368.summary: This is a funerary monument for Aelia Secundina, set up by her husband, Aelius Caesianus, a beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-16400370.text_edition: Iovi Dulcheno sic Publius Aelius Lucilius |(centurio) cohortis I Alpinorum peditatae
EDCS-16400370.translation: To Jupiter Dolichenus, Publius Aelius Lucilius, centurion of the First Cohort of Alpinorum infantry, (dedicated this).
EDCS-16400370.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by a centurion of an auxiliary infantry cohort, likely on an altar or votive tablet.

EDCS-16400404.text_edition: Deo Optimo Maximo  Aelius Dasius veteranus votum libens  solvit
EDCS-16400404.translation: To the Best and Greatest God, Aelius Dasius, a veteran, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16400404.summary: This inscription records a veteran's fulfillment of a vow to the supreme deity, likely on a votive offering.

EDCS-16700131.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Septimi Severi Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Britannici maximi filI(io)#fil(io)#FII divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio
EDCS-16700131.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, son of the deified Septimius Severus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva.
EDCS-16700131.summary: This lengthy inscription is a monumental dedication to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius), detailing his imperial titles and prestigious ancestry, dating to his reign (early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-16800457.text_edition: Iustinianus sesquiplicarius ex voto posuit Liibero lL(aetus)#l(aetus)#L libens merito
EDCS-16800457.translation: Justinianus, a sesquiplicarius, placed this out of a vow to Liber, happily, willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-16800457.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Liber by a soldier of the rank sesquiplicarius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-17300082.text_edition: Dianae sacrum Caius Pompeius Crispinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17300082.translation: Sacred to Diana. Gaius Pompeius Crispinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17300082.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the goddess Diana by Gaius Pompeius Crispinus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-17300083.text_edition: Apollini conservatori Caius Calvisius Faustinianus tribunus militum legionis IIII Flaviae votum sovit libens merito
EDCS-17300083.translation: To Apollo the preserver. Gaius Calvisius Faustinianus, military tribune of the Fourth Flavian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17300083.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Apollo Conservator by a military tribune of Legio IIII Flavia, Gaius Calvisius Faustinianus.

EDCS-17300084.text_edition: Dianae conservatrici Caius Calvisius Faustinianus tribunus militum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17300084.translation: To Diana the preserver. Gaius Calvisius Faustinianus, military tribune, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17300084.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Diana Conservatrix by the military tribune Gaius Calvisius Faustinianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-17300087.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iul=AEio#Iulio#IAEO Verec=AIISOundo#Verecundo#VAIISOUND cG(ustodi)#c(ustodi)#G armorum legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum vixit annis I
EDCS-17300087.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Iulius Verecundus, armorer of the Second Adiutrix Legion, who lived for one year of service.
EDCS-17300087.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Gaius Iulius Verecundus, an armorer of Legio II Adiutrix, who died after only one year of military service.

EDCS-17300088.summary: This inscription is a simple mention of the name Septimius Potentianus, likely part of a larger, fragmented monument or a brief identification.

EDCS-17300089.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelio Sabiniano beneficiario consularis legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XXIIII vixit annos XLV Aelio Sabino patri eius et Aeliae Sabinillae filiae eius Aelius Donatus libertus patronis dignissimis faciendum curavit
EDCS-17300089.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aelius Sabinianus, beneficiarius consularis of the Second Adiutrix Legion, who served for 24 years and lived for 45 years. Aelius Donatus, his freedman, took care to have this made for his most worthy patrons, Aelius Sabinus, his father, and Aelia Sabinilla, his daughter.
EDCS-17300089.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Aelius Donatus, a freedman, to his patron Aelius Sabinianus, a veteran beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Adiutrix, and also commemorates Sabinianus' father, Aelius Sabinus, and daughter, Aelia Sabinilla.

EDCS-17300090.text_edition: Titus Mercasius Hermes sibi vivos sic et coniugi fecit
EDCS-17300090.translation: Titus Mercasius Hermes made this for himself while living, and likewise for his wife.
EDCS-17300090.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Titus Mercasius Hermes prepared a tomb for himself and his wife while they were still alive.

EDCS-17300093.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Reditus beneficiarius legati legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae votum solvit libens merito suscepta fide ex orco
EDCS-17300093.translation: Marcus Aurelius Reditus, *beneficiarius* of the legate of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow made after being spared from death.
EDCS-17300093.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from a Roman soldier, Marcus Aurelius Reditus, fulfilling a vow to a deity, likely after surviving a dangerous situation, possibly in the early 3rd century AD given the legionary title Severiana.

EDCS-17300094.summary: This is a short inscription, possibly funerary, identifying Basia, daughter of Avvus, and indicating her profession or role as a 'vallista' (possibly related to fortifications or a specific trade).

EDCS-17300097.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Lulo libens laetus merito A
EDCS-17300097.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lulo willingly and joyfully, deservedly [made this].
EDCS-17300097.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Lulo to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, expressing gratitude or fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-17300099.text_edition: Comatimara Quutai filia nnorum XV posuit titulum memoriae pater
EDCS-17300099.translation: Comatimara, daughter of Quutai, aged 15 years, her father placed this monument to her memory.
EDCS-17300099.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a father for his daughter, Comatimara, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-17300100.text_edition: Dis Manibus  LIucio#Lucio#IUCIO Septimio LIupiano#Lupiano#IUPIANO annorum sic I et Lucio Septimio Caesiano annorum sic I faciendum curavit Septimius CeleFr#Celer#CELFR sexvirum coloniae Aquincensium Aurelia Caensa parentes filiis caris fI(ecerunt)#f(ecerunt)#I Praesenti sic et Extricado sic consulibus
EDCS-17300100.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Septimius Celer, a *sexvir* of the colony of Aquincum, and Aurelia Caensa, the parents, saw to the erection of this for their dear sons, Lucius Septimius Lupianus, aged [1] year, and Lucius Septimius Caesianus, aged [1] year. Made during the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.
EDCS-17300100.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the early 3rd century AD (217 AD based on the consuls) erected by Septimius Celer, a local magistrate from Aquincum, and Aurelia Caensa for their two infant sons.

EDCS-17300101.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelii Marinianus et
EDCS-17300101.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aelii Marinianus and [---]
EDCS-17300101.summary: This is an incomplete dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Aelius Marinianus and at least one other person.

EDCS-17300102.text_edition: Nymphis perennibus Marcus Valerius Karus decurio coloniae Aquincensium IIvir votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17300102.translation: To the Eternal Nymphs. Marcus Valerius Karus, *decurio* of the colony of Aquincum, *duumvir*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-17300102.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Marcus Valerius Karus, a local magistrate from Aquincum, to the Nymphs, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-17300105.text_edition: Longinianus miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis et Iulia Spes coniux aram posuerunt pro pietate sua et suorum suo arbitratu fecit
EDCS-17300105.translation: Longinianus, a soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, and Iulia Spes, his wife, erected an altar out of their piety for themselves and their family, made according to their own judgment.
EDCS-17300105.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by a Roman soldier, Longinianus, and his wife Iulia Spes, who erected an altar out of devotion for themselves and their family.

EDCS-17300106.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  voverunt in espeditione sic Germica sic Septimius Quintianus et Aurelius GCentilis#Gentilis#CENTILIS  votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-17300106.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Septimius Quintianus and Aurelius Gentilis vowed during the Germanic expedition; they willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-17300106.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Septimius Quintianus and Aurelius Gentilis, who fulfilled a vow made during a Germanic military expedition, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-17300107.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelia Castorina quaeI#quae#QUI vixsit sic annis XLII mensibus tribus diebus III Iulius Iuventianus
EDCS-17300107.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelia Castorina, who lived for 42 years, 3 months, and 3 days. Julius Iuventianus (set this up).
EDCS-17300107.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Julius Iuventianus for Aurelia Castorina, who died at the age of 42.

EDCS-17300110.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus Marcus Aurelius Fronto milites legionis II adiutricis pro salute sua et suorum templum constituerunt
EDCS-17300110.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras. Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus and Marcus Aurelius Fronto, soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, established this temple for their own well-being and that of their families.
EDCS-17300110.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to Mithras by two soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion for their health and safety.

EDCS-17300111.translation: To Silvanus Anticessor. Lucius Claudius (set this up).
EDCS-17300111.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus Anticessor by Lucius Claudius.

EDCS-17300112.text_edition: Soli Socio Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus et Marcus Aurelius Fronto milites legionis II adiutricis fratres templum constituerunt Antonino IIII consule
EDCS-17300112.translation: To Sol Socius. Marcus Aurelius Frontinianus and Marcus Aurelius Fronto, brothers and soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, established this temple in the consulship of Antoninus for the fourth time.
EDCS-17300112.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to Sol Socius by two brothers, soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Legion, during the fourth consulship of Antoninus, which dates it to 213 AD.

EDCS-17300114.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum et Cai Iuli Castini legati Auggustorum pro praetore equites singulares curam agente Aurelio Victorino |(centurione) legionis II adiutricis administrante Aurelio Bito decurione Aelius Florianus Aurelius Maturus subcuratores ex voto
EDCS-17300114.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our Lords the Augusti and of Gaius Iulius Castinus, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power. The imperial horse guards (equites singulares) had this done, with Aurelius Victorinus, centurion of the Second Adiutrix Legion, acting as curator, and Aurelius Bitus, decurion, as administrator. Aelius Florianus and Aurelius Maturus, sub-curators, (did this) in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-17300114.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the imperial horse guards for the health of the emperors and their legate, supervised by military officers, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-17500070.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Respectus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis missus honesta missione et Aureliae Ianuariae coniugi eius et Aureliis Respectiano et Supero filiis et Aureliae Superae filiae eius vivi sibi posuerunt et Septimio Ianuario quondam patri et Septimiae Quintae quondam matri et Septimio Martino quondam qui militavit in legione II supra scripta pia fideli annIum#annum#ANIU I et Septimio Dubitato quondam qui militavit in legione II supra scripta pia fideli annos III et Septimio Quintiano
EDCS-17500070.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Respectus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, honorably discharged, and Aurelia Ianuaria, his wife, and their sons Aurelius Respectianus and Aurelius Superus, and their daughter Aurelia Supera, set this up for themselves during their lifetime. Also for Septimius Ianuarius, formerly his father, and Septimia Quinta, formerly his mother, and Septimius Martinus, formerly, who served in the aforesaid Second Pious and Faithful Legion for one year, and Septimius Dubitatus, formerly, who served in the aforesaid Second Pious and Faithful Legion for three years, and Septimius Quintianus.
EDCS-17500070.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by the veteran Aurelius Respectus and his wife for themselves and their children during their lifetime, also commemorating his deceased parents and two brothers who served in the Second Adiutrix Legion.

EDCS-17500071.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Ulpiae Valentinae quae vixit annos XVIII dies Ulpius Valentinus pater filiae carissimae
EDCS-17500071.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Ulpia Valentina, who lived 18 years and ... days. Ulpius Valentinus, her father, (set this up) for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-17500071.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Ulpius Valentinus for his beloved daughter, Ulpia Valentina, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-17500073.text_edition: Atressa Vindonis filia nnorum XXX et Proculus Busionis nnorum III hic siti pater Busio titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-17500073.translation: Atressa, daughter of Vindo, 30 years old, and Proculus, son of Busio, 3 years old, are buried here. Father Busio erected this monument to their memory.
EDCS-17500073.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atressa and Proculus, set up by Busio, who is identified as Proculus' father.

EDCS-17500074.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Granio Dato veterano legionis II adiutricis domo Africa Sufetula Granii Martinus et Felix liberti vivo patrono optimo pecunia sua titulum faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-17500074.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Marcus Granius Datus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, from Sufetula in Africa. Granius Martinus and Granius Felix, his freedmen, took care to have this monument made with their own money for their excellent patron during his lifetime.
EDCS-17500074.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Granius Datus, a veteran from Africa, set up by his freedmen during his lifetime.

EDCS-17500075.text_edition: Verondagus Vervici filius annorum cuadraginta sic et Verbacius Verondagi filius annorum trium
EDCS-17500075.translation: Verondagus, son of Vervicus, 40 years old, and Verbacius, son of Verondagus, 3 years old.
EDCS-17500075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Verondagus and his son Verbacius, indicating their ages at death.

EDCS-17500076.text_edition: Viator annorum Vibius annorum Silanus annorum II Severus nnni I Paulini principis fili hic siti sunt pater titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-17500076.translation: Viator, two years old, Vibius, two years old, Silanus, two years old, and Severus, one year old, sons of Princeps Paulinus, are buried here. Their father placed this monument in their memory.
EDCS-17500076.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of four young children and erected by their father.

EDCS-17500077.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Baebius Draco signifer legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17500077.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Juno. Baebius Draco, standard-bearer of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17500077.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno by a standard-bearer of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-17500078.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Marcus Ulpius Quadratus decurio municipii Aquinci quinquennalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17500078.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Marcus Ulpius Quadratus, decurion of the municipium of Aquincum, quinquennalis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17500078.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater by Marcus Ulpius Quadratus, a decurion and quinquennalis of Aquincum.

EDCS-17500079.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Luci P(ubli) Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti Pii consulis II patris patriae et Marci Aureli Antonini Caesaris Tiberius Claudius Claudianus legatus Augusti pro praetore praesidium vetustate collapsum mutato loco manu militum restitui iussit
EDCS-17500079.translation: For the health of Imperator Caesar Lucius Publius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius, consul for the second time, father of his country, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, Augustan legate with pro-praetorian power, ordered a stronghold, collapsed by age, to be restored by the hands of soldiers in a changed location.
EDCS-17500079.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a military stronghold by Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, an imperial legate, on behalf of Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), dating it to the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-17500080.text_edition: Deo  Soli Invicto Mithrae Septimius Messorinus |(centurio) ordinarius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17500080.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras. Septimius Messorinus, centurion ordinarius of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17500080.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Sol Invictus Mithras by a centurion of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-17500081.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Ulpiae Antonillae quondam Ulpi Candidiani |(centurionis) filiae Titus Aelius Verinus decurio coloniae Aquincensium flamem sic dumviralis sic sacerdos Urbis Romae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit annis XXIIII
EDCS-17500081.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Ulpia Antonilla, formerly daughter of Ulpius Candidianus, centurion. Titus Aelius Verinus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, flamen, duumviral, priest of the City of Rome, [set this up] for his most devoted wife, who lived 24 years.
EDCS-17500081.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Titus Aelius Verinus, a decurion and priest, for his deceased wife, Ulpia Antonilla, who died at age 24.

EDCS-17500082.text_edition: Libero Patri  et Liberae pro salute Claudi MarceIlli#Marcelli#MARCIL et Iuli Marcelli votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17500082.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, for the health of Claudius Marcellus and Iulius Marcellus, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17500082.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater and Libera for the well-being of Claudius Marcellus and Iulius Marcellus.

EDCS-17500083.text_edition: Dis Inferis Manibus Aurelio Vero memoria sic posuit coniux Audentia egOerunt#egerunt#EOERUNT coniugCes#coniuges#COIUCES annos I vixsit sic annos XXXV
EDCS-17500083.translation: To the Infernal Gods, the Manes of Aurelius Verus. His wife Audentia placed this monument. They lived together for one year. He lived 35 years.
EDCS-17500083.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Verus, erected by his wife Audentia, noting their brief marriage and his age at death.

EDCS-17500084.text_edition: Caius Iulius MagCimarus#Magimarus#MACIMARUS annorum XXXX Klaudia Namusonis filia Ressona annorum LIII#LII#III hic siti sunt Caius Iulius MagCio#Magio#MACIO et Caius Iulius Comatumarus filii titulum memoriae posuerunt
EDCS-17500084.translation: Gaius Iulius Magimarus, 40 years old, [and] Claudia, daughter of Namuso, Ressona, 52 years old, are buried here. Gaius Iulius Magio and Gaius Iulius Comatumarus, their sons, placed this monument in their memory.
EDCS-17500084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Magimarus and Claudia Ressona, erected by their sons Gaius Iulius Magio and Gaius Iulius Comatumarus.

EDCS-17500085.text_edition: AnnTamato#Annamato#ANTAMATO SeiamiT#Seiami#SEIAMT filio annorum trigintha sic
EDCS-17500085.translation: To Annamato, son of Seiami, 30 years old.
EDCS-17500085.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Annamato, son of Seiami, who died at age 30.

EDCS-17500086.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimiae Valentinae quondam filiae Septimiae Probes quaee vixit nnos fecerunt Septimii  et
EDCS-17500086.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Septimia Valentina, formerly the daughter of Septimia Probes, who lived for [---] years. The Septimii and [---] made this.
EDCS-17500086.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Septimia Valentina, dedicated by her parents, the Septimii, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500087.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Titus Caecilius Magnio ex voto solvit libens merito
EDCS-17500087.translation: To Silvanus Silvester, Titus Caecilius Magnio willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17500087.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Silvanus Silvester by Titus Caecilius Magnio, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-17500088.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Rituris Veladati filius nnorum LX hic situs est Vercombera Scupi filia uxor? an(norum) L hic sita est Ved fili-- posuit
EDCS-17500088.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Rituris, son of Veladatus, aged 60 years, is buried here. Vercombera, daughter of Scupus, his wife, aged 50 years, is buried here. Ved[---], her son, placed this.
EDCS-17500088.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Rituris, son of Veladatus, and his wife Vercombera, daughter of Scupus, placed by their son Ved[---], likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500089.text_edition: Flavius Iovinus ex praeposito militum Histricorum et Flavius Paulus biarcus pater et filius domum a fundamentis iusserunt fabricari
EDCS-17500089.translation: Flavius Iovinus, formerly a commander of Histrian soldiers, and Flavius Paulus, biarchus, father and son, ordered this house to be built from its foundations.
EDCS-17500089.summary: This inscription records the construction of a house from its foundations, commissioned by Flavius Iovinus and his son Flavius Paulus, who held military and administrative titles.

EDCS-17500091.text_edition: Annamatus Tripponis filius nnorum LXXX hic situs est et Basiae Bageetonis filia coniugi vivae fili
EDCS-17500091.translation: Annamatus, son of Trippo, aged 80 years, is buried here. And Basia, daughter of Bageeto, placed this for her living husband and her son.
EDCS-17500091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Annamatus, son of Trippo, with his wife Basia, daughter of Bageeto, dedicating it for her husband and son while still alive, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500092.text_edition: Atressus Avavilli filius nnorum LXXX hic situs est Ulpia Coimona patri pientissimo
EDCS-17500092.translation: Atressus, son of Avavillus, aged 80 years, is buried here. Ulpia Coimona [placed this] for her most devoted father.
EDCS-17500092.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atressus, son of Avavillus, dedicated by his daughter Ulpia Coimona, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500093.text_edition: Prae=ENsens#Praesens#PRENSENS Annamati filius nnorum VII hic situs est pater filio titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-17500093.translation: Praesens, son of Annamatus, aged 7 years, is buried here. His father placed this title of memory for his son.
EDCS-17500093.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Praesens, son of Annamatus, who died at age seven, erected by his grieving father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500094.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Provincialis annorum L hic situs est Ulpia Mattuia coniugi pientissimo et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-17500094.translation: Marcus Ulpius Provincialis, aged 50 years, is buried here. Ulpia Mattuia placed this for her most devoted husband and for herself while living.
EDCS-17500094.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Provincialis, dedicated by his wife Ulpia Mattuia, who also prepared the monument for herself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500095.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Memoriae quondam Publi Aeli Firmini qui vixit nnos VIII mensibus VII diebus XV Publius Aelius Firminianus veteranus legionis II adiutricis pater et Aelia Domitina mater filio dulcissimo faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-17500095.translation: To the Divine Spirits and the memory of Publius Aelius Firminus, who lived 8 years, 7 months, and 15 days. Publius Aelius Firminianus, veteran of the II Adiutrix legion, his father, and Aelia Domitina, his mother, arranged for this to be made for their sweetest son.
EDCS-17500095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Publius Aelius Firminus, dedicated by his father, a veteran of the II Adiutrix legion, and his mother, Aelia Domitina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17500096.text_edition: Publius Aelius MaExim=LLIus#Maximus#MEXLLIUS decurio municipii  Publius Aelius
EDCS-17500096.translation: Publius Aelius Maximus, decurion of the municipality of [---]. Publius Aelius [---].
EDCS-17500096.summary: This is a fragmented inscription for Publius Aelius Maximus, a decurion of a municipality, and another Publius Aelius, likely indicating a public office or family monument.

EDCS-17500097.summary: This inscription is a simple personal identification, likely part of a larger monument, identifying Flavianus by his patronymic.

EDCS-17500100.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publio Aelio FErontoni#Frontoni#ERONTONI decurioni coloniae Aquincensium flaminico qui vixit annis LIII et Publio Aelio Vindici filio qui vixit annis I eIt#et#IT Aeliae Victorinae filiae quae vixit nnis VIII et Publio Aelio Inpetrato filio qui vixit nnis VI cui vita parva mors valde citata fuit quem flentes doleunt miserique parentes Septimia Decorata coniugi pientissimo et filiis communibus et Aelii Inpetratus et Decoratus
EDCS-17500100.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Publius Aelius Frontonius, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, flamen, who lived for 53 years, and for Publius Aelius Vindex, his son, who lived for 1 year, and for Aelia Victorina, his daughter, who lived for 8 years, and for Publius Aelius Impetratus, his son, who lived for 6 years, for whom life was short and death very swift, whom their grieving and sorrowful parents mourn. Septimia Decorata for her most devoted husband and their common children, and Aelius Impetratus and Decoratus.
EDCS-17500100.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Septimia Decorata and Aelius Impetratus and Decoratus to commemorate Publius Aelius Frontonius, a decurion and flamen of Aquincum, and his three young children, dating likely to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-17500101.text_edition: Meitima Casamonis filia annorum XV titulum posuit pater filiae pientissimae
EDCS-17500101.translation: Meitima, daughter of Casamo, 15 years old. Her father placed this memorial for his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-17500101.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by a father for his deceased 15-year-old daughter, Meitima, daughter of Casamo, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17500102.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Septimio Constantino qui vixit annos XVI Septimiae Constantinae quae vixit annos XXXVI matri eiius Septimius Vibius avunculus et heres faciendum curavit ex testamento volete o anpli sic sorori bsene merenti posuit  opto sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-17500102.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Septimius Constantinus, who lived 16 years. For Septimia Constantina, his mother, who lived 36 years. Septimius Vibius, their uncle and heir, arranged for this to be made according to their will, and to his sister who deserved well, he placed this. I wish for you that the earth may be light upon you.
EDCS-17500102.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Septimius Vibius, as uncle and heir, for his deceased nephew, Septimius Constantinus (16 years old), and his sister, Septimia Constantina (36 years old), presumably in the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-17600045.text_edition: Ursulus  Dis Manibus Aelio Sereno militi cohortis I Ulpiae Pannoniorum stipendiorum VII vixit nnos XXVIII et Aelio Sereniano militi cohortis II Alpinorum stipendiorum X vixit nnos XXV et Aeliae Concordiae vixit nnos XXI
EDCS-17600045.translation: Ursulus. To the Divine Manes. For Aelius Serenus, soldier of the First Ulpian Cohort of Pannonians, with 7 years of service, who lived 28 years, and for Aelius Serenianus, soldier of the Second Alpine Cohort, with 10 years of service, who lived 25 years, and for Aelia Concordia, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-17600045.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to three individuals – two soldiers, Aelius Serenus and Aelius Serenianus, serving in different auxiliary cohorts, and Aelia Concordia – likely erected by a relative or fellow soldier, Ursulus, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-17600046.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cocceius Senecio decurio alae I Thracum ob natalem Marci Coccei Senecae fili votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600046.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Cocceius Senecio, decurion of the First Thracian Ala, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow on the birthday of his son, Marcus Cocceius Seneca.
EDCS-17600046.summary: This is a votive altar or tablet dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Cocceius Senecio, a decurion of an auxiliary cavalry unit, to commemorate his son's birthday, likely dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-17600047.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Optimus veteranus legionis II adiutricis    votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600047.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Ulpius Optimus, a veteran of the Second Adjutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600047.summary: This is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Ulpius Optimus, a veteran of Legio II Adiutrix, probably in gratitude for a wish fulfilled or for general well-being, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600050.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Ave Basse Caio Licinio Basso annorum XXI mensium II dierum IIII Caius Licinius Mansuetus
EDCS-17600050.translation: To the Divine Manes. Farewell, Bassus! To Gaius Licinius Bassus, 21 years, 2 months, 4 days old. Gaius Licinius Mansuetus.
EDCS-17600050.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Gaius Licinius Mansuetus for Gaius Licinius Bassus, a young man who died at the age of 21, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600051.text_edition: Iulia Matrona Acuilaes sic filia annorum hic sita est
EDCS-17600051.translation: Iulia Matrona, daughter of Aquila, 15 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-17600051.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Iulia Matrona, the 15-year-old daughter of Aquila, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600052.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Vibius Respectus miles cohortis IT#I#T Alpinorum annorum XXV Oxetia VerviciL#Vervici#VERVICL mater annorum LX
EDCS-17600052.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibius Respectus, soldier of the First Alpine Cohort, 25 years old. Oxetia Vervici, his mother, 60 years old.
EDCS-17600052.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Vibius Respectus, a 25-year-old soldier of Cohors I Alpinorum, and his 60-year-old mother, Oxetia Vervici, likely erected by a relative or comrade, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-17600053.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Iulia Ingenua ex voto posuit libens merito
EDCS-17600053.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Iulia Ingenua placed this willingly, deservedly, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-17600053.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a votive offering by Iulia Ingenua to the Roman deities Liber Pater and Libera.

EDCS-17600054.text_edition: Aveta Adnamati filia annorum LI hic sita est Marcus Ulpius Cassius matri titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-17600054.translation: Aveta, daughter of Adnamatus, aged 51, lies here. Marcus Ulpius Cassius placed this memorial title for his mother.
EDCS-17600054.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aveta, erected by her son Marcus Ulpius Cassius to honor his deceased mother.

EDCS-17600055.text_edition: Dianae sacrum Marcus Aurelius Constantinus veteranus ex praetorio votum solvit libens merito PRETO  AurA(elius)#Aur(elius)#A ConstantinMus#Constantinus#CONSTANTIM impensaE#impensa#IMPEE sua posuit
EDCS-17600055.translation: Sacred to Diana. Marcus Aurelius Constantinus, a veteran from the Praetorian Guard, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. Marcus Aurelius Constantinus placed this at his own expense.
EDCS-17600055.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the goddess Diana by Marcus Aurelius Constantinus, a veteran, made in fulfillment of a vow and at his own cost.

EDCS-17600057.text_edition: Te amo XXX  Adnama Asionis filia annorum XXXV hic sita est titulum memoriae posuit Magimarus coiunx sic  VV R
EDCS-17600057.translation: I love you. Adnama, daughter of Asio, aged 35, lies here. Magimarus, her husband, placed this memorial title. So be it. Live, live, live.
EDCS-17600057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Adnama, erected by her husband Magimarus, expressing love and remembrance.

EDCS-17600058.text_edition: Atuquus Ausquuti filius annorum XX Capacita Ausquuti filia annorum XVI titulus positus natis caris obitis
EDCS-17600058.translation: Atuquus, son of Ausquutus, aged 20. Capacita, daughter of Ausquutus, aged 16. This title was placed for dear children who have died.
EDCS-17600058.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for two children, Atuquus and Capacita, presumably siblings and children of Ausquutus, commemorating their untimely deaths.

EDCS-17600059.text_edition: Blatuna Vagaimoni filia annorum LXV hic sita est titulum memoriae posuit Tosuta
EDCS-17600059.translation: Blatuna, daughter of Vagaimo, aged 65, lies here. Tosuta placed this memorial title.
EDCS-17600059.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Blatuna, erected by Tosuta to commemorate her memory.

EDCS-17600060.text_edition: Domitinus Raudomaei filius nnorum XIV hic situs est Raudomaeus pater titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-17600060.translation: Domitinus, son of Raudomaeus, aged 14, lies here. Raudomaeus, his father, placed this memorial title.
EDCS-17600060.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitinus, erected by his father Raudomaeus in memory of his deceased son.

EDCS-17600061.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Trovcetimarus Angulati annorum L Ricmara Atali viva sibi et coniugi Domitius filius parentibus titulum posuerunt
EDCS-17600061.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Trovcetimarus, son of Angulatus, aged 50. Ricmara, daughter of Atali, alive, for herself and her husband. Domitius, their son, placed this title for his parents.
EDCS-17600061.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, for Trovcetimarus and his living wife Ricmara, placed by their son Domitius, suggesting a joint tomb prepared while Ricmara was still alive.

EDCS-17600062.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Vibiano Deivonis annorum LXX patri et Atezissae annorum XL matri et Valentino annorum XXX fratri Vibius Saturnus duplarius alae I Thracum bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-17600062.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vibius Saturnus, a duplicarius of the First Thracian Wing, placed this for his well-deserving father Vibianus, son of Deivo, aged 70; and for his mother Atezissa, aged 40; and for his brother Valentinus, aged 30.
EDCS-17600062.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Vibius Saturnus, a soldier, in honor of his deceased father, mother, and brother.

EDCS-17600063.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marciae Ulpiae Marci filiae Respectae quae vixit an(nis) L in matrimonio pia vixit annis  mensibus  diebus VI item pia vixit in matrimonio anteriori sic nnis V mense I diebus III et Marco Ulpio Iustino iuniori filio eius vivo Lucius Septimius Optatus  coniugi rarissimae posuit
EDCS-17600063.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcia Ulpia Respecta, daughter of Marcus, who lived 50 years; she lived dutifully in marriage for [unknown] years, [unknown] months, and 6 days. Likewise, she lived dutifully in her previous marriage for 5 years, 1 month, and 3 days. And to Marcus Ulpius Iustinus the Younger, her son, who is still alive. Lucius Septimius Optatus placed this for his most rare wife.
EDCS-17600063.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Septimius Optatus for his exceptionally devoted wife Marcia Ulpia Respecta, and also mentioning their living son, Marcus Ulpius Iustinus the Younger, with details about her marital fidelity across two marriages.

EDCS-17600065.text_edition: Putiolano Aurelius Valentianus miles? legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600065.translation: To Putiolanus, Aurelius Valentianus, soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600065.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix to a local deity named Putiolanus, fulfilling a vow sometime during the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600067.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Septimius Iulianus miles legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600067.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, Septimius Iulianus, soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600067.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Septimius Iulianus, a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix, to the forest deity Silvanus Silvestris, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600068.text_edition: Dis Manibus Canus Loi filius annorum LX Luba coiuce sic Vimbriplia annorum L fecit patri
EDCS-17600068.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Canus, son of Loi, sixty years old. Luba, his wife, and Vimbriplia, fifty years old, made this for their father.
EDCS-17600068.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Canus, son of Loi, by his wife Luba and his daughter Vimbriplia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600069.text_edition: Sibulla Iopari filia annorum XXX titulus positus
EDCS-17600069.translation: Sibulla, daughter of Ioparus, thirty years old. The tombstone was erected.
EDCS-17600069.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sibulla, daughter of Ioparus, erected upon her death at age thirty, typical of the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-17600078.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Publia Aelia Valentina pro se et suis ex voto posuit Sabino et Anullino consulibus
EDCS-17600078.translation: To Juno Regina, Publia Aelia Valentina, for herself and her family, placed this in accordance with a vow, during the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus.
EDCS-17600078.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Juno Regina by Publia Aelia Valentina for herself and her family, precisely dated to 286 CE by the specified consuls.

EDCS-17600079.summary: This brief inscription records a dedication to the goddess Diana by Aurelius Victorinus, likely a simple votive offering from the Imperial era.

EDCS-17600080.summary: This succinct inscription documents a dedication to the goddess Fortuna by Marcus Sosius Iulianus, likely a votive act from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600085.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelia Sura posuit matri suae Mamertinae an(norum) LX=IA#LX#IA
EDCS-17600085.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aelia Sura placed this for her mother Mamertina, sixty years old.
EDCS-17600085.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelia Sura for her mother, Mamertina, who died at age sixty, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600086.text_edition: inae vixit nnos XXIII coniugi merentissimae Claudius Valerius miles legionis I Italicae et Claudio Valerino filio et Claudiae Valerinae filiae vivis  opto tibi terram levem
EDCS-17600086.translation: To [...]ina, who lived twenty-three years, to his most deserving wife, Claudius Valerius, soldier of the First Italian Legion, and to his son Claudius Valerinus and his daughter Claudia Valerina, both living. I wish light earth for you.
EDCS-17600086.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Claudius Valerius, a soldier of the Legio I Italica, for his deceased wife, who lived twenty-three years, also mentioning his living children, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-17600838.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunius votum libens solvit
EDCS-17600838.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Iunius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600838.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Iunius in fulfillment of a vow, typical of dedications throughout the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17600842.text_edition: Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Aurelius Antonius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600842.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. Aurelius Antonius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600842.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Antonius' fulfillment of a vow to Liber Pater Augustus, likely on a votive altar or monument.

EDCS-17600843.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Flavius Ingenuinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600843.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus. Flavius Ingenuinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600843.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Flavius Ingenuinus to Silvanus Domesticus, likely thanking the deity for a favor or fulfilling a promise.

EDCS-17600844.text_edition: Iulius Candidus dis deabusque votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600844.translation: To the gods and goddesses. Iulius Candidus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600844.summary: This inscription records Iulius Candidus' fulfillment of a vow dedicated to a collective group of gods and goddesses, indicating a general act of piety.

EDCS-17600845.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary record likely indicating a monument or dedication by Tiberius in his capacity as a prefect responsible for wine.

EDCS-17600846.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudius Tyrannus tribunus legionis XVI Flaviae firmae tribunus cohortis III Batavorum Ulpia Saturnina coniugi pientissimo et Claudius Concordianus eques Romanus patri desidesid(eratissimo)
EDCS-17600846.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudius Tyrannus, tribune of the Legio XVI Flavia Firma, tribune of Cohors III Batavorum, and Ulpia Saturnina, his most devoted wife, along with Claudius Concordianus, an equestrian, dedicated this to his most longed-for father.
EDCS-17600846.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Claudius Tyrannus by his devoted wife Ulpia Saturnina and his son Claudius Concordianus, highlighting his military career.

EDCS-17600851.text_edition: filius et heres patri pientissimo titulum posuit et Ulpiae Vindeliae matri vivae
EDCS-17600851.translation: The son and heir placed this monument for his most devoted father and for his living mother Ulpia Vindelia.
EDCS-17600851.summary: This inscription records a tombstone or monument erected by a son and heir for his deceased father and his still-living mother, Ulpia Vindelia.

EDCS-17600852.text_edition: Deo Libero  Patiri Aurelius Meaximus veteranus votum solvit libens  merito
EDCS-17600852.translation: To the god Liber Pater. Aurelius Maximus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600852.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aurelius Maximus, a veteran, to the god Liber Pater, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-17600854.text_edition: Silvano Conservatori pro salute Iuli BIRSami veterani Septimius Celsus et Aurelius Atellanus votum solverunt
EDCS-17600854.translation: To Silvanus Conservator, for the well-being of the veteran Iulius BIRSami. Septimius Celsus and Aurelius Atellanus fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-17600854.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Septimius Celsus and Aurelius Atellanus to Silvanus Conservator, made for the health and well-being of the veteran Iulius BIRSami.

EDCS-17600855.text_edition: coniugi vivae et V  et   et Messalin filiis ius Firminus
EDCS-17600855.translation: Firminus [placed this] for his living wife, and for V. and Messalin[us], his sons.
EDCS-17600855.summary: This fragmented inscription likely details a monument, possibly a funerary one, erected by Firminus for his still-living wife and his sons, V. and Messalinus.

EDCS-17600856.translation: To Juno, Queen. Octavia Corneliana [dedicated this].
EDCS-17600856.summary: This is a fragmented votive inscription dedicated to Juno Regina by Octavia Corneliana, likely on an altar or statue base.

EDCS-17600862.text_edition: Terrae Matri Flavia Maximina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600862.translation: To Mother Earth, Flavia Maximina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-17600862.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Flavia Maximina to the goddess Terra Mater.

EDCS-17600864.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Veranus veteranus ex decurione alae I Thracum veteranae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17600864.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Veranus, a veteran formerly a decurion of the First Thracian Veteran Ala, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17600864.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by a veteran Roman cavalry officer, Publius Aelius Veranus, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-17800001.text_edition: Vindo Saturnini flius nnorum LX et Ammuia Mogetionis filia annorum L hic siti sunt Marcus Ulpius Brogimarus gener titulum posuit
EDCS-17800001.translation: Vindo, son of Saturninus, aged 60 years, and Ammuia, daughter of Mogetio, aged 50 years, are laid here. Marcus Ulpius Brogimarus, their son-in-law, erected this monument.
EDCS-17800001.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Vindo and Ammuia, erected by their son-in-law, Marcus Ulpius Brogimarus.

EDCS-17800002.text_edition: Bato Trantonis filius Araviscus sic nnorum L hic situs est Firmus hic situs est Mogitmarus titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-17800002.translation: Bato, son of Tranto, an Araviscus, aged 50 years, is laid here. Firmus is laid here. Mogitmarus erected this monument in memory.
EDCS-17800002.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bato, son of Tranto, and Firmus, erected by Mogitmarus.

EDCS-17800003.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Titus L Titus votum solvit libens decurio coloniae
EDCS-17800003.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Preserver, Titus L. Titus, a decurion of the colony, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17800003.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Titus L. Titus, a decurion of a colony.

EDCS-17800004.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a deity named Cautus by Septimius Valentinus, an optio (junior officer).

EDCS-17800005.translation: To the God of the Spring, Septimius Valentinus, optio.
EDCS-17800005.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a spring deity by Septimius Valentinus, an optio.

EDCS-17800006.translation: To the God of Passage, Septimius Valentinus, optio.
EDCS-17800006.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a deity associated with passage by Septimius Valentinus, an optio.

EDCS-17800035.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Hic iacet in tumulo Aurelia Sabina pientissima coniux nnum XXV quem lapis iste tegit rapta est de luce serena quae magis debuerat fessos sepelire parentes o dolor o pietas o funera tristia coniugis Marcus Aurelius Attianus decurio alae I Trh sicacum et Aurelia Sabinilla filia eorundum sic vivi sibi faciundum curarunt
EDCS-17800035.translation: To the Manes Gods. Here lies in this tomb Aurelia Sabina, a most devoted wife, aged 25 years, whom this stone covers. She was snatched from the serene light, though she ought rather to have buried her weary parents. O grief, O piety, O sad funerals of a wife! Marcus Aurelius Attianus, a decurion of the First Thracian Ala, and Aurelia Sabinilla, their daughter, had this made for themselves while living.
EDCS-17800035.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription for Aurelia Sabina, set up by her husband, Marcus Aurelius Attianus (a decurion), and their daughter, Aurelia Sabinilla, who also prepared the monument for themselves while still alive.

EDCS-17800036.text_edition: Resatus Sapurdae filius nnorum XXX hic situs est Sapurda et Ressila parentes filio pientissimo et sibi vivi
EDCS-17800036.translation: Resatus, son of Sapurda, aged 30 years, is laid here. Sapurda and Ressila, his parents, [had this made] for their most devoted son and for themselves while living.
EDCS-17800036.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Resatus, son of Sapurda, set up by his parents, Sapurda and Ressila, who also prepared it for their own eventual use.

EDCS-17800037.text_edition: Domno Domnae Titus Pompulenius Iunius |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-17800037.translation: To the Lord and Lady. Titus Pompulenius Iunius, centurion of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-17800037.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Titus Pompulenius Iunius, a centurion of the Legio II Adiutrix, to the Lord and Lady, likely deities.

EDCS-17800038.text_edition: eques Honori et Herculi votum solverunt libens merito
EDCS-17800038.translation: The knights willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Honos and Hercules.
EDCS-17800038.summary: This inscription records a collective vow made by a group of knights to the deities Honos and Hercules.

EDCS-17800040.translation: Cauti Tusdius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. [---] Aurelius [---]
EDCS-17800040.summary: This fragmented inscription records a vow fulfilled by Cauti Tusdius, with an additional name Aurelius also present, likely a dedicant or co-dedicant.

EDCS-17800392.text_edition: Lucia Septimia Mamertina Luci Septimi Mamertini filia Marci Aureli Casii coniux diem funi sic annorum XXX
EDCS-17800392.translation: Lucia Septimia Mamertina, daughter of Lucius Septimius Mamertinus, wife of Marcus Aurelius Casius, died at the age of 30.
EDCS-17800392.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Lucia Septimia Mamertina, recording her lineage, marital status, and age at death.

EDCS-17800393.text_edition: Marcus Tutilius Valens annorum Tutilius Paulus annorum  hic siti sunt
EDCS-17800393.translation: Marcus Tutilius Valens, of [---] years, [and] Tutilius Paulus, of [---] years, are buried here.
EDCS-17800393.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Marcus Tutilius Valens and Tutilius Paulus, indicating their burial place.

EDCS-17800394.text_edition: annorum X et Vibillae coniugi et Valentinae filiae et Saturnino filio tertio et suis vivis posuit Aurelius Sabinus et Aurelius IustIus#Iustus#USIUS heredes bene merentibus fecerunt
EDCS-17800394.translation: [---] of 10 years, and for his wife Vibilla, and for his daughter Valentina, and for his third son Saturninus, and for his living relatives, Aurelius Sabinus set this up. Aurelius Iustus and Aurelius Usius, heirs, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-17800394.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Sabinus for his family members, including his wife, children, and himself while still living, with his heirs completing it.

EDCS-17800396.text_edition: Saturnin(us) quondam armatura legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XVI oriundus ex regione Bassianensi desideratus in Dacia quondam qui vixit nnos XXXIIII Septimius Maximianus
EDCS-17800396.translation: Saturninus, formerly an armatura of the Second Adiutrix Legion, of 16 years of service, originating from the Bassianensian region, who lived 34 years, was missed in Dacia. Septimius Maximianus [set this up].
EDCS-17800396.summary: This inscription commemorates Saturninus, a former armatura of Legio II Adiutrix with 16 years of service, recording his origin, age, and death in Dacia, set up by Septimius Maximianus.

EDCS-18000394.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Severus Icepni filius Vangio missicius eques ala Hispanorum prima nnorum LX hic situs est Albanus frater titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-18000394.translation: Tiberius Claudius Severus, son of Icepnus, a Vangio, a discharged cavalryman of the First Wing of Spaniards, aged 60, is buried here. Albanus, his brother, placed this memorial plaque.
EDCS-18000394.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Tiberius Claudius Severus, a discharged cavalryman, erected by his brother Albanus.

EDCS-18300306.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aurelius Silvanus veteranus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-18300306.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-18300306.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran, to the deity Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-18300307.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Isauricius Verecundus Surus veteranus cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum ex decurione vixit nnos LXX Aurelia IsauricGia#Isauricia#ISAURIGIA Scribonia coniux et Isauricius Verecundus et Isauricius Marcus et Aurelia Verecunda fili conheredes vivi sibi et patri pientissimo posuerunt
EDCS-18300307.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Isauricius Verecundus Surus, a veteran of the Cohors Hemesenorum milliaria, formerly a decurion, lived 70 years. Aurelia Isauricia Scribonia, his wife, and Isauricius Verecundus, and Isauricius Marcus, and Aurelia Verecunda, his children and co-heirs, while living, set this up for themselves and for their most devoted father.
EDCS-18300307.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Aurelia Isauricia Scribonia and her children for themselves and for her husband, Aurelius Isauricius Verecundus Surus, a veteran and former decurion, commemorating his life and service.

EDCS-18300318.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Cornelius Crescens decurio alae Frontonianae pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-18300318.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Cornelius Crescens, decurion of the Ala Frontoniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-18300318.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Cornelius Crescens, a cavalry officer, on behalf of himself and his family.
