EDCS-18300323.text_edition: Invicto Soli Mithrae Valerius Valens veteranus ex decurione alae I Thr=RHacum#Thracum#TRHA veteranae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-18300323.translation: To the Unconquered Sun Mithras, Valerius Valens, a veteran formerly a decurion of the First Thracian Veteran Ala, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-18300323.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to Sol Invictus Mithras by Valerius Valens, a veteran cavalry officer, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-21000720.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription or ownership mark for Aurelius Ingenuus, a soldier of the Legion II Adiutrix.

EDCS-24100200.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Germanico Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli proconsuli patri patriae legio II adiutrix VI pia VI fidelis constans Claudiana numini maiestatique eius dicatissima
EDCS-24100200.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Germanicus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, consul, proconsul, father of his country, the Legion II Adiutrix, six times pious, six times faithful, constant, Claudian, most devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-24100200.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication to Emperor Claudius Gothicus by Legion II Adiutrix, praising his titles and virtues and expressing their devotion.

EDCS-24400401.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-24400401.text_edition: Nero Claudius divi Claudi filius Germanici Caesaris nepos Tiberi Caesaris Augusti pronepos divi Augusti abnepos Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator VII consul IIII equitibus qui militant in alis IIII quae apellantur I Hispanorum et Aravacorum et I Hispanorum Auriana et II Hispanorum et Aravacorum et II AstuOrum#Asturum#ASTORUM et sunt in Illyrico sub Lucio Salvidieno Salviano Rufo qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VI Nonas Iulias Cnaeo Pedanio Salinatore Lucio Velleio Paterculo consulibus alae II Hispanorum et Aravacorum cui praeest Sextus Gavius Gallus decurioni Dasio Carmai filio Breuco et Proculo filio eius et Priscillae filiae eius et Proculae filiae eius et Procellae filiae eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est in Capitolio ad latus sinistrum aedis thensarum extrinsecus  Nero Claudius divi Claudi filius Germanicii Caesaris nepos Tiberi Caesaris Augusti pronepos divi Augusti abnepos Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator VII consul IIII equitibus qui militant in alis IIII quae appellantur I Hispanorum et Aravacorum et I Hispanorum Auriana et II Hispanorum et Aravacorum et II AstuOrum#Asturum#ASTORUM et sunt in Illyrico sub Lucio Salvidieno Salviano Rufo qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerant quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus  Quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VI Nonas Iulias Cnaeo Pedanio Salinatore Lucio Velleio Paterculo consulibus alae II Hispanorum et Aravacorum cui praeest Sextus Gavius Gallus decurioni Dasio Carmai filio Breuco et Proculo filio eius et Priscillae filiae eius et Proculae filiae eius et Procellae filiae eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est in Capitolio ad latus sinistrum aedis thensarum extrinsecus  Marci Petroni Flacci equitis Romani Publi Corneli Crispini equitis Romani Sexti Pompili Flori equitis Romani Cai Titi Firmi Titi Minuci Modesti Luci Ovidi Prisci Publi Critti Narcissi Marci Petroni Narcissi
EDCS-24400401.translation: Nero Claudius, son of the divine Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the divine Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the seventh time, consul for the fourth time, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalrymen serving in the four alae which are called I Hispanorum et Aravacorum, and I Hispanorum Auriana, and II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, and II Asturum, and are in Illyricum under Lucius Salvidienus Salvianus Rufus, who had served twenty-five or more stipends, whose names are written below: to themselves, their children, and their descendants. With the wives they had at the time citizenship was granted to them, or if any were unmarried, with those they might later marry, provided each took only one wife, before the 6th day before the Nones of July, when Gnaeus Pedanius Salinator and Lucius Velleius Paterculus were consuls. To the decurion Dasio, son of Carmaus, of the Ala II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, which is commanded by Sextus Gavius Gallus, and to his son Proculus, and to his daughter Priscilla, and to his daughter Procula, and to his daughter Procella. Transcribed and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed on the Capitol on the left side of the outside of the temple of the sacred chariots. Nero Claudius, son of the divine Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the divine Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the seventh time, consul for the fourth time, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalrymen serving in the four alae which are called I Hispanorum et Aravacorum, and I Hispanorum Auriana, and II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, and II Asturum, and are in Illyricum under Lucius Salvidienus Salvianus Rufus, who had served twenty-five or more stipends, whose names are written below: to themselves, their children, and their descendants. With the wives they had at the time citizenship was granted to them, or if any were unmarried, with those they might later marry, provided each took only one wife, before the 6th day before the Nones of July, when Gnaeus Pedanius Salinator and Lucius Velleius Paterculus were consuls. To the decurion Dasio, son of Carmaus, of the Ala II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, which is commanded by Sextus Gavius Gallus, and to his son Proculus, and to his daughter Priscilla, and to his daughter Procula, and to his daughter Procella. Transcribed and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed on the Capitol on the left side of the outside of the temple of the sacred chariots. [Witnesses:] Marcus Petronius Flaccus, Roman knight; Publius Cornelius Crispinus, Roman knight; Sextus Pompilius Florus, Roman knight; Gaius Titius Firmus; Titus Minucius Modestus; Lucius Ovidius Priscus; Publius Critius Narcissus; Marcus Petronius Narcissus.
EDCS-24400401.summary: This is a military diploma from Emperor Nero, dated to 59 AD, granting Roman citizenship and the right of marriage to a cavalryman, Dasius, from the Ala II Hispanorum et Aravacorum, and his family, along with a list of witnesses.

EDCS-24500525.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aeliorum Faventinus vixit annis XLV Floriana annorum XL Faventinus miles cohortis |(miliariae) Maurorum Severianae stipendiorum IIII vixit annis DO
EDCS-24500525.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Faventinus lived 45 years. Floriana, 40 years old. Faventinus, soldier of the Severian Mauri cohort (milleniary), served 4 stipends, lived (age illegible).
EDCS-24500525.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Faventinus, a soldier of the Severian Mauri cohort, and a woman named Floriana, likely his wife or relative.

EDCS-24500526.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Valerio Papiriano tribuno legionis II adiutricis urbe Palaestina genitum me militiae res transtulit huc SPER tamdiuque tribunus insonti mulieri solvi duo pignora cara tiqui sume ac bene vive  Caius Valeri Turbo et Lus filii eius equites Romani  Πατρὶς ἐμοὶ Συρίης δάπεδον πόλεως Καπετωλιάδαο κεῖμε δ' ἐν Παίοσι κενὸν ὄργανον ἀνθρώποισι λεγεῶνος ἀρχηγὸς γεγονὼς ᾧ τοὔνομα Παπειριανὸς ὑπάρχει υἱοὶ ἱππικοὶ β πατρὶ χρηστῷ
EDCS-24500526.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Valerius Papirianus, tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion. Born in the city of Palestine, military service brought me here. So long a tribune, I solved for an innocent woman two dear pledges, be silent and live well. Gaius Valerius Turbo and Lus his sons, Roman knights. [Greek:] My homeland is the plain of Syria, the city of Capitoliada. I lie in Paeonia, an empty instrument for men, having become a commander of a legion, whose name is Papirianus. Two equestrian sons to a good father.
EDCS-24500526.summary: This is a bilingual (Latin and Greek) funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Papirianus, a tribune of Legion II Adiutrix, erected by his sons, Gaius Valerius Turbo and Lus.

EDCS-26600559.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Neptuno Serapidi pro salute et victoria et perpetuitate Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Lucius Alfenus Avitianus legatus eius pro praetore provinciae nnoniae inferioris
EDCS-26600559.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Neptune, Serapis, for the health and victory and perpetuity of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Lucius Alfenus Avitianus, his legate pro praetore of the province of Lower Pannonia.
EDCS-26600559.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Neptune, and Serapis by Lucius Alfenus Avitianus, a provincial governor, for the well-being and success of Emperor Caracalla.

EDCS-26600560.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurel[io] [Severo] Alexan[dro] Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis VIII consuli [III] patri patriae cohors I |(milliaria) nova Severiana Surorum sagittariorum devota numini eius
EDCS-26600560.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the eighth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, the First Severian Cohort (milleniary) of Syrian Archers, devoted to his divinity.
EDCS-26600560.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander by the First Severian Cohort of Syrian Archers, expressing their loyalty and devotion.

EDCS-26600561.text_edition: Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matri domini nostri Invicti Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et castrorum cohors I |(milliaria) nova Severiana Surorum sagittariorum devota numini eorum
EDCS-26600561.translation: To Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of our lord, the Unconquered Emperor Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and of the camp, the First Severian Cohort (milleniary) of Syrian Archers, devoted to their divinity.
EDCS-26600561.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication to Julia Mamaea, mother of Emperor Severus Alexander, by the First Severian Cohort of Syrian Archers, acknowledging her as mother of the emperor and the camp.

EDCS-26600562.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Titus Iulius Valens miles cohortis |(milliariae) novae Severianae Surorum sagittariorum et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600562.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our lords, Titus Iulius Valens, a soldier of the Millenarian New Cohort of Severian Syrian Archers, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, along with his own people.
EDCS-26600562.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the emperors' well-being, made by a soldier of a Syrian archer cohort, Titus Iulius Valens, likely dating to the Severan dynasty (early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-26600563.text_edition: Tito Flavio Titi filio Claudia  Agrippiniensi equiti legionis II adiutricis misso honesta missione vixit annos Tito Flavio Senecae  Primitivae et vivae Titus Flavius L Verus filius
EDCS-26600563.translation: To Titus Flavius, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, from Agrippina, a cavalryman of the Second Adjutrix Legion, honorably discharged. He lived for years. To Titus Flavius Seneca, to Primitiva, and while alive, Titus Flavius L. Verus, his son.
EDCS-26600563.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius, an honorably discharged cavalryman of Legion II Adiutrix, with additional mentions of Titus Flavius Seneca, Primitiva, and his son Titus Flavius L. Verus, possibly erected by family members.

EDCS-26600564.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Restitutus sibi vivus fecit Iustae annorum  coniugi et bene merenti M D T M P
EDCS-26600564.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Restitutus, while alive, made this for himself and for Iusta, his deserving wife, who lived for [---] years. May the earth be light upon her.
EDCS-26600564.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Flavius Restitutus for himself and his wife Iusta, while he was still alive, expressing a common funerary wish for the deceased.

EDCS-26600565.text_edition: Ava Magimari filia annorum XXV et Siliduna et Appinus filii heredes Sisi et Quintus et Medulius
EDCS-26600565.translation: Ava, daughter of Magimarus, 25 years old, and Siliduna and Appinus, her sons. The heirs are Sisi and Quintus and Medulius.
EDCS-26600565.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary record for Ava, daughter of Magimarus, and her sons Siliduna and Appinus, listing Sisi, Quintus, and Medulius as their heirs.

EDCS-26600566.text_edition: Retimarus vixit annos LX filii eius faciendum curaverunt Severus n posuit
EDCS-26600566.translation: Retimarus lived 60 years. His sons ensured this was made. Severus placed it.
EDCS-26600566.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Retimarus, who lived 60 years, commissioned by his sons and placed by Severus.

EDCS-26600588.text_edition: Valerius Proculus Savaria optio legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum VI nnorum XXV hic situs est Valeria Procula mater et Valerius Fuscus frater titulum memoriae posuerunt
EDCS-26600588.translation: Valerius Proculus, from Savaria, an optio of the Second Adjutrix Legion, with 6 years of service and 25 years of age, lies here. Valeria Procula, his mother, and Valerius Fuscus, his brother, erected this monument to his memory.
EDCS-26600588.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Proculus, an optio of Legion II Adiutrix, who died at 25, erected by his mother Valeria Procula and brother Valerius Fuscus.

EDCS-26600591.text_edition: Dis Manibus  in memoriam Aureli Minervini adulescentis nepotis aetate integra qui vixit nnos XVII fecit Aurelius Crispus decurio coloniae Equaestris sic eques Romanus avunculus
EDCS-26600591.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, in memory of Aurelius Minervinus, a young man, a grandson, in the prime of his life, who lived 17 years. Aurelius Crispus, decurion of the colony of Equaestris, thus a Roman knight, his uncle, made this.
EDCS-26600591.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Minervinus, a 17-year-old grandson, erected by his uncle Aurelius Crispus, a decurion and Roman knight.

EDCS-26600595.text_edition: Bonae memoriae coniugis carissimae  Hoc iacet in lapide Aurelia Marcellina pientissima coniunx quem lapis ipse tegit rapta de luce serena huic aetas primis cum florebat in annis invida fatorum genesis mihi sustulit illam superante genetrice sua quae se cupiebat ob illam ante morti dari quam sibi flebilis casus acciderit filiusque permansit innocentius et quicumque legis nostros tu nosti dolores sic at et Elysias senior descendis ad auras  Iulius Victorinus beneficiarius consularis faciendum curavit  et pater Aurelius Eptala pientissim--
EDCS-26600595.translation: To the good memory of a most dear wife. On this stone lies Aurelia Marcellina, a most devoted wife, whom the stone itself covers, snatched from the serene light. When her age was flourishing in its first years, the envious fate of her birth took her from me, while her grieving mother, who wished to be given to death for her before this mournful fate befell her, survived. Her innocent son remained. And whoever reads our sorrows, you know them, thus you too, an elder, descend to the Elysian breezes. Iulius Victorinus, a consular beneficiarius, ensured this was made, and her father Aurelius Eptala, most devoted [---].
EDCS-26600595.summary: This is a lengthy, emotional funerary inscription for Aurelia Marcellina, a young wife who died prematurely, erected by her husband Iulius Victorinus, a consular beneficiarius, and her grieving father Aurelius Eptala.

EDCS-26600596.text_edition: Libero Patri et Liberae Aelius Nianus
EDCS-26600596.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Aelius Nianus (dedicated this).
EDCS-26600596.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Nianus to the deities Liber Pater and Libera, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-26600597.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Caiae Valeriae Alpinae annorum VIII hic sita est pater et mater filiae bene merenti posuerunt et sibi vivis Aelius Ressatus explorator et Valeria Aelias mater
EDCS-26600597.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaia Valeria Alpina, 8 years old, lies here. Her father and mother placed this for their deserving daughter and for themselves while alive. Aelius Ressatus, an explorer, and Valeria Aelias, her mother.
EDCS-26600597.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaia Valeria Alpina, who died at age 8, erected by her parents, the explorer Aelius Ressatus and Valeria Aelias, also for themselves while living.

EDCS-26600598.text_edition: cohortis I Noricorum vixit annos XXXV  et Marco AcIIvo  vixit nnos nianus vivus sibi et patri bene m(erenti)
EDCS-26600598.translation: Of the 1st cohort of Noricans. He lived 35 years. And to Marcus Acilius, he lived N years. Nianus, alive, for himself and for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-26600598.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a larger monument, dedicated by Nianus to himself and his father, possibly also commemorating a soldier from the 1st cohort of Noricans and Marcus Acilius.

EDCS-26600599.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Proculino militi qui vixit annos XXXV dies L Aurelius Proculus veteranus legionis II adiutricis natione Surus domo Hemesa cum Claudia Candida parentes filio karissimo posuerunt cum Aelia Iustina coniuge
EDCS-26600599.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Proculinus, a soldier who lived 35 years and 50 days. Aurelius Proculus, a veteran of the II Adiutrix Legion, of Syrian nationality, from Emesa, along with Claudia Candida, his parents, placed this for their dearest son, with Aelia Iustina his wife.
EDCS-26600599.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Aurelius Proculinus, a soldier, placed by his parents, Aurelius Proculus (a Syrian veteran) and Claudia Candida, along with his wife Aelia Iustina.

EDCS-26600600.text_edition: veni MantiN  domi sic Velauno  coniugi V
EDCS-26600600.translation: I came, Mantine, at home thus, to Velaunus, my husband, V.
EDCS-26600600.summary: This is an enigmatic personal inscription, possibly a funerary lament or a private dedication, from an individual to her husband Velaunus and a person named Mantine.

EDCS-26600602.text_edition: Herculi sacrum Marcus Ulpius Gemellus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600602.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Marcus Ulpius Gemellus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600602.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules, dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Gemellus to fulfill a vow he made.

EDCS-26600603.text_edition: Herculi Augusti Marcus Domitius Secundinus decurio alae Brittonum votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-26600603.translation: To Hercules Augustus. Marcus Domitius Secundinus, decurion of the Ala Brittonum, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600603.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules Augustus, dedicated by Marcus Domitius Secundinus, a decurion of the Ala Brittonum, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26600604.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Marcellus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito legionis IIII Flaviae felicis
EDCS-26600604.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Ulpius Marcellus, beneficiarius consularis of the IIII Flavia Felix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600604.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Marcellus, a beneficiarius consularis from the IV Flavia Felix Legion.

EDCS-26600605.text_edition: Virtuti et Honori Lucius Ulpius Marcellus legatus Augusti pro praetore nniae inferioris votum solvit
EDCS-26600605.translation: To Valour and Honour. Lucius Ulpius Marcellus, imperial legate propraetor of Lower Pannonia, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600605.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Virtus and Honor by Lucius Ulpius Marcellus, an imperial legate of Lower Pannonia.

EDCS-26600606.text_edition: Caesernia Firmilla viva fecit sibi et Marcio Lucidiano coniugi annorum XLIIII et Marcio Lucido filio annorum XL et Marcio Firminiano militi annorum XXVII et Ulpio SI A Lucio candidato legionis II adiutricis et Ulpiae Proclinae niri sic et Luciae et Marcio Iuliano annorum VI  nepotibus
EDCS-26600606.translation: Caesernia Firmilla, alive, made this for herself and for Marcius Lucidianus, her husband, 44 years old, and for Marcius Lucidus, her son, 40 years old, and for Marcius Firminianus, a soldier, 27 years old, and for Ulpius SI A Lucius, a candidate of the II Adiutrix Legion, and for Ulpia Proclina Niri, thus, and for Lucia and Marcius Iulianus, 6 years old, her grandchildren.
EDCS-26600606.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Caesernia Firmilla while still alive, for herself, her husband, her sons (one a soldier and another a candidate in the II Adiutrix Legion), and her grandchildren.

EDCS-26600607.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marcio Restutiano patri defuncto annorum LXXV Septimiae Ursinae vivae parentibus pientissimis et Marcio Urso militi legionis III Italicae defuncto annorum XXV et Marciae Aquilinae defunctae annorum XXXV et Marcio Coto  defuncto annorum X fratribus item Suconiugi suae optimae et Ael
EDCS-26600607.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcius Restutianus, deceased father, 75 years old, to Septimia Ursina, living mother, for their most dutiful parents, and to Marcius Ursus, soldier of the III Italica Legion, deceased, 25 years old, and to Marcia Aquilina, deceased, 35 years old, and to Marcius Cotta, deceased, 10 years old, their siblings. Likewise to Suconiuga, his excellent wife, and Ael[---].
EDCS-26600607.summary: This is a complex family funerary monument dedicated to deceased parents Marcius Restutianus and Septimia Ursina, and their deceased children Marcius Ursus (a soldier), Marcia Aquilina, and Marcius Cotta, with an additional mention of a wife and another individual.

EDCS-26600608.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Ursus vixit annos VIIII posuit Aelius Ianuarius pater filio pientissimo
EDCS-26600608.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Ursus lived 9 years. Aelius Ianuarius, his father, placed this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-26600608.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Aelius Ursus, set up by his father, Aelius Ianuarius.

EDCS-26600609.text_edition: AE annos XXV Ulpius Pirus pater vixit annos XL posuit Ulpia Ruca mater filiis dulcissimis heres eorum
EDCS-26600609.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpius Pirus, his father, lived for 40 years; Ulpia Ruca, his mother, placed this for her dearest sons. He lived for 25 years. She is their heir.
EDCS-26600609.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lives of Ulpius Pirus and his sons, erected by Ulpia Ruca, his mother and their heir.

EDCS-26600610.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Caenio Secundino annorum LXX et Lucio Caenio Florentino
EDCS-26600610.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Caenius Secundinus, 70 years old, and Lucius Caenius Florentinus.
EDCS-26600610.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Caenius Secundinus and Lucius Caenius Florentinus.

EDCS-26600611.text_edition: Dis Manibus er vivus fecit sibi  C et Claudio ano et XXV et
EDCS-26600611.translation: To the spirits of the departed. He made this for himself while living, C. Claudius, 25 years old.
EDCS-26600611.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by C. Claudius for himself during his lifetime, noting he was 25 years old.

EDCS-26600612.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimius Probatus veteranus legionis II adiutricis vixit nnos XLV et Septimiae Boniatae coniugi eiusdem et Septimio Probiano et Probillae et Respecto et Proculino filiis Aurelius Servatus genero optimo filiae et nepotibus et Aureliae Respectae coniugi pientissimae memoriam posuit
EDCS-26600612.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Septimius Probatus, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, lived for 45 years. And for Septimia Boniata, his wife, and for Septimius Probianus, and Probilla, and Respectus, and Proculinus, his children. Aurelius Servatus placed this memorial for his excellent son-in-law, his daughter, and his grandchildren, and for Aurelia Respecta, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-26600612.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimius Probatus, a veteran, his wife, and children, erected by Aurelius Servatus for his son-in-law, daughter, grandchildren, and devoted wife.

EDCS-26600613.text_edition: Fortunae Salutari cohortis I Alpinorum equitatae Publius Clodius Severus praefectus duobus Auggustis ddominis nnostris consulibus
EDCS-26600613.translation: To Fortuna Salutar for the First Cohort of Alpinorum, cavalry. Publius Clodius Severus, prefect, in the consulship of our two lords Augusti.
EDCS-26600613.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Fortuna Salutar by Publius Clodius Severus, prefect of the First Cohort of Alpinorum, commemorating the consulship of the two Augusti.

EDCS-26600614.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto tribunicia potestate XVII consuli III cohors I Alpinorum peditata
EDCS-26600614.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, holding tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, the First Cohort of Alpinorum, infantry, [dedicated this].
EDCS-26600614.summary: This is a monumental inscription dedicated by the First Cohort of Alpinorum (infantry) to Emperor Caracalla, detailing his imperial titles and ancestry, likely dating to 214 CE.

EDCS-26600615.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Septimius Tatulo veteranus ex optione cohortis I Thracum Germanicae et Aurelia Verina coniux Lucius Aurelius qui et Septimius Constans filius miles cohortis supra scriptae Aurelius qui et Septimius Constantinus filius miles legionis I adiutricis Aurelius qui et Septimius Victorinus filius Aurelius qui et Septimius Verinus filius Aureli- qu- et Septimi- Aemili-) fili-) Mereccusae  mater sic vixit annos LXX vivi sibi fecerunt
EDCS-26600615.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Septimius Tatulo, a veteran by option from the First Thracian Germanica Cohort, and Aurelia Verina, his wife, Lucius Aurelius, also known as Septimius Constans, his son, a soldier of the aforementioned cohort, Aurelius, also known as Septimius Constantinus, his son, a soldier of the First Legion Adiutrix, Aurelius, also known as Septimius Victorinus, his son, Aurelius, also known as Septimius Verinus, his son, Aurelius, also known as Septimius Aemilius, his son. Mereccusa, his mother, lived for 70 years. They made this for themselves while living.
EDCS-26600615.summary: This is a funerary inscription made during their lifetime for Lucius Septimius Tatulo, a veteran, his wife Aurelia Verina, and their four sons (two of whom were soldiers), also mentioning Tatulo's mother, Mereccusa.

EDCS-26600616.text_edition: ri Quirina Sirmio Maximo quondam veterano ex signifero legionis II adiutricis et Cominiae Maximillae filiae eius et Aeliae Proculinae uxori eius E L Novllianus
EDCS-26600616.translation: To Quirina Sirmio Maximus, formerly a veteran from the standard-bearer of the Second Legion Adiutrix, and Cominia Maximilla, his daughter, and Aelia Proculina, his wife. E. L. Novellianus [placed this].
EDCS-26600616.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quirina Sirmio Maximus, a veteran standard-bearer, his daughter Cominia Maximilla, and his wife Aelia Proculina, erected by E. L. Novellianus.

EDCS-26600617.text_edition: eres  et Flaviae  filiae Firminae et Flaviae Firmae filiis Flavius Firmus veteranus legionis II adiutricis pientissimis? ---
EDCS-26600617.translation: And to Flavia Firmina, his daughter, and Flavia Firma, his daughter. Flavius Firmus, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, for his most devoted [family].
EDCS-26600617.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius Firmus, a veteran, to his two daughters, Flavia Firmina and Flavia Firma.

EDCS-26600618.text_edition: Oplus Laepocus Volsetis filius Aexilvas veteranus nnorum LIII stipendiorum XXIX hic situs est Lucius Petillius Cai filius heres posuit
EDCS-26600618.translation: Oplus Laepocus, son of Volsetis, Aexilvas, a veteran, 53 years old, 29 years of service, is buried here. Lucius Petillius, son of Caius, his heir, placed this.
EDCS-26600618.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Oplus Laepocus Aexilvas, a veteran, erected by his heir, Lucius Petillius.

EDCS-26600619.text_edition: Anaco pater et Atenatis pia mater Marciano et Salonino filiis p(osuerunt)
EDCS-26600619.translation: Anaco, the father, and Atenatis, the devoted mother, placed this for their sons Marcianus and Saloninus.
EDCS-26600619.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a burial monument set up by parents for their two sons.

EDCS-26600620.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Tullius Fortunatus veteranus cohortis |Dquingenariae Maurorum domo Africa vixit annos LXX Septimiae Dubitatae coniugi et filis eorum Fabius Baritio veteranus cohortis eiusdem heres eius amico bene merenti titulum
EDCS-26600620.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Tullius Fortunatus, a veteran of the five-hundred-strong cohort of Mauretanian soldiers, from Africa, lived 70 years. To his wife Septimia Dubitata and their sons, Fabius Baritio, a veteran of the same cohort and his heir, erected this monument to his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-26600620.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Tullius Fortunatus, a veteran, erected by his heir and fellow veteran Fabius Baritio, dedicated also to Fortunatus' wife and sons.

EDCS-26600621.text_edition: Deo Aeterno pro salute domini nostri Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matris Augusti votum reddidit libens Cosmius praepositus stationis Spondill a synagoga  Iudaeorum
EDCS-26600621.translation: To the Eternal God, for the welfare of our lord Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of the Augustus, Cosmius, the officer in charge of the Spondillae station, from the synagogue of the Jews, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-26600621.summary: This inscription records a vow made to the Eternal God for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander and his mother Julia Mamaea by Cosmius, an officer from a Jewish synagogue, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600622.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Culminari Aurelius HercOulanus#Herculanus#HEROULANUS eques cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum
EDCS-26600622.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminaris, Aurelius Herculanus, a cavalryman of the milliary cohort of the Hemeseni, [dedicates this].
EDCS-26600622.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminaris by Aurelius Herculanus, a cavalryman from a Hemesene military unit.

EDCS-26600623.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Calventius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-26600623.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Calventius, a beneficiarius consularis (officer of the governor), willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600623.summary: This is a votive inscription to Liber Pater, dedicated by Calventius, an officer attached to a consular governor.

EDCS-26600625.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III patri patriae consuli proconsuli cohors I |(miliaria) Hemesenorum Gordiana sagittariorum equitata civium Romanorum numini eius devotissima
EDCS-26600625.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, Father of the Fatherland, Consul, Proconsul, the First Millenary Cohort of Hemesene archers, cavalry, Roman citizens, most devoted to his divinity, [set this up].
EDCS-26600625.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Gordian III by the First Millenary Cohort of Hemesene archers and cavalry, likely dating to 240 AD (due to the tribunician power count).

EDCS-26600627.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Deisan domo Hemesa vetI(eranus)#vet(eranus)#VEI eIx#ex#IX statori tribuni cohortis I |(miliariae) HePmesenorum#Hemesenorum#HPM vivus sibi eIt#et#IT viven=Mtibus#viventibus#VIVMTIBUS Aureliis Monimo stratori et RegilIiano#Regiliano#REGIIIANO libDrario#librario#LIDR leIgati#legati#LIG Iegionis II adiutricis filiis posuit et Aurelio Abigeneo qui vixit nnos XVII menses X d(ies) XVII et Aurelio Prisciano mil=R(iti)#mil(iti)#MR legionis II adiutricis nepotibus
EDCS-26600627.translation: Marcus Aurelius Deisan, from Hemesa, a veteran, formerly a 'stator tribuni' (aide to a tribune) of the First Millenary Cohort of the Hemeseni, placed this for himself while living, and for his living sons Aurelius Monimus, a 'strator' (groom/orderly), and Aurelius Regilianus, a 'librarius' (clerk) of the legate of the Legio II Adiutrix, and for his grandchildren Aurelius Abigeneus, who lived 17 years, 10 months, 17 days, and Aurelius Priscianus, a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix.
EDCS-26600627.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Aurelius Deisan, a veteran, for himself and his living sons and deceased grandchildren, who served in various military roles.

EDCS-26600628.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Pusintulus protector qui vixit annos XXXVIIII in quadragensimo fatis animam reddidit heredes bonoI#bono#BONI patri memoriam fecerunt Andronico et Probo consulibus
EDCS-26600628.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Valerius Pusintulus, a protector, who lived 39 years, rendered his soul to fate in his fortieth year. His heirs made this memorial for their good father, in the consulship of Andronicus and Probus.
EDCS-26600628.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Pusintulus, a protector, erected by his heirs and dated to the consulship of Andronicus and Probus (310 AD).

EDCS-26600629.text_edition: Hoc lapide tegitur Aurelia Pia piissima coniux ex Nicia cives sic Bythynica sic originis orta ter undenos annos non plus adoleverat aetas florentem EDIIC matura vita perempta vos testor
EDCS-26600629.translation: Under this stone lies Aurelia Pia, a most devoted wife, a citizen from Nicaea, thus Bithynian by origin. Her age had not advanced beyond three times ten years; her flourishing life was cut short prematurely. I attest to you.
EDCS-26600629.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Pia, a young woman from Nicaea in Bithynia, buried by her husband.

EDCS-26600630.text_edition: Haec quoque nomine Kara i post lustra peremptam  est tradita morti  pompa comitante beata  g iunguntque falvillam memorabitur hu tinus ol
EDCS-26600630.translation: This woman too, by the name of Kara, after five years, was delivered to death, a blessed procession accompanying her. They join the small ash. She will be remembered from this time onward.
EDCS-26600630.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Kara, commemorating her death and the accompanying funeral procession.

EDCS-26600635.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Teutano pro salute Imperatorum Marci Aureli Antonini et Luci Aureli Commodi et ordinis Aquincensium Titus Flavius Macer et Publius Aelius Ianuarius IIviri municipii eiusdem posuerunt III Idus Iunias Orfito  et Rufo consulibus
EDCS-26600635.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Teutanus, for the health of the Emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius Commodus and of the order of Aquincum, Titus Flavius Macer and Publius Aelius Ianuarius, duoviri of the same municipality, erected this on the 3rd day before the Ides of June, during the consulship of Orfitus and Rufus.
EDCS-26600635.summary: This inscription records a dedication made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Teutanus for the well-being of the emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius Commodus and the local council of Aquincum, set up by two duoviri in 186 CE.

EDCS-26600636.text_edition: Sirmio optio cohortis I Alpinorum equitatae centuria Maximi annorum XLVI stipendiorum XXVI hic situs est titulum posuit Flavius Rufinus eques bucinator cohortis eiusdem heres ex testamento fT(ecit)#f(ecit)#T
EDCS-26600636.translation: Sirmio, optio of the First Cohort of Alpine Equites, in the century of Maximus, 46 years old, with 26 years of service, is buried here. Flavius Rufinus, an equestrian trumpeter of the same cohort, his heir by testament, had this title made.
EDCS-26600636.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sirmio, an optio of a cavalry unit, erected by his heir, Flavius Rufinus, a fellow soldier, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27600001.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Luci filius Papias domo ia nnorum LV hic situs est Lucius Valerius Germanus et Lucius Valerius Firmus heredes ex testamento faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-27600001.translation: Lucius Valerius Papias, son of Lucius, from his home, 55 years old, is buried here. Lucius Valerius Germanus and Lucius Valerius Firmus, his heirs by testament, took care to have this made.
EDCS-27600001.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Papias, set up by his heirs Lucius Valerius Germanus and Lucius Valerius Firmus according to his will, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27600002.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Claudius CAEN.
EDCS-27600002.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning Marcus Claudius, but is incomplete.

EDCS-27600003.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini [et Publi Septimi] Getae Caesaris Auggustorum nnostrorum Marcus Aurelius Priscinus speculator legionis II adiutricis
EDCS-27600003.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of our Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, Marcus Aurelius Priscinus, a speculator of the Second Adiutrix Legion, [dedicated this].
EDCS-27600003.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the Severan imperial family (Lucius Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta), made by Marcus Aurelius Priscinus, a scout of the Legio II Adiutrix, dating between 209 and 212 CE.

EDCS-27600004.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae pro salute domini nostri  Augusti totiusque domus eius ISER Maximinus RI
EDCS-27600004.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Juno Regina, for the health of our lord Augustus and his entire household, Maximinus ISER RI.
EDCS-27600004.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina for the health of an emperor and his household, made by Maximinus, and likely dates to the 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-27600006.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Septimius Longinus cornicularius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae
EDCS-27600006.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Septimius Longinus, cornicularius of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious, Faithful, Severan.
EDCS-27600006.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Silvanus Domesticus by Septimius Longinus, an administrative officer of Legio II Adiutrix, likely dating to the Severan period (late 2nd/early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-27600007.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinti Postumi Lepidini praefecti equitum
EDCS-27600007.translation: To the Divine Manes of Quintus Postumius Lepidinus, prefect of cavalry.
EDCS-27600007.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Postumius Lepidinus, a cavalry prefect, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700106.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Iustino militi legionis II adiutricis exacto ad praeAtoriu=ICOm#praetorium#PRAATICOM militavit ann
EDCS-27700106.translation: To the Divine Manes of Publius Aelius Iustinus, soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, assigned to the praetorium, he served for [---] years.
EDCS-27700106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Iustinus, a soldier of Legio II Adiutrix who served in the praetorium, but is incomplete, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700305.text_edition: Iulius Macrinus Aurelius PithLon#Pithon#PITLON Artius Quintus Caecilius Osor Pos Itonu Aurelius Bienn Iulius Euodus Aurelia Thaunura Flavia Thiodora sic VibiariusT#Vibiarius#VIBIARIUT Proculus Aurelius Quintus Aurelius Rusticus Titinius Sabinus    libertus    Tita Solva  Pithon  NOA Aurelius Primus   Augustalis    Avit L
EDCS-27700305.translation: Julius Macrinus, Aurelius Pithon, Artius Quintus, Caecilius Osor, Pos Itonu, Aurelius Bienn, Julius Euodus, Aurelia Thaunura, Flavia Thiodora, so Vibiarius Proculus, Aurelius Quintus, Aurelius Rusticus, Titinius Sabinus, freedman, Tita Solva, Pithon, NOA, Aurelius Primus, Augustalis, Avit L.
EDCS-27700305.summary: This inscription is a list of individuals, possibly members of a collegium or family group, including several Aurelii and an Augustalis, suggesting a date in the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700306.text_edition: Domo securitatis Flavia Calvena nata Ulmo vixit annos XXII lanxit dies XXVII posuit memoriam Aurelius Propincus miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis coniugi carissimae Flavia Cara mater dulcissimae filiae pietatis eius faciendum curaverunt  Φ(ῶς) Ζ(ωή)
EDCS-27700306.translation: To the home of security. Flavia Calvena, born in Ulm, lived 22 years, suffered for 27 days. Aurelius Propincus, soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, placed this memorial for his dearest wife. Flavia Cara, her sweetest mother, ensured its construction out of piety for her daughter. Light, Life.
EDCS-27700306.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Aurelius Propincus, a soldier, to his wife Flavia Calvena, with the assistance of her mother Flavia Cara, likely dating to the early-to-mid 2nd century AD given the legion's presence in Pannonia Inferior.

EDCS-27800691.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelio Messio veterano ex praetorio annorum LX Flavia Ingenua coniugi pientissimo posuit hic situs est
EDCS-27800691.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelius Messius, a veteran from the praetorian guard, 60 years old. Flavia Ingenua placed this for her most devoted husband. He lies here.
EDCS-27800691.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Flavia Ingenua for her very devoted husband, Aelius Messius, a 60-year-old veteran of the praetorian guard.

EDCS-28000245.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Aemilia usu stolae uExor#uxor#EXOR nata trium liberorum equitum Romanorum mater vixit nnos XXXVII Valerius Timotheus tribunus cohortis III Batavorum coniugi pientissimae
EDCS-28000245.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Aemilia, by custom a wife, the mother of three Roman knight children, lived 37 years. Valerius Timotheus, tribune of the Third Cohort of Batavians, [erected this] for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-28000245.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Valerius Timotheus, a military tribune, to his very devoted wife, Valeria Aemilia, who was the mother of three Roman knights.

EDCS-28000246.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aelio Karo veterano cohortis III Batavorum domo municipio Batavorum nnorum  ianus
EDCS-28000246.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Aelius Karus, a veteran of the Third Cohort of Batavians, from his home municipality of Batavians, [age and dedicant missing].
EDCS-28000246.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone for Titus Aelius Karus, a veteran of the Third Cohort of Batavians, originally from the municipality of Batavians, with the age and dedicant details missing.

EDCS-28000247.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Lucius Antonius Honoratus benefB(iciarius)#benef(iciarius)#BB consularis votum solvit libens merito Imperatore Commodo  III consule
EDCS-28000247.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Antonius Honoratus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the third consulship of Emperor Commodus.
EDCS-28000247.summary: This is a dedicatory altar or stele made by Lucius Antonius Honoratus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dated to the year 183 AD by the mention of Emperor Commodus's third consulship.

EDCS-28000249.text_edition: Dis Manibus  o Rom qui vixit annos
EDCS-28000249.translation: To the Divine Manes. [Name ending in] -o Rom, who lived [number] years.
EDCS-28000249.summary: This is a highly fragmentary tombstone dedicated to an individual named -o Rom, with their age partially preserved.

EDCS-28000276.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, preserving only parts of two lines, likely containing parts of names.

EDCS-28000277.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, preserving only parts of two lines, possibly indicating names or parts of words.

EDCS-28000279.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vergissa Magimari filius nnorum LXXXI hic situs est Appius filius patri pientissimo titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-28000279.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vergissa, son of Magimarus, 81 years old, lies here. Appius, his son, placed this title of memory for his most devoted father.
EDCS-28000279.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Appius for his very devoted father, Vergissa, son of Magimarus, who lived to be 81 years old.

EDCS-28100220.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto tribunicia potestate IIII proconsuli Imperatoris Severi Augusti nostri filio cohors VII Breucorum civium Romanorum equitata
EDCS-28100220.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power for the fourth time, proconsul, son of our Emperor Severus Augustus. The Seventh Cohort of Breuci, Roman citizens, equestrian.
EDCS-28100220.summary: This is a military dedication by the Cohors VII Breucorum civium Romanorum equitata, honoring Emperor Caracalla, dating to his fourth tribunician power, which corresponds to 201-202 AD.

EDCS-28100221.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate VIIII imperatori XI consuli II proconsuli cohors VII Breucorum civium Romanorum eqitata
EDCS-28100221.translation: To Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, father of his country, with tribunician power for the ninth time, acclaimed Imperator for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, proconsul. The Seventh Cohort of Breuci, Roman citizens, mounted.
EDCS-28100221.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the mounted Seventh Cohort of Breuci, Roman citizens, to Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus, likely dating to around 201 CE based on his titulature.

EDCS-28100222.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Imperatoris Severi Augusti nostri filio cohors I Noricorum Antoniniana equitata
EDCS-28100222.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of our Imperator Severus Augustus. The First Cohort of Noricans, Antoniniana, mounted.
EDCS-28100222.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the mounted First Cohort of Noricans, bearing the honorary title Antoniniana, to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28100223.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Lucius Iulius Quirina Passenianus praefectus pro sua salute et Marci Iulii Catuli patris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28100223.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Lucius Iulius Quirina Passenianus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being and that of his father, Marcus Iulius Catulus.
EDCS-28100223.summary: This inscription is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Dolichenus by the prefect Lucius Iulius Quirina Passenianus for himself and his father, Marcus Iulius Catulus.

EDCS-28100228.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Genio cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum Antoninianae Titus Claudius Procus beneficiarius consularis legionis II adiutricis piae fEidelis#fidelis#EID Antoninianae Imperatore Antonino IIII et Caelio Balbino II consulibus
EDCS-28100228.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Imperator Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, and to the Genius of the First Millliary Cohort of Hemeseni, Antoniniana. Titus Claudius Procus, beneficiarius consularis of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious, Faithful, Antoniniana, during the fourth consulship of Imperator Antoninus and the second consulship of Caelius Balbinus.
EDCS-28100228.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the First Milliary Cohort of Hemeseni by Titus Claudius Procus, a consular beneficiarius, made for the well-being of Emperor Caracalla, and precisely dated to 213 CE by the consuls.

EDCS-28100234.text_edition: AMALOGVERO domo Betavos sic eq(ues) ala Hispanorum O IN COLS et IC decurio et NO SVO heredes poserunt
EDCS-28100234.translation: To Amaloguerus from Betavos, thus an equestrian of the Ala Hispanorum, in COLS and IC, a decurion, and his heirs placed this for NO SVO.
EDCS-28100234.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument placed by the heirs of Amaloguerus, an equestrian and decurion of the Ala Hispanorum, who originated from Betavos.

EDCS-28100235.text_edition: Nertus Dumnotali sic filius veteranus ala Hispanorum I sesquiplicarius Lingauster nnorum LX stipendiorum XXXVI hic situs est Valens frater heres titulum memoriae posuit  F---
EDCS-28100235.translation: Nertus, son of Dumnotalus, thus, a veteran of the First Ala Hispanorum, a sesquiplicarius, from Lingauster, 60 years old, with 36 years of service, lies here. Valens, his brother, as heir, placed this memorial inscription. F[---].
EDCS-28100235.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Nertus, a veteran sesquiplicarius of the First Ala Hispanorum, placed by his brother Valens.

EDCS-28100236.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque Septimius Bauleus eques capsarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28100236.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all gods and goddesses. Septimius Bauleus, a mounted capsarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28100236.summary: This inscription is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other deities by Septimius Bauleus, an equestrian capsarius.

EDCS-28100237.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Augusto  Iulius Taurus miles sesquip(licarius) eques  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28100237.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus. Iulius Taurus, a mounted sesquiplicarius soldier, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28100237.summary: This inscription is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus by Iulius Taurus, a mounted sesquiplicarius soldier.

EDCS-28100238.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Priscus signifer cohortis I |(milliariae) novae Severianae Surorum sagittariorum  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28100238.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Priscus, standard-bearer of the First Milliary New Severian Cohort of Syrian archers, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28100238.summary: This inscription is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Priscus, a standard-bearer of the First Milliary New Severian Cohort of Syrian archers.

EDCS-28100250.text_edition: missicius alae II Aravacorum nnorum LX Tiberius Claudius Coslicim
EDCS-28100250.translation: Tiberius Claudius Coslicim, a missicius of the Second Ala Aravacorum, 60 years old.
EDCS-28100250.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Tiberius Claudius Coslicim, a missicius (discharged veteran) of the Second Ala Aravacorum, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-28500317.text_edition: Clausa iacet lapidi coniunx pia cara Sabina artibus edocta superabat sola maritum vox ei grata fuit pulsabat pollice cordas set sic cGito#cito#GITO rapta silet ter denos duxerat annos heu male quinque minus set sic plus tres menses habebat bis semptemque dies vixit haec ipsa superstes spectata in populo hydraula grata regebat sis felix quicumque leges te numina servent et pia voce cane Aelia Sabina vale Titus Aelius Iustus  hydraularius salariarius legionis II adiutricis coniugi faciendum curavit
EDCS-28500317.translation: Closed within this stone lies my loyal and dear wife Sabina. Taught in the arts, she alone surpassed her husband. Her voice was pleasing, she plucked the strings with her thumb. But thus swiftly snatched away, she is silent. She had lived for thirty years, alas, five less, but thus she had three months more and fourteen days. She herself lived on, a celebrated hydraula, pleasing, she ruled. May you be fortunate, whoever reads this, may the gods protect you, and sing with a pious voice: 'Farewell, Aelia Sabina.' Titus Aelius Iustus, a hydraulic engineer, a salaried member of the Second Legion Adiutrix, had this made for his wife.
EDCS-28500317.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life and musical talent of Aelia Sabina, a hydraula, erected by her husband Titus Aelius Iustus, a hydraulic engineer of the Legio II Adiutrix, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28500851.text_edition: Publius A T  RAQOF  FPVSE  POAT TRAD
EDCS-28500851.translation: Publius A. T. TRAQOF FPVSE POAT TRAD.
EDCS-28500851.summary: This inscription records fragmented and mostly indecipherable text, likely a personal name and some abbreviations, on a small stone fragment.

EDCS-28500854.summary: This inscription contains two abbreviated names, possibly the genitive forms of Oppius and Valerius, suggesting a dedication or ownership record.

EDCS-28500858.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Lucius Cannutius, likely identifying an individual owner, dedicant, or deceased person.

EDCS-28500859.summary: This inscription records the name Domitius Maximinus in the genitive case, likely indicating ownership or a dedication to this individual.

EDCS-28500860.summary: This inscription records the name Quintus Volusius Narcissus in the genitive case, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-28500861.summary: This inscription records the name Titus Fadius Verus in the genitive case, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-28500976.summary: This inscription expresses an act of loyalty or devotion to Constantinus, likely referring to one of the Roman emperors named Constantine, suggesting an imperial dedication or acclamation from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-28600008.text_edition: Cornelius Flavus signifer legionis  XIIII geminae
EDCS-28600008.translation: Cornelius Flavus, standard-bearer of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-28600008.summary: This inscription identifies Cornelius Flavus as a standard-bearer (signifer) of the Legio XIV Gemina, indicating a military context, likely a funerary monument or a dedication by a soldier.

EDCS-28600009.text_edition: Parthenopeni Flavius Cogitatus signifer legionis II adiutricis
EDCS-28600009.translation: To Parthenopenus, Flavius Cogitatus, standard-bearer of the Second Legion Adiutrix.
EDCS-28600009.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Flavius Cogitatus, a standard-bearer of the Legio II Adiutrix, to an individual named Parthenopenus, likely a funerary monument or an offering.

EDCS-28600010.text_edition: Quintus Frendullus Rufus Vicetinus vixit nnos XXXXIIX militavit nnos XXIII armorum custos
EDCS-28600010.translation: Quintus Frendullus Rufus, a man from Vicetia, lived 48 years, served 23 years as a custodian of arms.
EDCS-28600010.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Frendullus Rufus, a soldier from Vicetia who served as an armorum custos.

EDCS-28600011.text_edition: Iuli sic Atheni sic liberto et heredi Iuli Crescentis quondam |(centurionis) legionis X geminae qui post obitum eius vixit annum I menses tres Iulia Iulia coniugi carissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600011.translation: To Iulus and Athenius, freedmen and heirs of Iulus Crescens, formerly a centurion of the Tenth Legion Gemina, who lived one year and three months after his death. Iulia Iulia had this made for her dearest husband.
EDCS-28600011.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Iulia Iulia to her husband, Iulus Crescens, a former centurion, and his freedmen and heirs.

EDCS-28600012.text_edition: Hic quiescunt sic duas sic matres duas sic filias sic numero tres faciunt et advenas II parvolas sic qui suscitabit cuius condicio est Iulius Herculanus veteranus faciendum curavit in re sua
EDCS-28600012.translation: Here rest two mothers, two daughters, which makes three in number, and two small strangers. Whoever disturbs them, let their condition be that of Iulius Herculanus, a veteran, who had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-28600012.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Iulius Herculanus, a veteran, for multiple family members and two unnamed children, with a curse against anyone disturbing the grave.

EDCS-28600013.text_edition: Caio Iulio C(ai)? f(ilio) Ommentiaeo militi legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum XXII et Flaviae Gemellae coniugi eius Iulius Ampliatus libertus patronis bene merentibus et Iulius Maximus miles legionis II adiutricis
EDCS-28600013.translation: To Gaius Iulius, son of Gaius, of the Omentia tribe, a soldier of the First Auxiliary Legion, with 22 years of service, and to Flavia Gemella, his wife. Iulius Ampliatus, his freedman, to his well-deserving patrons, and Iulius Maximus, a soldier of the Second Auxiliary Legion.
EDCS-28600013.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Iulius Ampliatus, a freedman, and Iulius Maximus, a soldier, to their patrons, Gaius Iulius and his wife Flavia Gemella.

EDCS-28600014.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulio Tatuloni militi legionis II adiutricis beneficiario tribuni fuit in expeditione bello desideratus et qui vixit nnos XLV Septimi=IRa#Septimia#SEPTIIRA Quintilla coniux pientissima cum Probilla HERV et Quintilliano filio eorum ob memoriamIS et OM IMP II
EDCS-28600014.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Iulius Tatulo, a soldier of the Second Auxiliary Legion, a beneficiarius of the tribune, who was lost on a military expedition and lived 45 years. Septimia Quintilla, his most devoted wife, with Probilla and Quintillianus, their children, in memory of him.
EDCS-28600014.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Septimia Quintilla and her children to Iulius Tatulo, a soldier and tribune's beneficiarius who died on campaign.

EDCS-28600015.text_edition: Caius Iulius Vius Donatus veteranus legionis II adiutricis anV(norum)#an(norum)#AV LEX#LX#EX collegium fabrum et centonariorum posuit
EDCS-28600015.translation: Gaius Iulius Vius Donatus, a veteran of the Second Auxiliary Legion, aged 60 years, placed this for the guild of builders and felt-makers.
EDCS-28600015.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Gaius Iulius Vius Donatus, a veteran, to the guild of builders and felt-makers.

EDCS-28600016.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Victorinus |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae Sphinc
EDCS-28600016.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Victorinus, a centurion of the Fourth Flavian Legion, Sphinc(.)
EDCS-28600016.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Victorinus, a centurion of the Fourth Flavian Legion.

EDCS-28600017.text_edition: SSCISSIANI militi legionis II adiutricis armorum custodi et duplario stipendiorum XIIII nnorum XXXII Porolissenisi provincia Dacia LI(ucius)#L(ucius)#I Novius Provincialis miles legionis eiusdem heres fecit
EDCS-28600017.translation: To Siscissianus, a soldier of the Second Auxiliary Legion, custodian of arms and duplicarius, with 14 years of service, aged 32 years, from Porolissum in Dacia. Lucius Novius Provincialis, a soldier of the same legion, his heir, made this.
EDCS-28600017.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Siscissianus, a soldier from Dacia, erected by Lucius Novius Provincialis, his heir and fellow soldier.

EDCS-28600019.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pacato Mucaris militi legionis II adiutricis stupendiorum sic X vixit annis XXXV Bato Neritani commilitoni obsequentissimo et fratri ex testamento fieri curavit ex HS nummum DCCC
EDCS-28600019.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Pacatus Mucaris, a soldier of the Second Auxiliary Legion, with 10 years of service, who lived 35 years. Bato Neritani, his most obedient comrade and brother, had this made according to his will, from 800 sesterces.
EDCS-28600019.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pacatus Mucaris, a soldier, made by his comrade and brother, Bato Neritani, according to Pacatus' will.

EDCS-28600020.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Petilius Firmus veteranus ex beneficiario praefecti legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis A Gaius Petilius Fi=TErminianus#Firminianus#TERMINIANUS et Petilia
EDCS-28600020.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Petilius Firmus, a veteran, formerly a beneficiarius of the prefect of the Second Auxiliary Legion Pia Fidelis. Gaius Petilius Firminianus and Petilia.
EDCS-28600020.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Petilius Firmus, a veteran and former beneficiarius, set up by Gaius Petilius Firminianus and Petilia.

EDCS-28600021.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Gaio Pollio Gai filio domo colonia Aelia Mursa Martiano veterano legionis II adiutricis ex custode armorum qui vixit annos LX CCaii Pollii Marinianus et Marcellina et Martina filii et heredes patri piissimo faciendum curaverunt optamus sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-28600021.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Pollius Martianus, son of Gaius, from the colony of Aelia Mursa, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, formerly a custodian of arms, who lived for 60 years. Gaius Pollius Marinianus and Marcellina and Martina, his children and heirs, had this made for their most devoted father. We hope the earth rests lightly upon you.
EDCS-28600021.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Pollius Martianus, a veteran, erected by his children and heirs, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600022.text_edition: Pompulenio Adaucto Pompulenius Iunius |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis liberto merentissimo posuit
EDCS-28600022.translation: Pompulenius Iunius, centurion of the Second Adiutrix Legion, set this up for Pompulenius Adauctus, his most deserving freedman.
EDCS-28600022.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by a centurion, Pompulenius Iunius, to his freedman, Pompulenius Adauctus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600024.text_edition: vixit annos XLVI  Q Samius Sirmi  legionis II adiutricis coniugi bene merenti et sibi et Claudiae Tiberinae et Liciniae Valentinae neptibus vivus sibi fecit
EDCS-28600024.translation: He lived for 46 years. Quintus Samius, from Sirmium, of the Second Adiutrix Legion, while alive, made this for his well-deserving wife, and for himself, and for Claudia Tiberina and Licinia Valentina, his granddaughters.
EDCS-28600024.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Quintus Samius, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, for himself, his wife, and his granddaughters during his lifetime, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600025.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimia Valeria viva sibi et Septimiae Martinae filiae defunctae annorum XV item Caio Te Spectato veterano coniugi fecit
EDCS-28600025.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Septimia Valeria, while alive, made this for herself and for her deceased daughter Septimia Martina, who was 15 years old, and likewise for Gaius Te Spectatus, her veteran husband.
EDCS-28600025.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Septimia Valeria for herself, her deceased daughter Septimia Martina, and her veteran husband Gaius Te Spectatus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600026.text_edition: Publio Tarrutenio Stellatina Proculo Taurinis evocato legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XLVI annorum LXXVII hic situs est Quintus Cornelius Felix cornicularius praefecti legionis eiusdem heres ex testamento faciendum curavit et immunes et discentes
EDCS-28600026.translation: Publius Tarrutenius Stellatina Proculus, from Taurini, an evocatus of the Second Adiutrix Legion, with 46 years of service and 77 years of age, is buried here. Quintus Cornelius Felix, a cornicularius of the prefect of the same legion, his heir, arranged for this to be made according to his will, both exempts and students.
EDCS-28600026.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Tarrutenius Stellatina Proculus, an evocatus, erected by his heir, Quintus Cornelius Felix, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600027.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Torio Gemelliano quondam OC--- fabrum legionis II adiutricis et Toriae Geminae matri eius Aurelia Serena coniux et Toria Gemellina filia heredes vivi fecerunt Tit--- Au |(milia)
EDCS-28600027.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Titus Tori(us) Gemellianus, formerly O.C. [...], a craftsman of the Second Adiutrix Legion, and for Toria Gemina, his mother. Aurelia Serena, his wife, and Toria Gemellina, his daughter, his heirs, made this while alive. Titus Au[...].
EDCS-28600027.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Aurelia Serena and Toria Gemellina, the wife and daughter respectively, for Titus Tori(us) Gemellianus, a craftsman of the Second Adiutrix Legion, and for his mother, Toria Gemina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600028.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Luci Aniensi Crescens Vercellis miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Comini Paulini annorum XXXX stipendiorum XVIIII testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit hic situs est
EDCS-28600028.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Valerius Crescens, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe, from Vercellae, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, in the century of Cominius Paulinus, 40 years old, with 19 years of service. He ordered this to be made by will. His heir had it made. He is buried here.
EDCS-28600028.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Crescens, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, erected by his heir according to his will, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600029.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Publio Valerio Heliodoro trH(ibuno#tr(ibuno#TH legionis II adiutricis Lucius Publius Valerius Chilo filius eius
EDCS-28600029.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Publius Valerius Heliodorus, tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion. Lucius Publius Valerius Chilo, his son [erected this].
EDCS-28600029.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Publius Valerius Heliodorus, a tribune, by his son Lucius Publius Valerius Chilo, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600030.text_edition: Caio Valerio Cai filio Claudia Secundo Aemona veterano legionis II adiutricis annorum LX hic situs est collegium fabrum et centonariorum posuit
EDCS-28600030.translation: For Gaius Valerius Secundus, son of Gaius, from Aemona, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, 60 years old. He is buried here. The college of craftsmen and felt-makers set this up.
EDCS-28600030.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Valerius Secundus, a veteran, erected by a college of craftsmen and felt-makers, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600031.text_edition: Quinto Vatinio Adiutori quondam centurioni legionis II adiutricis honesta missione mV(isso)#m(isso)#V Aelius Diodotus clarissimus vir heres ePius#eius#PIUS fecit
EDCS-28600031.translation: For Quintus Vatinius Adiutor, formerly a centurion of the Second Adiutrix Legion, discharged with an honorable discharge. Aelius Diodotus, a most distinguished man, his heir, made this.
EDCS-28600031.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Vatinius Adiutor, a former centurion, erected by his heir, Aelius Diodotus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600032.text_edition: Hic sita sum matrona genus nomenque Veturia Fortunati coniux de patre Veturio nata ter novenos misera et nupta bis octo per annos unicuba uniiuga quae post sex partus uno superstite obii Titus Iulius Fortunatus |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis coniugi incomparabili et insigni in se pietate
EDCS-28600032.translation: Here I lie, Veturia by name and birth, a matron, wife of Fortunatus, born of my father Veturius. I lived for three times nine years (27 years) and was married for twice eight years (16 years), a wife to one husband, joined to one man. After six births, with one child surviving, I died. Titus Iulius Fortunatus, centurion of the Second Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, to his incomparable wife, outstanding in her devotion to him.
EDCS-28600032.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Veturia, erected by her husband Titus Iulius Fortunatus, a centurion, detailing her age, marital status, and number of children.

EDCS-28600033.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Iustino militi TER legionis II VLL
EDCS-28600033.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Ulpius Iustinus, soldier of the Second Legion VLL, TER.
EDCS-28600033.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Marcus Ulpius Iustinus, a soldier of the Second Legion, likely erected by his comrades or family.

EDCS-28600034.text_edition: V S MD C S EQ SES
EDCS-28600034.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly mentioning a name ending in -vs, a title like 'knight', and other incomplete details.

EDCS-28600035.text_edition: miles legionis II adiutricis faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600035.translation: a soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion had this made.
EDCS-28600035.summary: This inscription records that a soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion commissioned the making of the monument, likely a dedication or funerary stele.

EDCS-28600036.text_edition: Francus ego cives sic Romanus miles in armis egregia virtute tuli bello mea dextera semper
EDCS-28600036.publication: CIL 03, 03576 = CLE 00620 = D 02814 = CLEPann 00041 = CLENuovo p 108 = CLENuovo p 172 = TitAq-02, 00629
EDCS-28600036.translation: I, Francus, a Roman citizen and soldier, always bore my right hand in war with outstanding courage.
EDCS-28600036.summary: This is a commemorative inscription, likely a funerary monument for Francus, a Roman soldier, highlighting his bravery in battle.

EDCS-28600039.text_edition: Caius Cornelius Corinthus ob Augustalitatem splendidissimo sic templum sic IIiterum peperit
EDCS-28600039.translation: Gaius Cornelius Corinthus, on account of his Augustalitas, thus procured a splendid temple and a second for himself.
EDCS-28600039.summary: This inscription records Gaius Cornelius Corinthus's dedication of a temple, and possibly a second, due to his service as an Augustalis.

EDCS-28600040.text_edition: Caius Iulius Severus ob honorem magisterii collegii fabrum silanum pecunia sua fecit Muciano et FabDiano#Fabiano#FADIANO consulibus
EDCS-28600040.translation: Gaius Iulius Severus, on account of the honor of the magistracy of the college of craftsmen, made this silanum (water basin/fountain) with his own money, in the consulship of Mucianus and Fabianus.
EDCS-28600040.summary: This inscription details Gaius Iulius Severus's benefaction, a silanum, funded by himself to commemorate his magistracy in the college of craftsmen, dated by the consulship of Mucianus and Fabianus.

EDCS-28600045.text_edition: CVM DECIAN MISTORI AEDI MAGISTRATVI Lucius Atticius Patrinus heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600045.translation: When Decianus Mistorius was aedile and magistrate, Lucius Atticius Patrinus, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-28600045.summary: This inscription indicates that Lucius Atticius Patrinus, as heir, commissioned the monument during Decianus Mistorius's tenure as aedile and magistrate, likely a funerary or commemorative monument for Mistorius.

EDCS-28600046.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Marci filIio#fi]lio#IIO Triclili--- nnorum LXX et Aelius Catulliinus nnorum XVIII hic situs Aelia Catta coniugi pientissimo et
EDCS-28600046.translation: To Marcus Ulpius, son of Marcus, Triclilius [---] of 70 years, and Aelius Catullinus of 18 years, buried here. Aelia Catta to her most devoted husband and [---].
EDCS-28600046.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Catta to her very devoted husband Marcus Ulpius, who was 70 years old, and also commemorates Aelius Catullinus, who was 18 years old, likely their son or a relative.

EDCS-28600047.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publius Albucius Publi filius Valentinus
EDCS-28600047.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Albucius, son of Publius, Valentinus.
EDCS-28600047.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Publius Albucius Valentinus, erected to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-28600049.text_edition: Flavius Philaenius Aureli- C NE coniugi karissim Aurelius Candidus IN VP N
EDCS-28600049.translation: Flavius Philaenius, of Aurelius C. N. E., for his dearest wife. Aurelius Candidus... (remainder illegible or abbreviated).
EDCS-28600049.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Flavius Philaenius to his beloved wife, with Aurelius Candidus also involved in some capacity, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600050.text_edition: Claudianus Flavius A Aurelius Firmanus Valerius Luc Poblicius Optianus Flavius Quirinus Aurelius Maximus Aurelius Festi Aurelius Firminianus Aurelius Prima Flavius Victorinus Claudius cu Aurelius Valentinus Septimius Valentinus Septimius Maximianus Aurelius Montanus Aurelius Saturninus Aurelius Iustus Iulius Sabinianus QVE Aurelius Mercurialis Ulpius Salinator Antonius Marinianus [A]ur(elius) Maximus ex vo(to) pos(uerunt)? honori eorum posuerunt
EDCS-28600050.translation: Claudianus Flavius A., Aurelius Firmanus, Valerius Luc(ius), Poblicius Optianus, Flavius Quirinus, Aurelius Maximus, Aurelius Festi(nus), Aurelius Firminianus, Aurelius Prima, Flavius Victorinus, Claudius C.U., Aurelius Valentinus, Septimius Valentinus, Septimius Maximianus, Aurelius Montanus, Aurelius Saturninus, Aurelius Iustus, Iulius Sabinianus Q.V.E., Aurelius Mercurialis, Ulpius Salinator, Antonius Marinianus, Aurelius Maximus, placed this as a vow, or erected it in their honor.
EDCS-28600050.summary: This inscription, likely a dedication or an honorific monument, was erected by a large group of individuals, possibly a collegium or military unit, either in fulfillment of a vow or to honor specific persons, dating to the Roman Imperial era.

EDCS-28600051.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Baebius Valens nnorum LX hic situs est Iulia Casta coniugi pientissimo et sibi viva titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-28600051.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Baebius Valens, who lived 60 years, is buried here. Iulia Casta, while living, placed this memorial monument for her most devoted husband and for herself.
EDCS-28600051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebius Valens, placed by his devoted wife Iulia Casta, who also prepared it for herself, indicating a common Roman practice of shared burial monuments.

EDCS-28600052.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Battae Vibi filiae nnorum et Ressorae nnorum Memoris filiae hic sitae sunt et Sisiunae neptiae sic anniculae fecit Verbugia filia eius et sibi viva
EDCS-28600052.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Batta, daughter of Vibius, of [---] years, and Ressora, daughter of Memor, of [---] years, are buried here, and their granddaughter Sisiuna, a mere infant. Verbugia, her daughter, made this, and for herself while living.
EDCS-28600052.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Batta, Ressora, and their infant granddaughter Sisiuna, erected by Verbugia, who was Ressora's daughter, and also intended for Verbugia's future use, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600053.text_edition: Brogimara Dallonis filia nnorum XXV et Iantuna filia annorum II hic sitae sunt Magio coniugi et filiae titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-28600053.translation: Brogimara, daughter of Dallo, of 25 years, and Iantuna, her daughter, of 2 years, are buried here. Magio placed this memorial monument for his wife and daughter.
EDCS-28600053.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Brogimara and her daughter Iantuna, erected by Magio, who was Brogimara's husband and Iantuna's father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600054.summary: This brief inscription records the name of Servius Cornelius, indicating his status as a freedman of a man named Servius, likely from a tombstone or a dedicatory plaque.

EDCS-28600055.translation: Herennia Mar(cella) made this while living.
EDCS-28600055.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription indicating that Herennia Marcella prepared this monument for herself during her lifetime, a common practice in Roman funerary tradition.

EDCS-28600056.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Valentino vixit nnos XXX Iulia Sissa
EDCS-28600056.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Valentinus lived 30 years. Iulia Sissa (made this).
EDCS-28600056.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Valentinus, who lived 30 years, likely placed by Iulia Sissa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600057.text_edition: Iulia Utta Epponis filia Florina natione Anartia annorum LXXXV hic sita est Cobromara Regilli filia matri titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-28600057.translation: Iulia Utta, daughter of Eppo, a Florina of the Anartian nation, 85 years old, is buried here. Cobromara, daughter of Regillus, placed this memorial monument for her mother.
EDCS-28600057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Utta, an 85-year-old woman of Anartian nationality, erected by her daughter Cobromara, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600058.text_edition: Aulus Lucanius Auli filius Lepidus nnorum VIII hic situs est Lucanius Capito et Kania Utsia parentes posuerunt
EDCS-28600058.translation: Aulus Lucanius Lepidus, son of Aulus, who lived 8 years, is buried here. Lucanius Capito and Kania Utsia, his parents, placed this.
EDCS-28600058.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the young Aulus Lucanius Lepidus, who died at the age of 8, erected by his grieving parents, Lucanius Capito and Kania Utsia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600059.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lupo vixit annis III Alexandenr bene merenti
EDCS-28600059.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lupus, who lived for 3 years. Alexand(e)r to the well-deserving.
