EDCS-28600059.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Alexand(e)r to Lupus, a child who died at the age of three.

EDCS-28600060.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marciae Meroes sic Fabia Secundina mammulae bene merenti
EDCS-28600060.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Marcia Meroes, thus Fabia Secundina to her well-deserving little mother.
EDCS-28600060.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fabia Secundina to her 'little mother,' Marcia Meroes.

EDCS-28600061.text_edition: Matsiu Tuionis filius nnorum XL hic situs est
EDCS-28600061.translation: Matsius, son of Tuio, aged 40 years, lies here.
EDCS-28600061.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Matsius, son of Tuio, who died at the age of 40 and is buried here.

EDCS-28600062.text_edition: liae vixit Rufinae filiae vixit annum I  Mansuetae coniugi  et Saturninae filiae vivis fecerunt
EDCS-28600062.translation: To Ilia Rufina, their daughter, who lived for 1 year. For Mansueta, their wife, and Saturnina, their daughter, they made this while living.
EDCS-28600062.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by parents for their deceased daughter, Ilia Rufina, who lived one year, and for their living wife, Mansueta, and living daughter, Saturnina, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-28600063.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quint[us or -a] to his/her wife Venn[---].
EDCS-28600063.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a wife, Venn[---], by her spouse, Quint[---].

EDCS-28600064.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Septimius Archelaus quondam
EDCS-28600064.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Septimius Archelaus, formerly...
EDCS-28600064.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Septimius Archelaus, whose former status or occupation is implied to follow.

EDCS-28600065.text_edition: Aulus Vettius Auli libertus Crescens nnorum L hic situs est Vettia Auli liberta Nome patrono et coniugi suo pientissimo viva titulum memoriae posuit Lucius Vipstanus Luci libertus Senecio amicus faciundum curavit
EDCS-28600065.translation: Aulus Vettius Crescens, freedman of Aulus, aged 50 years, lies here. Vettia Nome, freedwoman of Aulus, while living, placed this memorial inscription for her most devoted patron and husband. Lucius Vipstanus Senecio, freedman of Lucius, a friend, saw to its construction.
EDCS-28600065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Vettius Crescens, placed by his freedwoman and wife, Vettia Nome, with the construction overseen by their friend Lucius Vipstanus Senecio.

EDCS-28600067.text_edition: M GAVALI Aelia Higia sic pEientissima#pientissima#EIET coniux et  filiae suae annorum XXX Laleae matri pRiaentissimae#piaentissimae#RIAEN ac fLiliae#filiae#LIL suae  annorum X
EDCS-28600067.translation: To M. Gavali. Aelia Higia, his most devoted wife, thus to her daughters, one aged 30 years and Laleae, her most devoted mother and to her daughter, aged 10 years.
EDCS-28600067.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Aelia Higia to M. Gavali, presumably her husband, and also to her daughters, Laleae (her most devoted mother), and another daughter aged 10 years, suggesting a complex family dedication.

EDCS-28600069.text_edition: Caio Lucilio Marciano quondam corniculI(ario)#cornicul(ario)#CORNICI legionis II adiutricis Caius Lucilius Marcianus iunior MAI COLEN V F EXS IN RVERES PANN INFE CI
EDCS-28600069.translation: To Gaius Lucilius Marcianus, formerly cornicularius of the Second Adjutrix Legion. Gaius Lucilius Marcianus Junior... (rest of the text is fragmentary and difficult to translate fully).
EDCS-28600069.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Lucilius Marcianus, a former cornicularius of Legion II Adiutrix, by Gaius Lucilius Marcianus Junior, likely his son.

EDCS-28600071.text_edition: IFRCE Vrosius et Aur(elia) Philumene parentes filiae carissimae faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28600071.translation: Vrosius and Aurelia Philumene, parents, had this made for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-28600071.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Vrosius and Aurelia Philumene for their beloved deceased daughter.

EDCS-28600076.text_edition: MA Aurelius Iu et heres ob incomparabilem eius dum supervixit in se pietatem
EDCS-28600076.translation: MA Aurelius Iu and his heir, because of his incomparable devotion to him while he was alive.
EDCS-28600076.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tombstone, dedicated by an heir to MA Aurelius Iu, expressing gratitude for his devotion.

EDCS-28600077.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae ceteris Dis Deabusque omnibus possessores vici Vindoniani ex voto pusuerunt quorum nomina sunt Aurelius Aepictetianus sacerdos Aurelius Vettianus equo publico Iulius Victorinus decurio Aurelius Candidianus dec(urio) Aurelius Ianuarius veteranus Aurelius Trofimus C Aurelius Androtanus -- Aurelius Maximus Antonius Quirinus III Idus Octobres Imperatore domino nostro Alexandro Severo Augusto III et Cassio Dione II consulibus  item Marcus Aurelius Aepictetianus decurio coloniae Aquincensium sacerdotalis aram donavit in honorem vicanorum vici Vindoniani  quae ara consecrata est in possessione Aureli Vettiani equitis Romani permissu eiusdem precario petentibus vicanis Vindoniani
EDCS-28600077.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva, and all other gods and goddesses, the proprietors of the vicus Vindonius, in fulfillment of a vow, erected this. Their names are Aurelius Aepictetianus, priest; Aurelius Vettianus, public horseman; Julius Victorinus, decurion; Aurelius Candidianus, decurion; Aurelius Ianuarius, veteran; Aurelius Trofimus; C. Aurelius Androtanus; Aurelius Maximus; Antonius Quirinus. On the Ides of October, in the consulship of our lord Emperor Alexander Severus Augustus for the third time and Cassius Dio for the second time. Also, Marcus Aurelius Aepictetianus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, a priestly office holder, donated an altar in honor of the villagers of the vicus Vindonius. This altar was consecrated on the property of Aurelius Vettianus, a Roman knight, with his permission, by the villagers of Vindonius, who requested it precariously.
EDCS-28600077.summary: This lengthy inscription records the dedication of an altar to multiple deities by the proprietors of the vicus Vindonius, made in 229 CE, and also details a separate donation of an altar by Marcus Aurelius Aepictetianus for the villagers.

EDCS-28600078.text_edition: Dis Manibus Canius Otiorix miEles#miles#MEL legionis II adiutricis qui defunctus est in Parthia titulum fecit Canius Speratus filius suus sibi vivo et coniugi vivae
EDCS-28600078.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Canius Otiorix, a soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion, who died in Parthia. Canius Speratus, his son, made this tombstone for himself while living and for his living wife.
EDCS-28600078.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Canius Otiorix, a soldier who died in Parthia, set up by his son Canius Speratus, who also dedicated it for himself and his wife while they were alive.

EDCS-28600079.text_edition: Marbu XX titulum faciendum curavit V et coniugi
EDCS-28600079.translation: Marbu XX arranged for the making of the tombstone for V and his wife.
EDCS-28600079.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription indicating that Marbu XX took care of setting up a tombstone for an individual named V and his wife.

EDCS-28600080.text_edition: et Marciae Otaciliae Severae sanctissimae Augustae coniugi Augusti nostri
EDCS-28600080.translation: and to Marcia Otacilia Severa, the most holy Augusta, wife of our Augustus.
EDCS-28600080.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger dedication, honoring Marcia Otacilia Severa, who was the wife of Emperor Philip the Arab (244-249 CE).

EDCS-28600081.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Marcus Ulpius Pusinnio |(centurio) exploratorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600081.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Ulpius Pusinnio, a centurion of the scouts, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600081.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Pusinnio, a centurion of the scouts, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600083.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Aurelius Firminus veteranus solvit votum libens merito
EDCS-28600083.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aurelius Firminus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600083.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Firminus, a veteran, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600085.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aelia Clita mater votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600085.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aelia Clita, his mother, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28600085.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Aelia Clita, in her capacity as mother, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600087.text_edition: co o votum otatus  est pro salute sua et Aureliaes sic Valentinaes! et Aurelio Alexandro et Aurel
EDCS-28600087.translation: vowed for his own safety and for Aurelia Valentina and for Aurelius Alexander and Aurel-
EDCS-28600087.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription indicating a vow made for the health and safety of the dedicator and several named individuals, including Aurelia Valentina and Aurelius Alexander.

EDCS-28600089.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Victorino nnorum XXX stipendiorum XIII duplicario alae I Thracum veteranae et Aelio Liciniano annorum XII filiis pientissimis Aelia Flavina infelicissima mater et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-28600089.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Flavina, a most unhappy mother, placed this for her most devoted sons, Aelius Victorinus, 30 years old, with 13 years of service as a duplicarius in the First Thracian Veteran Ala, and Aelius Licinianus, 12 years old, and for herself while living.
EDCS-28600089.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the grieving mother, Aelia Flavina, to her two deceased sons, Aelius Victorinus, a soldier, and Aelius Licinianus, and also for herself while still alive.

EDCS-28600090.text_edition: Aurelio Maximo veterano lIegionis#legionis#IEG II adiutricis patri et Aeliae Primae matri et Aeliae Ressillae aviae Aurelia Ingenua filia parentibus karissimis titulum de suo faciendum curavit Aurelius Valens miles legionis II adiutricis socris benignissimis
EDCS-28600090.translation: To Aurelius Maximus, veteran of the Second Adjutrix Legion, his father, and to Aelia Prima, his mother, and to Aelia Ressilla, his grandmother, Aurelia Ingenua, their daughter, saw to it that this inscription was made from her own funds for her dearest parents. Aurelius Valens, soldier of the Second Adjutrix Legion, [made this] for his most kind in-laws.
EDCS-28600090.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Ingenua to her parents and grandmother, and also by Aurelius Valens to his in-laws, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD given the Roman names and legionary service.

EDCS-28600091.text_edition: Titus Flavius Bonio Quirina Andautonia eques alae Frontonianae turma Ingenui annorum XXXIIII stipendiorum XVI hic situs est Campanus S eques alae eiundem sic heres  titulum memoriae posuit
EDCS-28600091.translation: Titus Flavius Bonio, of the Quirina tribe, from Andautonia, an equestrian of the Ala Frontoniana, in the troop of Ingenuus, aged 34, with 16 years of service, is laid here. Campanus S., an equestrian of the same ala, his heir, placed this inscription in his memory.
EDCS-28600091.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Flavius Bonio, a cavalryman, erected by his heir Campanus S., likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD based on the military context and Roman nomenclature.

EDCS-28600093.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Titi filio Quirina Severo Gogaeno annorum LVIII decurio coloniae Sirmi quaestori IIviro praefecto fabrum hic situs est Flavia Severina filia patri pientissimo posuit
EDCS-28600093.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Titus Flavius Severus Gogaenus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, aged 58, a decurion of the colony of Sirmium, a quaestor, a duumvir, a prefect of engineers, who is laid here. Flavia Severina, his daughter, placed this for her most dutiful father.
EDCS-28600093.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Titus Flavius Severus Gogaenus, a prominent municipal official in Sirmium, erected by his daughter Flavia Severina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600095.text_edition: Dis Manibus  DI    Rosionis XXXXXF et Atresso filio eius XX  Surus cum fratribus parentibus  simis posuit
EDCS-28600095.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Di[---] Rosionis, aged 50, and Atresso, his son, aged 20. Surus, with his brothers, placed this for his dearest parents.
EDCS-28600095.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Surus and his brothers for their parents, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-28600096.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimiae Mariae Iudeae quae vixit annis XVIII Actia Sabinilla mater
EDCS-28600096.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Septimia Maria Iudaea, who lived 18 years. Actia Sabinilla, her mother, [placed this].
EDCS-28600096.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimia Maria Iudaea, placed by her mother Actia Sabinilla, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-28600097.text_edition: Comatum ara Usionis filia annorum XXXVI de proprio titulum posuit hic est sita
EDCS-28600097.translation: Comata, daughter of Usio, aged 36, placed this inscription from her own funds. She is laid here.
EDCS-28600097.summary: This is a funerary monument for Comata, erected by herself during her lifetime, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-28600098.text_edition: Saetibolus annorum XIII et Quintus annorum VII hic situs est sic Magissaes fecit parens titulum posuit memoriae
EDCS-28600098.translation: Saetibolus, aged 13, and Quintus, aged 7, are laid here. Magissaes, their parent, made and placed this inscription in memory.
EDCS-28600098.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two children, Saetibolus and Quintus, erected by their parent Magissaes, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-28600204.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Ianuarius veteranus ex praetorio votum posuit
EDCS-28600204.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Iulius Ianuarius, a veteran from the praetorian guard, dedicated this vow.
EDCS-28600204.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iulius Ianuarius, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600205.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et omnibus dibus deabusque Ulpius Flaus veteranus ex decurione votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600205.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses. Ulpius Flaus, a veteran who was formerly a decurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600205.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses by Ulpius Flaus, a veteran and former decurion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600206.text_edition: coniugi bene merenti posuit S Faventinu(s) Aurelio Tertullino fratri et FIlavio#Flavio#IL Larrito decurioni alae
EDCS-28600206.translation: S. Faventinus placed this for his well-deserving wife, and for his brother Aurelius Tertullinus, and for Flavius Larritus, a decurion of the ala.
EDCS-28600206.summary: This inscription seems to be a funerary dedication made by S. Faventinus for his wife, his brother Aurelius Tertullinus, and Flavius Larritus, a cavalry officer, dating from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-28600207.text_edition: Iulio Aelio duplicario classis Flaviae nnonicae vixit nnos XLV Statoria Sura coniux et Iulius Ianuarius filius Aemilius Carus trierarcha
EDCS-28600207.translation: To Iulius Aelius, a duplicarius of the Flavian Pannonian fleet. He lived 45 years. Statoria Sura, his wife, and Iulius Ianuarius, his son, and Aemilius Carus, a trierarch, set this up.
EDCS-28600207.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Aelius, a fleet duplicarius, erected by his wife, son, and a trierarch.

EDCS-28600208.text_edition: Scemaes Liccaui filius Amantinus hosef anMnorum#annorum#AMNORUM decem gente Undius centuria secunda in flumen periit Hemona posuere Liccaus pater Loriqus et Licaios cognati
EDCS-28600208.translation: Scemaes Amantinus, son of Liccaus, of the Undian tribe, in the second centuria, aged ten years, perished in the river Hemona. Liccaus, his father, and Loriqus and Licaios, his kinsmen, set this up.
EDCS-28600208.summary: This is a funerary monument for a ten-year-old boy named Scemaes Amantinus, who drowned in the river Hemona, erected by his father and kinsmen.

EDCS-28600209.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Publicius Surio.
EDCS-28600209.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Titus Publicius Surio.

EDCS-28600212.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Monitori pro salute adque incolumitate domini nostri Gallieni Augusti et militum vexillationum legionum Germanicianarum  et Brittannicinarum cum auxiliis earum Vitalianus praefectus Augusti nostri somno moniitus posuit
EDCS-28600212.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Monitor, for the health and safety of our lord Gallienus Augustus and the soldiers of the vexillations of the German and British legions with their auxiliaries, Vitalianus, prefect of our Augustus, set this up, warned in a dream.
EDCS-28600212.summary: This is a dedicatory altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Monitor, set up by Prefect Vitalianus after a dream, for the well-being of Emperor Gallienus and the military units under his command, likely dating to the reign of Gallienus (253-268 AD).

EDCS-28600213.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Titus Flavius Candidianus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600213.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Candidianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-28600213.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Titus Flavius Candidianus on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-28600214.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Priscus Candidianus decurio coloniae Sirmiensis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600214.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Ulpius Priscus Candidianus, decurion of the colony of Sirmium, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600214.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Ulpius Priscus Candidianus, a decurion from the colony of Sirmium.

EDCS-28600216.text_edition: Iovi Optimo MN(aximo)#M(aximo)#N et Marti Custodi Publius Helvius Pertinax praefectus
EDCS-28600216.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars Custos. Publius Helvius Pertinax, prefect.
EDCS-28600216.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars Custos, set up by Publius Helvius Pertinax, a prefect.

EDCS-28600218.text_edition: Libero et Liberae Augusti Flavius Paternus votum solvit libens merito

EDCS-28600220.text_edition: Domnae et Domno sacrum abius Hilarus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600220.translation: Sacred to the Lord and Lady. Abius Hilarus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600220.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to an unnamed divine Lord and Lady, set up by Abius Hilarus.

EDCS-28600221.text_edition: Imp(eratori) Caes(ari) M(arco) Aur(elio) Augusto cohors I Campanorum voluntariorum civium Romanorum Antoniniana cura agente Publio Aelio Valerio tribuno ex veterano  Asppris consulibus
EDCS-28600221.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Augustus, the First Cohort of Campanian Volunteers of Roman Citizens, Antoniniana, with Publius Aelius Valerius, tribune, acting as curator, from a veteran. In the consulship of Aspris.
EDCS-28600221.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Cohors I Campanorum voluntariorum civium Romanorum Antoniniana to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Augustus, with a tribune as curator, set up by a veteran during an unspecified consulship of Aspris, likely dating to the reign of Marcus Aurelius (161-180 AD).

EDCS-28600224.text_edition: Publio Aelio Respecto equiti Romano a militiis filio piissimo et studiis omni praedito Aelius Trophimianus pater vir egregius posuit
EDCS-28600224.translation: To Publius Aelius Respectus, Roman knight, who served in several militias, a most devoted son, endowed with every talent, Aelius Trophimianus, his distinguished father, set this up.
EDCS-28600224.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelius Trophimianus for his son, Publius Aelius Respectus, a Roman knight who had served in various military capacities, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28600225.text_edition: Dis Manibus    XXXVII Salvio nnorum XXXIII frumentario legionis II adiutricis Iulius Asclepiades caris suis hunc titulum posui miserabile munus non satis ut meruistis quattuor amissis te quintum Salvi flere necesse est adsiduae dum mea fata resistent te nunc amisso domus inclinata recumbit deponam luctus cum te sequar care per umbras dulcem meum Salvium liceat mihi dicere semper optaveram tumulum tu mihi ut faceres tot bona tum carui merito te care requiro
EDCS-28600225.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Salvius, aged 33, a grain official of the Second Legion Adiutrix. Iulius Asclepiades set up this monument for his dear ones, a lamentable gift, not enough for what you deserved. With four lost, it is necessary to mourn you, Salvius, the fifth. As long as my fates resist, now with you lost, my house bows down and lies low. I will lay down my grief when I follow you, dear one, through the shadows. May I always be allowed to call you my sweet Salvius. I had wished that you would make a tomb for me. Then I was deprived of so many good things. I deservedly miss you, dear one.
EDCS-28600225.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Asclepiades to Salvius, a grain official of Legio II Adiutrix, expressing deep grief for the loss of Salvius and four others, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600226.text_edition: Civilis decurio coloniae nnorum L et Caius Iulius Cai filius Florus decurio coloniae nnorum XX et Iuliae Festae nnorum XII Anicia Macrina marito et filiis
EDCS-28600226.translation: To Civilis, decurion of the colony, aged 50, and Gaius Iulius Florus, son of Gaius, decurion of the colony, aged 20, and Iulia Festa, aged 12. Anicia Macrina set this up for her husband and sons.
EDCS-28600226.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Anicia Macrina to her husband, Civilis, a decurion, and her two sons, Gaius Iulius Florus, also a decurion, and Iulia Festa, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600228.text_edition: issia adulescenti ex provincia inferiore Aelia Victoria M Auggustorum nnostrorum fratri
EDCS-28600228.translation: To [---]issia, a young man from the lower province. Aelia Victoria, a freedwoman of our Augusti, to her brother.
EDCS-28600228.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelia Victoria, a freedwoman of the emperors, for her brother, a young man from a lower province, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600229.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-28600229.text_edition: In hanc arcam posita est Aurelia Macrina quae vixit annos XXXV et Aurelius Iustinianus filius pius qui vixit annum unum et menses quinque
EDCS-28600229.translation: In this sarcophagus is laid Aurelia Macrina, who lived 35 years, and Aurelius Iustinianus, her devoted son, who lived one year and five months.
EDCS-28600229.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Aurelia Macrina and her son, Aurelius Iustinianus, are interred in a sarcophagus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600230.text_edition: Titulum quem testamento fieri iussit faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600230.translation: He arranged for the inscription to be made which he ordered to be done in his will.
EDCS-28600230.summary: This inscription records that a monument or tablet was made according to the will of the deceased, indicating a funerary context, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600233.text_edition: Deo Silvano Domestico curans Hatena procurator ducenarius
EDCS-28600233.translation: To the domestic god Silvanus, Hatena, a ducenarian procurator, caring for it.
EDCS-28600233.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus Domesticus by Hatena, a ducenarian procurator, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600235.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno  Aurelius Iustianus decurio et Ulpius Silvinianus duplicarius alae PaXnnoniorum#Pannoniorum#PXNO pro se et suis
EDCS-28600235.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Aurelius Iustianus, a decurion, and Ulpius Silvinianus, a duplicarius of the Ala Pannoniorum, for themselves and their kin.
EDCS-28600235.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Aurelius Iustianus, a decurion, and Ulpius Silvinianus, a duplicarius of an ala of Pannonian cavalry, for themselves and their families, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600236.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Aurelii Sabinianus et Maximus et Apollinarius saccerdotes votum libentes laeti posuerunt
EDCS-28600236.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Aurelii Sabinianus, Maximus, and Apollinarius, priests, willingly and joyfully fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-28600236.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by three priests, Aurelii Sabinianus, Maximus, and Apollinarius, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600243.text_edition: Marci Quinti Valeri Severiani equitis cohortis III praetoriae qui vixit nnos XVIIII menses VIIII dies VIII Flavia Ianuaria mater viva sibi et eidem Severiano optimo carissimoque posuit
EDCS-28600243.translation: To Marcus Quintus Valerius Severianus, a cavalryman of the Third Praetorian Cohort, who lived 19 years, 9 months, and 8 days. Flavia Ianuaria, his mother, while alive, set this up for herself and for the same Severianus, a most excellent and beloved son.
EDCS-28600243.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Flavia Ianuaria for herself and her son, Marcus Quintus Valerius Severianus, a cavalryman of the Cohors III Praetoria, during her lifetime, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600246.text_edition: Dalmata miles cohortis II Alpinorum vexillarius nnorum XXX stipendiorum XVI
EDCS-28600246.translation: Dalmata, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Alpinorum, standard-bearer, 30 years old, with 16 years of service.
EDCS-28600246.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dalmata, a standard-bearer of the Second Cohort of Alpinorum, detailing his age and years of military service.

EDCS-28600248.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus divi Nervae f(ilius) Aug(ustus) ---
EDCS-28600248.translation: Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus, son of the deified Nerva, Augustus [---].
EDCS-28600248.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the Emperor Trajan, identifying him as the son of the deified Nerva.

EDCS-28600249.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription made by Carminius, a prefect, to the deity Somnus.

EDCS-28600250.text_edition: Libero et Liberae sacrum Marcus Ulpius Fronto Aemilianus decurio municipii Cibalensium libens fecit
EDCS-28600250.translation: Sacred to Liber and Libera, Marcus Ulpius Fronto Aemilianus, decurion of the municipality of Cibalae, willingly made this.
EDCS-28600250.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription for Liber and Libera, made by Marcus Ulpius Fronto Aemilianus, a decurion of Cibalae.

EDCS-28600251.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Aponio Memmio Celeri tribuno militum legionis primae Italicae Iulia Pia coniugi bene merenti et Aponia Gallitia fratri
EDCS-28600251.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Aponius Memmius Celer, military tribune of the First Italic Legion, a well-deserving husband, Julia Pia [made this] and Aponia Gallitia [made this] for her brother.
EDCS-28600251.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Aponius Memmius Celer, a military tribune, by his wife Julia Pia and his sister Aponia Gallitia.

EDCS-28600252.text_edition: Iovi Depulsori pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Domnae Augustae Marcus Sperati dispensatoris vikarius
EDCS-28600252.translation: To Jupiter Depulsor, for the well-being of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Domna Augusta, Marcus Speratus, assistant to the procurator, [made this].
EDCS-28600252.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Depulsor for the health of Emperor Caracalla and Empress Julia Domna, made by Marcus Speratus, a deputy dispenser, likely around 212-217 AD.

EDCS-28600253.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Oppius Baebius beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Severianae pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Augusto II et Marcello consulibus
EDCS-28600253.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Oppius Baebius, consular beneficiarius of the Second Italic Legion, loyal and faithful Severian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family, when our lord Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus was consul for the second time with Marcellus.
EDCS-28600253.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Gaius Oppius Baebius, a consular beneficiarius, in 226 AD, during the consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander and Marcellus.

EDCS-28600254.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Britti filio Valerio decurioni alae II Aravacorum domo Hispano nnum L stipendiorum XXX et Claudiae Ianuariae coniugi eius et Claudiae Hispanillae filiae vivis ex testamento Flaccus decurio frater et Hispanilla filia heredes faciundum curaverunt
EDCS-28600254.translation: For Tiberius Claudius Valerius, son of Brittus, decurion of the Second Ala Aravacorum, from Hispania, 50 years old, with 30 years of service, and for Claudia Ianuaria, his wife, and Claudia Hispanilla, his daughter, all living; Flaccus, a decurion and his brother, and Hispanilla, his daughter, as heirs, took care of having this monument made according to his will.
EDCS-28600254.summary: This is a funerary monument for Tiberius Claudius Valerius, a decurion from Hispania, and his family, commissioned by his brother Flaccus and daughter Hispanilla according to his will.

EDCS-28600255.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Super decurio alae praetoriae civium Romanorum ex summo curatore alae I civium Romanorum annorum XXXII stipendiorum XVI hic situs est Marcus Ulpius Similis sesquiplcarius alae I civium Romanorum frater et Ulpia Siscia soror fratri pientissimo iuventutique eius parum sibi frugi faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28600255.translation: Marcus Ulpius Super, decurion of the Praetorian Ala of Roman Citizens, formerly chief curator of the First Ala of Roman Citizens, 32 years old, with 16 years of service, is buried here. Marcus Ulpius Similis, a sesquiplicarius of the First Ala of Roman Citizens, his brother, and Ulpia Siscia, his sister, took care of having this made for their most devoted brother, who was too little provident for his youth.
EDCS-28600255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Super, a decurion, erected by his brother Marcus Ulpius Similis and his sister Ulpia Siscia.

EDCS-28600256.text_edition: Cominius  decurio missicius alae II Aravacorum annorum  hic situs est testamento fieri iussit sibi et Cominio Clementi fratri et Cominiae Antoniae sorori
EDCS-28600256.translation: Cominius, a decurion honorably discharged from the Second Ala Aravacorum, [---] years old, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will for himself, and for Cominius Clemens, his brother, and Cominia Antonia, his sister.
EDCS-28600256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cominius, a discharged decurion, and his siblings, commissioned according to his will.

EDCS-28600257.text_edition: Dibus Deabusque Aurelia votum solvit libens laeta
EDCS-28600257.translation: To the gods and goddesses, Aurelia willingly and joyfully fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28600257.summary: This inscription records Aurelia's joyful fulfillment of a vow to various deities, likely commemorating a successful prayer or offering.

EDCS-28600258.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius cornicularius procuratoris  Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600258.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, horn-blower of the imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600258.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, an official in the imperial administration, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600259.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Caius Iulius Demetrius votum libens
EDCS-28600259.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, Gaius Iulius Demetrius willingly [fulfilled] his vow.
EDCS-28600259.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Iulius Demetrius to Silvanus Domesticus, the god of the household woods, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28600260.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Mestrius Florus  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600260.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvestris, Mestrius Florus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600260.summary: This inscription records Mestrius Florus's willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus Silvestris, the god of wild woods.

EDCS-28600262.translation: To the Divine Hadrian, the people of Mursa, to their founder.
EDCS-28600262.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the citizens of Mursa to the deified Emperor Hadrian, acknowledging him as their founder, likely a statue base.

EDCS-28600263.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVII consul III pater patriae per legionem II adiutricem piam fidelem col(oniam) Aeliam Mursam fec(it)
EDCS-28600263.translation: The Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventeenth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, through the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, founded the colony Aelia Mursa.
EDCS-28600263.summary: This monumental inscription details the founding of the colony Aelia Mursa by Emperor Hadrian, indicating the involvement of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, likely from the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28600264.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinti Corvini Severiani Aemilianus procurator Auggustorum filio fecit
EDCS-28600264.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Quintus Corvinus Severianus, Aemilianus, procurator of the emperors, made this for his son.
EDCS-28600264.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aemilianus, an imperial procurator, for his deceased son, Quintus Corvinus Severianus.

EDCS-28600265.text_edition: Caius Iulius Geminius Capellianus legatus Augusti pro praetore Mursensibus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28600265.translation: Gaius Iulius Geminius Capellianus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, to the Mursenses by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28600265.summary: This inscription commemorates an act by Gaius Iulius Geminius Capellianus, an imperial governor, on behalf of the Mursenses, likely commissioned by the local council.

EDCS-28600266.text_edition: Titus Aurelius Avitus veteranus legionis II adiutricis nnorum LXXVI vivos sic sibi et Ulpiae Appianae coniugi pientissimae vivi sibi posuerunt
EDCS-28600266.translation: Titus Aurelius Avitus, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, aged 76, while living, they (he and his wife) placed this for themselves and for Ulpia Appiana, his most devoted wife, while living.
EDCS-28600266.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Aurelius Avitus, a veteran, and his wife Ulpia Appiana for themselves during their lifetimes, indicating his age and military service.

EDCS-28600267.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Secundo |(centurioni) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis qui vixit nnos XL menses VIII dies XV Aureli Annianus |(centurio) et Maximianus signifer parenti bene merito posuerunt
EDCS-28600267.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Aurelius Secundus, a centurion of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, who lived 40 years, 8 months, 15 days. Aureli Annianus, a centurion, and Maximianus, a standard-bearer, placed this for their well-deserving parent.
EDCS-28600267.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by two soldiers, Aureli Annianus (a centurion) and Maximianus (a standard-bearer), to their deceased father, Aurelius Secundus, a centurion of Legio XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-28600268.text_edition: Sisiae et Aurelio Sibiaeno vexillario et communicipii
EDCS-28600268.translation: To Sisia and Aurelius Sibiaenus, vexillarius, and of the fellow citizens.
EDCS-28600268.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, possibly by fellow citizens, to Sisia and Aurelius Sibiaenus, a vexillarius (standard-bearer).

EDCS-28600269.text_edition: Niger Bataronis filius Sveltrius eques ala I Aravacorum nnorum XXXVII stipendiorum XVII hic situs est Marcellus et Publius consobrini idem heredes posuIerunt#posuerunt#POSIERUNT
EDCS-28600269.translation: Niger, son of Bataro, Sveltrius, a cavalryman of the First Aravacan Wing, aged 37, with 17 years of service, lies here. Marcellus and Publius, his cousins and also his heirs, had this placed.
EDCS-28600269.summary: This is a funerary monument for Niger, a cavalryman, set up by his cousins and heirs, dating to a period when a Roman auxiliary cavalry unit was stationed in the area.

EDCS-28600271.text_edition: Caius Aemilius Cai filius Sergia Homullinus decurio coloniae Mursae ob honorem flaminatus tabernas L cum porticibus duplicibus in quibus mercatus ageretur pecunia sua fecit
EDCS-28600271.translation: Gaius Aemilius Homullinus, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, decurion of the colony of Mursa, for the honor of the flaminate, built with his own money 50 shops with double porticoes, in which a market might be held.
EDCS-28600271.summary: This inscription records the public benefaction of Gaius Aemilius Homullinus, a decurion of Mursa, who funded the construction of shops and porticoes in honor of his flaminate, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600272.text_edition: Dis Manibus Augenis dulcis animae quae vixit nnos VIII dies XXV Valerius Aug
EDCS-28600272.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Augenis, a sweet soul, who lived 8 years and 25 days. Valerius Aug[ustalis/ustinus].
EDCS-28600272.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of a young child named Augenis by Valerius Aug[ustalis or Augustinus].

EDCS-28600274.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Callimorphus Augustalis coloniae pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600274.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius Callimorphus, Augustalis of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-28600274.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Publius Aelius Callimorphus, an Augustalis of a colony, on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-28600275.text_edition: Sacrum Dis Magnis Maioribus et Sanctissimae Sanctitati voto libens posuit Marcus Flavius Felix
EDCS-28600275.translation: Sacred to the Great Elder Gods and to the Most Holy Sanctity. Marcus Flavius Felix willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-28600275.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the 'Great Elder Gods' and 'Most Holy Sanctity' by Marcus Flavius Felix.

EDCS-28600276.text_edition: Iustus  cives Sopianenses vivus sibi et  cuO(m)#cu(m)#CO qu[a vixit &
EDCS-28600276.translation: Justus, a citizen of Sopianae, alive, for himself and with whom he lived...
EDCS-28600276.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tomb, that Justus, a citizen of Sopianae, dedicated to himself while still alive, possibly also for his partner.

EDCS-28600277.text_edition: Deo Libero Patri Aurelius Conserus ex procuratore armamentarii instrumentorumqcui viribus suis inseruit per instantia Venanti filii sui vineae arpennes CCCC ex his V CVPENS V TERMINIID V VALLESIBUS V caballi RI
EDCS-28600277.translation: To Father Liber, Aurelius Conserus, former procurator of the armamentarium, who with his own resources incorporated instruments, through the diligence of his son Venantius, 400 iugera of vineyards, of these 5 CVPENS, 5 TERMINIID, 5 VALLESIBUS, RI horses.
EDCS-28600277.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Father Liber by Aurelius Conserus, a former procurator, detailing a significant endowment of vineyards and possibly other assets, achieved through his son's efforts.

EDCS-28600278.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the god Liber Pater, with the name Sext[us/a] likely being that of the dedicant or a related party.

EDCS-28600279.text_edition: cilla c-rissi pietati vit et SAP mater
EDCS-28600279.translation: ..., a pious and wise mother, lived...
EDCS-28600279.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for a woman described as a pious and wise mother.

EDCS-28600281.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Domitius Magnus praefectus alae X X alarBum#alarum#ALABUM Severianae et=IE#et#IE I TPhracE(um)#Thrac(um)#PHRAE veteranae
EDCS-28600281.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, Domitius Magnus, prefect of the ala Severiana and of the veteran ala I Thracum, [dedicates this].
EDCS-28600281.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aesculapius and Hygia by Domitius Magnus, an equestrian officer holding two prefectures in cavalry units, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600282.text_edition: Genio Titi Iuli Oviniani exs sic voto solvit merito libens laetus
EDCS-28600282.translation: To the Genius of Titus Iulius Ovinianus, having escaped, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, joyful.
EDCS-28600282.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow made by an unnamed individual to the Genius of Titus Iulius Ovinianus, possibly after a fortunate escape, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600283.text_edition: Herculi Augusto pro salute dddominorum nnnostrorum Augggustorum Oetcius VB Corvinus I Faustino et Rufino consulibus
EDCS-28600283.translation: To Hercules Augustus, for the health of our lords the Augusti, Oetcius Corvinus, on the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus.
EDCS-28600283.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Hercules Augustus made by Oetcius Corvinus for the well-being of the Augusti, dated to the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus in 210 AD.

EDCS-28600284.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Severi et Antonini Auggustorum [[et Gaetae n]]obilissmi Caesaris Caius Iulius Euticus sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600284.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of our lords Severus and Antoninus Augusti [[and Geta nobilissimus Caesar]], Gaius Iulius Euticus willingly and deservedly fulfilled this vow.
EDCS-28600284.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Iulius Euticus for the health of the Severan emperors and Geta, likely dating to between 198 and 209 AD, with the mention of Geta subsequently erased.

EDCS-28600285.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Aurelius Regulianus actarius alae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600285.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aurelius Regulianus, accountant of the ala, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600285.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Aurelius Regulianus, an accountant of a cavalry unit, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600286.text_edition: Silvanas Auggustas Aurelius Lipor veteranus alae et Aurelius Valens miles legionis II adiutricis piae fE(idelis)#f(idelis)#E Severianae fil=ET(ius)#fil(ius)#ET eius et Aurelia Severa filia
EDCS-28600286.translation: To the Augustan Silvanas, Aurelius Lipor, veteran of an ala, and Aurelius Valens, soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana, his son, and Aurelia Severa, his daughter.
EDCS-28600286.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Augustan Silvanas made by Aurelius Lipor, a veteran cavalryman, and his children, Aurelius Valens (a legionary) and Aurelia Severa, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600287.text_edition: Mucatra duplicarius alae I Thracum veteranae Antoninianae pro salIute#salute#SAIUTE sua et suaorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600287.translation: Mucatra, duplicarius of the veteran ala I Thracum Antoniniana, for his own health and that of his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600287.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Mucatra, a duplicarius in a cavalry unit, made for his own and his family's well-being, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD during the Antonine dynasty.

EDCS-28600288.text_edition: Aurelio Iulio ex decurione alae I Thracum vixit annos et Aureliae Ie vivae coniugi eius et Aureliae Maximianae vixit nnos XXVII et Aurelio Caesiano veterano cohortis X praetoriae vixit annos  et Aurelio Caesio evokato sic exsercitatori eqquitum praetorianorum vixit nnos X filiis eorum et Aurelio Crescentino militi legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum III vixit nnos XXI Claudius Pertinax strator consularis parentibus bene merentibus faciundum curavit
EDCS-28600288.translation: For Aurelius Iulius, formerly a decurion of the ala I Thracum, who lived for [---] years, and for Aurelia [Ie] (alive), his wife, and for Aurelia Maximiana, who lived 27 years, and for Aurelius Caesianus, veteran of the X Praetorian Cohort, who lived for [---] years, and for Aurelius Caesius, evocatus and exercitator of praetorian cavalry, who lived 10 years, their sons. And for Aurelius Crescentinus, soldier of Legio II Adiutrix, with 3 years of service, who lived 21 years. Claudius Pertinax, strator consularis, had this made for his well-deserving parents.
EDCS-28600288.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Claudius Pertinax for his parents Aurelius Iulius (a former decurion) and Aurelia [Ie], and their children and grandchildren, listing their military careers and ages, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600290.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Probo veterano legionis II adiutricis qui vixit nnos LIIII patri et Caio Iulio Honorato militi cohortis |(milliariae) Numidarum stipendiorum III fratri qui vixit nnos XXII et Aeliae Decoratae matri vivae Caius Iulius Probianus candidatus legionis supra scriptae - quam et ceteri fratres patri et fratri matri vivae faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28600290.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Iulius Probus, veteran of Legio II Adiutrix, who lived 54 years, his father. And for Gaius Iulius Honoratus, soldier of the Cohors I Milliaria Numidarum, with 3 years of service, his brother, who lived 22 years. And for Aelia Decorata, his mother (alive). Gaius Iulius Probianus, candidatus of the aforementioned legion, and his other brothers, had this made for their father, brother, and living mother.
EDCS-28600290.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the deceased Iulius Probus (a legionary veteran) and his brother Gaius Iulius Honoratus (a cohort soldier), and for their living mother Aelia Decorata, commissioned by their brother Gaius Iulius Probianus and other siblings, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600291.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimiae Iunillae coniugi karae quae vixit annos XXV Publius Aelius Constans quaestor armorum legionis II adiutricis vivus sibi fecit et Constantinae filiae  optamus cuncti
EDCS-28600291.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Septimia Iunilla, beloved wife, who lived 25 years. Publius Aelius Constans, quaestor armorum of Legio II Adiutrix, made this for himself while living, and for Constantina, his daughter. We all hope for [her/them].
EDCS-28600291.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Aelius Constans, a legionary quaestor armorum, for his beloved wife Septimia Iunilla and for himself while living, also mentioning his daughter Constantina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600292.text_edition: Terso Precionis filius Scordiscus eques alae Frontonianae turma Lobasini armorum custos annorum XXX stipendiorum XVI hic situs est
EDCS-28600292.translation: Terso, son of Precio, a Scordiscan, a horseman of the Ala Frontoniana, of the troop of Lobasinus, keeper of arms, aged 30, with 16 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-28600292.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a Scordiscan cavalryman and arms keeper of the Ala Frontoniana, dating to the Roman Imperial period based on military structure.

EDCS-28600293.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpio Valentino equiti alae annorum XLVX#XLV#XLX hic situs est et Valentino filio annorum XII Meitimae f<iliae=EC>#f<iliae=EC>#FILIE=EC Aelius Provincialis heres posuit
EDCS-28600293.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Ulpius Valentinus, a horseman of the ala, aged 45, who is buried here, and for his son Valentinus, aged 12, and for his daughter Meitima. Aelius Provincialis, his heir, set this up.
EDCS-28600293.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by an heir, Aelius Provincialis, to Ulpius Valentinus, a cavalryman, and his two children, Valentinus and Meitima.

EDCS-28600294.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonius Filoquirius Augustalis coloniae Aquincensium Vibiae Serapiae
EDCS-28600294.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonius Filoquirius, Augustalis of the colony of Aquincum, for Vibia Serapia.
EDCS-28600294.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Antonius Filoquirius, an Augustalis of Aquincum, to Vibia Serapia, likely a family member or patroness.

EDCS-28600295.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aeliae Marcellae coniugi pientissimae vixit annos XVIIII et Aeliae Verae socrae sic vixit annos LV et sibi vivus et Aurelio Magniano filio suo Marcus Aurelius Zosimus posuit si qui huic titulo manus intulerit extra bonos mores opto tale dolorem AT--V
EDCS-28600295.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius Zosimus set this up while living, for Aelia Marcella, his most devoted wife, who lived 19 years, and for Aelia Vera, his mother-in-law, who lived 55 years, and for himself and his son Aurelius Magnianus. If anyone lays hands on this title against good morals, I pray for such pain AT--V.
EDCS-28600295.summary: This is a funerary monument for a family, set up by Marcus Aurelius Zosimus for his wife, mother-in-law, himself, and his son, including a curse against anyone who defiles the inscription, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600296.translation: To Artio, son of Miletumarus, C.
EDCS-28600296.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for Artio, son of Miletumarus.

EDCS-28600298.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Sabinus vilicus stationis et Claudia Pusinna parentes filiae Sabinae nnorum XIIII hic sita est Dis Manibus
EDCS-28600298.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Sabinus, overseer of the station, and Claudia Pusinna, parents, for their daughter Sabina, aged 14, who is buried here.
EDCS-28600298.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by parents, Marcus Ulpius Sabinus and Claudia Pusinna, to their 14-year-old daughter Sabina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600299.text_edition: Caio Iulio Vale--- vixit an(nos) LX dies
EDCS-28600299.translation: For Gaius Julius Vale[---], he lived 60 years and [---] days.
EDCS-28600299.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Vale[---], noting his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600300.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, recording Matomarus, son of Ttus.

EDCS-28600301.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marcus Ulpius Italicus annorum XL et Aelia Publi filia Verodumna annorum XXX hic siti sunt Ulpii Veranus Proculus Verina filii et heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28600301.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Italicus, aged 40, and Aelia Publi filia Verodumna, aged 30, are buried here. Their children and heirs, Ulpii Veranus, Proculus, and Verina, saw to its erection.
EDCS-28600301.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Ulpius Italicus and Aelia Verodumna, erected by their children and heirs, Ulpii Veranus, Proculus, and Verina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28600303.text_edition: Aesculapio  et Hygiae Augustis Aurelius Artemidorus beneficiarius legati legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae agens curam custodiarum votum solvit libens merito Modesto et Probo consulibus
EDCS-28600303.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia Augusti, Aurelius Artemidorus, beneficiarius of the legate of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana, performing the duty of guarding, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-28600303.summary: This is a votive inscription to Aesculapius and Hygia, made by Aurelius Artemidorus, a beneficiarius of Legio II Adiutrix, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow, dated to 228 AD by the named consuls.

EDCS-28600304.text_edition: Asclepio et Hygiae Marcius Marcellus medicus sub capsario Valerio Praesente evokato sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600304.translation: To Asclepius and Hygieia, Marcius Marcellus, a doctor under the supervision of Valerius Praesens, an evocatus (veteran recalled to service), willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600304.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcius Marcellus, a doctor, dedicated to the gods Asclepius and Hygieia, fulfilling a vow under the supervision of Valerius Praesens.

EDCS-28600306.text_edition: Deo Arimanio Libella leo fratribus voto dicavit
EDCS-28600306.translation: To the god Arimanius, Libella Leo dedicated this by vow for his brothers.
EDCS-28600306.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Arimanius by Libella Leo on behalf of his brothers.

EDCS-28600307.text_edition: Danuvio Defluenti Haterius Callinicus votum V
EDCS-28600307.translation: To the Flowing Danube, Haterius Callinicus made a vow.
EDCS-28600307.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the personified river Danube by Haterius Callinicus.

EDCS-28600308.text_edition: August- ceterisque dis huiusce loci Caius Valerius Sabinianus legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-28600308.translation: To Augustus and the other gods of this place, Caius Valerius Sabinianus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power.
EDCS-28600308.summary: This is a dedication by Caius Valerius Sabinianus, a Roman governor, to Augustus and local deities, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-28600310.text_edition: Dis Conservatoribus Caius Iulius Geminus Capellianus legatus Augusti pro praetore sodalis Titius
EDCS-28600310.translation: To the Preserving Gods, Caius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, imperial legate with pro-praetorian power, and Titius, a sodalis (member of a priestly college).
EDCS-28600310.summary: This is a dedication to the Preserving Gods by Caius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, a provincial governor, and Titius, a priest.

EDCS-28600312.text_edition: Fortunae Reduci Publius Cosinius Felix vir clarissimus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore
EDCS-28600312.translation: To Fortuna Redux (Fortune who brings back), Publius Cosinius Felix, a most distinguished man, imperial legate of the Augusti with pro-praetorian power.
EDCS-28600312.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Redux by Publius Cosinius Felix, a vir clarissimus and governor, likely from a period with multiple emperors (Augusti).

EDCS-28600313.translation: To the Genius of the centuria, Iulius Aelianus, tesserarius (orderly), placed this.
EDCS-28600313.summary: This is a dedication by Iulius Aelianus, a military orderly, to the Genius (guardian spirit) of his centuria.

EDCS-28600314.text_edition: Genio collegio scaenicorum Titus Flavius Secundus monitor dono dedit
EDCS-28600314.translation: To the Genius of the guild of actors, Titus Flavius Secundus, a monitor (supervisor or instructor), gave this as a gift.
EDCS-28600314.summary: This is a gift inscription by Titus Flavius Secundus, a monitor, dedicated to the Genius (guardian spirit) of the guild of actors.

EDCS-28600315.text_edition: Genio  Imperatoris Publi Licini Gallieni Invicti Augusti Clementius Silvius vir egregius agens vices praesidis et Valerius Marcellinus praefectus legionis protector Augusti nostri agens vices legati municipes ex provincia Raetia solverunt laeti libentes merito Paterno et Archesilao consulibus
EDCS-28600315.translation: To the Genius of the Emperor Publius Licinius Gallienus, the Invincible Augustus, Clementius Silvius, a distinguished man acting as vice-president, and Valerius Marcellinus, prefect of the legion, protector of our Augustus, acting as vice-legate, and the citizens from the province of Raetia, happily, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled their vow, in the consulship of Paternus and Archesilaus.
EDCS-28600315.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Genius of Emperor Gallienus by Clementius Silvius and Valerius Marcellinus, along with citizens from Raetia, dated to 260 AD based on the consuls Paternus and Archesilaus.

EDCS-28600317.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Titus Flavius Victor tribunus militum pro praefecto legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600317.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Titus Flavius Victor, military tribune acting as prefect of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600317.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Flavius Victor, a military tribune and acting legionary prefect, dedicated to Hercules Augustus.

EDCS-28600318.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum  ob salutem et reditum domini nostri Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matris Augusti nostri et castrorum Gaius Iulius Carinus praefectus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis A[lexandrianae] Severianae ex trecenario votum solvit libens merito Maximo et Paterno consulibus
EDCS-28600318.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus, for the safety and return of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and of Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of our Augustus and of the camps. Gaius Julius Carinus, prefect of the Second Adjutrix legion, dutiful, loyal, Alexandrian, Severian, having been a trecenarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of Maximus and Paternus.
EDCS-28600318.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Carinus, a prefect, dedicating a monument to Hercules Augustus for the safe return of Emperor Severus Alexander and his mother Julia Mamaea, dating to AD 233.

EDCS-28600319.text_edition: Iovi Accioni Patrio Suetrius Sabinus legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-28600319.translation: To Jupiter Accion Patrius. Suetrius Sabinus, legate of Augustus with praetorian power.
EDCS-28600319.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Accion Patrius by Suetrius Sabinus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-28600320.text_edition: Dis Reducibus Patriis Suetrius Sabinus legatus Augusti pro praetore et consularis
EDCS-28600320.translation: To the Returning Ancestral Gods. Suetrius Sabinus, legate of Augustus with praetorian power and a former consul.
EDCS-28600320.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the ancestral gods of return (Dis Reducibus Patriis) by Suetrius Sabinus, an imperial legate and former consul.

EDCS-28600322.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ob salutem Hadriani Augusti vici X publice posuerunt
EDCS-28600322.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of Hadrian Augustus, the tenth vicus publicly erected this.
EDCS-28600322.summary: This inscription records the public dedication of a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the tenth vicus (neighborhood) for the safety of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-28600323.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute et reditu et victoria Imperatorum duorum Augustorum Titus Aelius Celsus cum Petronia Respecta coniuge et Tito Aelio CelsinoU#Celsino#CELSINU filio Kalendis Augustis dedicaverunt feliciter Macrino et Celso consulibus votum libentes merito  Marti  Titus Aelius Celsinus  Genio Augusto Titus Aelius Celsus  Minervae Aug(ustae) Petr(onia) Resp(ecta)
EDCS-28600323.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety, return, and victory of the two Augusti Emperors. Titus Aelius Celsus with his wife Petronia Respecta and son Titus Aelius Celsinus dedicated this happily on the Kalends of August, in the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus, having willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow. To Mars, Titus Aelius Celsinus; to the Augustan Genius, Titus Aelius Celsus; to Minerva Augusta, Petronia Respecta.
EDCS-28600323.summary: This inscription records a family's dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the welfare of two emperors, along with individual dedications to Mars, Genius Augustus, and Minerva Augusta, dating to AD 217.

EDCS-28600324.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Rufus mensor legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600324.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aelius Rufus, surveyor of the Second Adjutrix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600324.summary: This inscription records Aelius Rufus, a military surveyor, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600325.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Lucius Aemilius Optatus tribunus militum legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600325.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Aemilius Optatus, military tribune of the Second Adjutrix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600325.summary: This inscription records Lucius Aemilius Optatus, a military tribune, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600326.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Arsinius Sita veteranus legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600326.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Arsinius Sita, veteran of the Second Adjutrix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600326.summary: This inscription records Titus Arsinius Sita, a military veteran, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600327.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Pompeius decurio coloniae Aquincensium votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-28600327.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Pompeius, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600327.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Pompeius, a decurion from Aquincum, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600328.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Batsio Gupi filius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600328.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Batsius, son of Gupius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600328.summary: This inscription records Batsius, son of Gupius, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600329.text_edition: [Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute] [Imperatoris Caesaris ?] []]  Claudius Pompeius Faustus decurio coloniae Aquincensium aedilicius IIviralis praefectus collegii fabrum itemque patronus duxit collegium supra scriptum in ambulativis V Kalendas Augustas [Imperatore  et ?] [ consulibus]
EDCS-28600329.translation: [To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being] [of the Emperor Caesar ---] Claudius Pompeius Faustus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, twice aedile, twice duumvir, prefect of the guild of craftsmen and also its patron, led the aforementioned guild in the walking-places on the fifth day before the Kalends of August, [when the Emperor --- and ---] were consuls.
EDCS-28600329.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Claudius Pompeius Faustus, a local dignitary, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the emperor's well-being, commemorating his leadership of a craftsmen's guild on a specific date.

EDCS-28600330.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Valens beneficiarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600330.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tiberius Claudius Valens, a beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600330.summary: This is a votive inscription by Tiberius Claudius Valens, a beneficiarius, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600331.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavius Decimus votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-28600331.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Flavius Decimus willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600331.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Decimus, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600332.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus HostiFus#Hostius#HOSTFUS Fortunatus beneficiarius procuratoris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600332.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Quintus Hostius Fortunatus, a beneficiarius of the procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600332.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Hostius Fortunatus, an assistant to a procurator, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600333.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Livius Iuvenalis beneficiarius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens animo
EDCS-28600333.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Livius Iuvenalis, a beneficiarius of the imperial procurator, willingly and with a grateful spirit fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600333.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Livius Iuvenalis, an assistant to an imperial procurator, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600334.text_edition: I     NLIL M  Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Verus BENE VONLIL solvit libens merito m
EDCS-28600334.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Juno, Verus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600334.summary: This is a votive inscription by Verus, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno.

EDCS-28600335.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Oratius Proculus eques cohortis |(milliariae) Maurorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600335.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Oratius Proculus, a cavalryman of the milliary cohort of the Mauri, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600335.summary: This is a votive inscription by Oratius Proculus, a cavalryman, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600336.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  pro salute domini nostri Marci Aureli A[nton]ini Pii Felicis Augusti Lucius Septimius Constantinus optio spei legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito [Imperatore Antonino] et Advento consulibus
EDCS-28600336.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our master Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Lucius Septimius Constantinus, optio of the 'hope' detachment of the Second Adiutrix Legion, dutiful, loyal, Antoninian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Emperor Antoninus and Adventus were consuls.
EDCS-28600336.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Septimius Constantinus, an optio of the Second Adiutrix Legion, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), dating to 218 CE.

EDCS-28600337.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Lucius Septimius Lister veteranus alae I Etureorum ex IC votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600337.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Septimius Lister, a veteran of the First Ala Itureorum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600337.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Septimius Lister, a veteran of a cavalry unit, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600338.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Victor beneficiarius procuratoris votum libens merito
EDCS-28600338.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Valerius Victor, a beneficiarius of the procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600338.summary: This is a votive inscription by Valerius Victor, an assistant to a procurator, dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28600339.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Vibius Augustalis beneficiarius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-28600339.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Vibius Augustalis, a beneficiary of the Augustan procurator, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600339.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Vibius Augustalis, a beneficiarius of the imperial procurator, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600340.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo votum vovit Victor Ressati libertus libens
EDCS-28600340.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Victor, freedman of Ressatus, gladly made a vow.
EDCS-28600340.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Victor, a freedman of Ressatus, making a vow.

EDCS-28600341.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Carus beneficiarius procuratoris votum libens merito
EDCS-28600341.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Carus, a beneficiary of the procurator, gladly and deservedly made a vow.
EDCS-28600341.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Carus, a beneficiarius of the procurator, making a vow.

EDCS-28600342.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Quintianus votum libens merito
EDCS-28600342.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Quintianus gladly and deservedly made a vow.
EDCS-28600342.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Quintianus, making a vow.

EDCS-28600343.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Ulpius Romanus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-28600343.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Ulpius Romanus, a veteran of the Second Adjutrix Legion, Pious and Faithful, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600343.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Ulpius Romanus, a veteran of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600344.text_edition: Iovi Optimo CO   Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Ursinus beneficiarius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600344.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Ursinus, a beneficiary of the Augustan procurator, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600344.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Ulpius Ursinus, a beneficiarius of the imperial procurator, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600345.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Dianae Patr sacrum Caius Iulius Artemo tribunus militum legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600345.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Diana of Patara, Gaius Julius Artemo, tribune of soldiers of the Second Adjutrix Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600345.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Diana Patr by Gaius Julius Artemo, a military tribune of Legio II Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600346.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dis Deabusque omnibus GAI Atilius Pollianus decurio aedilicius et Pollio Augustalis coloniae Aquincensium votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-28600346.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and all gods and goddesses, Gaius Atilius Pollianus, decurion and aedilician, and Pollio Augustalis of the colony of Aquincum, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-28600346.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses by Gaius Atilius Pollianus, a decurion and former aedile, and Pollio Augustalis, both from the colony of Aquincum, fulfilling their vows.

EDCS-28600347.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio |(centuriae)  Septimius Cogitatus custos armorum et Septimius Carus milites legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae votum solverunt libentes merito Pompeiano  et Paeligniano consulibus
EDCS-28600347.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Genius of the centuria, Septimius Cogitatus, keeper of arms, and Septimius Carus, soldiers of the Second Adjutrix Legion, Pious, Faithful, and Severan, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vows during the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus (241 AD).
EDCS-28600347.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of a centuria by Septimius Cogitatus, an arms keeper, and Septimius Carus, soldiers of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana, made in 241 AD.

EDCS-28600348.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  et Iunoni Aurelius Vindex duplarius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-28600348.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno, Aurelius Vindex, a duplarius of the Second Adjutrix Legion, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600348.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno by Aurelius Vindex, a duplarius of Legio II Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28600349.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Gaius Titius Eutyches votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600349.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to Juno Regina, Gaius Titius Eutyches willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600349.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated by Gaius Titius Eutyches to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28600350.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Laribus militaribus ceterisque dis Caius Iulius Pisibanus Maximus Aemilius Papus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600350.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the military Lares, and to all other gods, Caius Iulius Pisibanus Maximus, and Aemilius Papus, laticlavius tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-28600350.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by two individuals, including a laticlavius tribune of the Legio II Adiutrix, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the military Lares, and other gods, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28600351.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Caius Roscius Crispinus decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600351.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Preserver, Caius Roscius Crispinus, decurion of the colony, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600351.summary: This is a votive dedication by Caius Roscius Crispinus, a decurion, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28600352.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dulceno sic Heliopolitano sacrum feciit Aurelius Secundus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae pro se et suis posuit votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600352.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dulcenus, likewise to Heliopolitanus, I, Aurelius Secundus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Pia Legion, made this sacred offering for himself and his family, and willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600352.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Aurelius Secundus, a veteran of Legio II Adiutrix Pia, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dulcenus Heliopolitanus, made for himself and his family, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600353.text_edition: Hammoni Iovi Optimo Maximo et Laribus militaribus ceterisque Dis Marcus Caecilius Rufinus Marianus tribunus laticlavius legionis IV Flaviae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600353.translation: To Hammon Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the military Lares, and to all other gods, Marcus Caecilius Rufinus Marianus, laticlavius tribune of the Fourth Flavian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600353.summary: This is a votive dedication by Marcus Caecilius Rufinus Marianus, a laticlavius tribune of Legio IIII Flavia, to Hammon Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the military Lares, and other gods, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28600354.text_edition: Libero Augusto Caius Clodius Saturninus primus pilus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-28600354.translation: To Liber Augustus, Caius Clodius Saturninus, primus pilus of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion.
EDCS-28600354.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Caius Clodius Saturninus, a primus pilus of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, to Liber Augustus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600355.text_edition: Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Valerius Emeritus ex decurione alae I Thracum posuit
EDCS-28600355.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus, Valerius Emeritus, formerly a decurion of the First Thracian Ala, placed this.
EDCS-28600355.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication by Valerius Emeritus, a former decurion of Ala I Thracum, to Liber Pater Augustus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600356.text_edition: Libero et Liberae  Augustis Marcus Aurelius Latio aram votum posuit pro salute Marci Aureli Iovini frumentarii legionis  II adiutricis stipendiorum VIIII
EDCS-28600356.translation: To Liber and Libera Augusti, Marcus Aurelius Latio placed an altar and a vow for the health of Marcus Aurelius Iovinus, a frumentarius of the Second Adiutrix Legion with nine years of service.
EDCS-28600356.summary: This inscription is an altar dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Latio to Liber and Libera Augusti for the health of Marcus Aurelius Iovinus, a frumentarius of Legio II Adiutrix, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600357.text_edition: Liberae=EI#Liberae#LIBEREI Aucci Sineca sexvir coloniae Aquincensium pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600357.translation: To Libera, Auccius Sineca, a sexvir of the colony of Aquincum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-28600357.summary: This is a votive dedication by Auccius Sineca, a sexvir of Colonia Aquincensium, to Libera, made for himself and his family, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600358.text_edition: Maioribus sanctis Lucius Naevius Campanus kastrensis legionis IIII Flaviae pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600358.translation: To the Holy Ancestors, Lucius Naevius Campanus, a camp follower of the Fourth Flavian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-28600358.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Lucius Naevius Campanus, a 'kastrensis' of Legio IIII Flavia, to the 'Sancti Maiores' (Holy Ancestors) for his own and his family's health, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28600359.text_edition: Marti Augusto pro salute et incolumitate domini nostri Carini Pii Felicis Augusti Aelius Paternianus vir egregius praefectus legionis II adiutricis agens vices legati votum solvit libens merito
