EDCS-28600359.translation: To Mars Augustus, for the safety and well-being of our lord Carinus Pius Felix Augustus, Aelius Paternianus, a most distinguished man, prefect of the Second Adiutrix legion, acting as legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600359.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Augustus by Aelius Paternianus, a prefect acting as legate of Legio II Adiutrix, made for the well-being of Emperor Carinus (283-285 CE).

EDCS-28600360.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum Caius Cuspius Secundus exercitator legionis II adiutricis pro salute militum et sua quod evocatus vovit centurio solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600360.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Gaius Cuspius Secundus, exercitator of the Second Adiutrix legion, for the safety of his soldiers and himself, fulfilled his vow as a centurion, willingly and deservedly, which he vowed when he was evoked.
EDCS-28600360.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mars Augustus by Gaius Cuspius Secundus, an exercitator of Legio II Adiutrix, made for the safety of himself and his soldiers, fulfilling a vow he made upon his promotion to centurion.

EDCS-28600362.text_edition: Dis Militaribus et Genio loci pro salute et reditu Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Invicti Augusti Clodius Marcellinus S C tribuni militum legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae translatus ex legione X Fretensi Antoniniana numini eius semper devotissimus
EDCS-28600362.translation: To the Military Gods and the Genius of the place, for the safety and return of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Invictus Augustus, Clodius Marcellinus, S C (signifer centurionis/sacrum) of the tribune of the soldiers of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Antoniniana legion, transferred from the Tenth Fretensis Antoniniana legion, always most devoted to his divinity.
EDCS-28600362.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Military Gods and the Genius Loci by Clodius Marcellinus, a centurion of Legio II Adiutrix, for the safety and return of Emperor Caracalla (198-217 CE).

EDCS-28600363.text_edition: Dis Militaribus Salutaribus Haterius Saturninus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore cum Latroniano filio tribuno militum
EDCS-28600363.translation: To the Health-giving Military Gods, Haterius Saturninus, legate of the Augusti, with his son Latronianus, military tribune.
EDCS-28600363.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Health-giving Military Gods by Haterius Saturninus, a provincial governor, together with his son Latronianus, a military tribune, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600364.text_edition: Minitrae Aurelius Florianus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis pro salutem sic meam sic et omnium meorum numini eius deIdicavi#dedicavi#DIDICAVI Sabino  II et Venusto consulibus
EDCS-28600364.translation: To Minitra, Aurelius Florianus, a veteran from a beneficiarius consularis, dedicated this for my own safety and that of all my family, to her divinity, in the consulship of Sabinus II and Venustus (242 CE).
EDCS-28600364.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Minitra by Aurelius Florianus, a veteran, made for the safety of himself and his family in 242 CE.

EDCS-28600366.text_edition: Invicto Devo votum solvit libens merito P BO CO M CO T B
EDCS-28600366.translation: To the Unconquered God, P. Bo. Co. M. Co. T. B. willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600366.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Unconquered God by an individual, likely related to the Mithraic cult, whose name is abbreviated.

EDCS-28600367.text_edition: Deo Invicto Allidius Hermes votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600367.translation: To the Unconquered God, Allidius Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600367.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Unconquered God by Allidius Hermes, likely a freedman, probably associated with Mithraism.

EDCS-28600368.text_edition: Deo Invicto Cornelius Abascantus libertus Corneli Paulli primi pili legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600368.translation: To the Unconquered God, Cornelius Abascantus, freedman of Cornelius Paullus, primus pilus of the Second Adiutrix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600368.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Unconquered God by Cornelius Abascantus, a freedman of a senior centurion of Legio II Adiutrix, probably dedicated to Mithras.

EDCS-28600369.text_edition: Deo Invicto pro salute familiae Tiberi Hateri Saturnini legati Auggustorum pro praetore Arpocras pater posuit
EDCS-28600369.translation: To the Unconquered God, Arpocras the father placed this for the safety of the family of Tiberius Haterius Saturninus, legate of the Augusti.
EDCS-28600369.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Unconquered God by Arpocras, made for the safety of the family of Tiberius Haterius Saturninus, a provincial governor, indicating devotion to Mithras.

EDCS-28600370.text_edition: Deo Invicto  Mithrae Caius Iulius Castinus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore
EDCS-28600370.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Gaius Iulius Castinus, legate of the Augusti.
EDCS-28600370.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Unconquered God Mithras by Gaius Iulius Castinus, a provincial governor, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28600371.text_edition: Invicto Mythrae sic Nabarze Tiberius Pontius Pontianus tribunus laticlavius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis dat dedicat votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600371.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, thus Nabarze, Tiberius Pontius Pontianus, laticlavian tribune of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, gives, dedicates, and willingly and deservedly fulfills his vow.
EDCS-28600371.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mithras by Tiberius Pontius Pontianus, a military tribune, likely for a votive offering or a small monument.

EDCS-28600372.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute Sexti Iuventi NIL ex visu  Pannonius libertus
EDCS-28600372.translation: To the Unconquered Sun Mithras, for the health of Sextus Iuventus NIL, from a vision, Pannonius, his freedman, [dedicates this].
EDCS-28600372.summary: This is a votive dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Pannonius, a freedman, on behalf of Sextus Iuventus, apparently prompted by a vision.

EDCS-28600373.text_edition: Soli Invicto Valerius Vitalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600373.translation: To the Unconquered Sun, Valerius Vitalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600373.summary: This inscription records Valerius Vitalis fulfilling a vow to Sol Invictus, likely commemorating a successful request or event.

EDCS-28600374.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Marcus Aurelius Valens quot sic clavicularius vovit veteranus legionis II adiutricis Antoninianae persolvit Imperatore domino nostro Antonino II SacerdoteI#Sacerdote#SACERDOTI consulibus
EDCS-28600374.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, Marcus Aurelius Valens, clavicularius, veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix Antoniniana, fulfilled what he vowed, in the consulship of our lord Emperor Antoninus II Sacerdos.
EDCS-28600374.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Valens, a veteran and clavicularius, fulfilling a vow to Nemesis Augusta during the consulship of Emperor Antoninus II Sacerdos, dating it to a specific year.

EDCS-28600375.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Augustae  pro salute Aureliae Audentiae filiae suae et pro sua incolumitate Aurelius Audentius curator rei publicae Aquincensium e sacerdotalibus provinciae libenti animo suscepta  vota conplevit
EDCS-28600375.translation: To the Augustan goddess Nemesis, for the health of his daughter Aurelia Audentia and for his own safety, Aurelius Audentius, curator of the Aquincum municipality, from the priestly ranks of the province, completed the vows he had willingly undertaken.
EDCS-28600375.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Nemesis Augusta by Aurelius Audentius, a municipal curator and provincial priest, on behalf of his daughter and for his own well-being.

EDCS-28600376.text_edition: Neptuno Caius Iulius Geminus Capellianus legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-28600376.translation: To Neptune, Gaius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, [dedicates this].
EDCS-28600376.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Neptune by Gaius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, a high-ranking imperial official.

EDCS-28600377.text_edition: Numini Augusto et Genio Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini collegium Augustalium impendiis suis fecerunt praefecto Caio Iulio Crescente locus publice datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28600377.translation: To the Augustan Divinity and the Genius of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus, the college of Augustales made this at their own expense; the place was publicly given by decree of the decurions, with Gaius Iulius Crescens as prefect.
EDCS-28600377.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the imperial cult (Divinity and Genius of Antoninus Pius) by the Augustales college, constructed on publicly granted land by decree of the decurions under the prefect Gaius Iulius Crescens.

EDCS-28600378.text_edition: Nymphis Marcus FovNiacius#Foviacius#FONIACIUS Verus quinquennalis sacerdotalis dignissimis merito
EDCS-28600378.translation: To the most worthy Nymphs, Marcus Foviacius Verus, quinquennalis and sacerdotalis, deservedly [dedicates this].
EDCS-28600378.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs by Marcus Foviacius Verus, who held quinquennalis and sacerdotalis priestly offices, implying a votive offering or small shrine.

EDCS-28600379.text_edition: NyImphis#Nymphis#NIMPHIS sacrum Iulius Pusinnio immunis legionis II adiutricis pR(iae)#p(iae)#R fidelis votum libens merito
EDCS-28600379.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, Iulius Pusinnio, immunis of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600379.summary: This inscription records Iulius Pusinnio, an immunis (privileged soldier) of the Second Legion Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow to the Nymphs.

EDCS-28600380.text_edition: I(ovi) O(ptimo) M(aximo) D(olicheno) Harfuare=IInus#Harfuarenus#HARFUARIINUS Surus ex regione Dolica vico Arfuaris Silva votum solvit Modesto et Probo consulibus
EDCS-28600380.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Harfuarenus Surus from the Dolica region, Arfuaris Silva district, fulfilled his vow in the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-28600380.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Harfuarenus Surus, originating from a specific region, and is precisely dated to the consulship of Modestus and Probus.

EDCS-28600382.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri  Accius Maximus deIcurio#decurio#DIC coloniae Aquincensium quinquennalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600382.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris. Accius Maximus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum for five years, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600382.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to Silvanus Silvestris by Accius Maximus, a quinquennial decurion of Aquincum, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600383.text_edition: Silvano Domestico  Aurelius Ph=Fyylargus#Phyylargus#FYYLARGUS pro=OR#pro#POR se et suis  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600383.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus. Aurelius Phylargus, for himself and his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600383.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus Domesticus, made by Aurelius Phylargus on behalf of himself and his household.

EDCS-28600384.text_edition: Silvano Domestico  |(sacrum) Cornelius Genetivos sic decurio ex singularibus votum libens merito II Silanis consulibus
EDCS-28600384.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Cornelius Genetivos, thus a decurion from the singulars, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the second consulship of Silanus.
EDCS-28600384.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Cornelius Genetivos, a decurion, and can be dated precisely to a second consulship of a Silanus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600385.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Gaius Secundinius Sabinianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600385.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus. Gaius Secundinius Sabinianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600385.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Domesticus by Gaius Secundinius Sabinianus.

EDCS-28600387.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Lucius Seranus EFrotius#Erotius#FROTIUS IIIIIIvir aedilis decurio colI(oniae)#col(oniae)#COI Aquincensium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600387.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Lucius Seranus Erotius, a sevir, aedile, and decurion of the colony of Aquincum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600387.summary: This is a sacred votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus by Lucius Seranus Erotius, who held multiple civic offices in the colony of Aquincum, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600388.text_edition: Silvano Herbario Septimia Constantina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600388.translation: To Silvanus Herbaris. Septimia Constantina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28600388.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Silvanus Herbaris, made by Septimia Constantina.

EDCS-28600389.text_edition: Silvano Silvestro sic Publius Aelius Capito votum solvit libens merito  Imperatore Antonino Augusto II et Advento consulibus
EDCS-28600389.translation: To Silvanus Silvester. Publius Aelius Capito willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of Emperor Antoninus Augustus for the second time and Adventus.
EDCS-28600389.summary: This votive inscription to Silvanus Silvester by Publius Aelius Capito is dated to 218 AD, during the second consulship of Emperor Antoninus (Elagabalus) and Adventus.

EDCS-28600390.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Corinthus nummularius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600390.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvestris. Corinthus, the money-changer, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600390.summary: This is a sacred votive inscription to Silvanus Silvestris, dedicated by Corinthus, a money-changer.

EDCS-28600391.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Tiberius Iulius Masculus princeps legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600391.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvestris. Tiberius Iulius Masculus, princeps of the Second Adiutrix Legion, dutiful and loyal, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600391.summary: This sacred votive inscription to Silvanus Silvestris was made by Tiberius Iulius Masculus, a high-ranking officer (princeps) of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-28600392.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri pro inpedimentis |(centuriae) Iuli Alexandri Licinius Candidus dono dedit
EDCS-28600392.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, for the baggage of the century of Iulius Alexander. Licinius Candidus gave this as a gift.
EDCS-28600392.summary: This inscription records a dedication or gift to Silvanus Silvestris by Licinius Candidus, specifically for the baggage belonging to the century of Iulius Alexander.

EDCS-28600393.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri  Septimius Firmanus candidatus domini nostri pro salute sua votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600393.translation: To Silvanus Silvester, Septimius Firmanus, candidate of our lord, for his own well-being, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600393.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Septimius Firmanus, a candidate of the emperor, to Silvanus Silvester, for his own health, likely on an altar or dedication stone.

EDCS-28600394.text_edition: Silvano Silvestro sic Valerius Maximianus optio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600394.translation: To Silvanus Silvester, Valerius Maximianus, optio, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28600394.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Valerius Maximianus, an optio (military officer), to Silvanus Silvester, presumably as an altar dedication.

EDCS-28600395.text_edition: Volcano sacrum veterani et cives Romani consistentes ad legionem II adiutricem curam agentibus Valerio Respecto et Utedio Maximino magC(istris)#mag(istris)#MAC
EDCS-28600395.translation: Sacred to Vulcan. The veterans and Roman citizens residing at Legion II Adiutrix, with Valerius Respectus and Utedius Maximinus acting as masters, took care of this.
EDCS-28600395.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Vulcan by veterans and Roman citizens living near Legion II Adiutrix, organized by their masters, Valerius Respectus and Utedius Maximinus.

EDCS-28600396.text_edition: Libero et Liberae pro salute Augusti et aeternitatis sic imperii in honorem Marci Minici Martialis e(quo) publico Marcus Minicius Hermaiscus locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28600396.translation: To Liber and Libera, for the health of Augustus and the eternity of the empire, in honor of Marcus Minicius Martialis, a public eques. Marcus Minicius Hermaiscus. The place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28600396.summary: This inscription commemorates a dedication to Liber and Libera for the emperor's health and the empire's endurance, honoring Marcus Minicius Martialis, a public eques, by Marcus Minicius Hermaiscus, with the location approved by the decurions.

EDCS-28600397.text_edition: Fortunae Conservatrici et Bonae Deae Iunoni Aelia Theodora votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600397.translation: To Fortuna Conservatrix and Bona Dea Juno, Aelia Theodora willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28600397.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aelia Theodora to Fortuna Conservatrix and Bona Dea Juno, likely on an altar.

EDCS-28600398.text_edition: --- leg(ionis) II adiutricis votum solvit libens merito Laterano et Rufino consulibus
EDCS-28600398.translation: [...] of Legion II Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow in the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus.
EDCS-28600398.summary: This inscription, dated to the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus (219 CE), records a votive offering by an unnamed member of Legion II Adiutrix.

EDCS-28600399.text_edition: missus honesta missione Apro et Maximo consulibus ex voto
EDCS-28600399.translation: Discharged with an honorable discharge in the consulship of Aper and Maximus, according to a vow.
EDCS-28600399.summary: This inscription commemorates an honorable military discharge received in the consulship of Aper and Maximus (207 CE), fulfilled as part of a vow.

EDCS-28600400.text_edition: ]  Marcus Aurelius Licinius adiutor officii consularis cum suis votum solvit libens merito Imperatore domino nostro Alexandro Augusto III et Dione consulibus
EDCS-28600400.translation: Marcus Aurelius Licinius, assistant of the consular office, with his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow in the consulship of our lord Emperor Alexander Augustus for the third time and Dio.
EDCS-28600400.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Marcus Aurelius Licinius, an assistant in the consular office, and his family, dated to the consulship of Emperor Alexander Augustus (third time) and Dio (229 CE).

EDCS-28600401.text_edition: legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae votum solvit libens merito Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro et Cassio Dione consulibus
EDCS-28600401.translation: [...] of Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow in the consulship of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander and Cassius Dio.
EDCS-28600401.summary: This inscription, dated to the consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander and Cassius Dio (229 CE), records a votive offering by an unnamed individual from Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana.

EDCS-28600403.text_edition: sacrum  Sextus Pomius curator veteranorum legionis XIII gC(eminae)#g(eminae)#C
EDCS-28600403.translation: Sacred. Sextus Pomius, curator of the veterans of Legion XIII Gemina.
EDCS-28600403.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication by Sextus Pomius, identifying him as the curator of the veterans of Legion XIII Gemina.

EDCS-28600404.text_edition: Sedes A Atilia Cai filia Prisca pro se suisque votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28600404.translation: A. Atilia Prisca, daughter of Gaius, for herself and her family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28600404.summary: This inscription records a vow made and fulfilled by A. Atilia Prisca for herself and her family.

EDCS-28600406.translation: Odia willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-28600406.summary: This inscription records a vow made and fulfilled by someone named Odia.

EDCS-28600407.text_edition: VM us pro sua suorumque omnium salute
EDCS-28600407.translation: [...fulfils a vo]w for his own safety and that of all his family.
EDCS-28600407.summary: This inscription is a partial record of a vow made for the safety of an individual and their family.

EDCS-28600408.text_edition: pro salute Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Aq=OAuincenses#Aquincenses#OAUINC publice
EDCS-28600408.translation: For the safety of Imperator Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus and Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, the people of Aquincum publicly [dedicate this].
EDCS-28600408.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the citizens of Aquincum for the safety of Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600409.text_edition: O sacrum  pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi Britannici maximi
EDCS-28600409.translation: O sacred [dedication] for the safety of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthian, greatest Briton.
EDCS-28600409.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication for the safety of Emperor Caracalla, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600410.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli proconsuli patri patriae legio II adiutrix pia fidelis Gordiana devota numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-28600410.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the third time, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland: the Second Adiutrix legion, Pious, Loyal, Gordian, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-28600410.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Legio II Adiutrix to Emperor Gordian III, dating to his third year of tribunician power (around 241 AD).

EDCS-28600411.text_edition: ]   pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Maximiano Iovio InvictoI#Invicto#INVICTI Augusto et Maximino nobilissimo Caesari Iulius Valerianus et Aurelius Maximus dduumvviri coloniae Aquincensium
EDCS-28600411.translation: [...] for the safety of our Lords Maximian, Jovius, Invictus Augustus, and Maximinus, most noble Caesar, Julius Valerianus and Aurelius Maximus, duumvirs of the colony of Aquincum [dedicated this].
EDCS-28600411.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two duumvirs of the colony of Aquincum for the safety of the emperors Maximian and Maximinus Daia, likely dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-28600412.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Fideli ex praepositis qui vixit nnos LX coniugi maerentissimo sic Valeria Decorata faciendum cQuravit#curavit#QURAVIT
EDCS-28600412.translation: To the spirits of the departed: for Marcus Aurelius Fidelis, one of the praepositi, who lived 60 years. To a most grieving husband, Valeria Decorata had this made.
EDCS-28600412.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Valeria Decorata to her deceased husband, Marcus Aurelius Fidelis, a former praepositus, who died at age 60.

EDCS-28600413.text_edition: Schola speculatorum legionum I et II adiutricium piarum fidelium Severianarum refecta per eosdem quorum nomina infra scripta sunt dedicante Flavio Aeliano legato Augusti pro praetore Kalendis Octobribus Modesto et Probo consulibus Aelius Optatus Aelius Candidus Macrius Sabinianus Iulius Secundianus Iulius Emeritus Cassius Fuscinus Iulius Probinus Aelius Urbanus Publius Martinus Valerius Festus Iulius Alexander Flavius Emeritus Valerius Clementinus Iulius Priscanus Ver Verissimus Iulius Ingenuus Iulius Celerinus Iunius Adiutor Aurelius Lupus Flavius Celsinus curante Aurelio Pertinace frumentario
EDCS-28600413.translation: The schola of the scouts of the First and Second Adiutrix legions, Pious and Loyal, Severian, was rebuilt by those whose names are written below, with Flavius Aelianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, dedicating it on the Kalends of October, in the consulship of Modestus and Probus. Aelius Optatus, Aelius Candidus, Macrius Sabinianus, Julius Secundianus, Julius Emeritus, Cassius Fuscinus, Julius Probinus, Aelius Urbanus, Publius Martinus, Valerius Festus, Julius Alexander, Flavius Emeritus, Valerius Clementinus, Julius Priscanus, Verissimus, Julius Ingenuus, Julius Celerinus, Junius Adiutor, Aurelius Lupus, Flavius Celsinus, with Aurelius Pertinax, frumentarius, supervising.
EDCS-28600413.summary: This inscription records the restoration of the schola (clubhouse) for the scouts of two legions by a list of individuals, dedicated by the provincial governor, and overseen by a frumentarius, dating to October 1st, 228 AD (the consulship of Modestus and Probus).

EDCS-28600414.text_edition: Thermas maiores legionis II adiutricis Claudianae magno tempore intermissas et destiLtutas#destitutas#DESTLTUTAS retracatis porticibus aditibusque prius refecit exhiberi inde incepisse militibus iussit pridie Kalendas Iulias praeside provinciae egregio viro Clementio Silvino et cura Aureli Frontini praefecti legionis eiusdem Paterno II et Mariniano consulibus
EDCS-28600414.translation: The great baths of the Legio II Adiutrix Claudiana, having been closed and neglected for a long time, were rebuilt with their porticoes and entrances restored, and their use for soldiers was ordered to commence on the day before the Kalends of July, with the excellent man Clementius Silvinus as provincial governor and under the supervision of Aurelius Frontinus, prefect of the same legion, in the consulship of Paternus II and Marinianus.
EDCS-28600414.summary: This inscription records the restoration and reopening of the great baths of Legio II Adiutrix Claudiana for soldiers, overseen by a legionary prefect and provincial governor, dating to June 30th, 268 AD (the consulship of Paternus II and Marinianus).

EDCS-28600415.text_edition: Excubitorium ad tutelam signorum et imaginum sacrarum Publius Turranius Firminus veteranus ex corniculario legionis II adiutricis Antoninianae pecunia sua a solo restituit Sabino II et Anullino consulibus
EDCS-28600415.translation: Publius Turranius Firminus, a veteran formerly a cornicularius of the Second Adiutrix Legion, restored from its foundations at his own expense this guardhouse for the protection of sacred standards and images, when Sabinus II and Anullinus were consuls.
EDCS-28600415.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a guardhouse for sacred military standards and images by a veteran, Publius Turranius Firminus, during the consulate of Sabinus II and Anullinus (243 CE).

EDCS-28600416.text_edition: P Publio Aelio Iusto nnorum III Aeliae Verinae nnum VII Publius Aelius Provincialis veteranus legionis II adiutricis avos sic et Publius Aelius Provincialis sevir coloniae Aquincensis et Aelia Concordia parentes filiis dulcissimis fecerunt
EDCS-28600416.translation: To Publius Aelius Iustus, aged 3, and Aelia Verina, aged 7. Publius Aelius Provincialis, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, their grandfather, and Publius Aelius Provincialis, a sevir of the colony of Aquincum, and Aelia Concordia, their parents, made this for their sweetest children.
EDCS-28600416.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two young children, Publius Aelius Iustus and Aelia Verina, dedicated by their parents and paternal grandfather, Publius Aelius Provincialis, who was a veteran of Legio II Adiutrix and a sevir of Aquincum.

EDCS-28600417.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Claudia Lusimacus sic TheFssalonicia#Thessalonicia#TFSSALONICIA miles legionis II adiutricis annorum XXX stipendiorum XIII centuria Ulpi Etrusci ETVS BITVS M
EDCS-28600417.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Lusimacus, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion from Thessalonica, aged 30, with 13 years of service, from the century of Ulpius Etruscus.
EDCS-28600417.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Lusimacus, a soldier of Legio II Adiutrix, who died at age 30 after 13 years of service.

EDCS-28600418.text_edition: Dis Manibus  memoriae Publi Aeli Martialis quondam veterani ex custode armorum legionis II adiutricis patris et Flaviae Agathes sic matris Aelius Aelianus praefectus legionis supra scriptae protector Augusti parentibus carissimis regressus ad Lares patrios faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600418.translation: To the Divine Manes and the memory of Publius Aelius Martialis, formerly a veteran and custos armorum of the Second Adiutrix Legion, his father, and Flavia Agathe, his mother. Aelius Aelianus, prefect of the aforesaid legion and protector of Augustus, returning to his ancestral home, took care to have this made for his dearest parents.
EDCS-28600418.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aelius Aelianus, prefect of Legio II Adiutrix and a protector of the emperor, to the memory of his parents, Publius Aelius Martialis (a veteran) and Flavia Agathe.

EDCS-28600419.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publius Aelius Publi filius Maecia Mestrius Pelagonia optio legionis II adiutricis centuria Attei Dextri annorum XXXVIIII stipendiorum XVIIII hic situs est optiones legionis eiusdem faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28600419.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Mestrius, son of Publius, from the Maecia tribe, from Pelagonia, an optio of the Second Adiutrix Legion, in the century of Atteius Dexter, aged 39, with 19 years of service, lies here. The optiones of the same legion took care to have this made.
EDCS-28600419.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Mestrius, an optio of Legio II Adiutrix, dedicated by his fellow optiones of the same legion.

EDCS-28600421.text_edition: Memoriae quondam Marci Aureli  Augustalis coloniae Aquinci vixit nnos XLI MMarci Aureli Augendus et Amandus liberti et heredes ex voluntate testamenti curantibus Decimio Dionisio Augustale coloniae supra scriptae et Aurelio Papia veterano legionis II adiutricis faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-28600421.translation: To the memory of Marcus Aurelius Augustalis of the colony of Aquincum, who lived for 41 years. Marcus Aurelius Augendus and Amandus, freedmen and heirs, by the will of his testament, arranged for this to be made, with the oversight of Decimius Dionysius, Augustalis of the aforesaid colony, and Aurelius Papia, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion.
EDCS-28600421.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Marcus Aurelius Augustalis of Aquincum, erected by his freedmen and heirs under the supervision of another Augustalis and a veteran of Legio II Adiutrix.

EDCS-28600422.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Antonino signifero legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XVIII qui vixit annos XLV Septimia Lupa coniux et Aurelia Antonia filia curante Aurelium Aristomenetem libertum eorum faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600422.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelius Antoninus, standard-bearer of the Second Adiutrix Legion, with 18 years of service, who lived for 45 years. Septimia Lupa, his wife, and Aurelia Antonia, his daughter, arranged for this to be made, with Aurelius Aristomenes, their freedman, overseeing it.
EDCS-28600422.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Antoninus, a standard-bearer of Legio II Adiutrix, dedicated by his wife Septimia Lupa and daughter Aurelia Antonia, with their freedman Aurelius Aristomenes as curator.

EDCS-28600423.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Florentinus benefciarius consularis qui vixit annis XXXIII Maesia CanpanillIa#Canpanilla#CANPANILIA coniugi carissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600423.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius Florentinus, a beneficiarius consularis, who lived for 33 years. Maesia Campanilla had this made for her dearest husband.
EDCS-28600423.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Florentinus, a beneficiarius consularis, dedicated by his wife Maesia Campanilla.

EDCS-28600424.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Maximus veteranus cohortis equitatae ex corniVc[ulario#corniculario#CORNVC sibi et Aeliae Vconiugi pientIissimae#pientissimae#PIENIIS vivis et Aeliae Maximaeae
EDCS-28600424.translation: Marcus Aurelius Maximus, a veteran of an equestrian cohort, formerly a cornicularius, made this for himself and for Aelia, his most devoted wife, while they are alive, and for Aelia Maxima.
EDCS-28600424.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Marcus Aurelius Maximus, a veteran and former cornicularius of an equestrian cohort, for himself, his devoted wife Aelia, and Aelia Maxima, while they are still living.

EDCS-28600425.text_edition: Hermiae medico legionis IIII Flaviae marito pientissimo Aurelia Mama uxor infelicissima
EDCS-28600425.translation: To Hermias, doctor of the Fourth Flavian Legion, her most devoted husband. Aurelia Mama, his most unhappy wife.
EDCS-28600425.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Mama, a grieving wife, to her husband Hermias, a doctor of Legio IIII Flavia.

EDCS-28600426.text_edition: Memoriae quondam Marci Aureli Melliti librarii legati legionis II adiutricis vixit nnos XVIII stipendiorum IIII Marcus Aurelius Martialis veteranus ex signifero legionis supra scriptae filio dulcissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600426.translation: To the memory of Marcus Aurelius Mellitus, formerly a clerk to the legate of the Second Legion Adiutrix, who lived 18 years and served 4 years. Marcus Aurelius Martialis, a veteran formerly a standard-bearer of the aforesaid legion, had this made for his sweetest son.
EDCS-28600426.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Aurelius Mellitus, a young clerk, set up by his father, Marcus Aurelius Martialis, a veteran standard-bearer, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600427.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Dis Manibus Aureliae Bonosae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit annis XXXV Valerius Valerianus |(centurio) legionis bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28600427.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelia Bonosa, his most devoted wife, who lived 35 years. Valerius Valerianus, a centurion of the legion, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28600427.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valerius Valerianus, a legionary centurion, to his most devoted wife, Aurelia Bonosa, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600428.text_edition: Dis Manibus  memoriae quondam Aureliae Martiae domo Aquinco vixit nnos XLI dies IIII Marcus Ulpius Dasianus veteranus legionis II adiutricis coniugi karissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-28600428.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To the memory of Aurelia Martia, originally from Aquincum, who lived 41 years and 4 days. Marcus Ulpius Dasianus, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, had this made for his dearest wife.
EDCS-28600428.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Ulpius Dasianus, a veteran of the Second Legion Adiutrix, for his dearest wife, Aurelia Martia, who hailed from Aquincum, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600429.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Valentina quae vixit nnos X menses V dies V G Lucidus legionis II adiutricis MIVA VAR SEX A vivus coniugi benignissimae
EDCS-28600429.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelia Valentina, who lived 10 years, 5 months, and 5 days. G. Lucidus, of the Second Legion Adiutrix, MIVA VAR SEX A, while living, dedicated this to his most kind wife.
EDCS-28600429.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by G. Lucidus of the Second Legion Adiutrix to his most kind wife, Aurelia Valentina, who died young, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600430.text_edition: Aureliae Victorinae quae vixit nnis XVII mensibus VI diebus X Aurelius Viator miles cohortis |(milliariae) Maurorum coniugi karissimae
EDCS-28600430.translation: To Aurelia Victorina, who lived 17 years, 6 months, and 10 days. Aurelius Viator, a soldier of the cohort of 1000 Mauri, [set this up] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-28600430.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Viator, a soldier of a Mauri cohort, to his dearest wife, Aurelia Victorina, who died at a young age, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600432.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Tito Claudio Efficaci quondam militi legionis II sic piae fidelis beneficiario consularis domo Luceria Apul=Ia#Apula#APIA qui vixit nnos XXXVII Calpurnia Hermione coniugCi#coniugi#CONIUCI bene merentisisimo fecit
EDCS-28600432.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Titus Claudius Efficax, formerly a soldier of the Second Legion 'Pia Fidelis,' a beneficiary of the consular governor, from Luceria Apula, who lived 37 years. Calpurnia Hermione made this for her most well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28600432.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Calpurnia Hermione to her most well-deserving husband, Titus Claudius Efficax, a soldier and beneficiary of the Second Legion 'Pia Fidelis,' originally from Luceria in Apulia, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600433.text_edition: Claudius  veteranus ex cohorte |(milliaria) Maurorum vixit annos LX Claudius Vitalianus miles cohortis novae filius et Flavia Marcella coniux posuit sic Aelius Iucundianus Aelius Valerianus milites
EDCS-28600433.translation: Claudius, a veteran from the cohort of 1000 Mauri, lived 60 years. Claudius Vitalianus, a soldier of the new cohort, his son, and Flavia Marcella, his wife, placed this. Thus also Aelius Iucundianus and Aelius Valerianus, soldiers.
EDCS-28600433.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius, a veteran of a Mauri cohort, set up by his son Claudius Vitalianus, a soldier, and his wife Flavia Marcella, with Aelius Iucundianus and Aelius Valerianus also participating, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28600434.text_edition: Marcus Cocceius Moesicus annorum XV hic situs est Marcus Cocceius Matumari filius Florus princeps Cocceia Oxidubna Quintionis filia coniux filio et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-28600434.translation: Marcus Cocceius Moesicus, 15 years old, is buried here. Marcus Cocceius Matumari, son of Florus the chief, and Cocceia Oxidubna, daughter of Quintio, his wife, placed this for their son and for themselves while living.
EDCS-28600434.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Cocceius Matumari, son of Florus the chief, and his wife Cocceia Oxidubna for their son, Marcus Cocceius Moesicus, who died at 15, and for themselves in anticipation of their own deaths, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28701210.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis VII pater patriae restituit milia passuum XV
EDCS-28701210.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, in his seventh year of tribunician power, Father of the Country, restored 15 miles.
EDCS-28701210.summary: This is a milestone or public inscription recording the restoration of 15 miles of road by Emperor Severus Alexander, likely dating to 228 AD based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-28701212.text_edition: Impperatores Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Arabicus Adiabenicus maximus et Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Auggusti et Publius Septimius Geta nobilissimus Caesar [] [] coloniae splendidissimae curante Lucio Baebio Caeciliano legato Augustorum pro praetore
EDCS-28701212.translation: The Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Arabicus Adiabenicus Maximus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, and Publius Septimius Geta Nobilissimus Caesar, [...] for the most splendid colony, with Lucius Baebius Caecilianus, legate of the Augusti pro praetore, overseeing.
EDCS-28701212.summary: This is a public dedication, likely for a building or restoration project for a colony, made by the Imperial family of Septimius Severus (including Caracalla and Geta) and overseen by a pro-praetorian legate, dating to the early 3rd century AD (before Geta's damnatio memoriae in 211 AD).

EDCS-28701213.text_edition: legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae curam agente Pontio Pontiano legato Augusti pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum III
EDCS-28701213.translation: Of the Second Adjutrix Legion, loyal and faithful, Antoninian, under the care of Pontius Pontianus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, three miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701213.summary: This inscription records the measurement of three miles from Aquincum, likely marking a milestone erected or maintained by the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Antoniniana under the command of Pontius Pontianus, a provincial governor, during the Antonine period.

EDCS-28701215.text_edition: Domino nostro Imperatori Liciniano Licinio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto ab Aquinco milia passuum V
EDCS-28701215.translation: To our Lord, the Emperor Licinianus Licinius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, five miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701215.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Licinianus Licinius and marks a distance of five miles from Aquincum, probably indicating a milestone from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-28701216.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus [Alexander Pius] Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum VI
EDCS-28701216.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, restored this, six miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701216.summary: This inscription commemorates Emperor Severus Alexander's restoration of a milestone or road, located six miles from Aquincum, dated to his ninth year of tribunician power and third consulship (around 230 CE).

EDCS-28701217.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli et Caio Iulio Vero Maximo nobilissimo Caesari  ab Aquinco milia passuum VI  Dominis nnostris Flavio Valerio Constantio et Galerio Valerio Maximiano nobilissimis Caesaribus  DDominis nnostris  Con[stantino &
EDCS-28701217.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of his country, consul, proconsul, and to Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, six miles from Aquincum. To our Lords Flavius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesars. To our Lords Constantinus and [---].
EDCS-28701217.summary: This inscription is a multi-layered dedication to several emperors: first to Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, then to Constantius Chlorus and Galerius, and finally beginning a dedication to Constantine, indicating reuse or updating of a milestone over several imperial reigns.

EDCS-28701218.text_edition: Imperator  FIRIO M  Maximianus  F C milia passuum
EDCS-28701218.translation: Emperor Firius M. Maximianus F. C. miles.
EDCS-28701218.summary: This fragmentary inscription is likely a milestone dedicated to Emperor Maximianus, marking a certain number of miles from an unnamed point, probably dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-28701219.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari [Marco Aurelio] Antonino [Pio Felici] Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V consuli IIII proconsuli ab Aquinco milia passum VIII
EDCS-28701219.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the fifth time, consul for the fourth time, proconsul, eight miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701219.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla, identifying his imperial titles and marking a distance of eight miles from Aquincum, likely a milestone from around 212 CE.

EDCS-28701221.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus A[lexander] Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum VIII
EDCS-28701221.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, restored this, eight miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701221.summary: This inscription commemorates Emperor Severus Alexander's restoration of a milestone or road, located eight miles from Aquincum, dated to his ninth year of tribunician power and third consulship (around 230 CE).

EDCS-28701223.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis consuli patri patriae proconsuli et Marciae Otaciliae Severae sanctissimae Augustae coniugi Augusti nostri ab Aquinco milia passuum VIII
EDCS-28701223.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, father of his country, proconsul, and to Marcia Otacilia Severa, most holy Augusta, wife of our Augustus, eight miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701223.summary: This inscription is a milestone dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab and his wife, Otacilia Severa, dating to their joint reign (244-249 CE), and marks a distance of eight miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-28701224.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis consuli patri patriae proconsuli et Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari et Marciae Otaciliae Severae sanctissimae coniugi Augusti nostri ab Aquinco milia passuum VIII
EDCS-28701224.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, father of his country, proconsul, and to Marcus Iulius Philippus, most noble Caesar, and to Marcia Otacilia Severa, most holy wife of our Augustus, eight miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701224.summary: This inscription is a milestone dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab, his son Philip II as Caesar, and his wife Otacilia Severa, dating to their joint reign (244-247 CE), and marks a distance of eight miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-28701227.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum VIIII
EDCS-28701227.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, consul for the third time, father of his country, restored this, nine miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701227.summary: This inscription commemorates Emperor Severus Alexander's restoration of a milestone or road, located nine miles from Aquincum, dated to his ninth year of tribunician power and third consulship (around 230 CE).

EDCS-28701229.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifI(ex)#pontif(ex)#PONTII maximus tribunicia potestas sic VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum XXXIIII
EDCS-28701229.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, restored this at 34 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701229.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a milestone (or road) by Emperor Severus Alexander, indicating a distance of 34 miles from Aquincum, and can be dated to his reign.

EDCS-28701234.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli ab Aquinco milia passuum XXXVI
EDCS-28701234.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul, this is erected at 36 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701234.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Decius, likely from a milestone marking 36 miles from Aquincum, and can be dated to his reign.

EDCS-28701260.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit ab Aquinco milia passuum LXXIII
EDCS-28701260.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, restored this at 73 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701260.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a milestone (or road) by Emperor Severus Alexander, indicating a distance of 73 miles from Aquincum, and can be dated to his reign.

EDCS-28701262.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Sevevero Pio PertiEnaci#Pertinaci#PERTENAC Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo divi Marci Aureli filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti imperatori XI tribunicia potestate VII consuli II proconsuli Imperatori Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Luci Septimi Severi Pii PertiEnaci#Pertinaci#PERTENAC Augusti filio divi Antonini nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Publio Septimio Getae Caesa[re] Augusti? filio curante Lucio Baebio Caeciliano legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco
EDCS-28701262.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia (for the greatest victory), son of the divine Marcus Aurelius, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, hailed as imperator for the 11th time, holding tribunician power for the 7th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul; and to the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, grandson of the divine Antoninus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva; and to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of the Augusti, with Lucius Baebius Caecilianus, legate of the Augusti with pro-praetorian power, as curator, from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701262.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a milestone to the co-reigning emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), and to their brother Geta Caesar, likely set by the provincial governor Lucius Baebius Caecilianus, indicating its origin from Aquincum during the Severan dynasty.

EDCS-28701265.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Augusto G D S tribunicia potestate consul procons(uli) et Caio Iulio Vero Maximo
EDCS-28701265.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, Germanicus Dacicus Sarmaticus, holding tribunician power, consul, proconsul, and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus.
EDCS-28701265.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, likely from a milestone, and can be dated to their co-reign.

EDCS-28701281.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate bis imperator III consul proconsul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar filius Augusti nostri Dacici Germanici Sarmatici imperatores maximi ab Aquinco milia passuum CLX
EDCS-28701281.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the second time, hailed as imperator for the 3rd time, consul, proconsul, father of the country, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, son of our Augustus, victor over the Dacians, Germans, and Sarmatians, the greatest emperors, this is erected at 160 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701281.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a milestone to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, celebrating their titles and victories, and marking 160 miles from Aquincum during their co-reign.

EDCS-28701283.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIII consul III pater patriae restituit  ab Aquinco milia passuum VIII
EDCS-28701283.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, restored this at 8 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701283.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a milestone (or road) by Emperor Severus Alexander, indicating a distance of 8 miles from Aquincum, and can be dated to his reign.

EDCS-28701284.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio [Vero Ma][ximino] Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli procons(uli) et Imperatori Caio Iul[io] [Vero Maxi]mo no[bilissimo] Caesari Pio Felici Augusto ab Aquinco milia passuum CII
EDCS-28701284.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Invictus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul; and to the Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Pius Felix Augustus, this is erected at 102 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701284.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a milestone to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, marking 102 miles from Aquincum, and can be dated to their co-reign.

EDCS-28701285.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari [Caio Iulio Ve][ro Maximi][no] Pio Felici Augusto Germanico maximo Dacico maximo Sarmatico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli proconsuli et Imperatori Caio Iulio Vero Maximo Germanico maximo Dacico maximo Sarmatico maximo nobilissimo Caesari Pio Felici Augusto ab Aquinco milia passuum VIII
EDCS-28701285.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest victor over the Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul; and to the Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, greatest victor over the Germans, Dacians, and Sarmatians, most noble Caesar, Pius Felix Augustus, this is erected at 8 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701285.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a milestone to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, highlighting their military victories, and marking 8 miles from Aquincum during their co-reign.

EDCS-28701286.text_edition: Imperator Caesar [] [] []  Pius Felix  Augustus pontifex maximus pater patriae ab Aquinco milia passuum CII
EDCS-28701286.translation: The Emperor Caesar [name illegible] Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, father of the country, this is erected at 102 miles from Aquincum.
EDCS-28701286.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a milestone to an unnamed emperor, likely from the Severan or 3rd-century crisis period given the titles, and indicates a distance of 102 miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-28701287.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio][Iulio Maximino Invicto Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis patri patriae proconsuli legio II adiutrix pia fidelis <et=LI>#<et>#LI  [] ab Aquinco milia passuum III
EDCS-28701287.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Gaius Julius Maximinus, the Invincible, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul. The Second Auxiliary Legion, Pious and Faithful, [set this up]. From Aquincum, three miles.
EDCS-28701287.summary: This is a milestone dedicated to Emperor Maximinus Thrax by the Legio II Adiutrix, marking a distance of three miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-28701289.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus tribunicia potestate XV consul III et Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus tribunicia potestate consul II divi Augusti Antonini filii divi Hadriani nepotes divi Traiani Parthici pronepotes divi Nervae abnepotes ab Aquinco milia passuum
EDCS-28701289.translation: The Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, consul for the 3rd time, and the Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, with tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time, sons of the divine Augustus Antoninus, grandsons of the divine Hadrian, great-grandsons of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandsons of the divine Nerva. From Aquincum, [number of] miles.
EDCS-28701289.summary: This is a milestone dedicated to co-emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, detailing their imperial titles and lineage, indicating a distance from Aquincum.

EDCS-28701290.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo divi Marci filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti imperatori XI tribunicia potestate VI consuli II proconsuli et Imperatori Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti nostri filio divi Antonini nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti et [] curante Tiberio Claudio Claudiano legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum
EDCS-28701290.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus, Pious, Pertinax, Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, son of the divine Marcus, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Imperator for the 11th time, with tribunician power for the 6th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul. And to the Imperator Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of our Lucius Septimius Severus, Pious, Pertinax, Augustus, grandson of the divine Antoninus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva. [Erected] under the care of Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, legate of the Augusti, propraetor. From Aquincum, [number of] miles.
EDCS-28701290.summary: This milestone is dedicated to Emperor Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), listing their full imperial titles and lineage, with the legate Tiberius Claudius Claudianus overseeing its erection, and marking a distance from Aquincum.

EDCS-28701291.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis bis consuli bis designato ter et Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio nobilissimo Caesari consuli designato et Caio Valenti Hostiliano Messio Quinto nobilissimo Caesari
EDCS-28701291.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, designated for the third time. And to Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, most noble Caesar, consul designate. And to Gaius Valens Hostilianus Messius Quintus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28701291.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Decius and his sons Herennius Etruscus and Hostilian, who were designated as Caesars and consuls, honoring their imperial roles and noble status.

EDCS-28701292.text_edition: Flavio Aeliano clarissimo  viro legato Augusti pro praetore ab Aquinco milia passuum III
EDCS-28701292.translation: To Flavius Aelianus, most distinguished man, legate of the Augusti pro praetore. From Aquincum, three miles.
EDCS-28701292.summary: This is a milestone dedicated to Flavius Aelianus, a distinguished legate, indicating a distance of three miles from Aquincum.

EDCS-28900084.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus ER Augustus Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate  consul pater patriae milia passuum XXXII
EDCS-28900084.translation: The Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, Augustus, Pious, Fortunate, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, consul, father of the country. Thirty-two miles.
EDCS-28900084.summary: This milestone is dedicated to Emperor Maximian, listing his imperial titles, and marking a distance of thirty-two miles.

EDCS-28900085.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Valerio Maximiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo
EDCS-28900085.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, Pious, Fortunate Augustus, Pontifex Maximus.
EDCS-28900085.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maximian, honoring him with his imperial titles.

EDCS-28900086.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici invicto Augusto Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis patri patriae proconsuli a Brigetione milia passuum XXXIII
EDCS-28900086.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul. From Brigetio, thirty-three miles.
EDCS-28900086.summary: This milestone is dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab, detailing his imperial titles and achievements, and marking a distance of thirty-three miles from Brigetio.

EDCS-28900089.text_edition: PRINIIANI Parthicus ET SE REN VSVS
EDCS-28900089.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to mention the title 'Parthicus' and possibly names or parts of names like 'Serenus', but its full context and purpose are unclear.

EDCS-29000454.text_edition: Tiberio Claudioo MARI F annorum L hic situs est
EDCS-29000454.translation: Tiberius Claudius, son of Marius, aged 50, lies here.
EDCS-29000454.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius, son of Marius, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-29000530.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III patri patriae consuli proconsuli cohors I Noricorum Gordiana equitata devota numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-29000530.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, Father of his Country, Consul, Proconsul: The First Cohort of Norici, Gordian's Own, mounted, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-29000530.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from the First Cohort of Norici, mounted, to Emperor Gordian III, likely commemorating a civic or military achievement during his reign (238-244 AD), given the titles and tribunician power count.

EDCS-29000533.text_edition: Aurelius Valerianus vixit annos LXX Maximinae nepoti et Saturnae coniugi Firmus et Firmanus titulum filii parentibus
EDCS-29000533.translation: Aurelius Valerianus lived for 70 years. To Maximina, his granddaughter, and Saturnae, his wife. Firmus and Firmanus, sons, placed this memorial for their parents.
EDCS-29000533.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Firmus and Firmanus for their parents, Aurelius Valerianus and Saturnae, and their granddaughter Maximina, commemorating their lives.

EDCS-29000534.text_edition: Pollitius Laelianus pro se et filiis suis votum solvit libens merito Serviano III et Varo consulibus
EDCS-29000534.translation: Pollitius Laelianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his sons, during the consulship of Servianus for the third time and Varus.
EDCS-29000534.summary: This is a votive inscription by Pollitius Laelianus, made for himself and his sons, fulfilling a vow in 202 AD, indicated by the mention of the consuls Servianus III and Varus.

EDCS-29000535.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae sacrum pro salute Aberiorum Vigoris et Vigoriani Lucani et Lucaniani Aberius Acutus pater et Claudia Matrona mater  votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-29000535.translation: Sacred to Juno Regina. For the well-being of Aberius Vigor and Vigorianus, Lucanus and Lucanianus, Aberius Acutus their father and Claudia Matrona their mother willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-29000535.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Juno Regina by Aberius Acutus and Claudia Matrona for the health and well-being of their four sons.

EDCS-29000538.translation: LI, brother, and also Aureli--- Iul---
EDCS-29000538.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication, mentioning a brother named LI and other individuals with partial names Aureli--- and Iul---.

EDCS-29000539.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Of Claudius Maximus, aged [---] years.
EDCS-29000539.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of Claudius Maximus, whose age is incompletely preserved.

EDCS-29000541.text_edition: m EF us Scoliannos XXX  INF ARO
EDCS-29000541.translation: m EF us Scoliannos XXX INF ARO
EDCS-29000541.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription with uncertain meaning, possibly containing part of a name or age ('Scoliannos XXX' might mean 'Scolianus, aged 30').

EDCS-29000546.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sexti sic Iuventio Primitivo domo Aquileia annorum XXVII Ulpia Epicharis fratri faciendum curavit ob merita eius faciendum curavit
EDCS-29000546.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Sextus Iuventius Primitivus, from Aquileia, aged 27 years. Ulpia Epicharis arranged for this to be made for her brother because of his merits; she arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-29000546.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sextus Iuventius Primitivus by his sister, Ulpia Epicharis, highlighting his origins in Aquileia and his young age at death.

EDCS-29000548.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Barsemis Abbei decurio alae firmae  KatafPractariae#Katafractariae#KATAPRACTARIAE ex numero Hosroenruorum magister cohortis |(miliariae) Hemesenorum domo Carris et Aurelia Iulia coniux ei=PLus#eius#PLUS votum solverunt libentes merito Aurelia Thicimim  et Aurelia Salia eFt#et#FT filias sic Barsimia Titia ? nepotes cum suprascriptis posuerunt
EDCS-29000548.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Barsemis Abbei, decurion of the Ala Firma Catafractaria, of the number of the Hosroenorum, master of the Cohort Milliaria Hemesenorum, from Carrhae, and Aurelia Julia, his wife, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow. Aurelia Thicimim and Aurelia Salia, and their daughters, as well as Barsimia Titia, their granddaughters, placed this with the aforementioned.
EDCS-29000548.summary: This is a complex votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Barsemis Abbei, a military officer from Carrhae, and his wife Aurelia Julia, with their daughters and granddaughters also participating in the dedication, highlighting his distinguished military career.

EDCS-29000549.text_edition: Deo Soli Augusto limus stationarius  publici votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29000549.translation: To the Augustan Sun God. Limus, a stationarius (customs official), willingly and deservedly fulfilled his public vow.
EDCS-29000549.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Augustan Sun God by Limus, a public customs official, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29000551.text_edition: Soli Invicto Deo Marcus Ulpius Petuernus
EDCS-29000551.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God. Marcus Ulpius Petuernus (set this up).
EDCS-29000551.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Sol Invictus, set up by Marcus Ulpius Petuernus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000555.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Gaiano veterano legionis I adiutricis vixit nnos LXX et Aureliae Valentinae vixit annos Aureliae Gaianae et Aurelio Ausiano stratore legati legionis II adiutricis vixit nnos XL stipendiorum XXII qui est desideratus bello Dacico Aurelia marito dulcissimo
EDCS-29000555.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelius Gaianus, veteran of the First Adiutrix Legion, who lived 70 years, and to Aurelia Valentina, who lived [---] years, [and to] Aurelia Gaiana and to Aurelius Ausianus, cavalry orderly of the legate of the Second Adiutrix Legion, who lived 40 years and served 22 years, who was lost in the Dacian War. Aurelia (set this up) for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-29000555.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Divine Spirits, honoring Aurelius Gaianus, an aged veteran, Aurelia Valentina, Aurelia Gaiana, and Aurelius Ausianus, a cavalry orderly lost in the Dacian War, erected by Aurelia for her beloved husband.

EDCS-29000557.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Claudiae Monimosae Monimi quondam Aglavi veterani filiae quae vixit nnos L Aelianus Genuilis veteranus legionis II adiutricis coniugi rarissimae
EDCS-29000557.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Claudia Monimosa, daughter of Monimus, formerly Aglavus, a veteran, who lived 50 years. Aelianus Genuilis, veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, (set this up) for his most rare wife.
EDCS-29000557.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Spirits for Claudia Monimosa, the daughter of a veteran, erected by her husband Aelianus Genuilis, also a veteran, who served in the Second Adiutrix Legion.

EDCS-29000558.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lissinia Galla vixit annis XXI Calpurnius Irenaeus tribunus coniugi rarissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29000558.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lissinia Galla lived 21 years. Calpurnius Irenaeus, tribune, placed this for his most rare and deserving wife.
EDCS-29000558.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Lissinia Galla, who died young, erected by her husband Calpurnius Irenaeus, a tribune.

EDCS-29000561.text_edition: Asulus Blatumari filius vivos sic sibi et Deivae Namiorigis filiae annorum XXX coniugi Blatumaro filio annorum VI hic siti sunt
EDCS-29000561.translation: Asulus, son of Blatomarus, while alive, (placed this) for himself and for Deiva, daughter of Namiorix, 30 years old, his wife, and for Blatomarus, his son, 6 years old. They are interred here.
EDCS-29000561.summary: This is a pre-mortem funerary inscription for Asulus, his wife Deiva, and their young son Blatomarus, indicating they are buried in this spot.

EDCS-29000562.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nemoratta Sperati filia annorum X titulus positus hic situs
EDCS-29000562.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Nemoratta, daughter of Speratus, 10 years old. This inscribed stone was placed; she is interred here.
EDCS-29000562.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Spirits for Nemoratta, a ten-year-old girl, marking her burial place.

EDCS-29000563.text_edition: esia Tutui filia annorum XXX hic sita est
EDCS-29000563.translation: [---]esia, daughter of Tutus, 30 years old. She is interred here.
EDCS-29000563.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named [---]esia, daughter of Tutus, who lived 30 years and is buried at this location.

EDCS-29000565.text_edition: Dis Manibus titulum posuit Florina NigCrino#Nigrino#NICRINO virginuo suo qui vixit annos L et mensis sic septem
EDCS-29000565.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Florina placed this inscription for her virgin [son/daughter] Nigrinus, who lived 50 years and 7 months.
EDCS-29000565.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits by Florina for her son Nigrinus, who died at the age of 50 years and 7 months.

EDCS-29000575.text_edition: Dis Manibus Suadilla Comonis liberta annorum LXXX titulum posu--- Vibius Moget  SIFLVI
EDCS-29000575.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Suadilla, freedwoman of Comon, 80 years old. Vibius Moget [---] placed this inscription [---].
EDCS-29000575.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Spirits for Suadilla, an 80-year-old freedwoman of Comon, with the dedicator being Vibius Moget.

EDCS-29000581.text_edition: Iunoni  Reginae Aurelius Respectus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis ex voto posuit aram dono dedit Agricola et Clementino consulibus
EDCS-29000581.translation: To Juno Regina. Aurelius Respectus, veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, dutiful and loyal, placed this from a vow. He gave this altar as a gift. In the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus.
EDCS-29000581.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Juno Regina by Aurelius Respectus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, fulfilling a vow, dated to the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus (162 CE).

EDCS-29200188.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et Deo Paterno Comageno Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris decurio municipii Murselensium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29200188.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, and to the Paternal God of Commagene, Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, decurion of the municipality of Mursella, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29200188.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, a local magistrate, to Jupiter Dolichenus and a Paternal God of Commagene, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29200189.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et Deo Paterno Comageno Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris decurio municipii Murselensium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29200189.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, and to the Paternal God of Commagene, Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, decurion of the municipality of Mursella, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29200189.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, a local magistrate, to Jupiter Dolichenus and a Paternal God of Commagene, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29300066.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aelius Messius duplicarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29300066.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Paternus, Aelius Messius, a duplicarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29300066.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aelius Messius, identified as a duplicarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Paternus, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300068.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Arubiano posuerunt Gemellinus et Vitalis libientes voto suo
EDCS-29300068.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Arubianus, Gemellinus and Vitalis willingly placed this according to their vow.
EDCS-29300068.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus by Gemellinus and Vitalis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300069.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Marcus Antonius Proculinus decurio coloniae Bassianae  votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-29300069.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Juno Regina, Marcus Antonius Proculinus, decurion of the colony of Bassiana, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-29300069.summary: This inscription records a vow placed by Marcus Antonius Proculinus, a decurion of the colony of Bassiana, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300070.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Ulpi Neratius et Nigrinus decuriones coloniae et Sabinus et Quintio posuerunt
EDCS-29300070.translation: Sacred to Silvanus, Ulpius Neratius and Nigrinus, decurions of the colony, and Sabinus and Quintio placed this.
EDCS-29300070.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus, erected by two decurions of a colony, Ulpius Neratius and Nigrinus, along with Sabinus and Quintio, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300072.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the colony of Bassiana, the exact purpose of which is unclear due to its incomplete state.

EDCS-29300073.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Ianuarino qui vixit nnos X Aurelius Ianuarius tribunus legionis II adiutricis et Aurelia Valentina filio carissimo et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-29300073.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelius Ianuarianus, who lived 10 years. Aurelius Ianuarius, tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion, and Aurelia Valentina, placed this for their dearest son and for themselves while living.
EDCS-29300073.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Ianuarius, a tribune of the Legio II Adiutrix, and Aurelia Valentina to their son Aurelius Ianuarianus, who died at age 10, also serving as a burial place for themselves, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300074.text_edition: Ene vixit nnos XXV Zosimus bene merenti
EDCS-29300074.translation: He lived 25 years. Zosimus (placed this) for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29300074.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that someone lived 25 years, dedicated by Zosimus to the deceased, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300075.translation: Dasmenus, his children and heirs placed this.
EDCS-29300075.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Dasmenus's children and heirs, likely for Dasmenus himself, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300078.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Didius Herculanus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29300078.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Didius Herculanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29300078.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Didius Herculanus, likely on a dedicatory altar or plaque.

EDCS-29300081.text_edition: Silvano Bellatori sacrum  Lucius Marcellus decurio coloniae IIvir quinquennalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29300081.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Bellator. Lucius Marcellus, decurion of the colony, duumvir quinquennalis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29300081.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the god Silvanus Bellator, made by Lucius Marcellus, a high-ranking municipal official, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29300083.text_edition: Dis Manibus HAE Ance vixit annos XXVI Iovius Tusculanus praefectus equitum alae ItuYraeorum#Ituraeorum#ITYR uxori optimae castissimae obsequentissimaeF#obsequentissimae#EQUENTISSIF piissimae
EDCS-29300083.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Hae Ance lived 26 years. Iovius Tusculanus, prefect of the cavalry of the Ala Ituraeorum, to his best, most chaste, most obedient, and most dutiful wife.
EDCS-29300083.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iovius Tusculanus, a cavalry prefect, for his deceased wife, Hae Ance, who lived 26 years.
