EDCS-29300085.text_edition: Titus Cominius Titi filius Voltinia Severus Vienna (centurio) legionis II adiutricis donis donatus ab imperatore Caesare Augusto bello Dacico torquibus armillis phaleris corona vallari vixit nnos XXXXV Titus Caesernius Macedo procurator Augusti heres ex testamento posuit
EDCS-29300085.translation: Titus Cominius Severus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, from Vienna, centurion of the Second Legion Adiutrix, honored with gifts by Emperor Caesar Augustus in the Dacian War: with torques, armbands, phalerae, and a vallary crown. He lived 45 years. Titus Caesernius Macedo, procurator Augusti, placed this as heir according to the will.
EDCS-29300085.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Cominius Severus, a decorated centurion, erected by his heir, Titus Caesernius Macedo, the procurator Augusti.

EDCS-29300086.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Simplicius vix(it) nnos V
EDCS-29300086.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Simplicius lived 5 years.
EDCS-29300086.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Aurelius Simplicius, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-29300087.text_edition: Genio ordinis ---ius Titi filius Quirina vius AR
EDCS-29300087.translation: To the Genius of the order. ---ius, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, ---ius AR.
EDCS-29300087.summary: This appears to be a dedication to the Genius of a specific order or association, with the name of the dedicator only partially preserved.

EDCS-29300092.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph, mentioning Aurelia Dorotheus.

EDCS-29300096.text_edition: A  ω  in pace quiescit Macarius diaconus curante Gerontia coniuge
EDCS-29300096.translation: Alpha, Omega. In peace rests Macarius the deacon, with his wife Gerontia taking care of it.
EDCS-29300096.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription for Macarius, a deacon, arranged by his wife Gerontia, likely dating from late antiquity.

EDCS-29300098.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29300098.text_edition: Aurelia UrbicaI#Urbica#URBICI Flavio Martiniano cumpare sic suo in pacae sic carissimo etI#et#EI dulcissimae filiae mater piissima doliens sic fTratri#fratri#TRATRI et filiae maemoriam sic posuit
EDCS-29300098.translation: To Aurelia Urbica and Flavius Martinianus, her dearest companion, in peace, and to her sweetest daughter. A most devoted mother, grieving for her brother and daughter, placed this memorial.
EDCS-29300098.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a grieving mother, Aurelia Urbica, dedicated to her companion Flavius Martinianus, her daughter, and also mentioning her brother.

EDCS-29300100.text_edition: iae in pace quae vixit annos VIIII menses quattuor V Iovinus
EDCS-29300100.translation: ...in peace, who lived 9 years, 4 months. V. Iovinus.
EDCS-29300100.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an unnamed individual who lived for nine years and four months, set up by Iovinus.

EDCS-29400006.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Septimius Valens custos armorum cohortis II votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-29400006.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Septimius Valens, armorer of the Second Cohort, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-29400006.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Septimius Valens, an armorer of a Roman cohort, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29400008.text_edition: Marco Domitio Marci filio Viminacio  legionis IIII Flaviae firmae stipendiorum XVII qui vixit annos ndio
EDCS-29400008.translation: To Marcus Domitius Viminacius, son of Marcus, of the Fourth Flavian Legion 'Firm,' who served for 17 years and lived for [---] years.
EDCS-29400008.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a Roman soldier, Marcus Domitius Viminacius, of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, recording his service and approximate age at death.

EDCS-29400009.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely containing part of a name, Aelius Ver[---], with insufficient text to determine its original purpose.

EDCS-29400012.text_edition: NI  votum solvit libens merito Maximo et Paterno consulibus
EDCS-29400012.translation: [---] willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, during the consulship of Maximus and Paternus.
EDCS-29400012.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by an unnamed individual, dated to the consulship of Maximus and Paternus (likely 207 CE or 244 CE).

EDCS-29400013.text_edition: Deo sancto Herculi Titus Flavius Macrianus praefectus cohortis I Hispanorum eqquitum tribunus cohortis II Augustae Dacorum piae fidelis |(miliariae) eqquitum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400013.translation: To the holy god Hercules, Titus Flavius Macrianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Spanish Cavalry, tribune of the Second Augustan Cohort of Dacians, loyal and faithful, one thousand strong, of cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled this vow.
EDCS-29400013.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by Titus Flavius Macrianus, an officer holding multiple military commands, including prefect and tribune.

EDCS-29400016.text_edition: Dis deabusque Publius Marius Saturninus ex voto posuit libens merito
EDCS-29400016.translation: To the gods and goddesses, Publius Marius Saturninus willingly and deservedly placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-29400016.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to all gods and goddesses (Dis Deabusque) by Publius Marius Saturninus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29400017.text_edition: Fortunae Casuali Aelius Balbinus tesserarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400017.translation: To Fortuna Casualis, Aelius Balbinus, a tesserarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-29400017.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna Casualis by Aelius Balbinus, who held the military rank of tesserarius (orderly sergeant).

EDCS-29400019.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius I[---]
EDCS-29400019.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual named Gaius, with the rest of the name or dedication missing.

EDCS-29400021.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Diomedes qui vixit annos menses VIIII dies  Marcus Aurelius Achilleus veteranus ex custode armorum legionis II adiutricis vivos sic fEecit#fecit#EECIT fEilio#filio#EILIO karissimo
EDCS-29400021.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Diomedes, who lived for [---] years, 9 months, and [---] days. Marcus Aurelius Achilleus, a veteran, formerly an armorer of the Second Adiutrix Legion, made this while living for his dearest son.
EDCS-29400021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Diomedes, erected by his father, Marcus Aurelius Achilleus, a veteran and former armorer of Legio II Adiutrix, while he was still alive.

EDCS-29400022.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aureliae SabinMae#Sabinae#SABIMAE quondam coniugi Aureli Abellonis veterani quae vi=Nxit#vixit#NXIT nnos XXX Aurelii Gratus et Grata filii heredes matri bene merenti fecerunt curante Aurelio Sabino tutore puerorum supra scriptorum hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-29400022.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelia Sabina, formerly the wife of Aurelius Abellonus, a veteran, who lived for 30 years. Aurelius Gratus and Grata, her children and heirs, made this for their well-deserving mother, with Aurelius Sabinus, guardian of the aforementioned children, overseeing. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-29400022.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Sabina, wife of a veteran, erected by her children (Aurelius Gratus and Grata) and explicitly states that the monument is not transferable to future heirs, indicating a private family tomb.

EDCS-29400024.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the names of some masters or officials, with Aurelius being one of them.

EDCS-29400032.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Zenobius [dedicates this].
EDCS-29400032.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aurelius Zenobius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the chief Roman deity.

EDCS-29400033.text_edition: Danuvio sacrum Vetulenus Apronianus legatus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-29400033.translation: Sacred to the Danube. Vetulenus Apronianus, legate of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion.
EDCS-29400033.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Danube River by Vetulenus Apronianus, a legate of the II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion.

EDCS-29400036.text_edition: Dis Manibus domo sic aeternae et perpetuae securitati permittente patrono Alfius Vitalis libertus eius vivus sibi fecit
EDCS-29400036.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. From home, so for eternal and perpetual security, with the permission of his patron, Alfius Vitalis, his freedman, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-29400036.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Alfius Vitalis, a freedman, for himself during his lifetime, ensuring his perpetual security after death, with his patron's permission.

EDCS-29400037.text_edition: Bonae Deae et Panthaeo sic Dianae Silvanabus Gaius Iulius Valens praefectus legionis adiutricis piae fidelis ex voto
EDCS-29400037.translation: To the Good Goddess and to the Pantheon, likewise to Diana and the Silvanae. Gaius Julius Valens, prefect of the Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29400037.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Gaius Julius Valens, a prefect of the Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, dedicated to the Bona Dea, the Pantheon, Diana, and the Silvanae.

EDCS-29400038.text_edition: Dis et Genio provinciae Pannoniae Caius Valerius Pudens legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-29400038.translation: To the Gods and the Genius of the province of Pannonia. Gaius Valerius Pudens, propraetorian legate of Augustus.
EDCS-29400038.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Valerius Pudens, the propraetorian legate of Augustus, to the gods and the protective spirit of the province of Pannonia.

EDCS-29400039.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Caius Valerius Valentinus IIvir et Caius Iulius Victorinus et Publius Petronius Clemens aedilicii deccuriones municipii Aquincensium aram ex voto posuerunt III Idus Semptembres Falcone et Claro consulibus
EDCS-29400039.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Gaius Valerius Valentinus, duumvir, and Gaius Julius Victorinus, and Publius Petronius Clemens, aedilicii decurions of the municipality of Aquincum, placed this altar in fulfillment of a vow on the third day before the Ides of September, with Falcon and Clar as consuls.
EDCS-29400039.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Fortuna Augusta by three municipal officials of Aquincum on September 11th, 238 AD, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29400040.text_edition: Fortunae huius loci Caius Valerius Pudens legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-29400040.translation: To the Fortune of this place. Gaius Valerius Pudens, propraetorian legate of Augustus.
EDCS-29400040.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the local Fortune by Gaius Valerius Pudens, the propraetorian legate of Augustus.

EDCS-29400041.translation: To the Genius of the century. Aurelius Victor [dedicates this].
EDCS-29400041.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aurelius Victor to the protective spirit of a military century.

EDCS-29400042.text_edition: Genio |(centuriae) Claudi Tricci sacrum Aelius Sabinus signifer ex voto posuit libens merito
EDCS-29400042.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the century of Claudius Triccus. Aelius Sabinus, standard-bearer, willingly and deservedly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29400042.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Aelius Sabinus, a standard-bearer, to the protective spirit of Claudius Triccus' military century.

EDCS-29400044.text_edition: Iunoni Caelesti Quintus Caecilius Rufinus Crepereianus consularis legatus Auggustorum pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400044.translation: To Juno Caelestis, Quintus Caecilius Rufinus Crepereianus, consular legate of the Augusti, propraetor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29400044.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno Caelestis by Quintus Caecilius Rufinus Crepereianus, a high-ranking imperial official, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29400045.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Cornelia Iulia pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400045.translation: To Juno Regina, Cornelia Iulia, for herself and her household, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-29400045.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Juno Regina by Cornelia Iulia, made on behalf of herself and her family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29400047.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Caecilius Rufinus Crepereianus consularis legatus Auggustorum pro praetore votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400047.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Quintus Caecilius Rufinus Crepereianus, consular legate of the Augusti, propraetor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29400047.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Caecilius Rufinus Crepereianus, a high-ranking imperial official, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29400048.text_edition: I Iovi Optimo Maximo  Caius Iulius Rogatus miles legionis III Augustae veteranus legionis II adiutricis ex voto aram posuit libens Gentiano et Basso  consulibus
EDCS-29400048.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Iulius Rogatus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly erected this altar in accordance with a vow, during the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus.
EDCS-29400048.summary: This inscription records an altar dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a veteran soldier, Gaius Iulius Rogatus, fulfilling a vow during the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus (likely 211 CE).

EDCS-29400049.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Lucretius Priscus veteranus legionis II adiutricis votum solvit libens
EDCS-29400049.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucretius Priscus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29400049.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucretius Priscus, a veteran of the Legio II Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29400050.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Geminus Capellianus legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-29400050.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, legate of the Augustus pro praetore.
EDCS-29400050.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Iulius Geminus Capellianus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-29400052.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Dis Deabusque omnibus Titus Clementius Silvius vir egregius agens vicess praesidis solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-29400052.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to all the Gods and Goddesses, Titus Clementius Silvius, an excellent man acting as vice-president, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29400052.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all other gods and goddesses by Titus Clementius Silvius, a provincial administrator, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29400053.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Custodi Dis Deabusque Quintus Fabius Commodus tribunus legionis II adiutricis
EDCS-29400053.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Custos, and to the Gods and Goddesses, Quintus Fabius Commodus, tribune of the Second Adiutrix Legion.
EDCS-29400053.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Custos and other deities by Quintus Fabius Commodus, a military tribune.

EDCS-29400054.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  et Iunoni Reginae Iulius Quintus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis votum solvit libens merito Arriano et Papo cons(ulibus)
EDCS-29400054.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, Iulius Quintus, a veteran from the governor's staff, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of Arrianus and Papus.
EDCS-29400054.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina by Iulius Quintus, a veteran of a governor's staff, fulfilling a vow during the consulship of Arrianus and Papus (likely 243 CE).

EDCS-29400055.text_edition: H OIV   PIS  Iovi Optimo Maximo  Iunoni Reginae sacrum Septimius Iulianus miles pequarius sic legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis et Aelius Hilarus pro salute sua et suorum votum solverunt libentes merito Pio et Proculo consulibus
EDCS-29400055.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, Septimius Iulianus, a cavalry soldier (pecuarii miles sic) of the Second Adiutrix Legion, dutiful and loyal, and Aelius Hilarus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow for their own well-being and that of their household, during the consulship of Pius and Proculus.
EDCS-29400055.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina by Septimius Iulianus, a soldier, and Aelius Hilarus, on behalf of themselves and their family, fulfilling a vow during the consulship of Pius and Proculus (likely 248 CE).

EDCS-29400056.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Iunoni Reginae sacrum Marcus Ulpius Emeritus et Tiberius Claudius Exuperatus beneficiarii consulares legionis II adiutricis agentes curam legionis et coloniae Aquincensium votum libentes merito solverunt  Fausstino et Rufino consulibus
EDCS-29400056.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Juno Queen. Marcus Ulpius Emeritus and Tiberius Claudius Exuperatus, beneficiarii consulares of the Second Adiutrix Legion, serving as caretakers of the legion and the colony of Aquincum, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow. In the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus.
EDCS-29400056.summary: This inscription records a dedication made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina by two consular beneficiarii of Legio II Adiutrix, acting as caretakers for the legion and the colony of Aquincum, around the year 217 AD.

EDCS-29400057.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Neptuno Marti Lucius Valerius Italus optimo collegio negotiantium donum dedit
EDCS-29400057.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, Neptune, [and] Mars. Lucius Valerius Italus gave this gift to the excellent guild of merchants.
EDCS-29400057.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to multiple deities by Lucius Valerius Italus to an esteemed guild of merchants.

EDCS-29400061.text_edition: Minervae Victrici Caius Valerius Pudens legatus Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-29400061.translation: To Minerva the Victorious. Gaius Valerius Pudens, imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-29400061.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Minerva Victrix by Gaius Valerius Pudens, an imperial legate.

EDCS-29400062.text_edition: Adonio Septimia Marcella et Septimius Silvanus et Septimius Marcianus pro filiis ex voto posuit sic
EDCS-29400062.translation: To Adonius. Septimia Marcella and Septimius Silvanus and Septimius Marcianus placed this for their sons, by vow, thus.
EDCS-29400062.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Adonius made by Septimia Marcella and Septimius Silvanus and Septimius Marcianus on behalf of their sons, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29400063.text_edition: Balti DeIae#Deae#DIAE Divinae et DeIae#Deae#DIA SyUriae#Syriae#SURIAE templum fecit Flavius
EDCS-29400063.translation: Flavius built a temple to Balta, the Divine Goddess, and to the Syrian Goddess.
EDCS-29400063.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple by Flavius dedicated to the divine goddess Balta and the Syrian goddess.

EDCS-29400065.text_edition: Herculi Auggustorum Aurelius Firminus praefectus legionis II adiutricis ex protectore votum solvit libens merito Impperatoribus ddominis nnostris Diocletiano III et Maximiano Auggustis conssulibus
EDCS-29400065.translation: To Hercules of the Emperors. Aurelius Firminus, prefect of the Second Adiutrix Legion, formerly a protector, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of our emperors and lords Diocletian for the third time and Maximian Augustus.
EDCS-29400065.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by Aurelius Firminus, prefect of Legio II Adiutrix, in fulfillment of a vow, dated to 300 AD during the consulship of Diocletian and Maximian.

EDCS-29400066.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Publius Aelius Maximinus tabularius civitatis Eraviscorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400066.translation: To Juno Queen. Publius Aelius Maximinus, archivist of the civitas Eraviscorum, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29400066.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno Regina by Publius Aelius Maximinus, an archivist of the Eraviscan community, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29400067.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Kapito votum solvit libens merito Apro et Maximo consulibus
EDCS-29400067.translation: To Juno Queen. Capito gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of Aper and Maximus.
EDCS-29400067.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno Regina by Capito in fulfillment of a vow, dating to 207 AD during the consulship of Aper and Maximus.

EDCS-29400068.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Marcus Aurelius Eptacentus strator legati legionis II adiutricis piae fE(idelis)#f(idelis)#E Severianae ex voto votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400068.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Aurelius Eptacentus, strator to the legate of the Second Adiutrix Legion, Pious, Faithful, Severian, fulfilled his vow gladly and deservedly, as vowed.
EDCS-29400068.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Eptacentus, a strator for the legate of Legio II Adiutrix, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29400069.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus? Aurelius anus s
EDCS-29400069.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus? Aurelius...
EDCS-29400069.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, seemingly made by a Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-29400070.text_edition: consul---)  Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Genialius Martialis OMIVA dono dedit
EDCS-29400070.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Genialius Martialis gave this gift.
EDCS-29400070.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Genialius Martialis.

EDCS-29400071.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Fabia Romanus Roma beneficiarius procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400071.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Flavius Fabia Romanus, a beneficiarius of the imperial procurator from Rome, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29400071.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Flavius Fabia Romanus, an imperial official from Rome.

EDCS-29400072.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Teutano pro salute  adque incolumitate Imperatoris Caesaris Aureliani Pii Felicis Invicti Augusti totiusque domus divinae eius et civitatis Eraviscorum Titus Flavius Titanus augur et Marcus Aurelius     o consulibus
EDCS-29400072.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Teutanus, for the health and safety of Imperator Caesar Aurelianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus and his entire divine household, and of the community of the Eravisci, Titus Flavius Titanus, augur, and Marcus Aurelius... (missing name) as consuls.
EDCS-29400072.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Teutanus for the welfare of Emperor Aurelian, his family, and the Eraviscan community, likely from the late 3rd century AD during Aurelian's reign.

EDCS-29400073.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  et Iunoni Reginae Aurelius Victorinus stratorT#strator#STRAOT consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29400073.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, Aurelius Victorinus, a strator of consular rank, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29400073.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina by Aurelius Victorinus, a consular cavalryman.

EDCS-29500102.text_edition: Pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Aurelii Florus et Mercator IIviri quinquennales coloniae Aquincensium tempulum Nemesis vetustate conlapsum restituerunt Messalla et Sabino consulibus VIII Kalendas Iulias  Aurelio Suro salariario
EDCS-29500102.translation: For the health of our lord Imperator Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Aurelii Florus and Mercator, quinquennial duumvirs of the colony of Aquincum, restored the temple of Nemesis, which had collapsed from old age, on the 8th Kalends of July, with Messalla and Sabinus as consuls, and Aurelius Surus as salariarius.
EDCS-29500102.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a dilapidated temple of Nemesis by two quinquennial duumvirs in Aquincum, dedicated for the health of Emperor Caracalla in 214 AD.

EDCS-29500103.text_edition: Deae Dianae Nemesi Augustae honoribus et favoribus Gaio Iulio VictorinoI#Victorino#VICTORINI equo publico aedilicio IIvirali et Tito Flavio Luciano quaestoricio IIvirali pontificibus quinquennalibus coloniae Aquincensium Pupilius Hyliatianus sic antesstis sic numini eius deae posuit V Kalendas Iulias  Aemiliano  et Basso consulibus
EDCS-29500103.translation: To the goddess Diana Nemesis Augusta, for the honors and favors granted to Gaius Julius Victorinus, a public equestrian, aedile, and duumvir, and to Titus Flavius Lucianus, a quaestor and duumvir, both quinquennial pontiffs of the colony of Aquincum, Pupilius Hyliatianus, thus a priest and thus devoted to her divinity, erected this on the 5th Kalends of July, with Aemilianus and Bassus as consuls.
EDCS-29500103.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Nemesis Augusta by Pupilius Hyliatianus, acknowledging the divine favor bestowed upon two prominent local officials in Aquincum in 220 AD.

EDCS-29500104.text_edition: Nemesi Omnipotenti Augustae Marcus Ulpius Zosimus XII Kalendas Septembres Rustico II et Aquilino
EDCS-29500104.translation: To Nemesis Omnipotens Augusta, Marcus Ulpius Zosimus [dedicated this] on the 12th Kalends of September, during the second consulship of Rusticus and Aquilinus.
EDCS-29500104.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Nemesis Omnipotens Augusta by Marcus Ulpius Zosimus, dated to 219 AD.

EDCS-29500105.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Nemesis by Tetedius Agathimerus.

EDCS-29500106.text_edition: Genio loci Marcus Aurelius Surus Iunior Imperatore domino nostro Severo
EDCS-29500106.translation: To the Genius of the place, Marcus Aurelius Surus Junior [dedicated this] during the reign of our lord Emperor Severus.
EDCS-29500106.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the local Genius by Marcus Aurelius Surus Junior, dating to the reign of Emperor Severus (likely Septimius Severus, late 2nd/early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-29500108.text_edition: coloniae Aquincensium  Popilius Hyliatianus sic V Kalendas Iulias --- co(n)s(ulibus)
EDCS-29500108.translation: To the colony of Aquincum, Popilius Hyliatianus [dedicated this] on the 5th Kalends of July, during the consulship of...
EDCS-29500108.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication to the colony of Aquincum by Popilius Hyliatianus, dated to the 5th Kalends of July during an unpreserved consulship.

EDCS-29500110.text_edition: LR  Lucius Fabi nus P IIvir
EDCS-29500110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Fabinus, who held the office of *princeps* and *duumvir*, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-29500116.text_edition: Deo Silvano votum libiens solvi ego Legitimus
EDCS-29500116.translation: To the god Silvanus, I, Legitimus, willingly fulfilled my vow.
EDCS-29500116.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Legitimus to the god Silvanus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29500118.text_edition: Silvano Domestico  Titius Faustinus beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-29500118.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Titius Faustinus, a beneficiarius consularis.
EDCS-29500118.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Titius Faustinus, a beneficiarius consularis, likely made in a domestic or rural context.

EDCS-29500119.text_edition: Silvano Domestico Valerius Calboforus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29500119.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Valerius Calboforus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29500119.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Valerius Calboforus to Silvanus Domesticus, fulfilling a vow deservedly.

EDCS-29500120.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Augusto Gaius Iulius  Proculinus quaestionarius legionis I adiutricis et Marci Aurelii Vibianus et Lucidus milites legionis II adiutricis
EDCS-29500120.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris Augustus, Gaius Julius Proculinus, an interrogator of the First Auxiliary Legion, and Marcus Aurelius Vibianus and Lucidus, soldiers of the Second Auxiliary Legion.
EDCS-29500120.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Silvestris Augustus by Gaius Julius Proculinus, an interrogator of Legio I adiutrix, and soldiers Marcus Aurelius Vibianus and Lucidus from Legio II adiutrix, indicating military religious devotion.

EDCS-29500121.text_edition: Silvano et Silvanis sacrum Flavia Secundina  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29500121.translation: Sacred to Silvanus and the Silvanae, Flavia Secundina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-29500121.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Flavia Secundina to Silvanus and the Silvanae, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29500122.text_edition: Deo Cautopati Marcus Antonius Victorinus decurio coloniae Aquincensium  aedilis
EDCS-29500122.translation: To the god Cautopates, Marcus Antonius Victorinus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, aedile.
EDCS-29500122.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Cautopates by Marcus Antonius Victorinus, a decurion and aedile of the colony of Aquincum.

EDCS-29500123.text_edition: Fonti perenni Marcus Antonius Victorinus decurio  coloniae Aquincensium aedilis
EDCS-29500123.translation: To the eternal spring, Marcus Antonius Victorinus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, aedile.
EDCS-29500123.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to an eternal spring by Marcus Antonius Victorinus, a decurion and aedile of the colony of Aquincum.

EDCS-29500124.text_edition: Deo Cauti Marcus Antonius Victorinus decurio coloniae Aquincensium aedilis
EDCS-29500124.translation: To the god Cautus, Marcus Antonius Victorinus, decurion of the colony of Aquincum, aedile.
EDCS-29500124.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Cautus by Marcus Antonius Victorinus, a decurion and aedile of the colony of Aquincum.

EDCS-29500125.translation: Decurion of the colony of Aquincum, aedile.
EDCS-29500125.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription recording the titles of a decurion and aedile of the colony of Aquincum, likely part of a larger dedication or monument.

EDCS-29500126.text_edition: Invicto Deo Soli Mithrae  Claudius Patasio votum solvit libens merito Pedone et Bradua consulibus VIII Idus Iunias
EDCS-29500126.translation: To the Unconquered God Sol Mithras, Claudius Patasio willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow on the 8th day before the Ides of June, when Pedo and Bradua were consuls.
EDCS-29500126.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sol Mithras by Claudius Patasio, dated to the consulship of Pedo and Bradua (221 AD).

EDCS-29500127.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae sacrum Gaius Flavius Avitus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29500127.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered Sol Mithras, Gaius Flavius Avitus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29500127.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Gaius Flavius Avitus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29500128.text_edition: Terrae Matri Aurelius Droles miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29500128.translation: To Mother Earth, Aurelius Droles, a soldier of the Second Auxiliary Legion, Dutiful and Loyal, Severian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29500128.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mother Earth by Aurelius Droles, a soldier of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana.

EDCS-29500129.text_edition: Urbi Romae Lucius Cassius Marcellinus legatus Augusti pro praetore consul designatus
EDCS-29500129.translation: To the City of Rome, Lucius Cassius Marcellinus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, consul-designate.
EDCS-29500129.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the City of Rome by Lucius Cassius Marcellinus, an imperial legate and consul-designate.

EDCS-29500130.text_edition: ]  Caius Iulius Septimius Castinus consul designatus legatus Aug[ggustorum] pro praetore Pannoniae inferioris legatus leg(ionis) I Minerviae ex praecepto dominorum nnnostrorum dux vexillationum IIII Germanicarum VIII Augustae XVIII Primigeniae I Minerviae XXX Ulpiae adversus defectores et rebelles proconsul Cretae et Cyrenarum iuridicus per Apuliam Calabriam Lucaniam Bruttios curator viae Salariae curator Aeclanensium praetor tutelarius tribunus plebis quaestor tribunus militum legionis I adiutricis item V Macedonicae
EDCS-29500130.translation: Gaius Julius Septimius Castinus, consul-designate, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power for Lower Pannonia, legate of the First Minerva Legion, by order of our masters, commander of four German vexillations: the Eighth Augusta, Eighteenth Primigenia, First Minerva, Thirtieth Ulpia, against defectors and rebels; proconsul of Crete and Cyrene, legal officer for Apulia, Calabria, Lucania, and Bruttium; curator of the Via Salaria, curator of the Aeclanenses, praetor tutelarius, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, military tribune of the First Auxiliary Legion, also of the Fifth Macedonian.
EDCS-29500130.summary: This inscription provides a detailed cursus honorum for Gaius Julius Septimius Castinus, highlighting his military and administrative career under the emperors, including his role in suppressing rebels.

EDCS-29500131.text_edition: Caius Iulius Septimius Castinus consul designatus legatus Augggustorum pro praetore Pannoniae inferioris legatus legionis I Minerviae ex praecepto dominorum nnnostrorum dux vexillationum IIII Germanicarum VIII Augustae XVIII Primigeniae I Minerviae XXX Ulpiae adversus defectores et rebelles proconsul Cretae et Cyrenarum iuridicus per Apuliam Calabriam Lucaniam Bruttios curator viae Salariae curator AecTlanensium#Aeclanensium#AETLAN praetor tutelarius tribunus plebis quaestor tribunus militum legionis I adiutricis item V Macedonicae
EDCS-29500131.translation: Gaius Julius Septimius Castinus, consul-designate, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power for Lower Pannonia, legate of the First Minerva Legion, by order of our masters, commander of four German vexillations: the Eighth Augusta, Eighteenth Primigenia, First Minerva, Thirtieth Ulpia, against defectors and rebels; proconsul of Crete and Cyrene, legal officer for Apulia, Calabria, Lucania, and Bruttium; curator of the Via Salaria, curator of the Aeclanenses, praetor tutelarius, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, military tribune of the First Auxiliary Legion, also of the Fifth Macedonian.
EDCS-29500131.summary: This inscription details the extensive career of Gaius Julius Septimius Castinus, listing his military commands, administrative roles, and senatorial offices, notably serving in Pannonia and against rebels.

EDCS-29500132.text_edition: ]  Caius Iulius Septimius Castinus consul designatus legatus Augggustorum pro praetore Pannoniae inferioris legatus legionis I Minerviae ex praecepto dominorum nnnostrorum dux vexillationum IIII Germanicarum VIII Augustae XXVIII Primigeniae I Minerviae XXX Ulpiae adversus defectores et rebelles proconsul Cretae et Cyrenarum iuridicus per Apuliam Calabriam Lucaniam Bruttios curator viae Salariae curator AecTlanensium#Aeclanensium#AETLAN praetor tutelarius tribunus plebis quaestor tribunus militum legionis I adiutricis item V Macedonicae
EDCS-29500132.translation: Gaius Julius Septimius Castinus, consul-designate, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power for Lower Pannonia, legate of the First Minerva Legion, by order of our masters, commander of four German vexillations: the Eighth Augusta, Twenty-eighth Primigenia, First Minerva, Thirtieth Ulpia, against defectors and rebels; proconsul of Crete and Cyrene, legal officer for Apulia, Calabria, Lucania, and Bruttium; curator of the Via Salaria, curator of the Aeclanenses, praetor tutelarius, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, military tribune of the First Auxiliary Legion, also of the Fifth Macedonian.
EDCS-29500132.summary: This inscription enumerates the distinguished career of Gaius Julius Septimius Castinus, including his consul-designate status, various legateships, and proconsular and curatorial duties, along with his military commands.

EDCS-29500133.text_edition: Aurelius Maximus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae
EDCS-29500133.translation: Aurelius Maximus, veteran of the Second Auxiliary Legion, Dutiful and Loyal, Antoninian.
EDCS-29500133.summary: This inscription identifies Aurelius Maximus as a veteran of Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Antoniniana.

EDCS-29500134.text_edition: Claudius Pompeius Faustus decurio coloniae Aquincensium aedilicius IIviralis praefectus collegii fabrum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-29500134.translation: Claudius Pompeius Faustus, decurion of the Aquincum colony, aedile, twice duumvir, prefect of the college of artisans, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29500134.summary: This inscription records Claudius Pompeius Faustus, a local dignitary from Aquincum, fulfilling a vow, detailing his various civic and religious offices.

EDCS-29500135.text_edition: Caius Iulius Mar signifer Lucius Septimius Vitalis tesserarius Lucius Septimius Ianuarius custos armorum  Maximo et Aeliano consulibus  votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-29500135.translation: Gaius Julius Mar, standard-bearer; Lucius Septimius Vitalis, tesserarius; Lucius Septimius Ianuarius, custos armorum; when Maximus and Aelianus were consuls, they willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-29500135.summary: This inscription details a collective vow fulfillment by Gaius Julius Mar (standard-bearer), Lucius Septimius Vitalis (tesserarius), and Lucius Septimius Ianuarius (custos armorum), dated to the consulship of Maximus and Aelianus (227 AD).
